# CATEGORГҚA: 3D TO 3D
_7 modelos_

## fal-ai/hunyuan-3d/v3.1/part

**Title:** Hunyuan 3D Part Splitter
**Description:** Split 3D models into parts with Hunyuan 3D
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/hunyuan-3d/v3.1/part`
**Category:** 3d-to-3d
**Tags:** 3d, hunyuan, mesh

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `PartInput`
**Required fields:** `input_file_url`

- **`input_file_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of FBX file to split into parts. ONLY FBX format supported. Max size: 100MB, face count вүӨ30,000. Recommended: AIGC-generated models.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `PartOutput`
- **`result_files`** *(list<File>)* вҖ” List of generated part files in FBX format

---

## fal-ai/hunyuan-3d/v3.1/smart-topology

**Title:** Hunyuan 3D Smart Topology
**Description:** Optimize 3D mesh topology with Hunyuan 3D Smart Topology.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/hunyuan-3d/v3.1/smart-topology`
**Category:** 3d-to-3d
**Tags:** 3d, hunyuan, topology

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SmartTopologyInput`
**Required fields:** _none_

- **`input_file_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"https://v3b.fal.media/files/b/0a8c09c0/VYDiCTcDGK55qY2-idGbX_model.glb"` вҖ” URL of GLB or OBJ file to optimize topology. Max size: 200MB.
- **`input_file_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"glb"` вҖ” Input 3D file format.
  Possible values: `glb`, `obj`
- **`polygon_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"triangle"` вҖ” Output polygon type. triangle: triangular faces only. quadrilateral: mixed quad and triangle faces.
  Possible values: `triangle`, `quadrilateral`
- **`face_level`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"medium"` вҖ” Target polygon density. high: more detail/polygons, medium: balanced, low: fewer polygons.
  Possible values: `high`, `medium`, `low`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SmartTopologyOutput`
- **`model_urls`** *()* вҖ” URLs for different 3D model formats
- **`model_glb`** *()* вҖ” Processed 3D model with optimized topology (primary file).

---

## fal-ai/hunyuan-part

**Title:** Hunyuan Part
**Description:** Use the capabilities of hunyuan part to generate point clouds from your 3D files.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/hunyuan-part`
**Category:** 3d-to-3d
**Tags:** 3D-to-3D, point-cloud

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `HunyuanPartInput`
**Required fields:** `model_file_url`

- **`model_file_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the 3D model file (.glb or .obj) to process for segmentation.
- **`point_prompt_x`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” X coordinate of the point prompt for segmentation (normalized space -1 to 1).
  Range: min: -1, max: 1
- **`point_prompt_y`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Y coordinate of the point prompt for segmentation (normalized space -1 to 1).
  Range: min: -1, max: 1
- **`point_prompt_z`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Z coordinate of the point prompt for segmentation (normalized space -1 to 1).
  Range: min: -1, max: 1
- **`point_num`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `100000` вҖ” Number of points to sample from the mesh.
  Range: min: 10000, max: 500000
- **`use_normal`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use normal information for segmentation.
- **`noise_std`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Standard deviation of noise to add to sampled points.
  Range: min: 0, max: 0.02
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and input will produce the same segmentation results.
        

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `HunyuanPartOutput`
- **`iou_scores`** *(list<number>)* вҖ” IoU scores for each of the three masks.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed value used for generation.
- **`segmented_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Segmented 3D mesh with mask applied.
- **`mask_2_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Mesh showing segmentation mask 2.
- **`best_mask_index`** *(integer)* вҖ” Index of the best mask (1, 2, or 3) based on IoU score.
- **`mask_3_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Mesh showing segmentation mask 3.
- **`mask_1_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Mesh showing segmentation mask 1.

---

## fal-ai/meshy/v5/remesh

**Title:** Meshy 5 Remesh
**Description:** Meshy-5 remesh allows you to remesh and export existing 3D models into various formats
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/meshy/v5/remesh`
**Category:** 3d-to-3d
**Tags:** 3d-to-3d

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `RemeshInput`
**Required fields:** `model_url`

- **`model_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL or base64 data URI of a 3D model to remesh. Supports .glb, .gltf, .obj, .fbx, .stl formats. Can be a publicly accessible URL or data URI with MIME type application/octet-stream.
- **`target_formats`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” Default: `["glb"]` вҖ” List of target formats for the remeshed model.
- **`topology`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"triangle"` вҖ” Specify the topology of the generated model. Quad for smooth surfaces, Triangle for detailed geometry.
  Possible values: `quad`, `triangle`
- **`target_polycount`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30000` вҖ” Target number of polygons in the generated model. Actual count may vary based on geometry complexity.
  Range: min: 100, max: 300000
- **`resize_height`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Resize the model to a certain height measured in meters. Set to 0 for no resizing.
  Range: min: 0
- **`origin_at`** *(string)* вҖ” Position of the origin. None means no effect.
  Possible values: `bottom`, `center`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `RemeshOutput`
- **`model_glb`** *(?)* вҖ” Remeshed 3D object in GLB format (if GLB was requested).
- **`model_urls`** *()* вҖ” URLs for different 3D model formats

---

## fal-ai/meshy/v5/retexture

**Title:** Meshy 5 Retexture
**Description:** Meshy-5 retexture applies new, high-quality textures to existing 3D models using either text prompts or reference images. It supports PBR material generation for realistic, production-ready results.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/meshy/v5/retexture`
**Category:** 3d-to-3d
**Tags:** 3d-to-3d

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `RetextureInput`
**Required fields:** `model_url`

- **`model_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL or base64 data URI of a 3D model to texture. Supports .glb, .gltf, .obj, .fbx, .stl formats. Can be a publicly accessible URL or data URI with MIME type application/octet-stream.
- **`text_style_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Describe your desired texture style using text. Maximum 600 characters. Required if image_style_url is not provided.
- **`image_style_url`** *(string)* вҖ” 2D image to guide the texturing process. Supports .jpg, .jpeg, and .png formats. Required if text_style_prompt is not provided. If both are provided, image_style_url takes precedence.
- **`enable_original_uv`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Use the original UV mapping of the model instead of generating new UVs. If the model has no original UV, output quality may be reduced.
- **`enable_pbr`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Generate PBR Maps (metallic, roughness, normal) in addition to base color.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, input data will be checked for safety before processing.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `RetextureOutput`
- **`image_style_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The image URL used for texturing (if provided)
- **`model_glb`** *()* вҖ” Retextured 3D object in GLB format.
- **`text_style_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt used for texturing (if provided)
- **`texture_urls`** *(list<TextureFiles>)* вҖ” Array of texture file objects
- **`model_urls`** *()* вҖ” URLs for different 3D model formats
- **`thumbnail`** *(?)* вҖ” Preview thumbnail of the retextured model

---

## fal-ai/sam-3/3d-align

**Title:** Sam 3
**Description:** SAM 3D enables full scene reconstructions, placing objects and humans in a shared context together.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sam-3/3d-align`
**Category:** 3d-to-3d
**Tags:** align, 3D, 

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SAM3DAlignmentInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `body_mesh_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the original image used for MoGe depth estimation
- **`body_mesh_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the SAM-3D Body mesh file (.ply or .glb) to align
- **`body_mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the human mask image. If not provided, uses full image.
- **`object_mesh_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional URL of SAM-3D Object mesh (.glb) to create combined scene
- **`focal_length`** *(number)* вҖ” Focal length from SAM-3D Body metadata. If not provided, estimated from MoGe.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SAM3DAlignmentOutput`
- **`model_glb`** *()* вҖ” Aligned body mesh in GLB format (for 3D preview)
- **`scene_glb`** *(?)* вҖ” Combined scene with body + object meshes in GLB format (only when object_mesh_url provided)
- **`body_mesh_ply`** *()* вҖ” Aligned body mesh in PLY format
- **`visualization`** *()* вҖ” Visualization of aligned mesh overlaid on input image
- **`metadata`** *()* вҖ” Alignment info (scale, translation, etc.)

---

## fal-ai/ultrashape

**Title:** Ultrashape
**Description:** UltraShape-1.0 is a 3D diffusion framework that generates high-fidelity 3D geometry through coarse-to-fine geometric refinement.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ultrashape`
**Category:** 3d-to-3d
**Tags:** 3d-to-3d

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `UltraShapeRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `model_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the reference image for mesh refinement.
- **`model_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the coarse mesh (.glb or .obj) to refine.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” Diffusion steps.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`octree_resolution`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1024` вҖ” Marching cubes resolution.
  Range: min: 128, max: 1024
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `42` вҖ” Random seed.
- **`remove_background`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Remove image background.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `UltraShapeResponse`
- **`model_glb`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D object.

---

# CATEGORГҚA: AUDIO TO AUDIO
_26 modelos_

## fal-ai/ace-step/audio-inpaint

**Title:** ACE-Step
**Description:** Modify a portion of provided audio with lyrics and/or style using ACE-Step
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ace-step/audio-inpaint`
**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** audio-to-audio, audio-inpaint, audio-repaint

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ACEStepAudioInpaintRequest`
**Required fields:** `tags`, `audio_url`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the audio file to be inpainted.
- **`start_time_relative_to`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"start"` вҖ” Whether the start time is relative to the start or end of the audio.
  Possible values: `start`, `end`
- **`start_time`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” start time in seconds for the inpainting process.
  Range: min: 0, max: 240
- **`end_time_relative_to`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"start"` вҖ” Whether the end time is relative to the start or end of the audio.
  Possible values: `start`, `end`
- **`end_time`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” end time in seconds for the inpainting process.
  Range: min: 0, max: 240
- **`tags`** *(string)* вҖ” Comma-separated list of genre tags to control the style of the generated audio.
- **`lyrics`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Lyrics to be sung in the audio. If not provided or if [inst] or [instrumental] is the content of this field, no lyrics will be sung. Use control structures like [verse], [chorus] and [bridge] to control the structure of the song.
- **`variance`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Variance for the inpainting process. Higher values can lead to more diverse results.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`number_of_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `27` вҖ” Number of steps to generate the audio.
  Range: min: 3, max: 60
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If not provided, a random seed will be used.
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"euler"` вҖ” Scheduler to use for the generation process.
  Possible values: `euler`, `heun`
- **`guidance_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"apg"` вҖ” Type of CFG to use for the generation process.
  Possible values: `cfg`, `apg`, `cfg_star`
- **`granularity_scale`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `10` вҖ” Granularity scale for the generation process. Higher values can reduce artifacts.
  Range: min: -100, max: 100
- **`guidance_interval`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Guidance interval for the generation. 0.5 means only apply guidance in the middle steps (0.25 * infer_steps to 0.75 * infer_steps)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`guidance_interval_decay`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Guidance interval decay for the generation. Guidance scale will decay from guidance_scale to min_guidance_scale in the interval. 0.0 means no decay.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” Guidance scale for the generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 200
- **`minimum_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Minimum guidance scale for the generation after the decay.
  Range: min: 0, max: 200
- **`tag_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Tag guidance scale for the generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`lyric_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.5` вҖ” Lyric guidance scale for the generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ACEStepAudioInpaintResponse`
- **`lyrics`** *(string)* вҖ” The lyrics used in the generation process.
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The random seed used for the generation process.
- **`tags`** *(string)* вҖ” The genre tags used in the generation process.

---

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the audio file to be outpainted.
- **`extend_before_duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Duration in seconds to extend the audio from the start.
  Range: min: 0, max: 240

  Range: min: 0, max: 240
- **`tags`** *(string)* вҖ” Comma-separated list of genre tags to control the style of the generated audio.
- **`lyrics`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Lyrics to be sung in the audio. If not provided or if [inst] or [instrumental] is the content of this field, no lyrics will be sung. Use control structures like [verse], [chorus] and [bridge] to control the structure of the song.
- **`number_of_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `27` вҖ” Number of steps to generate the audio.
  Range: min: 3, max: 60
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If not provided, a random seed will be used.
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"euler"` вҖ” Scheduler to use for the generation process.
  Possible values: `euler`, `heun`
- **`guidance_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"apg"` вҖ” Type of CFG to use for the generation process.
  Possible values: `cfg`, `apg`, `cfg_star`
- **`granularity_scale`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `10` вҖ” Granularity scale for the generation process. Higher values can reduce artifacts.
  Range: min: -100, max: 100
- **`guidance_interval`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Guidance interval for the generation. 0.5 means only apply guidance in the middle steps (0.25 * infer_steps to 0.75 * infer_steps)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`guidance_interval_decay`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Guidance interval decay for the generation. Guidance scale will decay from guidance_scale to min_guidance_scale in the interval. 0.0 means no decay.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” Guidance scale for the generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 200
- **`minimum_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Minimum guidance scale for the generation after the decay.
  Range: min: 0, max: 200
- **`tag_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Tag guidance scale for the generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`lyric_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.5` вҖ” Lyric guidance scale for the generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ACEStepResponse`
- **`lyrics`** *(string)* вҖ” The lyrics used in the generation process.
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The random seed used for the generation process.
- **`tags`** *(string)* вҖ” The genre tags used in the generation process.

## fal-ai/ace-step/audio-to-audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ACEStepAudioToAudioRequest`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the audio file to be outpainted.
- **`edit_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"remix"` вҖ” Whether to edit the lyrics only or remix the audio.

- **`tags`** *(string)* вҖ” Comma-separated list of genre tags to control the style of the generated audio.
- **`lyrics`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Lyrics to be sung in the audio. If not provided or if [inst] or [instrumental] is the content of this field, no lyrics will be sung. Use control structures like [verse], [chorus] and [bridge] to control the structure of the song.
- **`number_of_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `27` вҖ” Number of steps to generate the audio.
  Range: min: 3, max: 60
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If not provided, a random seed will be used.
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"euler"` вҖ” Scheduler to use for the generation process.
  Possible values: `euler`, `heun`
- **`guidance_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"apg"` вҖ” Type of CFG to use for the generation process.
  Possible values: `cfg`, `apg`, `cfg_star`
- **`granularity_scale`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `10` вҖ” Granularity scale for the generation process. Higher values can reduce artifacts.
  Range: min: -100, max: 100
- **`guidance_interval`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Guidance interval for the generation. 0.5 means only apply guidance in the middle steps (0.25 * infer_steps to 0.75 * infer_steps)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`guidance_interval_decay`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Guidance interval decay for the generation. Guidance scale will decay from guidance_scale to min_guidance_scale in the interval. 0.0 means no decay.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” Guidance scale for the generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 200
- **`minimum_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Minimum guidance scale for the generation after the decay.
  Range: min: 0, max: 200
- **`tag_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Tag guidance scale for the generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`lyric_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.5` вҖ” Lyric guidance scale for the generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`original_seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Original seed of the audio file.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ACEStepAudioToAudioResponse`

- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio file.

- **`tags`** *(string)* вҖ” The genre tags used in the generation process.

## fal-ai/audio-understanding

**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** utility, audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `AudioUnderstandingInput`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`, `prompt`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the audio file to analyze
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The question or prompt about the audio content.
- **`detailed_analysis`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to request a more detailed analysis of the audio

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `AudioUnderstandingOutput`
- **`output`** *(string)* вҖ” The analysis of the audio content based on the prompt

## fal-ai/deepfilternet3

**Title:** DeepFilterNet 3
**Description:** Enhance speech audio by removing background noise and upsampling to 48KHz
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/deepfilternet3`
**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** speech-enhancement

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `DeepFilterNet3Input`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the audio to enhance.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`audio_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"mp3"` вҖ” The format for the output audio.
  Possible values: `mp3`, `aac`, `m4a`, `ogg`, `opus`, `flac`, `wav`
- **`bitrate`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"192k"` вҖ” The bitrate of the output audio.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `DeepFilterNet3Output`
- **`timings`** *()* вҖ” Timings for each step in the pipeline.
- **`audio_file`** *()* вҖ” The audio file that was enhanced.

## fal-ai/demucs

**Title:** Demucs
**Description:** SOTA stemming model for voice, drums, bass, guitar and more.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/demucs`
**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `DemucsInput`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the audio file to separate into stems
- **`model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"htdemucs_6s"` вҖ” Demucs model to use for separation
  Possible values: `htdemucs`, `htdemucs_ft`, `htdemucs_6s`, `hdemucs_mmi`, `mdx`, `mdx_extra`, `mdx_q`, `mdx_extra_q`
- **`stems`** *(array)* вҖ” Default: `["vocals","drums","bass","other","guitar","piano"]` вҖ” Specific stems to extract. If None, extracts all available stems. Available stems depend on model: vocals, drums, bass, other, guitar, piano (for 6s model)
- **`segment_length`** *(integer)* вҖ” Length in seconds of each segment for processing. Smaller values use less memory but may reduce quality. Default is model-specific.
- **`shifts`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of random shifts for equivariant stabilization. Higher values improve quality but increase processing time.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`overlap`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.25` вҖ” Overlap between segments (0.0 to 1.0). Higher values may improve quality but increase processing time.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"mp3"` вҖ” Output audio format for the separated stems
  Possible values: `wav`, `mp3`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `DemucsOutput`
- **`drums`** *(?)* вҖ” Separated drums audio file
- **`piano`** *(?)* вҖ” Separated piano audio file (only available for 6s models)
- **`other`** *(?)* вҖ” Separated other instruments audio file
- **`guitar`** *(?)* вҖ” Separated guitar audio file (only available for 6s models)
- **`bass`** *(?)* вҖ” Separated bass audio file
- **`vocals`** *(?)* вҖ” Separated vocals audio file

---

## fal-ai/dia-tts/voice-clone

**Title:** Dia Tts
**Description:** Clone dialog voices from a sample audio and generate dialogs from text prompts using the Dia TTS which leverages advanced AI techniques to create high-quality text-to-speech.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/dia-tts/voice-clone`
**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** speech

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `CloneRequest`
**Required fields:** `text`

- **`text`** *(string)* вҖ” The text to be converted to speech.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `DiaCloneOutput`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated speech audio

---

## fal-ai/elevenlabs/audio-isolation

**Title:** ElevenLabs Audio Isolation
**Description:** Isolate audio tracks using ElevenLabs advanced audio isolation technology.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/elevenlabs/audio-isolation`
**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `AudioIsolationRequest`
**Required fields:** _none_

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the audio file to isolate voice from
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Video file to use for audio isolation. Either `audio_url` or `video_url` must be provided.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `TTSOutput`
- **`timestamps`** *(array)* вҖ” Timestamps for each word in the generated speech. Only returned if `timestamps` is set to True in the request.
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio file

---

## fal-ai/elevenlabs/voice-changer

**Title:** ElevenLabs Voice Changer
**Description:** Change the voices in your audios with voices in ElevenLabs!
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/elevenlabs/voice-changer`
**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** voice-change, audio-to-audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `VoiceChangerRequest`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The input audio file
- **`voice`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"Rachel"` вҖ” The voice to use for speech generation
- **`remove_background_noise`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set, will remove the background noise from your audio input using our audio isolation model.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"mp3_44100_128"` вҖ” Output format of the generated audio. Formatted as codec_sample_rate_bitrate.
  Possible values: `mp3_22050_32`, `mp3_44100_32`, `mp3_44100_64`, `mp3_44100_96`, `mp3_44100_128`, `mp3_44100_192`, `pcm_8000`, `pcm_16000`, `pcm_22050`, `pcm_24000`, `pcm_44100`, `pcm_48000`, `ulaw_8000`, `alaw_8000`, `opus_48000_32`, `opus_48000_64`, `opus_48000_96`, `opus_48000_128`, `opus_48000_192`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `VoiceChangerOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio file

---

## fal-ai/ffmpeg-api/merge-audios

**Title:** FFmpeg API [Merge Audios]
**Description:** Merge audios into a single audio using FFmpeg API!
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ffmpeg-api/merge-audios`
**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** ffmpeg

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MergeAudiosInput`
**Required fields:** `audio_urls`

- **`audio_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” List of audio URLs to merge in order. The 0th stream of the audio will be considered as the merge candidate.
- **`output_format`** *(string)* вҖ” Output format of the combined audio. If not used, will be determined automatically using FFMPEG. Formatted as codec_sample_rate_bitrate.
  Possible values: `mp3_22050_32`, `mp3_44100_32`, `mp3_44100_64`, `mp3_44100_96`, `mp3_44100_128`, `mp3_44100_192`, `pcm_8000`, `pcm_16000`, `pcm_22050`, `pcm_24000`, `pcm_44100`, `pcm_48000`, `ulaw_8000`, `alaw_8000`, `opus_48000_32`, `opus_48000_64`, `opus_48000_96`, `opus_48000_128`, `opus_48000_192`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MergeAudiosOutput`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” Merged audio file

---

**Description:** Create Voices to be used with Kling Models Voice Control

- **`voice_id`** *(string)* вҖ” Unique identifier for the created voice

## fal-ai/lava-sr

**Title:** Lava SR
**Description:** Enhance muffled 16 kHz speech audio into crystal-clear 48 kHz, with denoising for particularly bad inputs.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/lava-sr`
**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** lava-sr, audo-upscaler

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LavaSRInput`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the audio file to enhance.
- **`denoise`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, applies UL-UNAS noise filtering before bandwidth extension.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`audio_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"mp3"` вҖ” The format for the output audio.
  Possible values: `mp3`, `aac`, `m4a`, `ogg`, `opus`, `flac`, `wav`
- **`bitrate`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"192k"` вҖ” The bitrate of the output audio.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LavaSROutput`
- **`timings`** *()* вҖ” Timings for each step in the pipeline.
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The enhanced audio file.

---

## fal-ai/nova-sr

**Title:** Nova SR
**Description:** Enhance muffled 16 kHz speech audio into crystal-clear 48 kHz
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/nova-sr`

- **`audio_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"mp3"` вҖ” The format for the output audio.
  Possible values: `mp3`, `aac`, `m4a`, `ogg`, `opus`, `flac`, `wav`
- **`bitrate`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"192k"` вҖ” The bitrate of the output audio.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `NovaSROutput`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The enhanced audio file.
- **`timings`** *()* вҖ” Timings for each step in the pipeline.

## fal-ai/personaplex

**Title:** Personaplex
**Description:** PersonaPlex is a real-time, full-duplex speech-to-speech conversational model that enables persona control through text-based role prompts and audio-based voice conditioning.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/personaplex`
**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ConversationInput`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"You are a wise and friendly teacher. Answer questions or provide advice in a clear and engaging way."` вҖ” Text prompt describing the AI persona and conversation context.
- **`voice`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"NATF2"` вҖ” Voice ID for the AI response. NAT = natural, VAR = variety. F = female, M = male. Ignored when voice_audio_url is provided.
  Possible values: `NATF0`, `NATF1`, `NATF2`, `NATF3`, `NATM0`, `NATM1`, `NATM2`, `NATM3`, `VARF0`, `VARF1`, `VARF2`, `VARF3`, `VARF4`, `VARM0`, `VARM1`, `VARM2`, `VARM3`, `VARM4`
- **`voice_audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to a voice sample audio for on-the-fly voice cloning. When provided, the AI responds in the cloned voice instead of the preset 'voice'. 10+ seconds of clear speech recommended. Billed at 2x rate.
- **`temperature_audio`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.8` вҖ” Audio sampling temperature. Higher values produce more diverse outputs.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`temperature_text`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” Text sampling temperature. Higher values produce more diverse outputs.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`top_k_audio`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `250` вҖ” Top-K sampling for audio tokens.
  Range: min: 1, max: 2048
- **`top_k_text`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” Top-K sampling for text tokens.
  Range: min: 1, max: 1000
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the input audio file (user's speech).

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ConversationOutput`
- **`duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Duration of the generated audio in seconds.
- **`text`** *(string)* вҖ” Transcribed text of the AI's response.
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated AI response audio (WAV, 24kHz).
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.

---

## fal-ai/personaplex/realtime

**Title:** Personaplex
**Description:** PersonaPlex is a real-time, full-duplex speech-to-speech conversational model that enables persona control through text-based role prompts and audio-based voice conditioning.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/personaplex/realtime`
**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** audio-to-audio, realtime, conversational

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `RealtimeConversationInput`
**Required fields:** `audio`

- **`voice_audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `null` вҖ” URL to a voice sample audio for on-the-fly voice cloning. When provided, the AI responds in the cloned voice instead of the preset 'voice'. 10+ seconds of clear speech recommended. Billed at 2x rate.
- **`top_k_audio`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `250` вҖ” Top-K sampling for audio tokens.
  Range: min: 1, max: 2048
- **`voice`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"NATF2"` вҖ” Voice ID for the AI response. NAT = natural, VAR = variety. F = female, M = male. Ignored when voice_audio_url is provided.
  Possible values: `NATF0`, `NATF1`, `NATF2`, `NATF3`, `NATM0`, `NATM1`, `NATM2`, `NATM3`, `VARF0`, `VARF1`, `VARF2`, `VARF3`, `VARF4`, `VARM0`, `VARM1`, `VARM2`, `VARM3`, `VARM4`
- **`temperature_audio`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.8` вҖ” Audio sampling temperature. Higher values produce more diverse outputs.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`top_k_text`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” Top-K sampling for text tokens.
  Range: min: 1, max: 1000
- **`temperature_text`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” Text sampling temperature. Higher values produce more diverse outputs.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"You are a wise and friendly teacher. Answer questions or provide advice in a clear and engaging way."` вҖ” Text prompt describing the AI persona and conversation context.
- **`audio`** *(string)* вҖ” Input audio chunk (PCM s16le, 24kHz mono). Base64-encoded in JSON transport.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `null` вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `RealtimeConversationOutput`
- **`text`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Generated text tokens for this chunk.
- **`audio`** *(string)* вҖ” Generated audio chunk (PCM s16le, 24kHz mono). Base64-encoded in JSON transport.

---

**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** clone-voice, voice-clone

- **`reference_text`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional reference text that was used when creating the speaker embedding. Providing this can improve synthesis quality when using a cloned voice.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Qwen3CloneVoiceOutput`
- **`speaker_embedding`** *()* вҖ” The generated speaker embedding file in safetensors format.

- **`reference_text`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional reference text that was used when creating the speaker embedding. Providing this can improve synthesis quality when using a cloned voice.

## fal-ai/sam-audio/separate

**Title:** Sam Audio
**Description:** Audio separation with SAM Audio. Isolate any sound using natural languageвҖ”professional-grade audio editing made simple for creators, researchers, and accessibility applications.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sam-audio/separate`
**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** audio-to-audio, sam-audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SAMAudioInput`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`, `prompt`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the audio file to process (WAV, MP3, FLAC supported)
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt describing the sound to isolate.
- **`predict_spans`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Automatically predict temporal spans where the target sound occurs.
- **`reranking_candidates`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of candidates to generate and rank. Higher improves quality but increases latency and cost.
  Range: min: 1, max: 7
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `quality`
- **`max_chunk_duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `60` вҖ” Maximum audio duration (in seconds) to process in a single pass. Longer audio will be chunked with overlap and blended.
  Range: min: 10, max: 60
- **`chunk_overlap`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Overlap duration (in seconds) between chunks for crossfade blending.
  Range: min: 0, max: 30
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"wav"` вҖ” Output audio format.
  Possible values: `wav`, `mp3`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SAMAudioSeparateOutput`
- **`residual`** *()* вҖ” Everything else in the audio.
- **`target`** *()* вҖ” The isolated target sound.
- **`duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Duration of the output audio in seconds.
- **`sample_rate`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `48000` вҖ” Sample rate of the output audio in Hz.

---

## fal-ai/sam-audio/span-separate

**Title:** Sam Audio
**Description:** Audio separation with SAM Audio. Isolate any sound using natural languageвҖ”professional-grade audio editing made simple for creators, researchers, and accessibility applications.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sam-audio/span-separate`
**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** audio-to-audio, sam-audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SAMAudioSpanInput`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`, `spans`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the audio file to process.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt describing the sound to isolate. Optional but recommended - helps the model identify what type of sound to extract from the span.
- **`spans`** *(list<AudioTimeSpan>)* вҖ” Time spans where the target sound occurs which should be isolated.
- **`reranking_candidates`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of candidates to generate and rank. Higher improves quality but increases latency and cost. Requires text prompt; ignored for span-only separation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 7
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `quality`
- **`max_chunk_duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `60` вҖ” Maximum audio duration (in seconds) to process in a single pass. Longer audio will be chunked with overlap and blended.
  Range: min: 10, max: 60
- **`chunk_overlap`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Overlap duration (in seconds) between chunks for crossfade blending.
  Range: min: 0, max: 30
- **`use_sound_activity_ranking`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Use sound activity detection to rank reranking candidates based on how well each candidate's non-silent regions match the provided spans. Enables effective reranking even without a text prompt (span-only separation). Requires reranking_candidates > 1.
- **`trim_to_span`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Trim output audio to only include the specified span time range. If False, returns the full audio length with the target sound isolated throughout.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"wav"` вҖ” Output audio format.
  Possible values: `wav`, `mp3`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SAMAudioSpanSeparateOutput`
- **`residual`** *()* вҖ” Everything else in the audio.
- **`target`** *()* вҖ” The isolated target sound.
- **`duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Duration of the output audio in seconds.
- **`sample_rate`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `48000` вҖ” Sample rate of the output audio in Hz.

---

## fal-ai/stable-audio-25/audio-to-audio

**Title:** Stable Audio 2.5
**Description:** Generate high quality music and sound effects using Stable Audio 2.5 from StabilityAI
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/stable-audio-25/audio-to-audio`
**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `AudioToAudioInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `audio_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to guide the audio generation
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The audio clip to transform
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.8` вҖ” Sometimes referred to as denoising, this parameter controls how much influence the `audio_url` parameter has on the generated audio. A value of 0 would yield audio that is identical to the input. A value of 1 would be as if you passed in no audio at all.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” The number of steps to denoise the audio for
  Range: min: 4, max: 8
- **`total_seconds`** *(integer)* вҖ” The duration of the audio clip to generate. If not provided, it will be set to the duration of the input audio.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” How strictly the diffusion process adheres to the prompt text (higher values make your audio closer to your prompt). 
  Range: min: 1, max: 25
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`seed`** *(integer)*

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `AudioToAudioOutput`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio clip
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The random seed used for generation

---

## fal-ai/stable-audio-25/inpaint

**Title:** Stable Audio 25
**Description:** Generate high quality music and sound effects using Stable Audio 2.5 from StabilityAI
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/stable-audio-25/inpaint`
**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `InpaintInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `audio_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to guide the audio generation
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The audio clip to inpaint
- **`seconds_total`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `190` вҖ” The duration of the audio clip to generate. If not provided, it will be set to the duration of the input audio.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” How strictly the diffusion process adheres to the prompt text (higher values make your audio closer to your prompt). 
  Range: min: 1, max: 25
- **`mask_start`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” The start point of the audio mask
  Range: min: 0, max: 190
- **`mask_end`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `190` вҖ” The end point of the audio mask
  Range: min: 0, max: 190
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” The number of steps to denoise the audio for
  Range: min: 4, max: 8
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`seed`** *(integer)*

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `InpaintOutput`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio clip
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The random seed used for generation

---

## fal-ai/tada/1b/text-to-speech

**Title:** Tada TTS 1B
**Description:** A unified speech-language model that synchronizes speech and text into a single, cohesive stream via 1:1 alignment. Lighter 1B variant
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/tada/1b/text-to-speech`
**Category:** audio-to-audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Input`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`, `prompt`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the reference audio file for voice cloning. The model will replicate this speaker's voice characteristics.
- **`transcript`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Transcript of the reference audio. For non-English audio, providing a transcript is required since the built-in ASR is English-only.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text to synthesize into speech using the reference speaker's voice.
- **`language`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"en"` вҖ” Language for text alignment. Use the appropriate code for non-English synthesis.
  Possible values: `en`, `ar`, `ch`, `de`, `es`, `fr`, `it`, `ja`, `pl`, `pt`
- **`num_extra_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Number of extra autoregressive steps for speech continuation beyond the input text. Useful for generating trailing prosody or silence.
  Range: min: 0, max: 50
- **`temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.6` вҖ” Sampling temperature for text token generation. Higher values produce more varied output.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`top_p`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.9` вҖ” Top-p (nucleus) sampling parameter for text generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`repetition_penalty`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.1` вҖ” Penalty applied to repeated tokens during generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 2
- **`acoustic_cfg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.6` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale for acoustic feature generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`noise_temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.9` вҖ” Temperature for noise in the flow matching diffusion process.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `20` вҖ” Number of ODE solver steps for flow matching acoustic generation. More steps improve quality at the cost of speed.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`speed_up_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Factor to speed up or slow down the generated speech. Values > 1.0 speed up, < 1.0 slow down.
  Range: min: 0.5, max: 2
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"wav"` вҖ” The format of the output audio file.
  Possible values: `wav`, `mp3`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MiniOutput`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated speech audio file.

---

## fal-ai/tada/3b/text-to-speech

**Title:** Tada
**Description:** A unified speech-language model that synchronizes speech and text into a single, cohesive stream via 1:1 alignment.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/tada/3b/text-to-speech`
**Category:** audio-to-audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Input`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`, `prompt`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the reference audio file for voice cloning. The model will replicate this speaker's voice characteristics.
- **`transcript`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Transcript of the reference audio. For non-English audio, providing a transcript is required since the built-in ASR is English-only.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text to synthesize into speech using the reference speaker's voice.
- **`language`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"en"` вҖ” Language for text alignment. Use the appropriate code for non-English synthesis.
  Possible values: `en`, `ar`, `ch`, `de`, `es`, `fr`, `it`, `ja`, `pl`, `pt`
- **`num_extra_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Number of extra autoregressive steps for speech continuation beyond the input text. Useful for generating trailing prosody or silence.
  Range: min: 0, max: 50
- **`temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.6` вҖ” Sampling temperature for text token generation. Higher values produce more varied output.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`top_p`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.9` вҖ” Top-p (nucleus) sampling parameter for text generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`repetition_penalty`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.1` вҖ” Penalty applied to repeated tokens during generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 2
- **`acoustic_cfg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.6` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale for acoustic feature generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`noise_temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.9` вҖ” Temperature for noise in the flow matching diffusion process.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `20` вҖ” Number of ODE solver steps for flow matching acoustic generation. More steps improve quality at the cost of speed.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`speed_up_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Factor to speed up or slow down the generated speech. Values > 1.0 speed up, < 1.0 slow down.
  Range: min: 0.5, max: 2
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"wav"` вҖ” The format of the output audio file.
  Possible values: `wav`, `mp3`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated speech audio file.

---

## fal-ai/workflow-utilities/audio-compressor

**Title:** Workflow Utilities
**Description:** FFMPEG Utility for Audio Compression
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/workflow-utilities/audio-compressor`
**Category:** audio-to-audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `AudioCompressorInput`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the audio file to compress
- **`threshold`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `-18` вҖ” Threshold level in dB above which compression is applied (-60 to 0)
  Range: min: -60, max: 0
- **`ratio`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Compression ratio (1 = no compression, higher = more compression)
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`attack`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Attack time in milliseconds (how fast compression starts)
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 2000
- **`release`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” Release time in milliseconds (how fast compression stops)
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 9000
- **`makeup`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” Makeup gain in dB to compensate for volume reduction
  Range: min: 0, max: 64
- **`knee`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.83` вҖ” Knee width in dB for soft knee compression (0 = hard knee)
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`output_bitrate`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"192k"` вҖ” Output audio bitrate
  Possible values: `128k`, `192k`, `256k`, `320k`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `AudioCompressorOutput`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The compressed audio file

---

## fal-ai/workflow-utilities/impulse-response

**Title:** Workflow Utilities
**Description:** FFMPEG Utility for Impulse Response
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/workflow-utilities/impulse-response`
**Category:** audio-to-audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImpulseResponseInput`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`, `impulse_response_url`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the main audio file to process
- **`impulse_response_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the impulse response WAV file (reverb/effect profile)
- **`dry_level`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” Level of the original (dry) signal in the mix (0.0-1.0)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`wet_level`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.3` вҖ” Level of the processed (wet) signal in the mix (0.0-1.0)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`loudness_i`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `-18` вҖ” Target integrated loudness in LUFS (typically -24 to -14)
  Range: min: -70, max: 0
- **`loudness_lra`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” Loudness Range target in LU (typically 5-15)
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`loudness_tp`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `-1.5` вҖ” Maximum true peak in dBTP (typically -2 to -1)
  Range: min: -10, max: 0
- **`output_bitrate`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"192k"` вҖ” Output audio bitrate
  Possible values: `128k`, `192k`, `256k`, `320k`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ImpulseResponseOutput`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The processed audio file with reverb applied

---

## sonauto/v2/extend

**Title:** Sonauto V2
**Description:** Extend an existing song
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/sonauto/v2/extend`
**Category:** audio-to-audio
**Tags:** music, text-to-music, text-to-audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ExtendInput`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`, `side`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” A description of the track you want to generate. This prompt will be used to automatically generate the tags and lyrics unless you manually set them. For example, if you set prompt and tags, then the prompt will be used to generate only the lyrics.
- **`tags`** *(array)* вҖ” Tags/styles of the music to generate. You can view a list of all available tags at https://sonauto.ai/tag-explorer.
- **`lyrics_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The lyrics sung in the generated song. An empty string will generate an instrumental track.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed to use for generation. Will pick a random seed if not provided. Repeating a request with identical parameters (must use lyrics and tags, not prompt) and the same seed will generate the same song.
- **`prompt_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.8` вҖ” Controls how strongly your prompt influences the output. Greater values adhere more to the prompt but sound less natural. (This is CFG.)
  Range: min: 1.4, max: 3.1
- **`balance_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” Greater means more natural vocals. Lower means sharper instrumentals. We recommend 0.7.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`num_songs`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Generating 2 songs costs 1.5x the price of generating 1 song. Also, note that using the same seed may not result in identical songs if the number of songs generated is changed.
  Range: min: 1, max: 2
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"wav"`
  Possible values: `flac`, `mp3`, `wav`, `ogg`, `m4a`
- **`output_bit_rate`** *(integer)* вҖ” The bit rate to use for mp3 and m4a formats. Not available for other formats.
  Possible values: `128`, `192`, `256`, `320`
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the audio file to alter. Must be a valid publicly accessible URL.
- **`side`** *(enum)* вҖ” Add more to the beginning (left) or end (right) of the song
  Possible values: `left`, `right`
- **`extend_duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Duration in seconds to extend the song. If not provided, will attempt to automatically determine.
- **`crop_duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Duration in seconds to crop from the selected side before extending from that side.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ExtendOutput`
- **`tags`** *(array)* вҖ” The style tags used for generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation. This can be used to generate an identical song by passing the same parameters with this seed in a future request.
- **`extend_duration`** *(number)* вҖ” The duration in seconds that the song was extended by.
- **`audio`** *(list<File>)* вҖ” The generated audio files.
- **`lyrics`** *(string)* вҖ” The lyrics used for generation.

---

# CATEGORГҚA: AUDIO TO TEXT
_3 modelos_

## fal-ai/nemotron/asr

**Title:** Nemotron
**Description:** Use the fast speed and pin point accuracy of nemotron to transcribe your texts.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/nemotron/asr`
**Category:** audio-to-text

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SpeechInput`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the audio file.
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” Controls the speed/accuracy trade-off. 'none' = best accuracy (1.12s chunks, ~7.16% WER), 'low' = balanced (0.56s chunks, ~7.22% WER), 'medium' = faster (0.16s chunks, ~7.84% WER), 'high' = fastest (0.08s chunks, ~8.53% WER).
  Possible values: `none`, `low`, `medium`, `high`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SpeechOutput`
- **`output`** *(string)* вҖ” The transcribed text from the audio.
- **`partial`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” True if this is an intermediate result during streaming.

---

**Title:** Nemotron
**Description:** Use the fast speed and pin point accuracy of nemotron to transcribe your texts.

**Category:** audio-to-text

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SpeechInput`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the audio file.
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” Controls the speed/accuracy trade-off. 'none' = best accuracy (1.12s chunks, ~7.16% WER), 'low' = balanced (0.56s chunks, ~7.22% WER), 'medium' = faster (0.16s chunks, ~7.84% WER), 'high' = fastest (0.08s chunks, ~8.53% WER).
  Possible values: `none`, `low`, `medium`, `high`

**Required fields:** `avatar`, `audio_url`

- **`avatar`** *(enum)*
  Possible values: `Mia outdoor (UGC)`, `Lara (Masterclass)`, `Ines (UGC)`, `Maria (Masterclass)`, `Emma (UGC)`, `Sienna (Masterclass)`, `Elena (UGC)`, `Jasmine (Masterclass)`, `Amara (Masterclass)`, `Ryan podcast (UGC)`, `Tyler (Masterclass)`, `Jayse (Masterclass)`, `Paul (Masterclass)`, `Matteo (UGC)`, `Daniel car (UGC)`, `Dario (Masterclass)`, `Viva (Masterclass)`, `Chen (Masterclass)`, `Alex (Masterclass)`, `Vanessa (UGC)`, `Laurent (UGC)`, `Noemie car (UGC)`, `Brandon (UGC)`, `Byron (Masterclass)`, `Calista (Masterclass)`, `Milo (Masterclass)`, `Fabien (Masterclass)`, `Rose (UGC)`
- **`audio_url`** *(string)*
- **`remove_background`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Enabling the remove background feature will result in a 50% increase in the price.

## fal-ai/echomimic-v3

**Title:** EchoMimic V3
**Description:** EchoMimic V3 generates a talking avatar model from a picture, audio and text prompt.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/echomimic-v3`
**Category:** audio-to-video
**Tags:** echomimic, talking-head, audio-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `EchoMimicRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `audio_url`, `prompt`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as a reference for the video generation.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the audio to use as a reference for the video generation.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for the video generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The negative prompt to use for the video generation.
- **`num_frames_per_generation`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `121` вҖ” The number of frames to generate at once.
  Range: min: 49, max: 161
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4.5` вҖ” The guidance scale to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`audio_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.5` вҖ” The audio guidance scale to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed to use for the video generation.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `EchoMimicResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.

## fal-ai/elevenlabs/dubbing

**Title:** ElevenLabs Dubbing
**Description:** Generate dubbed videos or audios using ElevenLabs Dubbing feature!
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/elevenlabs/dubbing`
**Category:** audio-to-video
**Tags:** dubbing, audio-to-audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `DubbingRequest`
**Required fields:** `target_lang`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the audio file to dub. Either audio_url or video_url must be provided.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video file to dub. Either audio_url or video_url must be provided. If both are provided, video_url takes priority.
- **`target_lang`** *(string)* вҖ” Target language code for dubbing (ISO 639-1)
- **`source_lang`** *(string)* вҖ” Source language code. If not provided, will be auto-detected.
- **`num_speakers`** *(integer)* вҖ” Number of speakers in the audio. If not provided, will be auto-detected.
- **`highest_resolution`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use the highest resolution for dubbing.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `DubbingVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The dubbed video file. Will be populated if video_url was provided in the request.
- **`target_lang`** *(string)* вҖ” The target language of the dubbed content

---

## fal-ai/longcat-multi-avatar/image-audio-to-video

**Title:** Longcat Multi Avatar
**Description:** LongCat-Video-Avatar is an audio-driven video generation model that can generates super-realistic, lip-synchronized long video generation with natural dynamics and consistent identity.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/longcat-multi-avatar/image-audio-to-video`
**Category:** audio-to-video
**Tags:** audio-to-video, image-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MultiSpeakerImageAudioToVideoRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image containing two speakers.
- **`audio_url_person1`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/meituan-longcat/LongCat-Video/refs/heads/main/assets/avatar/multi/sing_man.WAV"` вҖ” The URL of the audio file for person 1 (left side).
- **`audio_url_person2`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/meituan-longcat/LongCat-Video/refs/heads/main/assets/avatar/multi/sing_woman.WAV"` вҖ” The URL of the audio file for person 2 (right side).
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"Two people are having a conversation with natural expressions and movements."` вҖ” The prompt to guide the video generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"Close-up, Bright tones, overexposed, static, blurred details, subtitles, style, works, paintings, images, static, overall gray, worst quality, low quality, JPEG compression residue, ugly, incomplete, extra fingers, poorly drawn hands, poorly drawn faces, deformed, disfigured, misshapen limbs, fused fingers, still picture, messy background, three legs, many people in the background, walking backwards"` вҖ” The negative prompt to avoid in the video generation.
- **`bbox_person1`** *(?)* вҖ” Bounding box for person 1. If not provided, defaults to left half of image.
- **`bbox_person2`** *(?)* вҖ” Bounding box for person 2. If not provided, defaults to right half of image.
- **`audio_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"para"` вҖ” How to combine the two audio tracks. 'para' (parallel) plays both simultaneously, 'add' (sequential) plays person 1 first then person 2.
  Possible values: `para`, `add`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” The number of inference steps to use.
  Range: min: 10, max: 100
- **`text_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” The text guidance scale for classifier-free guidance.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`audio_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” The audio guidance scale. Higher values may lead to exaggerated mouth movements.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"480p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video (480p or 720p). Billing is per video-second (16 frames): 480p is 1 unit per second and 720p is 4 units per second.
  Possible values: `480p`, `720p`
- **`num_segments`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of video segments to generate. Each segment adds ~5 seconds of video. First segment is ~5.8s, additional segments are 5s each.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable safety checker.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MultiSpeakerImageAudioToVideoResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.

---

## fal-ai/longcat-single-avatar/audio-to-video

**Title:** Longcat Single Avatar
**Description:** LongCat-Video-Avatar is an audio-driven video generation model that can generates super-realistic, lip-synchronized long video generation with natural dynamics and consistent identity.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/longcat-single-avatar/audio-to-video`
**Category:** audio-to-video
**Tags:** audio-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `AudioToVideoRequest`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the audio file to drive the avatar.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"A person is talking naturally with natural expressions and movements."` вҖ” The prompt to guide the video generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"Close-up, Bright tones, overexposed, static, blurred details, subtitles, style, works, paintings, images, static, overall gray, worst quality, low quality, JPEG compression residue, ugly, incomplete, extra fingers, poorly drawn hands, poorly drawn faces, deformed, disfigured, misshapen limbs, fused fingers, still picture, messy background, three legs, many people in the background, walking backwards"` вҖ” The negative prompt to avoid in the video generation.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” The number of inference steps to use.
  Range: min: 10, max: 100
- **`text_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” The text guidance scale for classifier-free guidance.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`audio_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” The audio guidance scale. Higher values may lead to exaggerated mouth movements.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"480p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video (480p or 720p). Billing is per video-second (16 frames): 480p is 1 unit per second and 720p is 4 units per second.
  Possible values: `480p`, `720p`
- **`num_segments`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of video segments to generate. Each segment adds ~5 seconds of video. First segment is ~5.8s, additional segments are 5s each.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable safety checker.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion using an LLM to enhance the prompt for better video quality.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `AudioToVideoResponse`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.

## fal-ai/longcat-single-avatar/image-audio-to-video

**Title:** Longcat Single Avatar
**Description:** LongCat-Video-Avatar is an audio-driven video generation model that can generates super-realistic, lip-synchronized long video generation with natural dynamics and consistent identity.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/longcat-single-avatar/image-audio-to-video`
**Category:** audio-to-video
**Tags:** audio-to-video, image-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageAudioToVideoRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `audio_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to animate.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the audio file to drive the avatar.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"A person is talking naturally with natural expressions and movements."` вҖ” The prompt to guide the video generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"Close-up, Bright tones, overexposed, static, blurred details, subtitles, style, works, paintings, images, static, overall gray, worst quality, low quality, JPEG compression residue, ugly, incomplete, extra fingers, poorly drawn hands, poorly drawn faces, deformed, disfigured, misshapen limbs, fused fingers, still picture, messy background, three legs, many people in the background, walking backwards"` вҖ” The negative prompt to avoid in the video generation.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” The number of inference steps to use.
  Range: min: 10, max: 100
- **`text_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” The text guidance scale for classifier-free guidance.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`audio_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” The audio guidance scale. Higher values may lead to exaggerated mouth movements.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"480p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video (480p or 720p). Billing is per video-second (16 frames): 480p is 1 unit per second and 720p is 4 units per second.
  Possible values: `480p`, `720p`
- **`num_segments`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of video segments to generate. Each segment adds ~5 seconds of video. First segment is ~5.8s, additional segments are 5s each.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable safety checker.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion using an LLM to enhance the prompt for better video quality.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ImageAudioToVideoResponse`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.

---

## fal-ai/ltx-2-19b/audio-to-video

**Title:** LTX-2 19B
**Description:** Generate video with audio from audio, text and images using LTX-2
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltx-2-19b/audio-to-video`
**Category:** audio-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LTX2AudioToVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `audio_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video from.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the audio to generate the video from.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional URL of an image to use as the first frame of the video.
- **`end_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as the end of the video.
- **`match_audio_length`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When enabled, the number of frames will be calculated based on the audio duration and FPS. When disabled, use the specified num_frames.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `121` вҖ” The number of frames to generate.
  Range: min: 9, max: 481
- **`video_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"landscape_4_3"` вҖ” The size of the generated video. Use 'auto' to match the input image dimensions if provided.
  Possible values: `auto`, `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`use_multiscale`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use multi-scale generation. If True, the model will generate the video at a smaller scale first, then use the smaller video to guide the generation of a video at or above your requested size. This results in better coherence and details.
- **`fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The frames per second of the generated video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The guidance scale to use.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `40` вҖ” The number of inference steps to use.
  Range: min: 8, max: 50
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`, `full`
- **`camera_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Possible values: `dolly_in`, `dolly_out`, `dolly_left`, `dolly_right`, `jib_up`, `jib_down`, `static`, `none`
- **`camera_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"blurry, out of focus, overexposed, underexposed, low contrast, washed out colors, excessive noise, grainy texture, poor lighting, flickering, motion blur, distorted proportions, unnatural skin tones, deformed facial features, asymmetrical face, missing facial features, extra limbs, disfigured hands, wrong hand count, artifacts around text, inconsistent perspective, camera shake, incorrect depth of field, background too sharp, background clutter, distracting reflections, harsh shadows, inconsistent lighting direction, color banding, cartoonish rendering, 3D CGI look, unrealistic materials, uncanny valley effect, incorrect ethnicity, wrong gender, exaggerated expressions, wrong gaze direction, mismatched lip sync, silent or muted audio, distorted voice, robotic voice, echo, background noise, off-sync audio,incorrect dialogue, added dialogue, repetitive speech, jittery movement, awkward pauses, incorrect timing, unnatural transitions, inconsistent framing, tilted camera, flat lighting, inconsistent tone, cinematic oversaturation, stylized filters, or AI artifacts."` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the video from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`end_image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the end image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Audio conditioning strength. Values below 1.0 will allow the model to change the audio, while a value of exactly 1.0 will use the input audio without modification.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`preprocess_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the audio before using it as conditioning.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTX2AudioToVideoOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the random number generator.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.

---

- **`fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `24` вҖ” The frames per second of the generated video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `40` вҖ” The number of inference steps to use.
  Range: min: 8, max: 50
- **`video_cfg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) scale for the video. Higher values result in more consistent and focused video content.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`video_stg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The Spatiotemporal Guidance (STG) scale for the video. Higher values result in more consistent and focused video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`video_rescaling_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” The rescaling scale for the video. Controls the ratio between classifier-free guidance and spatiotemporal guidance.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`video_modality_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The modality scale for the video. Controls the ratio between video and audio modalities.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`audio_cfg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7` вҖ” The Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) scale for the audio. Higher values result in more consistent and focused audio content.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`audio_stg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The Spatiotemporal Guidance (STG) scale for the audio. Higher values result in more consistent and focused audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`audio_rescaling_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” The rescaling scale for the audio. Controls the ratio between classifier-free guidance and spatiotemporal guidance.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_modality_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The modality scale for the audio. Controls the ratio between video and audio modalities.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`gradient_estimation_gamma`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” The gamma of gradient estimation during denoising. Set to 0 to disable.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`use_restart_sampling`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to use restart sampling. This will inject a small amount of noise during each denoising step, which can help improve the quality of the generated video.
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"ltx2"` вҖ” The scheduler to use.
  Possible values: `ltx2`, `linear_quadratic`, `beta`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`, `full`
- **`camera_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Possible values: `dolly_in`, `dolly_out`, `dolly_left`, `dolly_right`, `jib_up`, `jib_down`, `static`, `none`
- **`camera_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"news broadcast, 3d animation, computer graphics, pc game, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, watermark, logo, text, on screen text, subtitles, titles, signature, slowmo, static"` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the video from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`distill_lora_first_pass_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.2` вҖ” The scale of the distill LoRA to use for the first pass. Set to 0 to disable.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`distill_lora_second_pass_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The scale of the distill LoRA to use for the second and subsequent passes.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`end_image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the end image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Audio conditioning strength. Values below 1.0 will allow the model to change the audio, while a value of exactly 1.0 will use the input audio without modification.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`preprocess_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the audio before using it as conditioning.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTX23AudioToVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the random number generator.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the generation.
