## fal-ai/ltx-2.3-22b/audio-to-video/lora

**Title:** LTX-2.3 22B
**Description:** Generate video with audio from audio, text and images using LTX-2.3 and custom LoRA
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltx-2.3-22b/audio-to-video/lora`
**Category:** audio-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LTX23LoRAAudioToVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `loras`, `prompt`, `audio_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video from.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the audio to generate the video from.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional URL of an image to use as the first frame of the video.
- **`end_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as the end of the video.
- **`match_audio_length`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When enabled, the number of frames will be calculated based on the audio duration and FPS. When disabled, use the specified num_frames.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `121` вҖ” The number of frames to generate.
  Range: min: 9, max: 481
- **`video_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"landscape_16_9"` вҖ” The size of the generated video. Use 'auto' to match the input image dimensions if provided.
  Possible values: `auto`, `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`use_multiscale`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use multi-scale generation. If True, the model will generate the video at a smaller scale first, then use the smaller video to guide the generation of a video at or above your requested size. This results in better coherence and details.
- **`fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `24` вҖ” The frames per second of the generated video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `40` вҖ” The number of inference steps to use.
  Range: min: 8, max: 50
- **`video_cfg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) scale for the video. Higher values result in more consistent and focused video content.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`video_stg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The Spatiotemporal Guidance (STG) scale for the video. Higher values result in more consistent and focused video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`video_rescaling_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” The rescaling scale for the video. Controls the ratio between classifier-free guidance and spatiotemporal guidance.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`video_modality_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The modality scale for the video. Controls the ratio between video and audio modalities.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`audio_cfg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7` вҖ” The Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) scale for the audio. Higher values result in more consistent and focused audio content.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`audio_stg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The Spatiotemporal Guidance (STG) scale for the audio. Higher values result in more consistent and focused audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`audio_rescaling_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” The rescaling scale for the audio. Controls the ratio between classifier-free guidance and spatiotemporal guidance.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_modality_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The modality scale for the audio. Controls the ratio between video and audio modalities.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`gradient_estimation_gamma`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” The gamma of gradient estimation during denoising. Set to 0 to disable.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`use_restart_sampling`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to use restart sampling. This will inject a small amount of noise during each denoising step, which can help improve the quality of the generated video.
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"ltx2"` вҖ” The scheduler to use.
  Possible values: `ltx2`, `linear_quadratic`, `beta`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`, `full`
- **`camera_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Possible values: `dolly_in`, `dolly_out`, `dolly_left`, `dolly_right`, `jib_up`, `jib_down`, `static`, `none`
- **`camera_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"news broadcast, 3d animation, computer graphics, pc game, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, watermark, logo, text, on screen text, subtitles, titles, signature, slowmo, static"` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the video from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`loras`** *(list<LoRAInput>)* вҖ” The LoRAs to use for the generation.
- **`distill_lora_first_pass_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.2` вҖ” The scale of the distill LoRA to use for the first pass. Set to 0 to disable.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`distill_lora_second_pass_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The scale of the distill LoRA to use for the second and subsequent passes.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`end_image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the end image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Audio conditioning strength. Values below 1.0 will allow the model to change the audio, while a value of exactly 1.0 will use the input audio without modification.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`preprocess_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the audio before using it as conditioning.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTX23AudioToVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the random number generator.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the generation.

## fal-ai/ltx-2.3/audio-to-video

**Title:** LTX 2.3 Video Pro
**Description:** LTX-2.3 is a high-quality, fast AI video model available in Pro and Fast variants for text-to-video, image-to-video, and audio-to-video.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltx-2.3/audio-to-video`
**Category:** audio-to-video
**Tags:** stylized, transform, lipsync

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LTXV23AudioToVideoRequest`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the audio file to generate a video from. Duration must be between 2 and 20 seconds. Must be publicly accessible or base64 data URI.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of an image to use as the first frame of the video. If not provided, prompt is required.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text description of how the video should be generated. Required if image_url is not provided. When image_url is provided, this describes how the image should be animated.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Guidance scale for video generation. Higher values make the output more closely follow the prompt. Defaults to 5 for text-to-video, or 9 when providing an image.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the generated video. If 'auto', the aspect ratio will be determined automatically based on the input image, or defaults to 16:9 if no image is provided.
  Possible values: `auto`, `16:9`, `9:16`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTXV23AudioToVideoResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file

---

## fal-ai/ltx-2/audio-to-video

**Title:** LTX 2.0 Video Pro
**Description:** Generate video from audio using LTX-2
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltx-2/audio-to-video`
**Category:** audio-to-video
**Tags:** stylized, transform, lipsync

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LTXV20AudioToVideoRequest`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the audio file to generate a video from. Duration must be between 2 and 20 seconds. Must be publicly accessible or base64 data URI.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of an image to use as the first frame of the video. If not provided, prompt is required.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text description of how the video should be generated. Required if image_url is not provided. When image_url is provided, this describes how the image should be animated.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Guidance scale for video generation. Higher values make the output more closely follow the prompt. Defaults to 5 for text-to-video, or 9 when providing an image.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTXVAudioToVideoResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file

---

## fal-ai/stable-avatar

**Title:** Stable Avatar
**Description:** Stable Avatar generates audio-driven video avatars up to five minutes long
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/stable-avatar`
**Category:** audio-to-video
**Tags:** stable-avatar, talking-head, audio-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `StableAvatarRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `audio_url`, `prompt`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as a reference for the video generation.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the audio to use as a reference for the video generation.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for the video generation.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the video to generate. If 'auto', the aspect ratio will be determined by the reference image.
  Possible values: `16:9`, `1:1`, `9:16`, `auto`
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” The guidance scale to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`audio_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” The audio guidance scale to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” The number of inference steps to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 10, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed to use for the video generation.
- **`perturbation`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.1` вҖ” The amount of perturbation to use for the video generation. 0.0 means no perturbation, 1.0 means full perturbation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `StableAvatarResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.

---

## fal-ai/wan/v2.2-14b/speech-to-video

**Title:** Wan-2.2 Speech-to-Video 14B
**Description:** Wan-S2V is a video model that generates high-quality videos from static images and audio, with realistic facial expressions, body movements, and professional camera work for film and television applications
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan/v2.2-14b/speech-to-video`
**Category:** audio-to-video
**Tags:** audio-to-video, talking-head

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `WanS2VRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`, `audio_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt used for video generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Negative prompt for video generation.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `80` вҖ” Number of frames to generate. Must be between 40 to 120, (must be multiple of 4).
  Range: min: 40, max: 120
- **`frames_per_second`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `16` вҖ” Frames per second of the generated video. Must be between 4 to 60. When using interpolation and `adjust_fps_for_interpolation` is set to true (default true,) the final FPS will be multiplied by the number of interpolated frames plus one. For example, if the generated frames per second is 16 and the number of interpolated frames is 1, the final frames per second will be 32. If `adjust_fps_for_interpolation` is set to false, this value will be used as-is.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"480p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video (480p, 580p, or 720p).
  Possible values: `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `27` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 40
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, input data will be checked for safety before processing.
- **`enable_output_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, output video will be checked for safety after generation.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale. Higher values give better adherence to the prompt but may decrease quality.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Shift value for the video. Must be between 1.0 and 10.0.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the output video. Higher quality means better visual quality but larger file size.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the output video. Faster write mode means faster results but larger file size, balanced write mode is a good compromise between speed and quality, and small write mode is the slowest but produces the smallest file size.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input image. If the input image does not match the chosen aspect ratio, it is resized and center cropped.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the audio file.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanS2VResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.

---

## veed/avatars/audio-to-video

**Title:** Avatars

- **`audio_url`** *(string)*

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `AvatarsAppOutput`
- **`video`** *()*

# CATEGORГҚA: IMAGE TO 3D
_31 modelos_

## fal-ai/hunyuan_world/image-to-world

**Title:** Hunyuan World
**Description:** Hunyuan World 1.0 turns a single image into a panorama or a 3D world. It creates realistic scenes from the image, allowing you to explore and view it from different angles.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/hunyuan_world/image-to-world`
**Category:** image-to-3d

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageToWorldRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `labels_fg1`, `labels_fg2`, `classes`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to convert to a world.
- **`labels_fg1`** *(string)* вҖ” Labels for the first foreground object.
- **`labels_fg2`** *(string)* вҖ” Labels for the second foreground object.
- **`classes`** *(string)* вҖ” Classes to use for the world generation.
- **`export_drc`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to export DRC (Dynamic Resource Configuration).

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ImageToWorldResponse`
- **`world_file`** *()* вҖ” The generated world.

---

## fal-ai/hunyuan-3d/v3.1/pro/image-to-3d

**Title:** Hunyuan 3D Pro Image to 3D
**Description:** Generate 3D models from images with Hunyuan 3D Pro
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/hunyuan-3d/v3.1/pro/image-to-3d`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** 3d, hunyuan, image-to-3d

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ProImageTo3DInput`
**Required fields:** `input_image_url`

- **`input_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Front view image URL. Resolution: 128-5000px, max 8MB, formats: JPG/PNG/WEBP. Tips: simple background, single object, object >50% of frame.
- **`back_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional back/rear view image URL (JPG/PNG recommended).
- **`left_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional left side view image URL (JPG/PNG recommended).
- **`right_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional right side view image URL (JPG/PNG recommended).
- **`top_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional top view image URL (v3.1 exclusive, JPG/PNG recommended).
- **`bottom_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional bottom view image URL (v3.1 exclusive, JPG/PNG recommended).
- **`left_front_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional left-front 45 degree angle view image URL (v3.1 exclusive, JPG/PNG recommended).
- **`right_front_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional right-front 45 degree angle view image URL (v3.1 exclusive, JPG/PNG recommended).
- **`generate_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"Normal"` вҖ” Generation task type. Normal: textured model. Geometry: geometry-only white model (no textures). LowPoly/Sketch are not available in v3.1.
  Possible values: `Normal`, `Geometry`
- **`enable_pbr`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Enable PBR material generation (metallic, roughness, normal textures). Ignored when generate_type is Geometry.
- **`face_count`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `500000` вҖ” Target polygon face count. Range: 40,000-1,500,000. Default: 500,000.
  Range: min: 40000, max: 1500000

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ProImageTo3DOutput`
- **`model_urls`** *()* вҖ” URLs for different 3D model formats
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation
- **`thumbnail`** *(?)* вҖ” Preview thumbnail of the generated model
- **`model_glb`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D object in GLB format.

---

## fal-ai/hunyuan-3d/v3.1/rapid/image-to-3d

**Title:** Hunyuan 3D Rapid Image to 3D
**Description:** Rapidly generate 3D models from images using Hunyuan 3D.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/hunyuan-3d/v3.1/rapid/image-to-3d`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** 3d, hunyuan, image-to-3d

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `RapidImageTo3DInput`
**Required fields:** `input_image_url`

- **`input_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Front view image URL. Resolution: 128-5000px, max 8MB (recommended вүӨ6MB for base64 encoding), formats: JPG/PNG/WEBP. Tips: simple background, single object, object >50% of frame.
- **`enable_pbr`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Enable PBR material generation (metallic, roughness, normal textures). Does not take effect when enable_geometry is True.
- **`enable_geometry`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Generate geometry-only white model without textures. When enabled, enable_pbr is ignored and OBJ is not supported (default output is GLB).

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `RapidImageTo3DOutput`
- **`model_urls`** *()* вҖ” URLs for different 3D model formats.
- **`texture`** *(?)* вҖ” Texture image for the 3D model.
- **`material_mtl`** *(?)* вҖ” MTL material file for the OBJ model.
- **`thumbnail`** *(?)* вҖ” Preview thumbnail of the generated model
- **`model_glb`** *(?)* вҖ” Generated 3D model file. Contains GLB if available, otherwise OBJ.

---

## fal-ai/hunyuan3d-v3/image-to-3d

**Title:** Hunyuan3d V3
**Description:** Transform your photos into ultra-high-resolution 3D models in seconds. Film-quality geometry with PBR textures, ready for games, e-commerce, and 3D printing.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/hunyuan3d-v3/image-to-3d`
**Category:** image-to-3d

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageTo3DInput`
**Required fields:** `input_image_url`

- **`input_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to use while generating the 3D model.
- **`back_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional back view image URL for better 3D reconstruction.
- **`left_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional left view image URL for better 3D reconstruction.
- **`right_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional right view image URL for better 3D reconstruction.
- **`enable_pbr`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable PBR material generation. Does not take effect when generate_type is Geometry.
- **`face_count`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `500000` вҖ” Target face count. Range: 40000-1500000
  Range: min: 40000, max: 1500000
- **`generate_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"Normal"` вҖ” Generation type. Normal: textured model. LowPoly: polygon reduction. Geometry: white model without texture.
  Possible values: `Normal`, `LowPoly`, `Geometry`
- **`polygon_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"triangle"` вҖ” Polygon type. Only takes effect when GenerateType is LowPoly.
  Possible values: `triangle`, `quadrilateral`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ImageTo3DOutput`
- **`model_urls`** *()* вҖ” URLs for different 3D model formats
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation
- **`model_glb`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D object in GLB format.
- **`thumbnail`** *(?)* вҖ” Preview thumbnail of the generated model

---

## fal-ai/hunyuan3d-v3/sketch-to-3d

**Title:** Hunyuan3d V3
**Description:** Create your imagined 3D models with just text. Production-ready, export-ready professional assets with realistic lighting and materials in minutes.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/hunyuan3d-v3/sketch-to-3d`
**Category:** image-to-3d

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SketchTo3DInput`
**Required fields:** `input_image_url`, `prompt`

- **`input_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of sketch or line art image to transform into a 3D model. Image resolution must be between 128x128 and 5000x5000 pixels.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt describing the 3D content attributes such as color, category, and material.
- **`enable_pbr`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable PBR material generation.
- **`face_count`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `500000` вҖ” Target face count. Range: 40000-1500000
  Range: min: 40000, max: 1500000

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SketchTo3DOutput`
- **`model_glb`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D object in GLB format.
- **`model_urls`** *()* вҖ” URLs for different 3D model formats
- **`thumbnail`** *(?)* вҖ” Preview thumbnail of the generated model
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation

## fal-ai/hunyuan3d/v2

**Title:** Hunyuan3D
**Description:** Generate 3D models from your images using Hunyuan 3D. A native 3D generative model enabling versatile and high-quality 3D asset creation.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/hunyuan3d/v2`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** stylized

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Hunyuan3DInput`
**Required fields:** `input_image_url`

- **`input_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to use while generating the 3D model.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” Number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” Guidance scale for the model.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`octree_resolution`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `256` вҖ” Octree resolution for the model.
  Range: min: 1, max: 1024
- **`textured_mesh`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set true, textured mesh will be generated and the price charged would be 3 times that of white mesh.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ObjectOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed value used for generation.
- **`model_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D object file.

---

- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” Number of inference steps to perform.

  Range: min: 1, max: 1024
- **`textured_mesh`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set true, textured mesh will be generated and the price charged would be 3 times that of white mesh.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ObjectOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed value used for generation.
- **`model_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D object file.

---

## fal-ai/hyper3d/rodin

**Title:** Hyper3D Rodin
**Description:** Rodin by Hyper3D generates realistic and production ready 3D models from text or images.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/hyper3d/rodin`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** stylized

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Rodin3DInput`
**Required fields:** _none_

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” A textual prompt to guide model generation. Required for Text-to-3D mode. Optional for Image-to-3D mode.
- **`input_image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” URL of images to use while generating the 3D model. Required for Image-to-3D mode. Optional for Text-to-3D mode.
- **`condition_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"concat"` вҖ” For fuse mode, One or more images are required.It will generate a model by extracting and fusing features of objects from multiple images.For concat mode, need to upload multiple multi-view images of the same object and generate the model. (You can upload multi-view images in any order, regardless of the order of view.)
  Possible values: `fuse`, `concat`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed value for randomization, ranging from 0 to 65535. Optional.
- **`geometry_file_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"glb"` вҖ” Format of the geometry file. Possible values: glb, usdz, fbx, obj, stl. Default is glb.
  Possible values: `glb`, `usdz`, `fbx`, `obj`, `stl`
- **`material`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"PBR"` вҖ” Material type. Possible values: PBR, Shaded. Default is PBR.
  Possible values: `PBR`, `Shaded`
- **`quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"medium"` вҖ” Generation quality. Possible values: high, medium, low, extra-low. Default is medium.
  Possible values: `high`, `medium`, `low`, `extra-low`
- **`use_hyper`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to export the model using hyper mode. Default is false.
- **`tier`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"Regular"` вҖ” Tier of generation. For Rodin Sketch, set to Sketch. For Rodin Regular, set to Regular.
  Possible values: `Regular`, `Sketch`
- **`TAPose`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” When generating the human-like model, this parameter control the generation result to T/A Pose.
- **`bbox_condition`** *(array)* вҖ” An array that specifies the dimensions and scaling factor of the bounding box. Typically, this array contains 3 elements, Length(X-axis), Width(Y-axis) and Height(Z-axis).
- **`addons`** *(string)* вҖ” Generation add-on features. Default is []. Possible values are HighPack. The HighPack option will provide 4K resolution textures instead of the default 1K, as well as models with high-poly. It will cost triple the billable units.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ObjectOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed value used for generation.
- **`model_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D object file.
- **`textures`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Generated textures for the 3D object.

---

## fal-ai/hyper3d/rodin/v2

**Title:** Hyper3d
**Description:** Rodin by Hyper3D generates realistic and production ready 3D models from text or images.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/hyper3d/rodin/v2`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** image-to-3d, text-to-3d

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `RodinGen2Input`
**Required fields:** _none_

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” A textual prompt to guide model generation. Optional for Image-to-3D mode - if empty, AI will generate a prompt based on your images.
- **`input_image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” URL of images to use while generating the 3D model. Required for Image-to-3D mode. Up to 5 images allowed.
- **`use_original_alpha`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” When enabled, preserves the transparency channel from input images during 3D generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed value for randomization, ranging from 0 to 65535. Optional.
- **`geometry_file_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"glb"` вҖ” Format of the geometry file. Possible values: glb, usdz, fbx, obj, stl. Default is glb.
  Possible values: `glb`, `usdz`, `fbx`, `obj`, `stl`
- **`material`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"All"` вҖ” Material type. PBR: Physically-based materials with realistic lighting. Shaded: Simple materials with baked lighting. All: Both types included.
  Possible values: `PBR`, `Shaded`, `All`
- **`quality_mesh_option`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"500K Triangle"` вҖ” Combined quality and mesh type selection. Quad = smooth surfaces, Triangle = detailed geometry. These corresponds to `mesh_mode` (if the option contains 'Triangle', mesh_mode is 'Raw', otherwise 'Quad') and `quality_override` (the numeric part of the option) parameters in Hyper3D API.
  Possible values: `4K Quad`, `8K Quad`, `18K Quad`, `50K Quad`, `2K Triangle`, `20K Triangle`, `150K Triangle`, `500K Triangle`
- **`TAPose`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Generate characters in T-pose or A-pose format, making them easier to rig and animate in 3D software.
- **`bbox_condition`** *(array)* вҖ” An array that specifies the bounding box dimensions [width, height, length].
- **`addons`** *(string)* вҖ” The HighPack option will provide 4K resolution textures instead of the default 1K, as well as models with high-poly. It will cost **triple the billable units**.
- **`preview_render`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Generate a preview render image of the 3D model along with the model files.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ObjectOutputv2`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed value used for generation.
- **`model_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D object file.
- **`textures`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Generated textures for the 3D object.

---

## fal-ai/meshy/v5/multi-image-to-3d

**Title:** Meshy 5 Multi
**Description:** Meshy-5 multi image generates realistic and production ready 3D models from multiple images.

**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/meshy/v5/multi-image-to-3d`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** multi-image-to-3d

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MultiImageTo3DInput`
**Required fields:** `image_urls`

- **`image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” 1 to 4 images for 3D model creation. All images should depict the same object from different angles. Supports .jpg, .jpeg, .png formats, and AVIF/HEIF which will be automatically converted. If more than 4 images are provided, only the first 4 will be used.
- **`topology`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"triangle"` вҖ” Specify the topology of the generated model. Quad for smooth surfaces, Triangle for detailed geometry.
  Possible values: `quad`, `triangle`
- **`target_polycount`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30000` вҖ” Target number of polygons in the generated model
  Range: min: 100, max: 300000
- **`symmetry_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Controls symmetry behavior during model generation.
  Possible values: `off`, `auto`, `on`
- **`should_remesh`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the remesh phase. When false, returns triangular mesh ignoring topology and target_polycount.
- **`should_texture`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate textures. False provides mesh without textures for 5 credits, True adds texture generation for additional 10 credits.
- **`enable_pbr`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Generate PBR Maps (metallic, roughness, normal) in addition to base color. Requires should_texture to be true.
- **`is_a_t_pose`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Deprecated: use pose_mode instead. When true, generates a T-pose model.
- **`pose_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Pose mode for the generated model. 'a-pose' generates an A-pose, 't-pose' generates a T-pose, empty string for no specific pose.
  Possible values: `a-pose`, `t-pose`, ``
- **`texture_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt to guide the texturing process. Requires should_texture to be true.
- **`texture_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” 2D image to guide the texturing process. Requires should_texture to be true.
- **`enable_rigging`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Automatically rig the generated model as a humanoid character. Includes basic walking and running animations. Best results with humanoid characters that have clearly defined limbs.
- **`rigging_height_meters`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.7` вҖ” Approximate height of the character in meters. Only used when enable_rigging is true.
- **`enable_animation`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Apply an animation preset to the rigged model. Requires enable_rigging to be true.
- **`animation_action_id`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1001` вҖ” Animation preset ID from Meshy's library (500+ presets). Only used when enable_animation is true. See https://docs.meshy.ai/en/api/animation-library for available action IDs.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, input data will be checked for safety before processing.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MultiImageTo3DOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation (if available)
- **`animation_glb`** *(?)* вҖ” Animated 3D model in GLB format. Only present when enable_animation is true.
- **`animation_fbx`** *(?)* вҖ” Animated 3D model in FBX format. Only present when enable_animation is true.
- **`rigged_character_glb`** *(?)* вҖ” Rigged character in GLB format. Only present when enable_rigging is true.
- **`model_urls`** *()* вҖ” URLs for different 3D model formats
- **`model_glb`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D object in GLB format.
- **`rigged_character_fbx`** *(?)* вҖ” Rigged character in FBX format. Only present when enable_rigging is true.
- **`basic_animations`** *(?)* вҖ” Basic walking and running animations. Only present when enable_rigging is true.
- **`texture_urls`** *(list<TextureFiles>)* вҖ” Array of texture file objects
- **`rig_task_id`** *(string)* вҖ” Rigging task ID. Only present when enable_rigging is true.
- **`thumbnail`** *(?)* вҖ” Preview thumbnail of the generated model

---

## fal-ai/meshy/v6-preview/image-to-3d

**Title:** Meshy 6 Preview
**Description:** Meshy-6-Preview is the latest model from Meshy. It generates realistic and production ready 3D models.

**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/meshy/v6-preview/image-to-3d`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** image-to-3d

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageTo3DInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Image URL or base64 data URI for 3D model creation. Supports .jpg, .jpeg, and .png formats. Also supports AVIF and HEIF formats which will be automatically converted.
- **`topology`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"triangle"` вҖ” Specify the topology of the generated model. Quad for smooth surfaces, Triangle for detailed geometry.
  Possible values: `quad`, `triangle`
- **`target_polycount`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30000` вҖ” Target number of polygons in the generated model
  Range: min: 100, max: 300000
- **`symmetry_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Controls symmetry behavior during model generation. Off disables symmetry, Auto determines it automatically, On enforces symmetry.
  Possible values: `off`, `auto`, `on`
- **`should_remesh`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the remesh phase
- **`should_texture`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate textures
- **`enable_pbr`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Generate PBR Maps (metallic, roughness, normal) in addition to base color
- **`is_a_t_pose`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Deprecated: use pose_mode instead. When true, generates a T-pose model.
- **`pose_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Pose mode for the generated model. 'a-pose' generates an A-pose, 't-pose' generates a T-pose, empty string for no specific pose.
  Possible values: `a-pose`, `t-pose`, ``
- **`texture_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt to guide the texturing process
- **`texture_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” 2D image to guide the texturing process
- **`enable_rigging`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Automatically rig the generated model as a humanoid character. Includes basic walking and running animations. Best results with humanoid characters that have clearly defined limbs.
- **`rigging_height_meters`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.7` вҖ” Approximate height of the character in meters. Only used when enable_rigging is true.
- **`enable_animation`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Apply an animation preset to the rigged model. Requires enable_rigging to be true.
- **`animation_action_id`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1001` вҖ” Animation preset ID from Meshy's library (500+ presets). Only used when enable_animation is true. See https://docs.meshy.ai/en/api/animation-library for available action IDs.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, input data will be checked for safety before processing.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ImageTo3DOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation (if available)
- **`animation_glb`** *(?)* вҖ” Animated 3D model in GLB format. Only present when enable_animation is true.
- **`animation_fbx`** *(?)* вҖ” Animated 3D model in FBX format. Only present when enable_animation is true.
- **`rigged_character_glb`** *(?)* вҖ” Rigged character in GLB format. Only present when enable_rigging is true.
- **`model_urls`** *()* вҖ” URLs for different 3D model formats
- **`model_glb`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D object in GLB format.
- **`rigged_character_fbx`** *(?)* вҖ” Rigged character in FBX format. Only present when enable_rigging is true.
- **`basic_animations`** *(?)* вҖ” Basic walking and running animations. Only present when enable_rigging is true.
- **`texture_urls`** *(list<TextureFiles>)* вҖ” Array of texture file objects, matching Meshy API structure
- **`rig_task_id`** *(string)* вҖ” Rigging task ID. Only present when enable_rigging is true.
- **`thumbnail`** *(?)* вҖ” Preview thumbnail of the generated model

---

**Description:** Meshy-6 is the latest model from Meshy. It generates realistic and production ready 3D models.

**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** image-to-3d

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageTo3DInput`

- **`topology`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"triangle"` вҖ” Specify the topology of the generated model. Quad for smooth surfaces, Triangle for detailed geometry.
  Possible values: `quad`, `triangle`
- **`target_polycount`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30000` вҖ” Target number of polygons in the generated model

- **`symmetry_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Controls symmetry behavior during model generation. Off disables symmetry, Auto determines it automatically, On enforces symmetry.

- **`should_remesh`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the remesh phase
- **`should_texture`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate textures
- **`enable_pbr`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Generate PBR Maps (metallic, roughness, normal) in addition to base color
- **`is_a_t_pose`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Deprecated: use pose_mode instead. When true, generates a T-pose model.
- **`pose_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Pose mode for the generated model. 'a-pose' generates an A-pose, 't-pose' generates a T-pose, empty string for no specific pose.
  Possible values: `a-pose`, `t-pose`, ``
- **`texture_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt to guide the texturing process

- **`enable_rigging`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Automatically rig the generated model as a humanoid character. Includes basic walking and running animations. Best results with humanoid characters that have clearly defined limbs.
- **`rigging_height_meters`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.7` вҖ” Approximate height of the character in meters. Only used when enable_rigging is true.
- **`enable_animation`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Apply an animation preset to the rigged model. Requires enable_rigging to be true.
- **`animation_action_id`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1001` вҖ” Animation preset ID from Meshy's library (500+ presets). Only used when enable_animation is true. See https://docs.meshy.ai/en/api/animation-library for available action IDs.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, input data will be checked for safety before processing.

- **`rigged_character_glb`** *(?)* вҖ” Rigged character in GLB format. Only present when enable_rigging is true.

## fal-ai/omnipart

**Title:** Omnipart
**Description:** Image-to-3D endpoint for OmniPart, a part-aware 3D generator with semantic decoupling and structural cohesion.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/omnipart`
**Category:** image-to-3d

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `OmnipartInput`
**Required fields:** `input_image_url`

- **`input_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to use while generating the 3D model.
- **`minimum_segment_size`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `2000` вҖ” Minimum segment size (pixels) for the model.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10000
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `765464` вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” Guidance scale for the model.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`parts`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Specify which segments to merge (e.g., '0,1;3,4' merges segments 0&1 together and 3&4 together)

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MultiViewObjectOutput`
- **`output_zip`** *()* вҖ” All outputs file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed value used for generation.
- **`model_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D object file.
- **`full_model_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D object file.

---

## fal-ai/pshuman

**Title:** Pshuman
**Description:** Use the 6D pose estimation capabilities of PSHuman to generate 3D files from single image.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/pshuman`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** image-to-3D

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `PSHumanRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” A direct URL to the input image of a person.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” Guidance scale for the diffusion process. Controls how much the output adheres to the generated views.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed will be used.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `PSHumanResponse`
- **`model_obj`** *()* вҖ” The generated 3D model in OBJ format.
- **`preview_image`** *()* вҖ” A preview image showing the input and the generated multi-view outputs.

---

## fal-ai/reconviagen-0.5

**Title:** ReconViaGen 0.5
**Description:** Generate 3D models from one or more images using ReconViaGen 0.5
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/reconviagen-0.5`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** multi-view, 3d-reconstruction

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageInput`
**Required fields:** `image_urls`

- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `1024` вҖ” Output resolution; higher is slower but more detailed
  Possible values: `512`, `1024`, `1536`
- **`ss_source`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"mesh"` вҖ” Sparse structure source. 'mesh' gives best quality, 'direct' is fastest, 'mvtrellis2' uses multi-view TRELLIS.2.
  Possible values: `direct`, `mesh`, `mvtrellis2`
- **`ss_guidance_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” How closely the initial 3D structure follows the input. Higher values produce more faithful but potentially noisier results.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`ss_guidance_rescale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” Dampens artifacts from high guidance in stage 1.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`ss_sampling_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `12` вҖ” Number of denoising steps for the initial structure.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`ss_rescale_t`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Controls noise schedule sharpness for structure generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 6
- **`slat_guidance_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” Guidance strength for SLat stage (only used when ss_source='mesh').
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`slat_guidance_rescale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Guidance rescale for SLat stage (only used when ss_source='mesh').
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`slat_sampling_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `12` вҖ” Sampling steps for SLat stage (only used when ss_source='mesh').
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`slat_rescale_t`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Rescale T for SLat stage (only used when ss_source='mesh').
  Range: min: 1, max: 6
- **`shape_slat_guidance_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” How closely the detailed geometry follows the input.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`shape_slat_guidance_rescale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Dampens artifacts from high guidance in the shape stage.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`shape_slat_sampling_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `12` вҖ” Number of denoising steps for shape refinement.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`shape_slat_rescale_t`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Controls noise schedule sharpness for shape refinement.
  Range: min: 1, max: 6
- **`tex_slat_guidance_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” How closely the texture follows the input colors.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`tex_slat_guidance_rescale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Dampens artifacts from high guidance in the texture stage.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`tex_slat_sampling_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `12` вҖ” Number of denoising steps for texture generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`tex_slat_rescale_t`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Controls noise schedule sharpness for texture generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 6
- **`decimation_target`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `500000` вҖ” Target number of vertices in the final mesh.
  Range: min: 5000, max: 2000000
- **`texture_size`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `2048` вҖ” Resolution of the texture image baked onto the mesh.
  Possible values: `1024`, `2048`, `4096`
- **`image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” One or more views of the same object. Multiple views yield higher-quality 3D reconstruction.
- **`multi_image_strategy`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"adaptive_guidance_weight"` вҖ” Strategy for combining multi-view conditioning. 'adaptive_guidance_weight' works best in most cases. Only used when more than one image is provided.
  Possible values: `average_right`, `weighted_average`, `sequential`, `average`, `adaptive_guidance_weight`, `fixed_guidance_rescale`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ObjectOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed used for generation.
- **`model_glb`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D GLB file with PBR materials

---

## fal-ai/sam-3/3d-body

**Title:** Sam 3
**Description:** SAM 3D allows for accurate 3D reconstruction of human body shape and position from a single image.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sam-3/3d-body`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** 3d, human, pose

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SAM3DBodyInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image containing humans
- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional URL of a binary mask image (white=person, black=background). When provided, skips auto human detection and uses this mask instead. Bbox is auto-computed from the mask.
- **`export_meshes`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Export individual mesh files (.ply) per person
- **`include_3d_keypoints`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Include 3D keypoint markers (spheres) in the GLB mesh for visualization

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SAM3DBodyOutput`
- **`model_glb`** *()* вҖ” 3D body mesh in GLB format with optional 3D keypoint markers
- **`meshes`** *(array)* вҖ” Individual mesh files (.ply), one per detected person (when export_meshes=True)
- **`visualization`** *()* вҖ” Combined visualization image (original + keypoints + mesh + side view)
- **`metadata`** *()* вҖ” Structured metadata including keypoints and camera parameters

---

## fal-ai/sam-3/3d-objects

**Title:** Sam 3
**Description:** SAM 3D enables precise 3D reconstruction of objects from real images, while accurately reconstructing their geometry and texture.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sam-3/3d-objects`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** 3d, object

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SAM3DObjectInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to reconstruct in 3D
- **`mask_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” Optional list of mask URLs (one per object). If not provided, use prompt/point_prompts/box_prompts to auto-segment, or entire image will be used.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"car"` вҖ” Text prompt for auto-segmentation when no masks provided (e.g., 'chair', 'lamp')
- **`point_prompts`** *(list<PointPromptBase>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Point prompts for auto-segmentation when no masks provided
- **`box_prompts`** *(list<BoxPromptBase>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Box prompts for auto-segmentation when no masks provided. Multiple boxes supported - each produces a separate object mask for 3D reconstruction.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility
- **`pointmap_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional URL to external pointmap/depth data (NPY or NPZ format) for improved 3D reconstruction depth estimation
- **`detection_threshold`** *(number)* вҖ” Detection confidence threshold (0.1-1.0). Lower = more detections but less precise. If not set, uses the model's default.
- **`export_textured_glb`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If True, exports GLB with baked texture and UVs instead of vertex colors.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SAM3DObjectOutput`
- **`gaussian_splat`** *()* вҖ” Gaussian splat file (.ply) - combined scene splat for multi-object, single splat otherwise
- **`metadata`** *(list<SAM3DObjectMetadata>)* вҖ” Per-object metadata (rotation/translation/scale)
- **`model_glb`** *()* вҖ” Default: `"https://v3b.fal.media/files/b/0a8439e7/mqHMt17hzqDaqVMF7q0dB_combined_scene.glb"` вҖ” 3D mesh in GLB format - combined scene for multi-object, single mesh otherwise
- **`individual_glbs`** *(array)* вҖ” Individual GLB mesh files per object (only for multi-object scenes)
- **`artifacts_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” Zip bundle containing all artifacts and metadata
- **`individual_splats`** *(array)* вҖ” Individual Gaussian splat files per object (only for multi-object scenes)

---

## fal-ai/trellis

**Title:** Trellis
**Description:** Generate 3D models from your images using Trellis. A native 3D generative model enabling versatile and high-quality 3D asset creation.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/trellis`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** stylized

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `InputModel`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input image to convert to 3D
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility
- **`ss_guidance_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” Guidance strength for sparse structure generation
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`ss_sampling_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `12` вҖ” Sampling steps for sparse structure generation
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`slat_guidance_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Guidance strength for structured latent generation
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`slat_sampling_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `12` вҖ” Sampling steps for structured latent generation
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`mesh_simplify`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.95` вҖ” Mesh simplification factor
  Range: min: 0.9, max: 0.98
- **`texture_size`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `1024` вҖ” Texture resolution
  Possible values: `512`, `1024`, `2048`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ObjectOutput`
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” Processing timings
- **`model_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D mesh file

---

## fal-ai/trellis-2

**Title:** Trellis 2
**Description:** Generate 3D models from your images using Trellis 2. A native 3D generative model enabling versatile and high-quality 3D asset creation.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/trellis-2`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** image-to-3D

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SingleImageInputModel`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `1024` вҖ” Output resolution; higher is slower but more detailed
  Possible values: `512`, `1024`, `1536`
- **`ss_guidance_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” How closely the initial 3D structure follows the input image. Higher values produce more faithful but potentially noisier results.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`ss_guidance_rescale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” Dampens artifacts from high guidance in stage 1. Lower values allow stronger guidance effects, higher values stabilize the output.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`ss_sampling_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `12` вҖ” Number of denoising steps for the initial structure. More steps = slower but potentially higher quality.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`ss_rescale_t`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Controls noise schedule sharpness for structure generation. Higher values produce sharper transitions.
  Range: min: 1, max: 6
- **`shape_slat_guidance_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” How closely the detailed geometry follows the input image. Higher values add more detail but may introduce noise.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`shape_slat_guidance_rescale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Dampens artifacts from high guidance in the shape stage. Increase if you see noisy geometry.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`shape_slat_sampling_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `12` вҖ” Number of denoising steps for shape refinement. More steps = slower but potentially smoother geometry.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`shape_slat_rescale_t`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Controls noise schedule sharpness for shape refinement. Higher values produce sharper geometric details.
  Range: min: 1, max: 6
- **`tex_slat_guidance_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” How closely the texture follows the input image colors. Higher values produce more vivid but potentially oversaturated textures.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`tex_slat_guidance_rescale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Dampens artifacts from high guidance in the texture stage. Increase if textures look noisy or have color banding.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`tex_slat_sampling_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `12` вҖ” Number of denoising steps for texture generation. More steps = slower but potentially cleaner textures.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`tex_slat_rescale_t`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Controls noise schedule sharpness for texture generation. Higher values produce sharper texture details.
  Range: min: 1, max: 6
- **`decimation_target`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `500000` вҖ” Target number of vertices in the final mesh. Lower values produce smaller files but less detail. 500k is good for most uses, reduce to 20k-50k for web/mobile.
  Range: min: 5000, max: 2000000
- **`texture_size`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `2048` вҖ” Resolution of the texture image baked onto the mesh. Higher values capture finer surface details but produce larger files.
  Possible values: `1024`, `2048`, `4096`
- **`remesh`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Rebuild the mesh topology for cleaner triangles. Slower but usually produces better results for downstream use (animation, 3D printing, etc).
- **`remesh_band`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Controls how far remeshing can move vertices from the original surface. Higher values allow more smoothing but may lose fine details.
  Range: min: 0, max: 4
- **`remesh_project`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” How much to project remeshed vertices back onto the original surface. 0 = no projection (smoother), 1 = full projection (preserves detail).
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input image to convert to 3D

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ObjectOutput`
- **`model_glb`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D GLB file

---

## fal-ai/trellis-2/retexture

**Title:** Trellis 2
**Description:** Generate 3D models from your images using Trellis 2. A native 3D generative model enabling versatile and high-quality 3D asset creation.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/trellis-2/retexture`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** image-to-3D

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `RetextureInputModel`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `mesh_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the reference image for texturing
- **`mesh_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the untextured 3D mesh to retexture. Supports GLB, OBJ, PLY, and STL formats.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `1024` вҖ” Internal resolution for texture generation. Higher produces finer texture details but is slower.
  Possible values: `512`, `1024`
- **`tex_slat_guidance_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” How closely the texture follows the input image colors. Higher values produce more vivid but potentially oversaturated textures.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`tex_slat_guidance_rescale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Dampens artifacts from high guidance in the texture stage. Increase if textures look noisy or have color banding.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`tex_slat_sampling_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `12` вҖ” Number of denoising steps for texture generation. More steps = slower but potentially cleaner textures.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`tex_slat_rescale_t`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Controls noise schedule sharpness for texture generation. Higher values produce sharper texture details.
  Range: min: 1, max: 6
- **`texture_size`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `2048` вҖ” Resolution of the texture image baked onto the mesh. Higher values capture finer surface details but produce larger files.
  Possible values: `1024`, `2048`, `4096`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ObjectOutput`
- **`model_glb`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D GLB file

---

## fal-ai/trellis/multi

**Title:** Trellis
**Description:** Generate 3D models from multiple images using Trellis. A native 3D generative model enabling versatile and high-quality 3D asset creation.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/trellis/multi`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** stylized

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MultiImageInputModel`
**Required fields:** `image_urls`

- **`image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” List of URLs of input images to convert to 3D
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility
- **`ss_guidance_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” Guidance strength for sparse structure generation
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`ss_sampling_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `12` вҖ” Sampling steps for sparse structure generation
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`slat_guidance_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Guidance strength for structured latent generation
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`slat_sampling_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `12` вҖ” Sampling steps for structured latent generation
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`mesh_simplify`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.95` вҖ” Mesh simplification factor
  Range: min: 0.9, max: 0.98
- **`texture_size`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `1024` вҖ” Texture resolution
  Possible values: `512`, `1024`, `2048`
- **`multiimage_algo`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"stochastic"` вҖ” Algorithm for multi-image generation
  Possible values: `stochastic`, `multidiffusion`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ObjectOutput`
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” Processing timings
- **`model_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D mesh file

---

## fal-ai/triposr

**Title:** TripoSR
**Description:** State of the art Image to 3D Object generation
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/triposr`
**Category:** image-to-3d

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TripoSRInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Path for the image file to be processed.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"glb"` вҖ” Output format for the 3D model.
  Possible values: `glb`, `obj`
- **`do_remove_background`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to remove the background from the input image.
- **`foreground_ratio`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.9` вҖ” Ratio of the foreground image to the original image.
  Range: min: 0.5, max: 1
- **`mc_resolution`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `256` вҖ” Resolution of the marching cubes. Above 512 is not recommended.
  Range: min: 32, max: 1024

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ObjectOutput`
- **`remeshing_dir`** *(?)* вҖ” Directory containing textures for the remeshed model.
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” Inference timings.
- **`model_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D object file.

---

## tripo3d/h3.1/image-to-3d

**Title:** Tripo H3.1 Image to 3D
**Description:** Generate high-quality 3D models from a single image using Tripo H3.1.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/tripo3d/h3.1/image-to-3d`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** 3d, image-to-3d, 3d-generation, tripo

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FullImageTo3DInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input image for 3D model creation.
- **`face_limit`** *(integer)* вҖ” Target number of faces for the generated mesh. If not set, the model adaptively determines the count.
- **`texture`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate textures for the model.
- **`pbr`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate PBR (Physically Based Rendering) materials. If true, texture is also enabled.
- **`model_seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for geometry generation reproducibility.
- **`texture_seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for texture generation reproducibility.
- **`texture_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"standard"` вҖ” Quality level for textures. 'detailed' produces higher-resolution textures.
  Possible values: `standard`, `detailed`
- **`geometry_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"standard"` вҖ” Quality level for geometry.
  Possible values: `standard`, `detailed`
- **`texture_alignment`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"original_image"` вҖ” How textures are aligned. 'original_image' aligns to input image, 'geometry' aligns to generated geometry.
  Possible values: `original_image`, `geometry`
- **`auto_size`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Auto-scale the model to real-world dimensions (meters).
- **`orientation`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"default"` вҖ” Model orientation. 'align_image' auto-rotates to match the input image.
  Possible values: `default`, `align_image`
- **`quad`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Generate quad (4-sided) mesh topology instead of triangles.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Tripo3DOutput`
- **`model_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D model file in GLB format.
- **`model_urls`** *()* вҖ” URLs for different 3D model variants.
- **`rendered_image`** *(?)* вҖ” Preview render of the generated 3D model.

---

## tripo3d/h3.1/multiview-to-3d

**Title:** Tripo H3.1 Multiview to 3D
**Description:** Generate 3D models from multiple view images using Tripo H3.1.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/tripo3d/h3.1/multiview-to-3d`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** 3d, multiview-to-3d, 3d-generation, tripo

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FullMultiviewTo3DInput`
**Required fields:** `image_urls`

- **`image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” 2 to 4 image URLs of the same object from different angles. Order: [front, left, back, right]. Front view is required.
- **`face_limit`** *(integer)* вҖ” Target number of faces for the generated mesh. If not set, the model adaptively determines the count.
- **`texture`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate textures for the model.
- **`pbr`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate PBR (Physically Based Rendering) materials. If true, texture is also enabled.
- **`model_seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for geometry generation reproducibility.
- **`texture_seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for texture generation reproducibility.
- **`texture_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"standard"` вҖ” Quality level for textures.
  Possible values: `standard`, `detailed`
- **`geometry_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"standard"` вҖ” Quality level for geometry.
  Possible values: `standard`, `detailed`
- **`texture_alignment`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"original_image"` вҖ” How textures are aligned.
  Possible values: `original_image`, `geometry`
- **`auto_size`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Auto-scale the model to real-world dimensions (meters).
- **`orientation`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"default"` вҖ” Model orientation.
  Possible values: `default`, `align_image`
- **`quad`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Generate quad (4-sided) mesh topology instead of triangles.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Tripo3DOutput`
- **`model_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D model file in GLB format.
- **`model_urls`** *()* вҖ” URLs for different 3D model variants.
- **`rendered_image`** *(?)* вҖ” Preview render of the generated 3D model.

---

## tripo3d/p1/image-to-3d

**Title:** Tripo P1 Image to 3D
**Description:** Generate 3D models from a single image using Tripo P1.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/tripo3d/p1/image-to-3d`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** 3d, image-to-3d, 3d-generation, tripo

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `P1ImageTo3DInput`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input image for 3D model creation.
- **`face_limit`** *(integer)* вҖ” Target number of faces for the generated mesh. If not set, the model adaptively determines the count.
- **`texture`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate textures for the model.
- **`model_seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for geometry generation reproducibility.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Tripo3DOutput`
- **`model_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D model file in GLB format.
- **`model_urls`** *()* вҖ” URLs for different 3D model variants.
- **`rendered_image`** *(?)* вҖ” Preview render of the generated 3D model.

## tripo3d/tripo/v2.5/image-to-3d

**Title:** Tripo3D
**Description:** State of the art Image to 3D Object generation. Generate 3D model from a single image!
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/tripo3d/tripo/v2.5/image-to-3d`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** image-to-3d, stylized

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageTo3dInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” This is the random seed for model generation. The seed controls the geometry generation process, ensuring identical models when the same seed is used. This parameter is an integer and is randomly chosen if not set.
- **`face_limit`** *(integer)* вҖ” Limits the number of faces on the output model. If this option is not set, the face limit will be adaptively determined.
- **`pbr`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” A boolean option to enable pbr. The default value is True, set False to get a model without pbr. If this option is set to True, texture will be ignored and used as True.
- **`texture`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"standard"` вҖ” An option to enable texturing. Default is 'standard', set 'no' to get a model without any textures, and set 'HD' to get a model with hd quality textures.
  Possible values: `no`, `standard`, `HD`
- **`texture_seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” This is the random seed for texture generation. Using the same seed will produce identical textures. This parameter is an integer and is randomly chosen if not set. If you want a model with different textures, please use same seed and different texture_seed.
- **`auto_size`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Automatically scale the model to real-world dimensions, with the unit in meters. The default value is False.
- **`style`** *(enum)* вҖ” [DEPRECATED] Defines the artistic style or transformation to be applied to the 3D model, altering its appearance according to preset options (extra $0.05 per generation). Omit this option to keep the original style and apperance.
  Possible values: `person:person2cartoon`, `object:clay`, `object:steampunk`, `animal:venom`, `object:barbie`, `object:christmas`, `gold`, `ancient_bronze`
- **`quad`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Set True to enable quad mesh output (extra $0.05 per generation). If quad=True and face_limit is not set, the default face_limit will be 10000. Note: Enabling this option will force the output to be an FBX model.
- **`texture_alignment`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"original_image"` вҖ” Determines the prioritization of texture alignment in the 3D model. The default value is original_image.
  Possible values: `original_image`, `geometry`
- **`orientation`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"default"` вҖ” Set orientation=align_image to automatically rotate the model to align the original image. The default value is default.
  Possible values: `default`, `align_image`
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to use for model generation.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Tripo3dOutput`
- **`base_model`** *()* вҖ” Base model
- **`task_id`** *(string)* вҖ” The task id of the 3D model generation.
- **`rendered_image`** *()* вҖ” A preview image of the model
- **`model_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Model
- **`pbr_model`** *()* вҖ” Pbr model

---

## tripo3d/tripo/v2.5/multiview-to-3d

**Title:** Tripo3D
**Description:** State of the art Multiview to 3D Object generation. Generate 3D models from multiple images!
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/tripo3d/tripo/v2.5/multiview-to-3d`
**Category:** image-to-3d
**Tags:** stylized, multiview

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MultiviewTo3dInput`
**Required fields:** `front_image_url`

- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” This is the random seed for model generation. The seed controls the geometry generation process, ensuring identical models when the same seed is used. This parameter is an integer and is randomly chosen if not set.
- **`face_limit`** *(integer)* вҖ” Limits the number of faces on the output model. If this option is not set, the face limit will be adaptively determined.
- **`pbr`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” A boolean option to enable pbr. The default value is True, set False to get a model without pbr. If this option is set to True, texture will be ignored and used as True.
- **`texture`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"standard"` вҖ” An option to enable texturing. Default is 'standard', set 'no' to get a model without any textures, and set 'HD' to get a model with hd quality textures.
  Possible values: `no`, `standard`, `HD`
- **`texture_seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” This is the random seed for texture generation. Using the same seed will produce identical textures. This parameter is an integer and is randomly chosen if not set. If you want a model with different textures, please use same seed and different texture_seed.
- **`auto_size`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Automatically scale the model to real-world dimensions, with the unit in meters. The default value is False.
- **`style`** *(enum)* вҖ” [DEPRECATED] Defines the artistic style or transformation to be applied to the 3D model, altering its appearance according to preset options (extra $0.05 per generation). Omit this option to keep the original style and apperance.
  Possible values: `person:person2cartoon`, `object:clay`, `object:steampunk`, `animal:venom`, `object:barbie`, `object:christmas`, `gold`, `ancient_bronze`
- **`quad`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Set True to enable quad mesh output (extra $0.05 per generation). If quad=True and face_limit is not set, the default face_limit will be 10000. Note: Enabling this option will force the output to be an FBX model.
- **`texture_alignment`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"original_image"` вҖ” Determines the prioritization of texture alignment in the 3D model. The default value is original_image.
  Possible values: `original_image`, `geometry`
- **`orientation`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"default"` вҖ” Set orientation=align_image to automatically rotate the model to align the original image. The default value is default.
  Possible values: `default`, `align_image`
- **`front_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Front view image of the object.
- **`left_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Left view image of the object.
- **`back_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Back view image of the object.
- **`right_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Right view image of the object.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Tripo3dOutput`
- **`base_model`** *()* вҖ” Base model
- **`task_id`** *(string)* вҖ” The task id of the 3D model generation.
- **`rendered_image`** *()* вҖ” A preview image of the model
- **`model_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Model
- **`pbr_model`** *()* вҖ” Pbr model

---

## bria/reimagine/3.2

**Title:** Reimagine
**Description:** Reimagine uses a structure reference for generating new images while preserving the structure of an input image, guided by text prompts.
Perfect for transforming sketches, illustrations, or photos into new illustrations. Trained exclusively on licensed data
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/bria/reimagine/3.2`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** bria

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `InputModel`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Prompt for image generation.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” Number of inference steps.
  Range: min: 20, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `5555` вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1:1"` вҖ” Aspect ratio. Options: 1:1, 2:3, 3:2, 3:4, 4:3, 4:5, 5:4, 9:16, 16:9
  Possible values: `1:1`, `2:3`, `3:2`, `3:4`, `4:3`, `4:5`, `5:4`, `9:16`, `16:9`
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"Logo,Watermark,Ugly,Morbid,Extra fingers,Poorly drawn hands,Mutation,Blurry,Extra limbs,Gross proportions,Missing arms,Mutated hands,Long neck,Duplicate,Mutilated,Mutilated hands,Poorly drawn face,Deformed,Bad anatomy,Cloned face,Malformed limbs,Missing legs,Too many fingers"` вҖ” Negative prompt for image generation.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Guidance scale for text.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`truncate_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to truncate the prompt.
- **`prompt_enhancer`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to improve the prompt.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, returns the image directly in the response (increases latency).
- **`depth_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Depth control image (file or URL).
- **`depth_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Depth control strength (0.0 to 1.0).
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`depth_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Depth image preprocess.
- **`canny_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Canny edge control image (file or URL).
- **`canny_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Canny image preprocess.
- **`canny_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Canny edge control strength (0.0 to 1.0).
  Range: min: 0, max: 1

## fal-ai/bagel

**Title:** Bagel
**Description:** Bagel is a 7B parameter multimodal model from Bytedance-Seed that can generate both images and text.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bagel`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image-to-image, image-editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageGenInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed to use for the generation.
- **`use_thought`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to use thought tokens for generation. If set to true, the model will "think" to potentially improve generation quality. Increases generation time and increases the cost by 20%.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ImageOutput`
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
