## fal-ai/birefnet

**Title:** Birefnet Background Removal
**Description:** bilateral reference framework (BiRefNet) for high-resolution dichotomous image segmentation (DIS)
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/birefnet`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** background removal, segmentation, high-res, utility

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Input`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"General Use (Light)"` вҖ” 
            Model to use for background removal.
            The 'General Use (Light)' model is the original model used in the BiRefNet repository.
            The 'General Use (Heavy)' model is a slower but more accurate model.
            The 'Portrait' model is a model trained specifically for portrait images.
            The 'General Use (Light)' model is recommended for most use cases.

            The corresponding models are as follows:
            - 'General Use (Light)': BiRefNet-DIS_ep580.pth
            - 'General Use (Heavy)': BiRefNet-massive-epoch_240.pth
            - 'Portrait': BiRefNet-portrait-TR_P3M_10k-epoch_120.pth
        
  Possible values: `General Use (Light)`, `General Use (Heavy)`, `Portrait`
- **`operating_resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1024x1024"` вҖ” The resolution to operate on. The higher the resolution, the more accurate the output will be for high res input images.
  Possible values: `1024x1024`, `2048x2048`
- **`output_mask`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to output the mask used to remove the background
- **`refine_foreground`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to refine the foreground using the estimated mask
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to remove background from
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the output image
  Possible values: `webp`, `png`, `gif`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” Image with background removed
- **`mask_image`** *(?)* вҖ” Mask used to remove the background

---

## fal-ai/birefnet/v2

**Title:** Birefnet Background Removal
**Description:** bilateral reference framework (BiRefNet) for high-resolution dichotomous image segmentation (DIS)
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/birefnet/v2`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** background removal, segmentation, high-res, utility

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `InputV2`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"General Use (Light)"` вҖ” 
            Model to use for background removal.
            The 'General Use (Light)' model is the original model used in the BiRefNet repository.
            The 'General Use (Light 2K)' model is the original model used in the BiRefNet repository but trained with 2K images.
            The 'General Use (Heavy)' model is a slower but more accurate model.
            The 'Matting' model is a model trained specifically for matting images.
            The 'Portrait' model is a model trained specifically for portrait images.
            The 'General Use (Dynamic)' model supports dynamic resolutions from 256x256 to 2304x2304.
            The 'General Use (Light)' model is recommended for most use cases.

            The corresponding models are as follows:
            - 'General Use (Light)': BiRefNet
            - 'General Use (Light 2K)': BiRefNet_lite-2K
            - 'General Use (Heavy)': BiRefNet_lite
            - 'Matting': BiRefNet-matting
            - 'Portrait': BiRefNet-portrait
            - 'General Use (Dynamic)': BiRefNet_dynamic
        
  Possible values: `General Use (Light)`, `General Use (Light 2K)`, `General Use (Heavy)`, `Matting`, `Portrait`, `General Use (Dynamic)`
- **`operating_resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1024x1024"` вҖ” The resolution to operate on. The higher the resolution, the more accurate the output will be for high res input images. The '2304x2304' option is only available for the 'General Use (Dynamic)' model.
  Possible values: `1024x1024`, `2048x2048`, `2304x2304`
- **`output_mask`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to output the mask used to remove the background
- **`refine_foreground`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to refine the foreground using the estimated mask
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to remove background from
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the output image
  Possible values: `webp`, `png`, `gif`
- **`mask_only`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to return only the segmentation mask without applying it to the image. When set to `True`, only the mask will be returned and foreground refinement will be skipped. Useful for reducing computation and data transfer when only the mask is needed.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `OutputV2`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” Image with background removed. When `mask_only` is `True`, this contains the segmentation mask instead.
- **`mask_image`** *(?)* вҖ” Mask used to remove the background

---

## fal-ai/bria/background/remove

**Title:** Bria RMBG 2.0
**Description:** Bria RMBG 2.0 enables seamless removal of backgrounds from images, ideal for professional editing tasks. Trained exclusively on licensed data for safe and risk-free commercial use. Model weights for commercial use are available here: https://share-eu1.hsforms.com/2GLpEVQqJTI2Lj7AMYwgfIwf4e04
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bria/background/remove`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** background removal, image segmentation, high resolution, utility, rembg

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `BGRemoveInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Input Image to erase from
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `BGRemoveOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The generated image

---

## fal-ai/bria/background/replace

**Title:** Bria Background Replace
**Description:** Bria Background Replace allows for efficient swapping of backgrounds in images via text prompts or reference image, delivering realistic and polished results. Trained exclusively on licensed data for safe and risk-free commercial use 
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bria/background/replace`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `BGReplaceInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Input Image to erase from
- **`ref_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The URL of the reference image to be used for generating the new background. Use "" to leave empty. Either ref_image_url or bg_prompt has to be provided but not both. If both ref_image_url and ref_image_file are provided, ref_image_url will be used. Accepted formats are jpeg, jpg, png, webp.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt you would like to use to generate images.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The negative prompt you would like to use to generate images.
- **`refine_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to refine prompt
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`fast`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use the fast model
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of Images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `BGReplaceOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed value used for generation.

---

## fal-ai/bria/eraser

**Title:** Bria Eraser
**Description:** Bria Eraser enables precise removal of unwanted objects from images while maintaining high-quality outputs. Trained exclusively on licensed data for safe and risk-free commercial use. Access the model's source code and weights: https://bria.ai/contact-us
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bria/eraser`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image editing, object removal

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `EraserInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `mask_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Input Image to erase from
- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the binary mask image that represents the area that will be cleaned.
- **`mask_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"manual"` вҖ” You can use this parameter to specify the type of the input mask from the list. 'manual' opttion should be used in cases in which the mask had been generated by a user (e.g. with a brush tool), and 'automatic' mask type should be used when mask had been generated by an algorithm like 'SAM'.
  Possible values: `manual`, `automatic`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`preserve_alpha`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, attempts to preserve the alpha channel of the input image.
        

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `EraserOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The generated image

---

## fal-ai/bria/expand

**Title:** Bria Expand Image
**Description:** Bria Expand expands images beyond their borders in high quality. Trained exclusively on licensed data for safe and risk-free commercial use. Access the model's source code and weights: https://bria.ai/contact-us
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bria/expand`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** outpainting

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageExpansionInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `canvas_size`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the input image.
- **`canvas_size`** *(list<integer>)* вҖ” The desired size of the final image, after the expansion. should have an area of less than 5000x5000 pixels.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(string)* вҖ” The desired aspect ratio of the final image. Will be used over original_image_size and original_image_location if provided.
  Possible values: `1:1`, `2:3`, `3:2`, `3:4`, `4:3`, `4:5`, `5:4`, `9:16`, `16:9`
- **`original_image_size`** *(array)* вҖ” The desired size of the original image, inside the full canvas. Ensure that the ratio of input image foreground or main subject to the canvas area is greater than 15% to achieve optimal results. Will be ignored if aspect_ratio is provided.
- **`original_image_location`** *(array)* вҖ” The desired location of the original image, inside the full canvas. Provide the location of the upper left corner of the original image. The location can also be outside the canvas (the original image will be cropped). Will be ignored if aspect_ratio is provided.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Text on which you wish to base the image expansion. This parameter is optional. Bria currently supports prompts in English only, excluding special characters.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” You can choose whether you want your generated expension to be random or predictable. You can recreate the same result in the future by using the seed value of a result from the response. You can exclude this parameter if you are not interested in recreating your results. This parameter is optional.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The negative prompt you would like to use to generate images.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ImageExpansionOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed value used for generation.
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The generated image

---

**Title:** Bria GenFill
**Description:** Bria GenFill enables high-quality object addition or visual transformation. Trained exclusively on licensed data for safe and risk-free commercial use. Access the model's source code and weights: https://bria.ai/contact-us

**Tags:** image editing

- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the binary mask image that represents the area that will be cleaned.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt you would like to use to generate images.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The negative prompt you would like to use to generate images.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of Images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `GenFillOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Generated Images
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed value used for generation.

## fal-ai/bria/product-shot

**Title:** Bria Product Shot
**Description:** Place any product in any scenery with just a prompt or reference image while maintaining high integrity of the product. Trained exclusively on licensed data for safe and risk-free commercial use and optimized for eCommerce.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bria/product-shot`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** product photography

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ProductShotInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the product shot to be placed in a lifestyle shot. If both image_url and image_file are provided, image_url will be used. Accepted formats are jpeg, jpg, png, webp. Maximum file size 12MB.
- **`scene_description`** *(string)* вҖ” Text description of the new scene or background for the provided product shot. Bria currently supports prompts in English only, excluding special characters.
- **`ref_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The URL of the reference image to be used for generating the new scene or background for the product shot. Use "" to leave empty.Either ref_image_url or scene_description has to be provided but not both. If both ref_image_url and ref_image_file are provided, ref_image_url will be used. Accepted formats are jpeg, jpg, png, webp.
- **`optimize_description`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to optimize the scene description
- **`num_results`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of lifestyle product shots you would like to generate. You will get num_results x 10 results when placement_type=automatic and according to the number of required placements x num_results if placement_type=manual_placement.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`fast`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use the fast model
- **`placement_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"manual_placement"` вҖ” This parameter allows you to control the positioning of the product in the image. Choosing 'original' will preserve the original position of the product in the image. Choosing 'automatic' will generate results with the 10 recommended positions for the product. Choosing 'manual_placement' will allow you to select predefined positions (using the parameter 'manual_placement_selection'). Selecting 'manual_padding' will allow you to control the position and size of the image by defining the desired padding in pixels around the product.
  Possible values: `original`, `automatic`, `manual_placement`, `manual_padding`
- **`original_quality`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” This flag is only relevant when placement_type=original. If true, the output image retains the original input image's size; otherwise, the image is scaled to 1 megapixel (1MP) while preserving its aspect ratio.
- **`shot_size`** *(list<integer>)* вҖ” Default: `[1000,1000]` вҖ” The desired size of the final product shot. For optimal results, the total number of pixels should be around 1,000,000. This parameter is only relevant when placement_type=automatic or placement_type=manual_placement.
- **`manual_placement_selection`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"bottom_center"` вҖ” If you've selected placement_type=manual_placement, you should use this parameter to specify which placements/positions you would like to use from the list. You can select more than one placement in one request.
  Possible values: `upper_left`, `upper_right`, `bottom_left`, `bottom_right`, `right_center`, `left_center`, `upper_center`, `bottom_center`, `center_vertical`, `center_horizontal`
- **`padding_values`** *(list<integer>)* вҖ” The desired padding in pixels around the product, when using placement_type=manual_padding. The order of the values is [left, right, top, bottom]. For optimal results, the total number of pixels, including padding, should be around 1,000,000. It is recommended to first use the product cutout API, get the cutout and understand the size of the result, and then define the required padding and use the cutout as an input for this API.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ProductShotOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images

---

## fal-ai/bria/reimagine

**Title:** Bria
**Description:** Structure Reference allows generating new images while preserving the structure of an input image, guided by text prompts. Perfect for transforming sketches, illustrations, or photos into new illustrations. Trained exclusively on licensed data for safe and risk-free commercial use.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bria/reimagine`
**Category:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ReimagineInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt you would like to use to generate images.
- **`structure_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The URL of the structure reference image. Use "" to leave empty. Accepted formats are jpeg, jpg, png, webp.
- **`structure_ref_influence`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.75` вҖ” The influence of the structure reference on the generated image.
- **`num_results`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” How many images you would like to generate. When using any Guidance Method, Value is set to 1.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`fast`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use the fast model
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” The number of iterations the model goes through to refine the generated image. This parameter is optional.
  Range: min: 20, max: 50
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ReimagineOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed value used for generation.

## fal-ai/cartoonify

**Title:** Cartoonify
**Description:** Transform images into 3D cartoon artwork using an AI model that applies cartoon stylization while preserving the original image's composition and details.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/cartoonify`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** stylized, transform

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `CartoonifyInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to apply Pixar style to
- **`scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Scale factor for the Pixar effect
  Range: min: 0.1, max: 2
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” Guidance scale for the generation
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” Number of inference steps
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker
- **`use_cfg_zero`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to use CFG zero
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for image generation. Same seed with same parameters will generate same image.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.

## fal-ai/cat-vton

**Title:** try-on
**Description:** Image based high quality Virtual Try-On
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/cat-vton`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** try-on, fashion, clothing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `CATVTONInput`
**Required fields:** `human_image_url`, `garment_image_url`, `cloth_type`

- **`human_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Url for the human image.
- **`garment_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Url to the garment image.
- **`cloth_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” 
        Type of the Cloth to be tried on.

        Options:
        upper: Upper body cloth
        lower: Lower body cloth
        overall: Full body cloth
        inner: Inner cloth, like T-shirt inside a jacket
        outer: Outer cloth, like a jacket over a T-shirt
        
  Possible values: `upper`, `lower`, `overall`, `inner`, `outer`
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"portrait_4_3"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same input given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `CATVTONOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The output image.

---

## fal-ai/ccsr

**Title:** CCSR Upscaler
**Description:** SOTA Image Upscaler
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ccsr`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** upscaling

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `CCSRInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL or data URI of the image to upscale.
- **`scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” The scale of the output image. The higher the scale, the bigger the output image will be.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`tile_diffusion`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” If specified, a patch-based sampling strategy will be used for sampling.
  Possible values: `none`, `mix`, `gaussian`
- **`tile_diffusion_size`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1024` вҖ” Size of patch.
  Range: min: 256, max: 2048
- **`tile_diffusion_stride`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `512` вҖ” Stride of sliding patch.
  Range: min: 128, max: 1024
- **`tile_vae`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If specified, a patch-based sampling strategy will be used for VAE decoding.
- **`tile_vae_decoder_size`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `226` вҖ” Size of VAE patch.
  Range: min: 64, max: 2048
- **`tile_vae_encoder_size`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1024` вҖ” Size of latent image
  Range: min: 128, max: 2048
- **`steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” The number of steps to run the model for. The higher the number the better the quality and longer it will take to generate.
  Range: min: 10, max: 100
- **`t_max`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.6667` вҖ” The ending point of uniform sampling strategy.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`t_min`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.3333` вҖ” The starting point of uniform sampling strategy.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`color_fix_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"adain"` вҖ” Type of color correction for samples.
  Possible values: `none`, `wavelet`, `adain`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for reproducibility. Different seeds will make slightly different results.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `CCSROutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The generated image file info.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the generation.

---

**Category:** image-to-image

- **`scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Zoom scale in powers of 2
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`center_x`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” X coordinate of zoom center (0-1)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`center_y`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Y coordinate of zoom center (0-1)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`user_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Additional prompt text to guide the zoom enhancement
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

- **`zoom_center`** *(list<number>)* вҖ” Center coordinates used for zoom
- **`scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Actual linear zoom scale applied

## fal-ai/chrono-edit

**Title:** Chrono Edit
**Description:** NVIDIA's Logically Consistent and Physics-Aware Image Editing Model
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/chrono-edit`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image-editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ChronoEditInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `prompt`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The image to edit.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to edit the image.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The guidance scale for the inference.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`enable_temporal_reasoning`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable temporal reasoning.
- **`num_temporal_reasoning_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” The number of temporal reasoning steps to perform.
  Range: min: 2, max: 12
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"480p"` вҖ” The resolution of the output image.
  Possible values: `480p`, `720p`
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the inference.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the output image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to return the image in sync mode.
- **`turbo_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable turbo mode to use for faster inference.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ChronoEditOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the inference.
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The edited image.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the inference.

## fal-ai/chrono-edit-lora

**Title:** Chrono Edit Lora
**Description:** LoRA endpoint for the Chrono Edit model.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/chrono-edit-lora`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image-to-image, image-editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ChronoEditLoRAInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `prompt`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The image to edit.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to edit the image.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The guidance scale for the inference.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`enable_temporal_reasoning`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable temporal reasoning.
- **`num_temporal_reasoning_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” The number of temporal reasoning steps to perform.
  Range: min: 2, max: 12
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"480p"` вҖ” The resolution of the output image.
  Possible values: `480p`, `720p`
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the inference.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the output image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to return the image in sync mode.
- **`turbo_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable turbo mode to use for faster inference.
- **`loras`** *(list<ChronoLoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Optional additional LoRAs to merge for this request (max 3).

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ChronoEditOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the inference.
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The edited image.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the inference.

**Title:** Chrono Edit Lora Gallery

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The image to edit.
- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional mask image where black areas indicate regions to sketch/paint.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Describe how to transform the sketched regions.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” Number of denoising steps to run.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"480p"` вҖ” The resolution of the output image.
  Possible values: `480p`, `720p`
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale factor for the LoRA adapter.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the inference.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the output image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to return the image in sync mode.
- **`turbo_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable turbo mode to use faster inference.
- **`loras`** *(list<ChronoLoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Optional additional LoRAs to merge (max 3).

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ChronoEditOutput`

- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the inference.
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The edited image.

- **`upscale_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Target scale factor for the output resolution.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The guidance scale for the inference.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” Number of inference steps for the upscaling pass.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale factor for the LoRA adapter.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the inference.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the output image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to return the image in sync mode.
- **`loras`** *(list<ChronoLoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Optional additional LoRAs to merge (max 3).

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ChronoEditOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the inference.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the inference.
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The edited image.

## fal-ai/clarity-upscaler

**Title:** Clarity Upscaler
**Description:** Clarity upscaler for upscaling images with high very fidelity.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/clarity-upscaler`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** upscaling

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Input`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to upscale.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"masterpiece, best quality, highres"` вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`upscale_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” The upscale factor
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"(worst quality, low quality, normal quality:2)"` вҖ” The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want in the image.
- **`creativity`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.35` вҖ” 
            The creativity of the model. The higher the creativity, the more the model will deviate from the prompt.
            Refers to the denoise strength of the sampling.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`resemblance`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.6` вҖ” 
            The resemblance of the upscaled image to the original image. The higher the resemblance, the more the model will try to keep the original image.
            Refers to the strength of the ControlNet.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `18` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 4, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to false, the safety checker will be disabled.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” The timings of the different steps in the workflow.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used to generate the image.
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The URL of the generated image.

---

## fal-ai/codeformer

**Title:** CodeFormer
**Description:** Fix distorted or blurred photos of people with CodeFormer.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/codeformer`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image-restoration, faces, utility

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `CodeformerInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to be used for relighting
- **`fidelity`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Weight of the fidelity factor.
- **`upscale_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Upscaling factor
- **`aligned`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Should faces etc should be aligned.
- **`only_center_face`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Should only center face be restored
- **`face_upscale`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Should faces be upscaled
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducible generation.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ConformerOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The generated image file info.

---

## fal-ai/creative-upscaler

**Title:** Creative Upscaler
**Description:** Create creative upscaled images.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/creative-upscaler`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** upscaling

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `CreativeUpscalerInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`model_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"SD_1_5"` вҖ” The type of model to use for the upscaling. Default is SD_1_5
  Possible values: `SD_1_5`, `SDXL`
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The image to upscale.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results. If no prompt is provide BLIP2 will be used to generate a prompt.
- **`scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” The scale of the output image. The higher the scale, the bigger the output image will be.
  Range: min: 1, max: 5
- **`creativity`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” How much the output can deviate from the original
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`detail`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” How much detail to add
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`shape_preservation`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.25` вҖ” How much to preserve the shape of the original image
  Range: min: 0, max: 3
- **`prompt_suffix`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `" high quality, highly detailed, high resolution, sharp"` вҖ” The suffix to add to the prompt. This is useful to add a common ending to all prompts such as 'high quality' etc or embedding tokens.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"blurry, low resolution, bad, ugly, low quality, pixelated, interpolated, compression artifacts, noisey, grainy"` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use.Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 16
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `20` вҖ” 
            The number of inference steps to use for generating the image. The more steps
            the better the image will be but it will also take longer to generate.
        
  Range: min: 1, max: 200
- **`enable_safety_checks`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the resulting image will be checked whether it includes any
            potentially unsafe content. If it does, it will be replaced with a black
            image.
        
- **`skip_ccsr`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the image will not be processed by the CCSR model before
            being processed by the creativity model.
        
- **`override_size_limits`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            Allow for large uploads that could take a very long time.
        
- **`base_model_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL to the base model to use for the upscaling
- **`additional_lora_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL to the additional LORA model to use for the upscaling. Default is None
- **`additional_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the additional LORA model to use for the upscaling. Default is 1.0
- **`additional_embedding_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL to the additional embeddings to use for the upscaling. Default is None

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `CreativeUpscalerOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The generated image file info.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        

---

## fal-ai/esrgan

**Title:** Upscale Images
**Description:** Upscale images by a given factor.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/esrgan`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** upscaling, high-res

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `UpscaleInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Url to input image
- **`scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Rescaling factor
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`tile`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Tile size. Default is 0, that is no tile. When encountering the out-of-GPU-memory issue, please specify it, e.g., 400 or 200
- **`face`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Upscaling a face
- **`model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"RealESRGAN_x4plus"` вҖ” Model to use for upscaling
  Possible values: `RealESRGAN_x4plus`, `RealESRGAN_x2plus`, `RealESRGAN_x4plus_anime_6B`, `RealESRGAN_x4_v3`, `RealESRGAN_x4_wdn_v3`, `RealESRGAN_x4_anime_v3`
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” Output image format (png or jpeg)
  Possible values: `png`, `jpeg`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `UpscaleOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” Upscaled image

## fal-ai/evf-sam

**Title:** EVF-SAM2 Segmentation
**Description:** EVF-SAM2 combines natural language understanding with advanced segmentation capabilities, allowing you to precisely mask image regions using intuitive positive and negative text prompts.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/evf-sam`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** segmentation, mask

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate segmentation from.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Areas to exclude from segmentation (will be subtracted from prompt results)
- **`semantic_type`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Enable semantic level segmentation for body parts, background or multi objects
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input image
- **`mask_only`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Output only the binary mask instead of masked image
- **`use_grounding_dino`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Use GroundingDINO instead of SAM for segmentation
- **`revert_mask`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Invert the mask (background becomes foreground and vice versa)
- **`blur_mask`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Apply Gaussian blur to the mask. Value determines kernel size (must be odd number)
  Range: min: 0, max: 50
- **`expand_mask`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Expand/dilate the mask by specified pixels
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`fill_holes`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Fill holes in the mask using morphological operations

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ImageOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The segmented output image

---

## fal-ai/face-to-sticker

**Title:** Face to Sticker
**Description:** Create stickers from faces.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/face-to-sticker`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** sticker, editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FaceToStickerInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `prompt`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `20` вҖ” 
            Increasing the amount of steps tells Stable Diffusion that it should take more steps
            to generate your final result which can increase the amount of detail in your image.
        
  Range: min: 10, max: 40
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`instant_id_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” The strength of the instant ID.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`ip_adapter_weight`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.2` вҖ” The weight of the IP adapter.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`ip_adapter_noise`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The amount of noise to add to the IP adapter.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`upscale`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to upscale the image 2x.
- **`upscale_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `10` вҖ” The number of steps to use for upscaling. Only used if `upscale` is `true`.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to false, the safety checker will be disabled.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `FaceToStickerOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images.
- **`sticker_image`** *()* вҖ” The generated face sticker image.
- **`sticker_image_background_removed`** *()* вҖ” The generated face sticker image with the background removed.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed used during the inference.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(object)* вҖ” 
            Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
            The key is the image type and the value is a boolean.
        

---

## fal-ai/fashn/tryon/v1.5

**Title:** FASHN Virtual Try-On V1.5
**Description:** FASHN v1.5 delivers precise virtual try-on capabilities, accurately rendering garment details like text and patterns at 576x864 resolution from both on-model and flat-lay photo references.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/fashn/tryon/v1.5`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** try-on, fashion, clothing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Input`
**Required fields:** `model_image`, `garment_image`

- **`model_image`** *(string)* вҖ” URL or base64 of the model image
- **`garment_image`** *(string)* вҖ” URL or base64 of the garment image
- **`category`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Category of the garment to try-on. 'auto' will attempt to automatically detect the category of the garment.
  Possible values: `tops`, `bottoms`, `one-pieces`, `auto`
- **`mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” Specifies the mode of operation. 'performance' mode is faster but may sacrifice quality, 'balanced' mode is a balance between speed and quality, and 'quality' mode is slower but produces higher quality results.
  Possible values: `performance`, `balanced`, `quality`
- **`garment_photo_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Specifies the type of garment photo to optimize internal parameters for better performance. 'model' is for photos of garments on a model, 'flat-lay' is for flat-lay or ghost mannequin images, and 'auto' attempts to automatically detect the photo type.
  Possible values: `auto`, `model`, `flat-lay`
- **`moderation_level`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"permissive"` вҖ” Content moderation level for garment images. 'none' disables moderation, 'permissive' blocks only explicit content, 'conservative' also blocks underwear and swimwear.
  Possible values: `none`, `permissive`, `conservative`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Sets random operations to a fixed state. Use the same seed to reproduce results with the same inputs, or different seed to force different results.
- **`num_samples`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate in a single run. Image generation has a random element in it, so trying multiple images at once increases the chances of getting a good result.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`segmentation_free`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Disables human parsing on the model image.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” Output format of the generated images. 'png' is highest quality, while 'jpeg' is faster
  Possible values: `png`, `jpeg`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`images`** *(list<File>)*

---

**Title:** FASHN Virtual Try-On V1.6

  Possible values: `tops`, `bottoms`, `one-pieces`, `auto`
- **`mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” Specifies the mode of operation. 'performance' mode is faster but may sacrifice quality, 'balanced' mode is a balance between speed and quality, and 'quality' mode is slower but produces higher quality results.
  Possible values: `performance`, `balanced`, `quality`
- **`garment_photo_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Specifies the type of garment photo to optimize internal parameters for better performance. 'model' is for photos of garments on a model, 'flat-lay' is for flat-lay or ghost mannequin images, and 'auto' attempts to automatically detect the photo type.
  Possible values: `auto`, `model`, `flat-lay`
- **`moderation_level`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"permissive"` вҖ” Content moderation level for garment images. 'none' disables moderation, 'permissive' blocks only explicit content, 'conservative' also blocks underwear and swimwear.
  Possible values: `none`, `permissive`, `conservative`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Sets random operations to a fixed state. Use the same seed to reproduce results with the same inputs, or different seed to force different results.
- **`num_samples`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate in a single run. Image generation has a random element in it, so trying multiple images at once increases the chances of getting a good result.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`segmentation_free`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Disables human parsing on the model image.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” Output format of the generated images. 'png' is highest quality, while 'jpeg' is faster
  Possible values: `png`, `jpeg`

## fal-ai/fast-lcm-diffusion/image-to-image

**Title:** Latent Consistency Models (v1.5/XL)
**Description:** Run SDXL at the speed of light
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/fast-lcm-diffusion/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** lcm, diffusion, turbo, real-time, editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageToImageLCMInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `prompt`

- **`model_name`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"stabilityai/stable-diffusion-xl-base-1.0"` вҖ” The name of the model to use.
  Possible values: `stabilityai/stable-diffusion-xl-base-1.0`, `runwayml/stable-diffusion-v1-5`
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as a starting point for the generation.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use.Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `6` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 32
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.95` вҖ” determines how much the generated image resembles the initial image
  Range: min: 0.05, max: 1
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`safety_checker_version`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"v1"` вҖ” The version of the safety checker to use. v1 is the default CompVis safety checker. v2 uses a custom ViT model.
  Possible values: `v1`, `v2`
- **`expand_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the prompt will be expanded with additional prompts.
- **`format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`guidance_rescale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The rescale factor for the CFG.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`request_id`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            An id bound to a request, can be used with response to identify the request
            itself.
        
- **`preserve_aspect_ratio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
        If set to true, the aspect ratio of the generated image will be preserved even
        if the image size is too large. However, if the image is not a multiple of 32
        in width or height, it will be resized to the nearest multiple of 32. By default,
        this snapping to the nearest multiple of 32 will not preserve the aspect ratio.
        Set crop_output to True, to crop the output to the proper aspect ratio
        after generating.
        
- **`crop_output`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
        If set to true, the output cropped to the proper aspect ratio after generating.
        

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        

---

  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion

- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.

- **`safety_checker_version`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"v1"` вҖ” The version of the safety checker to use. v1 is the default CompVis safety checker. v2 uses a custom ViT model.
  Possible values: `v1`, `v2`
- **`expand_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the prompt will be expanded with additional prompts.
- **`format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`guidance_rescale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The rescale factor for the CFG.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`request_id`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            An id bound to a request, can be used with response to identify the request
            itself.
        

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.

        in width or height, it will be resized to the nearest multiple of 32. By default,

        Set crop_output to True, to crop the output to the proper aspect ratio

## fal-ai/fast-sdxl-controlnet-canny/image-to-image

**Title:** ControlNet SDXL
**Description:** Generate Images with ControlNet.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/fast-sdxl-controlnet-canny/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** diffusion, controlnet, editing, manipulation

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageToImageControlNetInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `prompt`, `control_image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`control_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`controlnet_conditioning_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The scale of the controlnet conditioning.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as a starting point for the generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use.Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image. Leave it none to automatically infer from the control image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 65
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.95` вҖ” determines how much the generated image resembles the initial image
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the function will wait for the image to be generated and uploaded
            before returning the response. This will increase the latency of the function but
            it allows you to get the image directly in the response without going through the CDN.
        
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The list of LoRA weights to use.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`expand_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the prompt will be expanded with additional prompts.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        

---

## fal-ai/fast-sdxl-controlnet-canny/inpainting

**Title:** ControlNet SDXL
**Description:** Generate Images with ControlNet.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/fast-sdxl-controlnet-canny/inpainting`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** diffusion, controlnet, editing, manipulation

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `InpaintingControlNetInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `mask_url`, `prompt`, `control_image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`control_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`controlnet_conditioning_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The scale of the controlnet conditioning.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as a starting point for the generation.
- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the mask to use for inpainting.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use.Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image. Leave it none to automatically infer from the control image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 65
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.95` вҖ” determines how much the generated image resembles the initial image
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the function will wait for the image to be generated and uploaded
            before returning the response. This will increase the latency of the function but
            it allows you to get the image directly in the response without going through the CDN.
        
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The list of LoRA weights to use.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`expand_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the prompt will be expanded with additional prompts.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.

## fal-ai/fast-sdxl/image-to-image

**Title:** Stable Diffusion XL
**Description:** Run SDXL at the speed of light
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/fast-sdxl/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** diffusion, high-res, lora, ip-adapter, controlnet

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `prompt`

- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`expand_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the prompt will be expanded with additional prompts.
- **`safety_checker_version`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"v1"` вҖ” The version of the safety checker to use. v1 is the default CompVis safety checker. v2 uses a custom ViT model.
  Possible values: `v1`, `v2`
- **`crop_output`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
        If set to true, the output cropped to the proper aspect ratio after generating.
        
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`preserve_aspect_ratio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
        If set to true, the aspect ratio of the generated image will be preserved even
        if the image size is too large. However, if the image is not a multiple of 32
        in width or height, it will be resized to the nearest multiple of 32. By default,
        this snapping to the nearest multiple of 32 will not preserve the aspect ratio.
        Set crop_output to True, to crop the output to the proper aspect ratio
        after generating.
        
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The list of LoRA weights to use.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 65
- **`request_id`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            An id bound to a request, can be used with response to identify the request
            itself.
        
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `null` вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.95` вҖ” determines how much the generated image resembles the initial image
  Range: min: 0.05, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use.Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as a starting point for the generation.
- **`embeddings`** *(list<Embedding>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The list of embeddings to use.
- **`format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        

---

## fal-ai/ffmpeg-api/extract-frame

**Title:** Ffmpeg Api
**Description:** ffmpeg endpoint for first, middle and last frame extraction from videos
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ffmpeg-api/extract-frame`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** utility, editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FrameInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video file to use as the video track
- **`frame_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"first"` вҖ” Type of frame to extract: first, middle, or last frame of the video
  Possible values: `first`, `middle`, `last`

- **`images`** *(list<Image>)*

## fal-ai/film

**Title:** FILM
**Description:** Interpolate images with FILM - Frame Interpolation for Large Motion
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/film`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** interpolation

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FILMImageInput`
**Required fields:** `start_image_url`, `end_image_url`

- **`start_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the first image to use as the starting point for interpolation.
- **`end_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the second image to use as the ending point for interpolation.
- **`output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"images"` вҖ” The type of output to generate; either individual images or a video.
  Possible values: `images`, `video`
- **`image_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the output images. Only applicable if output_type is 'images'.
  Possible values: `png`, `jpeg`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the output video. Only applicable if output_type is 'video'.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the output video. Only applicable if output_type is 'video'.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of frames to generate between the input images.
  Range: min: 1, max: 64
- **`include_start`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to include the start image in the output.
- **`include_end`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to include the end image in the output.
- **`fps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” Frames per second for the output video. Only applicable if output_type is 'video'.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `FILMImageOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The generated frames as individual images.
- **`video`** *(?)* вҖ” The generated video file, if output_type is 'video'.

---

## fal-ai/finegrain-eraser

**Title:** finegrain eraser
**Description:** Finegrain Eraser removes objectsвҖ”along with their shadows, reflections, and lighting artifactsвҖ”using only natural language, seamlessly filling the scene with contextually accurate content.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/finegrain-eraser`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** utility, editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `PromptEraseRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `prompt`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to edit
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text description of what to erase
- **`mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"standard"` вҖ” Erase quality mode
  Possible values: `express`, `standard`, `premium`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducible generation

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `EraseOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The edited image with content erased
- **`used_seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed used for generation

---

## fal-ai/finegrain-eraser/bbox

**Title:** finegrain eraser
**Description:** Finegrain Eraser removes any object selected with a bounding boxвҖ”along with its shadows, reflections, and lighting artifactsвҖ”seamlessly reconstructing the scene with contextually accurate content.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/finegrain-eraser/bbox`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** utility, editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `BBoxEraseRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `box_prompts`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to edit
- **`box_prompts`** *(list<BoxPromptBase>)* вҖ” List of bounding box coordinates to erase (only one box prompt is supported)
- **`mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"standard"` вҖ” Erase quality mode
  Possible values: `express`, `standard`, `premium`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducible generation

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `EraseOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The edited image with content erased
- **`used_seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed used for generation

---

## fal-ai/finegrain-eraser/mask

**Title:** finegrain eraser
**Description:** Finegrain Eraser removes any object selected with a maskвҖ”along with its shadows, reflections, and lighting artifactsвҖ”seamlessly reconstructing the scene with contextually accurate content.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/finegrain-eraser/mask`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** utility, editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MaskEraseRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `mask_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to edit
- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the mask image. Should be a binary mask where white (255) indicates areas to erase
- **`mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"standard"` вҖ” Erase quality mode
  Possible values: `express`, `standard`, `premium`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducible generation

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `EraseOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The edited image with content erased
- **`used_seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed used for generation

---

## fal-ai/firered-image-edit

**Title:** Firered Image Edit
**Description:** FireRed Image Edit is FireRed's state of the art open source editing model, re-trained from Qwen Image Edit 2509.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/firered-image-edit`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image-editing, firered

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FireRedImageEditInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_urls`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The editing instruction describing what changes to make to the image. Supports both English and Chinese instructions.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image. If None, uses the input image dimensions.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform. More steps generally produce higher quality results.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale. Higher values make the model follow the prompt more closely.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” The URLs of the images to edit. Supports single image editing and multi-image references (e.g., virtual try-on, style transfer from reference).
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The negative prompt for the generation.
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use for inference speed optimization.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `FireRedImageEditOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The edited image files info.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

---

**Title:** Firered Image Edit V1.1
**Description:** FireRed Image Edit v1.1 is an updated version of FireRed Image Edit, with improved image editing capabilities.

**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** firered-image-edit

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FireRedImageEdit11Input`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_urls`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The editing instruction describing what changes to make to the image. Supports both English and Chinese instructions.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image. If None, uses the input image dimensions.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform. More steps generally produce higher quality results.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale. Higher values make the model follow the prompt more closely.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” The URLs of the images to edit. Supports single image editing and multi-image references (e.g., virtual try-on, style transfer from reference, portrait makeup).
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The negative prompt for the generation.
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use for inference speed optimization.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `FireRedImageEdit11Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The edited image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
