## fal-ai/flowedit

**Title:** Flow-Edit
**Description:** The model provides you high quality image editing capabilities.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flowedit`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FlowEditInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `source_prompt`, `target_prompt`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to be used for relighting
- **`source_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Prompt of the image to be used.
- **`target_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Prompt of the image to be made.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducible generation. If set none, a random seed will be used.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” Steps for which the model should run.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`src_guidance_scale`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1.5` вҖ” Guidance scale for the source.
  Range: min: 0, max: 30
- **`tar_guidance_scale`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `5.5` вҖ” Guidance scale for target.
  Range: min: 0, max: 30
- **`n_avg`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Average step count
- **`n_max`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `23` вҖ” Control the strength of the edit
- **`n_min`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Minimum step for improved style edits

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `FlowEditOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The generated image file info.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        

---

## fal-ai/flux-1/dev/image-to-image

**Title:** FLUX.1 [dev]
**Description:** FLUX.1 [dev] is a 12 billion parameter flow transformer that generates high-quality images from text. It is suitable for personal and commercial use.

**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-1/dev/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `BaseFlux1ImageToInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `prompt`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to generate an image from.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.95` вҖ” The strength of the initial image. Higher strength values are better for this model.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `40` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 10, max: 50
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
        The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
        will output the same image every time.
    
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
        The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
        the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
    
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The speed of the generation. The higher the speed, the faster the generation.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.

---

**Title:** FLUX.1 [dev] Redux
**Description:** FLUX.1 [dev] Redux is a high-performance endpoint for the FLUX.1 [dev] model that enables rapid transformation of existing images, delivering high-quality style transfers and image modifications with the core FLUX capabilities.

  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50

        will output the same image every time.

        The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
        the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
    
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The speed of the generation. The higher the speed, the faster the generation.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.

## fal-ai/flux-2-lora-gallery/add-background

**Title:** Flux 2 Lora Gallery
**Description:** Add a background to images with white/clean background
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-2-lora-gallery/add-background`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** stylized, transform

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `AddBackgroundInput`
**Required fields:** `image_urls`

- **`image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” The URLs of the images. Provide an image with a white or clean background.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"Add Background forest"` вҖ” The prompt describing the background to add. Must start with 'Add Background' followed by your description.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image. If not provided, the size of the input image will be used.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.5` вҖ” The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale. Controls how closely the model follows the prompt.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `40` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 4, max: 50
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration level for image generation. 'regular' balances speed and quality.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. Same seed with same prompt will produce same result.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and won't be saved in history.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker for the generated image.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the output image
  Possible values: `png`, `jpeg`, `webp`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the add background effect.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `AddBackgroundOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images with added background

## fal-ai/flux-2-lora-gallery/apartment-staging

**Title:** Flux 2 Lora Gallery
**Description:** Virtually furnishes an empty apartment

  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.5` вҖ” The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale. Controls how closely the model follows the prompt.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `40` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 4, max: 50
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration level for image generation. 'regular' balances speed and quality.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. Same seed with same prompt will produce same result.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and won't be saved in history.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker for the generated image.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the output image
  Possible values: `png`, `jpeg`, `webp`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the apartment staging effect.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ApartmentStagingOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated furnished room images

## fal-ai/flux-2-lora-gallery/multiple-angles

**Title:** Flux 2 Lora Gallery
**Description:** Generates same object from different angles (azimuth/elevation)
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-2-lora-gallery/multiple-angles`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** stylized, transform

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MultipleAnglesInput`
**Required fields:** `image_urls`

- **`image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” The URL of the image to adjust camera angle for.
- **`horizontal_angle`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Horizontal rotation angle around the object in degrees. 0В°=front view, 90В°=right side, 180В°=back view, 270В°=left side, 360В°=front view again.
  Range: min: 0, max: 360
- **`vertical_angle`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Vertical camera angle in degrees. 0В°=eye-level shot, 30В°=elevated shot, 60В°=high-angle shot (looking down from above).
  Range: min: 0, max: 60
- **`zoom`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Camera zoom/distance. 0=wide shot (far away), 5=medium shot (normal), 10=close-up (very close).
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image. If not provided, the size of the input image will be used.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.5` вҖ” The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `40` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 4, max: 50
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration level for image generation.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If True, the media will be returned as a data URI.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the output image.
  Possible values: `png`, `jpeg`, `webp`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the multiple angles effect.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MultipleAnglesOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images with multiple camera angles

**Title:** Flux 2 Max
**Description:** FLUX.2 [max] delivers state-of-the-art image generation and advanced image editing with exceptional realism, precision, and consistency.

  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed to use for the generation.
- **`safety_tolerance`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"2"` вҖ” The safety tolerance level for the generated image. 1 being the most strict and 5 being the most permissive.

- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.

## fal-ai/flux-2/klein/4b

**Title:** FLUX.2 [klein] 4B
**Description:** Image-to-image editing with FLUX.2 [klein] 4B from Black Forest Labs. Precise modifications using natural language descriptions and hex color control.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-2/klein/4b`
**Category:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `KleinDistilledInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed to use for the generation. If not provided, a random seed will be used.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 4, max: 8
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"landscape_4_3"` вҖ” The size of the image to generate.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI. Output is not stored when this is True.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Klein4BDistilledT2IOutput`
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The generated images
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The generated images
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

## fal-ai/flux-2/klein/9b/base/edit/lora

**Title:** FLUX.2 [klein] 9B Base LoRA
**Description:** Image-to-image editing with LoRA support for FLUX.2 [klein] 9B Base from Black Forest Labs. Specialized style transfer and domain-specific modifications.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-2/klein/9b/base/edit/lora`
**Category:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `KleinBaseEditLoRAInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_urls`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to edit the image.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Negative prompt for classifier-free guidance. Describes what to avoid in the image.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Guidance scale for classifier-free guidance.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed to use for the generation. If not provided, a random seed will be used.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 4, max: 50
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image. If not provided, uses the input image size.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use for image generation.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI. Output is not stored when this is True.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” The URLs of the images for editing. A maximum of 4 images are allowed.
- **`loras`** *(list<fal-ai_flux-2-klein_LoRAInput>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of LoRA weights to apply (maximum 3).

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `KleinT2IOutput`
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The generated images
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.

- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” The URLs of the images for editing. A maximum of 4 images are allowed.
- **`loras`** *(list<fal-ai_flux-2-klein_LoRAInput>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of LoRA weights to apply (maximum 3).

## fal-ai/flux-2/klein/realtime

**Title:** Flux 2 [klein] Realtime
**Description:** Realtime generation with FLUX.2 [klein] from Black Forest Labs. 
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-2/klein/realtime`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** realtime, image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `RealtimeEditInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_size`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"square"` вҖ” The size of the generated image. square=768x768, square_hd=1024x1024.
  Possible values: `square`, `square_hd`
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Base64-encoded image data URI for editing. CDN URLs are not supported for realtime. For optimal performance, use 704x704 JPEG images with 50% quality. Other sizes will be resized automatically.
- **`enable_interpolation`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Enable RIFE frame interpolation between consecutive frames (doubles output frames).
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"Turn this into \"Living oil painting, melting gold and sapphire\""` вҖ” The prompt to guide image editing.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `3`
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`output_feedback_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Output feedback loop. 1.0 = pure noise (no feedback), 0.9 = 90% noise + 10% previous output latent.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`schedule_mu`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.3` вҖ” Schedule mu for time shift. 2.3=default, lower=more even denoising, 0.3=nearly linear.
  Range: min: 0.3, max: 2.5
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `35` вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `RealtimeEditOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<RawImage>)* вҖ” Generated images as raw bytes. When interpolation is enabled, returns [interpolated_frame, current_frame] in chronological order. Otherwise returns [current_frame].
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed used for generation.

---

## fal-ai/flux-control-lora-canny/image-to-image

**Title:** FLUX.1 [dev] Control LoRA Canny
**Description:** FLUX Control LoRA Canny is a high-performance endpoint that uses a control image using a Canny edge map to transfer structure to the generated image and another initial image to guide color.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-control-lora-canny/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** lora, style transfer

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`, `control_lora_image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to use for inpainting. or img2img
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 35
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`control_lora_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” 
            The image to use for control lora. This is used to control the style of the generated image.
        
- **`control_lora_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the control lora.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.85` вҖ” The strength to use for inpainting/image-to-image. Only used if the image_url is provided. 1.0 is completely remakes the image while 0.0 preserves the original.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`timings`** *(object)*

---

  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`control_lora_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” 
            The image to use for control lora. This is used to control the style of the generated image.
        
- **`control_lora_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the control lora.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.85` вҖ” The strength to use for inpainting/image-to-image. Only used if the image_url is provided. 1.0 is completely remakes the image while 0.0 preserves the original.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1

## fal-ai/flux-differential-diffusion

**Title:** FLUX.1 [dev] Differential Diffusion
**Description:** FLUX.1 Differential Diffusion is a rapid endpoint that enables swift, granular control over image transformations through change maps, delivering fast and precise region-specific modifications while maintaining FLUX.1 [dev]'s high-quality output.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-differential-diffusion`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** transformation

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `DiffInput`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to use as initial image.
- **`change_map_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of change map.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.85` вҖ” The strength to use for image-to-image. 1.0 is completely remakes the image while 0.0 preserves the original.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.

## fal-ai/flux-general/differential-diffusion

**Title:** FLUX.1 [dev] with Controlnets and Loras
**Description:** A specialized FLUX endpoint combining differential diffusion control with LoRA, ControlNet, and IP-Adapter support, enabling precise, region-specific image transformations through customizable change maps.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-general/differential-diffusion`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** lora, controlnet, ip-adapter

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `DifferentialDiffusionInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`, `change_map_image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`control_loras`** *(list<ControlLoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation which use a control image. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`controlnets`** *(list<ControlNet>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The controlnets to use for the image generation. Only one controlnet is supported at the moment.
        
- **`controlnet_unions`** *(list<ControlNetUnion>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The controlnet unions to use for the image generation. Only one controlnet is supported at the moment.
        
- **`ip_adapters`** *(list<IPAdapter>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
        IP-Adapter to use for image generation.
        
- **`easycontrols`** *(list<EasyControlWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
        EasyControl Inputs to use for image generation.
        
- **`fill_image`** *(?)* вҖ” Use an image input to influence the generation. Can be used to fill images in masked areas.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`real_cfg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`use_real_cfg`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            Uses classical CFG as in SD1.5, SDXL, etc. Increases generation times and price when set to be true.
            If using XLabs IP-Adapter v1, this will be turned on!.
        
- **`use_cfg_zero`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            Uses CFG-zero init sampling as in https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.18886.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate. This is always set to 1 for streaming output.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`reference_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of Image for Reference-Only
- **`reference_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.65` вҖ” Strength of reference_only generation. Only used if a reference image is provided.
  Range: min: -3, max: 3
- **`reference_start`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” 
            The percentage of the total timesteps when the reference guidance is to bestarted.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`reference_end`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” 
            The percentage of the total timesteps when the reference guidance is to be ended.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`base_shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Base shift for the scheduled timesteps
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 5
- **`max_shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.15` вҖ” Max shift for the scheduled timesteps
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 5
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`use_beta_schedule`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Specifies whether beta sigmas ought to be used.
- **`sigma_schedule`** *(string)* вҖ” Sigmas schedule for the denoising process.
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"euler"` вҖ” Scheduler for the denoising process.
  Possible values: `euler`, `dpmpp_2m`
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            Negative prompt to steer the image generation away from unwanted features.
            By default, we will be using NAG for processing the negative prompt.
        
- **`nag_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” 
            The scale for NAG. Higher values will result in a image that is more distant
            to the negative prompt.
        
  Range: max: 10
- **`nag_tau`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.5` вҖ” 
            The tau for NAG. Controls the normalization of the hidden state.
            Higher values will result in a less aggressive normalization,
            but may also lead to unexpected changes with respect to the original image.
            Not recommended to change this value.
        
- **`nag_alpha`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.25` вҖ” 
            The alpha value for NAG. This value is used as a final weighting
            factor for steering the normalized guidance (positive and negative prompts)
            in the direction of the positive prompt. Higher values will result in less
            steering on the normalized guidance where lower values will result in
            considering the positive prompt guidance more.
        
  Range: max: 1
- **`nag_end`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.25` вҖ” 
            The proportion of steps to apply NAG. After the specified proportion
            of steps has been iterated, the remaining steps will use original
            attention processors in FLUX.
        
  Range: max: 1
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to use as initial image.
- **`change_map_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of change map.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.85` вҖ” The strength to use for differential diffusion. 1.0 is completely remakes the image while 0.0 preserves the original.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

---

## fal-ai/flux-general/image-to-image

**Title:** FLUX.1 [dev] with Controlnets and Loras
**Description:** FLUX General Image-to-Image is a versatile endpoint that transforms existing images with support for LoRA, ControlNet, and IP-Adapter extensions, enabling precise control over style transfer, modifications, and artistic variations through multiple guidance methods.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-general/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** lora, controlnet, ip-adapter

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`control_loras`** *(list<ControlLoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation which use a control image. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`controlnets`** *(list<ControlNet>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The controlnets to use for the image generation. Only one controlnet is supported at the moment.
        
- **`controlnet_unions`** *(list<ControlNetUnion>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The controlnet unions to use for the image generation. Only one controlnet is supported at the moment.
        
- **`ip_adapters`** *(list<IPAdapter>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
        IP-Adapter to use for image generation.
        
- **`easycontrols`** *(list<EasyControlWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
        EasyControl Inputs to use for image generation.
        
- **`fill_image`** *(?)* вҖ” Use an image input to influence the generation. Can be used to fill images in masked areas.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`real_cfg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`use_real_cfg`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            Uses classical CFG as in SD1.5, SDXL, etc. Increases generation times and price when set to be true.
            If using XLabs IP-Adapter v1, this will be turned on!.
        
- **`use_cfg_zero`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            Uses CFG-zero init sampling as in https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.18886.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate. This is always set to 1 for streaming output.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`reference_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of Image for Reference-Only
- **`reference_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.65` вҖ” Strength of reference_only generation. Only used if a reference image is provided.
  Range: min: -3, max: 3
- **`reference_start`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” 
            The percentage of the total timesteps when the reference guidance is to bestarted.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`reference_end`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” 
            The percentage of the total timesteps when the reference guidance is to be ended.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`base_shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Base shift for the scheduled timesteps
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 5
- **`max_shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.15` вҖ” Max shift for the scheduled timesteps
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 5
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`use_beta_schedule`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Specifies whether beta sigmas ought to be used.
- **`sigma_schedule`** *(string)* вҖ” Sigmas schedule for the denoising process.
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"euler"` вҖ” Scheduler for the denoising process.
  Possible values: `euler`, `dpmpp_2m`
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            Negative prompt to steer the image generation away from unwanted features.
            By default, we will be using NAG for processing the negative prompt.
        
- **`nag_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” 
            The scale for NAG. Higher values will result in a image that is more distant
            to the negative prompt.
        
  Range: max: 10
- **`nag_tau`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.5` вҖ” 
            The tau for NAG. Controls the normalization of the hidden state.
            Higher values will result in a less aggressive normalization,
            but may also lead to unexpected changes with respect to the original image.
            Not recommended to change this value.
        
- **`nag_alpha`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.25` вҖ” 
            The alpha value for NAG. This value is used as a final weighting
            factor for steering the normalized guidance (positive and negative prompts)
            in the direction of the positive prompt. Higher values will result in less
            steering on the normalized guidance where lower values will result in
            considering the positive prompt guidance more.
        
  Range: max: 1
- **`nag_end`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.25` вҖ” 
            The proportion of steps to apply NAG. After the specified proportion
            of steps has been iterated, the remaining steps will use original
            attention processors in FLUX.
        
  Range: max: 1
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to use for inpainting. or img2img
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.85` вҖ” The strength to use for inpainting/image-to-image. Only used if the image_url is provided. 1.0 is completely remakes the image while 0.0 preserves the original.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

## fal-ai/flux-general/inpainting

**Title:** FLUX.1 [dev] with Controlnets and Loras
**Description:** FLUX General Inpainting is a versatile endpoint that enables precise image editing and completion, supporting multiple AI extensions including LoRA, ControlNet, and IP-Adapter for enhanced control over inpainting results and sophisticated image modifications.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-general/inpainting`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** lora, controlnet, ip-adapter

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `InpaintInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`, `mask_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50

            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`control_loras`** *(list<ControlLoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation which use a control image. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`controlnets`** *(list<ControlNet>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The controlnets to use for the image generation. Only one controlnet is supported at the moment.
        
- **`controlnet_unions`** *(list<ControlNetUnion>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The controlnet unions to use for the image generation. Only one controlnet is supported at the moment.
        
- **`ip_adapters`** *(list<IPAdapter>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
        IP-Adapter to use for image generation.
        
- **`easycontrols`** *(list<EasyControlWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
        EasyControl Inputs to use for image generation.
        
- **`fill_image`** *(?)* вҖ” Use an image input to influence the generation. Can be used to fill images in masked areas.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`real_cfg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`use_real_cfg`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ”

            If using XLabs IP-Adapter v1, this will be turned on!.
        
- **`use_cfg_zero`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            Uses CFG-zero init sampling as in https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.18886.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate. This is always set to 1 for streaming output.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`reference_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of Image for Reference-Only
- **`reference_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.65` вҖ” Strength of reference_only generation. Only used if a reference image is provided.
  Range: min: -3, max: 3
- **`reference_start`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” 
            The percentage of the total timesteps when the reference guidance is to bestarted.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`reference_end`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” 
            The percentage of the total timesteps when the reference guidance is to be ended.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`base_shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Base shift for the scheduled timesteps
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 5
- **`max_shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.15` вҖ” Max shift for the scheduled timesteps
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 5
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`use_beta_schedule`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Specifies whether beta sigmas ought to be used.
- **`sigma_schedule`** *(string)* вҖ” Sigmas schedule for the denoising process.
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"euler"` вҖ” Scheduler for the denoising process.
  Possible values: `euler`, `dpmpp_2m`
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            Negative prompt to steer the image generation away from unwanted features.
            By default, we will be using NAG for processing the negative prompt.
        
- **`nag_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” 
            The scale for NAG. Higher values will result in a image that is more distant
            to the negative prompt.
        
  Range: max: 10
- **`nag_tau`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.5` вҖ” 
            The tau for NAG. Controls the normalization of the hidden state.
            Higher values will result in a less aggressive normalization,
            but may also lead to unexpected changes with respect to the original image.
            Not recommended to change this value.
        
- **`nag_alpha`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.25` вҖ” 
            The alpha value for NAG. This value is used as a final weighting
            factor for steering the normalized guidance (positive and negative prompts)
            in the direction of the positive prompt. Higher values will result in less
            steering on the normalized guidance where lower values will result in
            considering the positive prompt guidance more.
        
  Range: max: 1
- **`nag_end`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.25` вҖ” 
            The proportion of steps to apply NAG. After the specified proportion
            of steps has been iterated, the remaining steps will use original
            attention processors in FLUX.
        
  Range: max: 1
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to use for inpainting. or img2img
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.85` вҖ” The strength to use for inpainting/image-to-image. Only used if the image_url is provided. 1.0 is completely remakes the image while 0.0 preserves the original.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1
- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ”

## fal-ai/flux-general/rf-inversion

**Title:** FLUX.1 [dev] with Controlnets and Loras
**Description:** A general purpose endpoint for the FLUX.1 [dev] model, implementing the RF-Inversion pipeline. This can be used to edit a reference image based on a prompt.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-general/rf-inversion`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** rf-inversion, editing, lora

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `RFInversionInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to edit the image with
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`control_loras`** *(list<ControlLoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation which use a control image. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`controlnets`** *(list<ControlNet>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The controlnets to use for the image generation. Only one controlnet is supported at the moment.
        
- **`controlnet_unions`** *(list<ControlNetUnion>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The controlnet unions to use for the image generation. Only one controlnet is supported at the moment.
        
- **`easycontrols`** *(list<EasyControlWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
        EasyControl Inputs to use for image generation.
        
- **`fill_image`** *(?)* вҖ” Use an image input to influence the generation. Can be used to fill images in masked areas.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`use_cfg_zero`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            Uses CFG-zero init sampling as in https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.18886.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate. This is always set to 1 for streaming output.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`reference_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of Image for Reference-Only
- **`reference_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.65` вҖ” Strength of reference_only generation. Only used if a reference image is provided.
  Range: min: -3, max: 3
- **`reference_start`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” 
            The percentage of the total timesteps when the reference guidance is to bestarted.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`reference_end`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” 
            The percentage of the total timesteps when the reference guidance is to be ended.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`base_shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Base shift for the scheduled timesteps
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 5
- **`max_shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.15` вҖ” Max shift for the scheduled timesteps
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 5
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`use_beta_schedule`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Specifies whether beta sigmas ought to be used.
- **`sigma_schedule`** *(string)* вҖ” Sigmas schedule for the denoising process.
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"euler"` вҖ” Scheduler for the denoising process.
  Possible values: `euler`, `dpmpp_2m`
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            Negative prompt to steer the image generation away from unwanted features.
            By default, we will be using NAG for processing the negative prompt.
        
- **`nag_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” 
            The scale for NAG. Higher values will result in a image that is more distant
            to the negative prompt.
        
  Range: max: 10
- **`nag_tau`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.5` вҖ” 
            The tau for NAG. Controls the normalization of the hidden state.
            Higher values will result in a less aggressive normalization,
            but may also lead to unexpected changes with respect to the original image.
            Not recommended to change this value.
        
- **`nag_alpha`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.25` вҖ” 
            The alpha value for NAG. This value is used as a final weighting
            factor for steering the normalized guidance (positive and negative prompts)
            in the direction of the positive prompt. Higher values will result in less
            steering on the normalized guidance where lower values will result in
            considering the positive prompt guidance more.
        
  Range: max: 1
- **`nag_end`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.25` вҖ” 
            The proportion of steps to apply NAG. After the specified proportion
            of steps has been iterated, the remaining steps will use original
            attention processors in FLUX.
        
  Range: max: 1
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to be edited
- **`controller_guidance_forward`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.6` вҖ” The controller guidance (gamma) used in the creation of structured noise.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 3
- **`controller_guidance_reverse`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.75` вҖ” The controller guidance (eta) used in the denoising process.Using values closer to 1 will result in an image closer to input.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 3
- **`reverse_guidance_start`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Timestep to start guidance during reverse process.
- **`reverse_guidance_end`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” Timestep to stop guidance during reverse process.
- **`reverse_guidance_schedule`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"constant"` вҖ” Scheduler for applying reverse guidance.
  Possible values: `constant`, `linear_increase`, `linear_decrease`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

## fal-ai/flux-kontext-lora

**Title:** Flux Kontext Lora
**Description:** Fast endpoint for the FLUX.1 Kontext [dev] model with LoRA support, enabling rapid and high-quality image editing using pre-trained LoRA adaptations for specific styles, brand identities, and product-specific outputs.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-kontext-lora`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image-editing, image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `BaseKontextEditInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to edit.

Max width: 14142px, Max height: 14142px, Timeout: 20s
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to edit the image.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 10, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The speed of the generation. The higher the speed, the faster the generation.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`
- **`resolution_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"match_input"` вҖ” 
            Determines how the output resolution is set for image editing.
            - `auto`: The model selects an optimal resolution from a predefined set that best matches the input image's aspect ratio. This is the recommended setting for most use cases as it's what the model was trained on.
            - `match_input`: The model will attempt to use the same resolution as the input image. The resolution will be adjusted to be compatible with the model's requirements (e.g. dimensions must be multiples of 16 and within supported limits).
            Apart from these, a few aspect ratios are also supported.
            
  Possible values: `auto`, `match_input`, `1:1`, `16:9`, `21:9`, `3:2`, `2:3`, `4:5`, `5:4`, `3:4`, `4:3`, `9:16`, `9:21`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `KontextEditOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.

---

## fal-ai/flux-kontext-lora/inpaint

**Title:** Flux Kontext Lora
**Description:** Fast inpainting endpoint for the FLUX.1 Kontext [dev] model with LoRA support, enabling rapid and high-quality image inpainting with reference images, while using pre-trained LoRA adaptations for specific styles, brand identities, and product-specific outputs.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-kontext-lora/inpaint`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image-editing, image-inpainting, image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `BaseKontextInpaintInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`, `reference_image_url`, `mask_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to be inpainted.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt for the image to image task.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 10, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The speed of the generation. The higher the speed, the faster the generation.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`
- **`reference_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the reference image for inpainting.
- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the mask for inpainting.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.88` вҖ” The strength of the initial image. Higher strength values are better for this model.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `KontextInpaintOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.

---

## fal-ai/flux-kontext/dev

**Title:** FLUX.1 Kontext [dev]
**Description:** Frontier image editing model.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-kontext/dev`
**Category:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `BaseKontextEditInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to edit the image.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to edit.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 10, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” Output format
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The speed of the generation. The higher the speed, the faster the generation.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`
- **`enhance_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enhance the prompt for better results.
- **`resolution_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"match_input"` вҖ” 
             Determines how the output resolution is set for image editing.
             - `auto`: The model selects an optimal resolution from a predefined set that best matches the input image's aspect ratio. This is the recommended setting for most use cases as it's what the model was trained on.
             - `match_input`: The model will attempt to use the same resolution as the input image. The resolution will be adjusted to be compatible with the model's requirements (e.g. dimensions must be multiples of 16 and within supported limits).
             Apart from these, a few aspect ratios are also supported.
             
  Possible values: `auto`, `match_input`, `1:1`, `16:9`, `21:9`, `3:2`, `2:3`, `4:5`, `5:4`, `3:4`, `4:3`, `9:16`, `9:21`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `KontextEditOutput`
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

---

## fal-ai/flux-krea-lora/image-to-image

**Title:** FLUX.1 Krea [dev] with LoRAs
**Description:** FLUX LoRA Image-to-Image is a high-performance endpoint that transforms existing images using FLUX models, leveraging LoRA adaptations to enable rapid and precise image style transfer, modifications, and artistic variations.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-krea-lora/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** lora, style transfer

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 35
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate. This is always set to 1 for streaming output.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” Acceleration level for image generation. 'regular' balances speed and quality.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to use for inpainting. or img2img
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.85` вҖ” The strength to use for inpainting/image-to-image. Only used if the image_url is provided. 1.0 is completely remakes the image while 0.0 preserves the original.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`timings`** *(object)*

            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model

            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. You can use any number of LoRAs

- **`fill_image`** *(?)* вҖ” Use an image fill input to fill in particular images into the masked area.

## fal-ai/flux-pro/kontext

**Title:** FLUX.1 Kontext [pro]
**Description:** FLUX.1 Kontext [pro] handles both text and reference images as inputs, seamlessly enabling targeted, local edits and complex transformations of entire scenes.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-pro/kontext`
**Category:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FluxKontextInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`safety_tolerance`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"2"` вҖ” The safety tolerance level for the generated image. 1 being the most strict and 5 being the most permissive.
  Possible values: `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `5`, `6`
- **`enhance_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enhance the prompt for better results.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(string)* вҖ” The aspect ratio of the generated image.
  Possible values: `21:9`, `16:9`, `4:3`, `3:2`, `1:1`, `2:3`, `3:4`, `9:16`, `9:21`
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Image prompt for the omni model.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `FluxKontextOutput`
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<fal__toolkit__image__image__Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.

**Title:** FLUX1.1 [pro] ultra Redux
**Description:** FLUX1.1 [pro] ultra Redux is a high-performance endpoint for the FLUX1.1 [pro] model that enables rapid transformation of existing images, delivering high-quality style transfers and image modifications with the core FLUX capabilities.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-pro/v1.1-ultra/redux`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** style transfer, high-res

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FluxProUltraTextToImageInputRedux`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`safety_tolerance`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"2"` вҖ” The safety tolerance level for the generated image. 1 being the most strict and 5 being the most permissive.
  Possible values: `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `5`, `6`
- **`enhance_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enhance the prompt for better results.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The image URL to generate an image from. Needs to match the dimensions of the mask.
- **`image_prompt_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.1` вҖ” The strength of the image prompt, between 0 and 1.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(string | string)* вҖ” Default: `"16:9"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the generated image.
  Possible values: `21:9`, `16:9`, `4:3`, `3:2`, `1:1`, `2:3`, `3:4`, `9:16`, `9:21`
- **`raw`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Generate less processed, more natural-looking images.

- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.

**Tags:** editing

- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ”

**Title:** FLUX.1 [pro] Fill Fine-tuned
**Description:** FLUX.1 [pro] Fill Fine-tuned is a high-performance endpoint for the FLUX.1 [pro] model with a fine-tuned LoRA that enables rapid transformation of existing images, delivering high-quality style transfers and image modifications with the core FLUX capabilities.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-pro/v1/fill-finetuned`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FluxProFillFinetunedInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`, `mask_url`, `finetune_id`, `finetune_strength`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to fill the masked part of the image.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`safety_tolerance`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"2"` вҖ” The safety tolerance level for the generated image. 1 being the most strict and 5 being the most permissive.
  Possible values: `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `5`, `6`
- **`enhance_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enhance the prompt for better results.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The image URL to generate an image from. Needs to match the dimensions of the mask.
- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The mask URL to inpaint the image. Needs to match the dimensions of the input image.
- **`finetune_id`** *(string)* вҖ” References your specific model
- **`finetune_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” 
        Controls finetune influence.
        Increase this value if your target concept isn't showing up strongly enough.
        The optimal setting depends on your finetune and prompt
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 2

            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.

## fal-ai/flux-pulid

**Title:** PuLID Flux
**Description:** An endpoint for personalized image generation using Flux as per given description.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flux-pulid`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** personalization, style transfer

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FluxPulidInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `reference_image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`reference_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to use for inpainting.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"landscape_4_3"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `20` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`start_step`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The number of steps to start the CFG from.
  Range: min: 0, max: 50
- **`true_cfg`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The weight of the CFG loss.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`id_weight`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The weight of the ID loss.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`max_sequence_length`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"128"` вҖ” The maximum sequence length for the model.
  Possible values: `128`, `256`, `512`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
