## fal-ai/kling-image/o1

**Title:** Kling O1 Image
**Description:** Perform precise image edits using strong reference control, transforming subjects, styles, and local details while preserving visual consistency.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/kling-image/o1`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** edit, realism, typography

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `OmniImageRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_urls`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt for image generation. Reference images using @Image1, @Image2, etc. (or @Image if only one image). Max 2500 characters.
- **`image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” List of reference images. Reference images in prompt using @Image1, @Image2, etc. (1-indexed). Max 10 images.
- **`elements`** *(array)* вҖ” Elements (characters/objects) to include in the image. Reference in prompt as @Element1, @Element2, etc. Maximum 10 total (elements + reference images).
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1K"` вҖ” Image generation resolution. 1K: standard, 2K: high-res.
  Possible values: `1K`, `2K`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate (1-9).
  Range: min: 1, max: 9
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of generated images. 'auto' intelligently determines based on input content.
  Possible values: `auto`, `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`, `4:3`, `3:4`, `3:2`, `2:3`, `21:9`
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `OmniImageOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Generated images

---

## fal-ai/kling-image/o3/image-to-image

**Title:** Kling Image
**Description:** Kling Omni 3: Top-tier image-to-image with flawless consistency.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/kling-image/o3/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `O3ImageToImageRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_urls`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt for image generation. Reference images using @Image1, @Image2, etc. (or @Image if only one image). Max 2500 characters.
- **`image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” List of reference images. Reference images in prompt using @Image1, @Image2, etc. (1-indexed). Max 10 images.
- **`elements`** *(array)* вҖ” Optional: Elements (characters/objects) for face control. Reference in prompt as @Element1, @Element2, etc.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1K"` вҖ” Image generation resolution. 1K: standard, 2K: high-res, 4K: ultra high-res.
  Possible values: `1K`, `2K`, `4K`
- **`result_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"single"` вҖ” Result type. 'single' for one image, 'series' for a series of related images.
  Possible values: `single`, `series`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate (1-9). Only used when result_type is 'single'.
  Range: min: 1, max: 9
- **`series_amount`** *(integer)* вҖ” Number of images in series (2-9). Only used when result_type is 'series'.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of generated images. 'auto' intelligently determines based on input content.
  Possible values: `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`, `4:3`, `3:4`, `3:2`, `2:3`, `21:9`, `auto`
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `I2IO3ImageOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Generated images

---

## fal-ai/kling-image/v3/image-to-image

**Title:** Kling Image
**Description:** Kling Image V3: Latest kling image model
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/kling-image/v3/image-to-image`

**Tags:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `V3ImageToImageRequest`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Reference image for image-to-image generation.

Max file size: 10.0MB, Min width: 300px, Min height: 300px, Min aspect ratio: 0.40, Max aspect ratio: 2.50, Timeout: 20.0s
- **`elements`** *(array)* вҖ” Optional: Elements (characters/objects) to include in the image for face control.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1K"` вҖ” Image generation resolution. 1K: standard, 2K: high-res.
  Possible values: `1K`, `2K`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate (1-9).
  Range: min: 1, max: 9
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"16:9"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of generated images.
  Possible values: `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`, `4:3`, `3:4`, `3:2`, `2:3`, `21:9`
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `I2IV3ImageOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Generated images

- **`garment_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Url to the garment image.

## fal-ai/kolors/image-to-image

**Title:** Kolors Image to Image
**Description:** Photorealistic Image-to-Image
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/kolors/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** realism, editing, diffusion

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `KolorsImg2ImgInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small
            details (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show
            you.
        
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 150
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the function will wait for the image to be generated and
            uploaded before returning the response. This will increase the latency of
            the function but it allows you to get the image directly in the response
            without going through the CDN.
        
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable safety checker.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"EulerDiscreteScheduler"` вҖ” The scheduler to use for the model.
  Possible values: `EulerDiscreteScheduler`, `EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler`, `DPMSolverMultistepScheduler`, `DPMSolverMultistepScheduler_SDE_karras`, `UniPCMultistepScheduler`, `DEISMultistepScheduler`
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to use for image to image
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.85` вҖ” The strength to use for image-to-image. 1.0 is completely remakes the image while 0.0 preserves the original.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        

---

## fal-ai/lcm-sd15-i2i

**Title:** Optimized Latent Consistency (SDv1.5)
**Description:** Produce high-quality images with minimal inference steps. Optimized for 512x512 input image size.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/lcm-sd15-i2i`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** diffusion, lcm, real-time

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LCMI2IInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The image to use as a base.
- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Mask URL for compatibility with generic LCM processing.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.8` вҖ” The strength of the image.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use.Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 16
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” 
            The number of inference steps to use for generating the image. The more steps
            the better the image will be but it will also take longer to generate.
        
  Range: min: 1, max: 12
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” 
            The number of images to generate. The function will return a list of images
            with the same prompt and negative prompt but different seeds.
        
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`enable_safety_checks`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the resulting image will be checked whether it includes any
            potentially unsafe content. If it does, it will be replaced with a black
            image.
        
- **`request_id`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            An id bound to a request, can be used with response to identify the request
            itself.
        

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LCMOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`nsfw_content_detected`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” 
            A list of booleans indicating whether the generated image contains any
            potentially unsafe content. If the safety check is disabled, this field
            will have a false for each generated image.
        
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” 
            Number of inference steps used to generate the image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the default one in case none was passed.
        
- **`request_id`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            An id bound to a request, can be used with response to identify the request
            itself.
        

---

## fal-ai/leffa/pose-transfer

**Title:** Leffa Pose Transfer
**Description:** Leffa Pose Transfer is an endpoint for changing pose of an image with a reference image.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/leffa/pose-transfer`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** pose, utility

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `PoseTransferInput`
**Required fields:** `pose_image_url`, `person_image_url`

- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same input given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your input when generating the image.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`pose_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Url for the human image.
- **`person_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Url to the garment image.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `PoseTransferOutput`
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Whether the image contains NSFW concepts.
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The output image.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the inference.

---

## fal-ai/leffa/virtual-tryon

**Title:** Leffa Virtual TryOn
**Description:** Leffa Virtual TryOn is a high quality image based Try-On endpoint which can be used for commercial try on.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/leffa/virtual-tryon`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** try-on, fashion, clothing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `VTONInput`
**Required fields:** `human_image_url`, `garment_image_url`, `garment_type`

- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same input given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your input when generating the image.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`human_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Url for the human image.
- **`garment_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Url to the garment image.
- **`garment_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” The type of the garment used for virtual try-on.
  Possible values: `upper_body`, `lower_body`, `dresses`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `VTONOutput`
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Whether the image contains NSFW concepts.
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The output image.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the inference.

## fal-ai/live-portrait/image

**Title:** Live Portrait
**Description:** Transfer expression from a video to a portrait.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/live-portrait/image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** expression, animation

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LivePortraitImageInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to be animated
- **`blink`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Amount to blink the eyes
  Range: min: -30, max: 30
- **`eyebrow`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Amount to raise or lower eyebrows
  Range: min: -30, max: 30
- **`wink`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Amount to wink
  Range: min: 0, max: 25
- **`pupil_x`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Amount to move pupils horizontally
  Range: min: -45, max: 45
- **`pupil_y`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Amount to move pupils vertically
  Range: min: -45, max: 45
- **`aaa`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Amount to open mouth in 'aaa' shape
  Range: min: -200, max: 200
- **`eee`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Amount to shape mouth in 'eee' position
  Range: min: -40, max: 40
- **`woo`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Amount to shape mouth in 'woo' position
  Range: min: -100, max: 100
- **`smile`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Amount to smile
  Range: min: -2, max: 2
- **`rotate_pitch`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Amount to rotate the face in pitch
  Range: min: -45, max: 45
- **`rotate_yaw`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Amount to rotate the face in yaw
  Range: min: -45, max: 45
- **`rotate_roll`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Amount to rotate the face in roll
  Range: min: -45, max: 45
- **`flag_pasteback`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to paste-back/stitch the animated face cropping from the face-cropping space to the original image space.
- **`flag_do_crop`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to crop the source portrait to the face-cropping space.
- **`flag_do_rot`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to conduct the rotation when flag_do_crop is True.
- **`flag_lip_zero`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to set the lip to closed state before animation. Only takes effect when flag_eye_retargeting and flag_lip_retargeting are False.
- **`dsize`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `512` вҖ” Size of the output image.
- **`scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.3` вҖ” Scaling factor for the face crop.
- **`vx_ratio`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Horizontal offset ratio for face crop.
- **`vy_ratio`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `-0.125` вҖ” Vertical offset ratio for face crop. Positive values move up, negative values move down.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
        Whether to enable the safety checker. If enabled, the model will check if the input image contains a face before processing it.
        The safety checker will process the input image
        
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” Output format
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LivePortraitImageOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The generated image file.

---

## fal-ai/lora/image-to-image

**Title:** Stable Diffusion with LoRAs
**Description:** Run Any Stable Diffusion model with customizable LoRA weights.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/lora/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** diffusion, lora, customization, fine-tuning

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `model_name`, `prompt`

- **`model_name`** *(string)* вҖ” URL or HuggingFace ID of the base model to generate the image.
- **`unet_name`** *(string)* вҖ” URL or HuggingFace ID of the custom U-Net model to use for the image generation.
- **`variant`** *(string)* вҖ” The variant of the model to use for huggingface models, e.g. 'fp16'.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use.Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`prompt_weighting`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the prompt weighting syntax will be used.
            Additionally, this will lift the 77 token limit by averaging embeddings.
        
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to use for image to image/inpainting.
- **`noise_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The amount of noise to add to noise image for image. Only used if the image_url is provided. 1.0 is complete noise and values near 0 are minimal noise.
  Range: max: 1
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`embeddings`** *(list<Embedding>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The embeddings to use for the image generation. Only a single embedding is supported at the moment.
            The embeddings will be used to map the tokens in the prompt to the embedding weights.
        
- **`controlnets`** *(list<ControlNet>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The control nets to use for the image generation. You can use any number of control nets
            and they will be applied to the image at the specified timesteps.
        
- **`controlnet_guess_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the controlnet will be applied to only the conditional predictions.
        
- **`ip_adapter`** *(list<IPAdapter>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The IP adapter to use for the image generation.
        
- **`image_encoder_path`** *(string)* вҖ” 
            The path to the image encoder model to use for the image generation.
        
- **`image_encoder_subfolder`** *(string)* вҖ” 
            The subfolder of the image encoder model to use for the image generation.
        
- **`image_encoder_weight_name`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"pytorch_model.bin"` вҖ” 
            The weight name of the image encoder model to use for the image generation.
        
- **`ic_light_model_url`** *(string)* вҖ” 
            The URL of the IC Light model to use for the image generation.
        
- **`ic_light_model_background_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” 
            The URL of the IC Light model background image to use for the image generation.
            Make sure to use a background compatible with the model.
        
- **`ic_light_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” 
            The URL of the IC Light model image to use for the image generation.
        
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” 
            Increasing the amount of steps tells Stable Diffusion that it should take more steps
            to generate your final result which can increase the amount of detail in your image.
        
  Range: min: 1, max: 150
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`clip_skip`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” 
            Skips part of the image generation process, leading to slightly different results.
            This means the image renders faster, too.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`scheduler`** *(string)* вҖ” Scheduler / sampler to use for the image denoising process.
  Possible values: `DPM++ 2M`, `DPM++ 2M Karras`, `DPM++ 2M SDE`, `DPM++ 2M SDE Karras`, `Euler`, `Euler A`, `Euler (trailing timesteps)`, `LCM`, `LCM (trailing timesteps)`, `DDIM`, `TCD`
- **`timesteps`** *()* вҖ” Default: `{"array":[],"method":"default"}` вҖ” 
            Optionally override the timesteps to use for the denoising process. Only works with schedulers which support the `timesteps` argument in their `set_timesteps` method.
            Defaults to not overriding, in which case the scheduler automatically sets the timesteps based on the `num_inference_steps` parameter.
            If set to a custom timestep schedule, the `num_inference_steps` parameter will be ignored. Cannot be set if `sigmas` is set.
        
- **`sigmas`** *()* вҖ” Default: `{"array":[],"method":"default"}` вҖ” 
            Optionally override the sigmas to use for the denoising process. Only works with schedulers which support the `sigmas` argument in their `set_sigmas` method.
            Defaults to not overriding, in which case the scheduler automatically sets the sigmas based on the `num_inference_steps` parameter.
            If set to a custom sigma schedule, the `num_inference_steps` parameter will be ignored. Cannot be set if `timesteps` is set.
        
- **`prediction_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"epsilon"` вҖ” 
            The type of prediction to use for the image generation.
            The `epsilon` is the default.
        
  Possible values: `v_prediction`, `epsilon`
- **`rescale_betas_snr_zero`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            Whether to set the rescale_betas_snr_zero option or not for the sampler
        
- **`image_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” 
            Number of images to generate in one request. Note that the higher the batch size,
            the longer it will take to generate the images.
        
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`tile_width`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `4096` вҖ” The size of the tiles to be used for the image generation.
  Range: min: 128, max: 4096
- **`tile_height`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `4096` вҖ” The size of the tiles to be used for the image generation.
  Range: min: 128, max: 4096
- **`tile_stride_width`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `2048` вҖ” The stride of the tiles to be used for the image generation.
  Range: min: 64, max: 2048
- **`tile_stride_height`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `2048` вҖ” The stride of the tiles to be used for the image generation.
  Range: min: 64, max: 2048
- **`eta`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The eta value to be used for the image generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`debug_latents`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the latents will be saved for debugging.
- **`debug_per_pass_latents`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the latents will be saved for debugging per pass.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `OutputParameters`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`debug_per_pass_latents`** *(?)* вҖ” The latents saved for debugging per pass.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`debug_latents`** *(?)* вҖ” The latents saved for debugging.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        

---

## fal-ai/lora/inpaint

**Title:** Stable Diffusion with LoRAs
**Description:** Run Any Stable Diffusion model with customizable LoRA weights.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/lora/inpaint`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** diffusion, lora, customization, fine-tuning

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `InpaintInput`
**Required fields:** `model_name`, `prompt`

- **`model_name`** *(string)* вҖ” URL or HuggingFace ID of the base model to generate the image.
- **`unet_name`** *(string)* вҖ” URL or HuggingFace ID of the custom U-Net model to use for the image generation.
- **`variant`** *(string)* вҖ” The variant of the model to use for huggingface models, e.g. 'fp16'.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use.Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`prompt_weighting`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the prompt weighting syntax will be used.
            Additionally, this will lift the 77 token limit by averaging embeddings.
        
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to use for image to image/inpainting.
- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of black-and-white image to use as mask during inpainting.
- **`noise_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The amount of noise to add to noise image for image. Only used if the image_url is provided. 1.0 is complete noise and values near 0 are minimal noise.
  Range: max: 1
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`embeddings`** *(list<Embedding>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The embeddings to use for the image generation. Only a single embedding is supported at the moment.
            The embeddings will be used to map the tokens in the prompt to the embedding weights.
        
- **`controlnets`** *(list<ControlNet>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The control nets to use for the image generation. You can use any number of control nets
            and they will be applied to the image at the specified timesteps.
        
- **`controlnet_guess_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the controlnet will be applied to only the conditional predictions.
        
- **`ip_adapter`** *(list<IPAdapter>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The IP adapter to use for the image generation.
        
- **`image_encoder_path`** *(string)* вҖ” 
            The path to the image encoder model to use for the image generation.
        
- **`image_encoder_subfolder`** *(string)* вҖ” 
            The subfolder of the image encoder model to use for the image generation.
        
- **`image_encoder_weight_name`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"pytorch_model.bin"` вҖ” 
            The weight name of the image encoder model to use for the image generation.
        
- **`ic_light_model_url`** *(string)* вҖ” 
            The URL of the IC Light model to use for the image generation.
        
- **`ic_light_model_background_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” 
            The URL of the IC Light model background image to use for the image generation.
            Make sure to use a background compatible with the model.
        
- **`ic_light_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” 
            The URL of the IC Light model image to use for the image generation.
        
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” 
            Increasing the amount of steps tells Stable Diffusion that it should take more steps
            to generate your final result which can increase the amount of detail in your image.
        
  Range: min: 1, max: 150
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`clip_skip`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” 
            Skips part of the image generation process, leading to slightly different results.
            This means the image renders faster, too.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`scheduler`** *(string)* вҖ” Scheduler / sampler to use for the image denoising process.
  Possible values: `DPM++ 2M`, `DPM++ 2M Karras`, `DPM++ 2M SDE`, `DPM++ 2M SDE Karras`, `Euler`, `Euler A`, `Euler (trailing timesteps)`, `LCM`, `LCM (trailing timesteps)`, `DDIM`, `TCD`
- **`timesteps`** *()* вҖ” Default: `{"array":[],"method":"default"}` вҖ” 
            Optionally override the timesteps to use for the denoising process. Only works with schedulers which support the `timesteps` argument in their `set_timesteps` method.
            Defaults to not overriding, in which case the scheduler automatically sets the timesteps based on the `num_inference_steps` parameter.
            If set to a custom timestep schedule, the `num_inference_steps` parameter will be ignored. Cannot be set if `sigmas` is set.
        
- **`sigmas`** *()* вҖ” Default: `{"array":[],"method":"default"}` вҖ” 
            Optionally override the sigmas to use for the denoising process. Only works with schedulers which support the `sigmas` argument in their `set_sigmas` method.
            Defaults to not overriding, in which case the scheduler automatically sets the sigmas based on the `num_inference_steps` parameter.
            If set to a custom sigma schedule, the `num_inference_steps` parameter will be ignored. Cannot be set if `timesteps` is set.
        
- **`prediction_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"epsilon"` вҖ” 
            The type of prediction to use for the image generation.
            The `epsilon` is the default.
        
  Possible values: `v_prediction`, `epsilon`
- **`rescale_betas_snr_zero`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            Whether to set the rescale_betas_snr_zero option or not for the sampler
        
- **`image_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” 
            Number of images to generate in one request. Note that the higher the batch size,
            the longer it will take to generate the images.
        
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`tile_width`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `4096` вҖ” The size of the tiles to be used for the image generation.
  Range: min: 128, max: 4096
- **`tile_height`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `4096` вҖ” The size of the tiles to be used for the image generation.
  Range: min: 128, max: 4096
- **`tile_stride_width`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `2048` вҖ” The stride of the tiles to be used for the image generation.
  Range: min: 64, max: 2048
- **`tile_stride_height`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `2048` вҖ” The stride of the tiles to be used for the image generation.
  Range: min: 64, max: 2048
- **`eta`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The eta value to be used for the image generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`debug_latents`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the latents will be saved for debugging.
- **`debug_per_pass_latents`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the latents will be saved for debugging per pass.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `OutputParameters`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`debug_per_pass_latents`** *(?)* вҖ” The latents saved for debugging per pass.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`debug_latents`** *(?)* вҖ” The latents saved for debugging.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        

---

## fal-ai/lucidflux

**Title:** Lucidflux
**Description:** LucidFlux for upscaling images with very high fidelity
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/lucidflux`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LucidFluxRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `prompt`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to edit.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to edit the image.
- **`target_width`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1024` вҖ” The width of the output image.
  Range: min: 512, max: 1024
- **`target_height`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1024` вҖ” The height of the output image.
  Range: min: 512, max: 1024
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`guidance`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” The guidance to use for the diffusion process.
  Range: min: 1, max: 30
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `42` вҖ” Seed used for random number generation

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LucidFluxResponse`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed used for random number generation
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” Generated image

---

## fal-ai/luma-photon/flash/modify

**Title:** Luma Photon
**Description:** Edit images from your prompts using Luma Photon. Photon is the most creative, personalizable, and intelligent visual models for creatives, bringing a step-function change in the cost of high-quality image generation.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/luma-photon/flash/modify`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ModifyImageRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `strength`, `aspect_ratio`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Instruction for modifying the image
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input image to reframe
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” The strength of the initial image. Higher strength values are corresponding to more influence of the initial image on the output.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” The aspect ratio of the reframed image
  Possible values: `1:1`, `16:9`, `9:16`, `4:3`, `3:4`, `21:9`, `9:21`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `T2IOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<File>)* вҖ” The generated image

**Title:** Luma Photon Flash Reframe
**Description:** This advanced tool intelligently expands your visuals, seamlessly blending new content to enhance creativity and adaptability, offering unmatched speed and quality for creators at a fraction of the cost.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/luma-photon/flash/reframe`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** flash, reframe, outpainting

- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” The aspect ratio of the reframed image
  Possible values: `1:1`, `16:9`, `9:16`, `4:3`, `3:4`, `21:9`, `9:21`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional prompt for reframing
- **`grid_position_x`** *(integer)* вҖ” X position of the grid for reframing
- **`grid_position_y`** *(integer)* вҖ” Y position of the grid for reframing
- **`x_end`** *(integer)* вҖ” End X coordinate for reframing
- **`x_start`** *(integer)* вҖ” Start X coordinate for reframing
- **`y_end`** *(integer)* вҖ” End Y coordinate for reframing
- **`y_start`** *(integer)* вҖ” Start Y coordinate for reframing

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `T2IOutput`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the subject reference image to use for consistent character appearance
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1:1"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated image
  Possible values: `1:1`, `16:9`, `4:3`, `3:2`, `2:3`, `3:4`, `9:16`, `21:9`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate (1-9)
  Range: min: 1, max: 9
- **`prompt_optimizer`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable automatic prompt optimization

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to be used for image enhancement
- **`model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"indoor"` вҖ” Model to be used for dehazing
  Possible values: `indoor`, `outdoor`

## fal-ai/moondream-next/detection

**Title:** MoonDreamNext Detection
**Description:** MoonDreamNext Detection is a multimodal vision-language model for gaze detection, bbox detection, point detection, and more.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/moondream-next/detection`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** multimodal

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `DetectionInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `task_type`, `detection_prompt`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Image URL to be processed
- **`task_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Type of detection to perform
  Possible values: `bbox_detection`, `point_detection`, `gaze_detection`
- **`detection_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text description of what to detect
- **`use_ensemble`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to use ensemble for gaze detection
- **`combine_points`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to combine points into a single point for point detection. This has no effect for bbox detection or gaze detection.
- **`show_visualization`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to show visualization for detection

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `DetectionOutput`
- **`image`** *(?)* вҖ” Output image with detection visualization
- **`text_output`** *(string)* вҖ” Detection results as text

## fal-ai/moondream3-preview/segment

**Title:** Moondream3 Preview [Segment]
**Description:** Moondream 3 is a vision language model that brings frontier-level visual reasoning with native object detection, pointing, and OCR capabilities to real-world applications requiring fast, inexpensive inference at scale. 
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/moondream3-preview/segment`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** mask, segmentation, 

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MoondreamSegementationInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `object`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to be processed
- **`object`** *(string)* вҖ” Object to be segmented in the image
- **`spatial_references`** *(array)* вҖ” Spatial references to guide the segmentation. By feeding in references you can help the segmentation process. Must be either list of Point object with x and y members, or list of arrays containing either 2 floats (x,y) or 4 floats (x1,y1,x2,y2). 
**NOTE**: You can also use the [**point endpoint**](https://fal.ai/models/fal-ai/moondream3-preview/point) to get points for the objects, and pass them in here.
- **`settings`** *(?)* вҖ” Sampling settings for the segmentation model
- **`preview`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to preview the output and return a binary mask of the image

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MoondreamSegementationOutput`
- **`image`** *(?)* вҖ” Segmentation mask image. If no object detected or preview not requested, will be null.
- **`finish_reason`** *(string)* вҖ” Reason for finishing the output generation
- **`bbox`** *(?)* вҖ” Bounding box of the segmented object. If not detected, will be null.
- **`path`** *(string)* вҖ” SVG path data representing the segmentation mask. If not detected, will be null.
- **`usage_info`** *()* вҖ” Usage information for the request

---

## fal-ai/nano-banana

**Title:** Nano Banana
**Description:** Google's famous original image generation and editing model
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/nano-banana`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image-editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `NanoBananaTextToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to generate an image from.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1:1"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the generated image.
  Possible values: `21:9`, `16:9`, `3:2`, `4:3`, `5:4`, `1:1`, `4:5`, `3:4`, `2:3`, `9:16`
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`safety_tolerance`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"4"` вҖ” The safety tolerance level for content moderation. 1 is the most strict (blocks most content), 6 is the least strict.
  Possible values: `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `5`, `6`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`limit_generations`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Experimental parameter to limit the number of generations from each round of prompting to 1. Set to `True` to to disregard any instructions in the prompt regarding the number of images to generate.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `NanoBananaTextToImageOutput`
- **`description`** *(string)* вҖ” The description of the generated images.
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The generated images.

---

## fal-ai/nano-banana-2

**Title:** Nano Banana 2
**Description:** Nano Banana 2 is Google's new state-of-the-art image generation and editing model

**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/nano-banana-2`
**Category:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `NanoBanana2TextToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to generate an image from.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the generated image. Supports extreme ratios: 4:1, 1:4, 8:1, 1:8. Use "auto" to let the model decide based on the prompt.
  Possible values: `auto`, `21:9`, `16:9`, `3:2`, `4:3`, `5:4`, `1:1`, `4:5`, `3:4`, `2:3`, `9:16`, `4:1`, `1:4`, `8:1`, `1:8`
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`safety_tolerance`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"4"` вҖ” The safety tolerance level for content moderation. 1 is the most strict (blocks most content), 6 is the least strict.
  Possible values: `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `5`, `6`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1K"` вҖ” The resolution of the image to generate.
  Possible values: `0.5K`, `1K`, `2K`, `4K`
- **`limit_generations`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Experimental parameter to limit the number of generations from each round of prompting to 1. Set to `True` to to disregard any instructions in the prompt regarding the number of images to generate and ignore any intermediate images generated by the model. This may affect generation quality.
- **`enable_web_search`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Enable web search for the image generation task. This will allow the model to use the latest information from the web to generate the image.
- **`thinking_level`** *(string)* вҖ” When set, enables model thinking with the given level ('minimal' or 'high') and includes thoughts in the generation. Omit to disable.
  Possible values: `minimal`, `high`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `NanoBanana2TextToImageOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The generated images.
- **`description`** *(string)* вҖ” The description of the generated images.

---

## fal-ai/object-removal

**Title:** Object Removal
**Description:** Removes objects and their visual effects using natural language, replacing them with contextually appropriate content

**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** utility, editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `PromptInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `prompt`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to remove objects from.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text description of the object to remove.
- **`model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"best_quality"`
  Possible values: `low_quality`, `medium_quality`, `high_quality`, `best_quality`
- **`mask_expansion`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” Amount of pixels to expand the mask by. Range: 0-50
  Range: min: 0, max: 50

## fal-ai/object-removal/bbox

**Description:** Removes box-selected objects and their visual effects, seamlessly reconstructing the scene with contextually appropriate content.

**Tags:** utility, editing

- **`box_prompts`** *(list<BBoxPromptBase>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of bounding box coordinates to erase (only one box prompt is supported)
- **`model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"best_quality"`
  Possible values: `low_quality`, `medium_quality`, `high_quality`, `best_quality`
- **`mask_expansion`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” Amount of pixels to expand the mask by. Range: 0-50
  Range: min: 0, max: 50

- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images with objects removed.

## fal-ai/object-removal/mask

**Title:** Object Removal
**Description:** Removes mask-selected objects and their visual effects, seamlessly reconstructing the scene with contextually appropriate content.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/object-removal/mask`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** utility, editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MaskInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `mask_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to remove objects from.
- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the mask image. White pixels (255) indicate areas to remove.
- **`model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"best_quality"`
  Possible values: `low_quality`, `medium_quality`, `high_quality`, `best_quality`
- **`mask_expansion`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” Amount of pixels to expand the mask by. Range: 0-50
  Range: min: 0, max: 50

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images with objects removed.

---

## fal-ai/omni-zero

**Title:** Omni Zero
**Description:** Any pose, any style, any identity
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/omni-zero`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** style transfer

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `OmniZeroInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`, `composition_image_url`, `style_image_url`, `identity_image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Prompt to guide the image generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Negative prompt to guide the image generation.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Input image url.
- **`composition_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Composition image url.
- **`style_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Style image url.
- **`identity_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Identity image url.
- **`image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.75` вҖ” Image strength.
- **`composition_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Composition strength.
- **`depth_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Depth strength.
- **`style_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Style strength.
- **`face_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Face strength.
- **`identity_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Identity strength.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Guidance scale.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `42` вҖ” Seed.
- **`number_of_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `OmniZeroOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The generated image.

---

## fal-ai/onereward

**Title:** Onereward
**Description:** OneReward is a finetuned version of Flux 1.0 Fill with intelligent editing capabilities.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/onereward`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** onereward

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Input`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `mask_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the source image to edit.
- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the mask image. White pixels indicate the area to fill or modify; black pixels preserve the original content.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text description of what to generate in the masked area. 
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"nsfw"` вҖ” Text describing what to avoid in the generated output.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Guidance scale for the base diffusion process. The model uses true_cfg for classifier-free guidance; this controls the distillation guidance.
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`true_cfg`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” True classifier-free guidance scale. Controls how strongly the model follows the prompt. Values above 1.0 enable CFG.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” Number of denoising steps. More steps generally produce higher quality results.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration level for image generation.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” Format of the output image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is used.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If True, returns the image as a data URI instead of uploading to CDN. The image will not be available in the request history.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If True, runs a safety checker on the output and filters NSFW content.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `OneRewardOutput`
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The edited image files.

---

## fal-ai/pasd

**Title:** PASD
**Description:** Pixel-Aware Diffusion Model for Realistic Image Super-Resolution and Personalized Stylization
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/pasd`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** utility, editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Input`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Input image to super-resolve
- **`scale`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Upscaling factor (1-4x)
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” Number of inference steps (10-50)
  Range: min: 10, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7` вҖ” Guidance scale for diffusion (1.0-20.0)
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`conditioning_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.8` вҖ” ControlNet conditioning scale (0.1-1.0)
  Range: min: 0.1, max: 1
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Additional prompt to guide super-resolution
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"blurry, dirty, messy, frames, deformed, dotted, noise, raster lines, unclear, lowres, over-smoothed, painting, ai generated"` вҖ” Negative prompt to avoid unwanted artifacts

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated super-resolved images
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” Timing information for different processing stages

---

## fal-ai/patina

**Title:** PATINA
**Description:** PATINA creates seamless high-resolution normal, roughness, basecolor (albedo), height (displacement) and metalness maps from images
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/patina`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** pbr, displacement, metalness, normal, roughness, basecolor, albedo, height, 

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `PatinaInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input image (photograph or render).
- **`maps`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” Default: `["basecolor","normal","roughness","metalness","height"]` вҖ” Which PBR maps to predict. Defaults to all five.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducible denoising. If not set, a random seed is used.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If True, return images as data URIs instead of CDN URLs.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable the safety checker for images.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” Output image format.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `PatinaOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for denoising.
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” Timing breakdown (seconds).
- **`images`** *(list<MapImageFile>)* вҖ” Predicted PBR material maps.

---

## fal-ai/patina/material/extract

**Title:** PATINA
**Description:** Extract seamless tiling textures with PBR attribute maps from images
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/patina/material/extract`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** material, pbr, extraction

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ExtractTextureInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Describe which texture to extract from the image.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” Output texture dimensions in pixels.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” Number of denoising steps for texture generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducible generation.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of texture images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Expand prompt with an LLM for richer detail. Adds ~0.0025 credits.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable the safety checker for generated images.
- **`tiling_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"both"` вҖ” Tiling direction: 'both' (omnidirectional), 'horizontal', or 'vertical'.
  Possible values: `both`, `horizontal`, `vertical`
- **`tile_size`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `128` вҖ” Tile size in latent space (64 = 512px, 128 = 1024px).
  Range: min: 32, max: 256
- **`tile_stride`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `64` вҖ” Tile stride in latent space.
  Range: min: 16, max: 128
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to extract a texture from.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.75` вҖ” How much to transform the input image. Only used when image_url is provided.
  Range: max: 1
- **`maps`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” Default: `["basecolor","normal","roughness","metalness","height"]` вҖ” Which PBR maps to predict. Deselect all to skip PBR estimation entirely. Defaults to all five.
- **`upscale_factor`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Upscale factor for predicted PBR maps via SeedVR seamless upscaling. 0 = no upscaling, 2 = 2Г— resolution, 4 = 4Г— resolution. The base texture image is not upscaled.
  Possible values: `0`, `2`, `4`
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” Output image format for textures and PBR maps.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ExtractTextureOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed used for texture generation.
- **`images`** *(list<any>)* вҖ” Generated tileable texture image(s) from z-image and predicted PBR material maps from PATINA.
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” End-to-end timing breakdown (seconds).
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for texture generation (possibly expanded).

---

## fal-ai/phota

**Title:** Phota
**Description:** Phota's model enables personalized photo editing, preserving identity while erasing distractions seamlessly.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/phota`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** edit, personalization, typography, phota

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `PhotaImageGenerateInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text description of the desired image. In case you wish to use specific profiles, refer to them as [[profile_id_1]], [[profile_id_2]], etc.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1K"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated image.
  Possible values: `1K`, `4K`
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated image.
  Possible values: `auto`, `1:1`, `16:9`, `4:3`, `3:4`, `9:16`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `PhotaImageOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The URL of the generated image.

---

## fal-ai/phota/enhance

**Title:** Phota Enhance
**Description:** Enhance images while preserving identities with Phota
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/phota/enhance`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** stylized, transform, typography, phota

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `PhotaImageEnhanceInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL/Base64 data URI of the image to enhance.
- **`profile_ids`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” List of profile IDs to use for the image enhancement. The profiles sent over will be used as candidates for identity preservation.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `PhotaImageEnhanceOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The URL of the enhanced image.
