## fal-ai/recraft/v3/image-to-image

**Title:** Recraft V3
**Description:** Recraft V3 is a text-to-image model with the ability to generate long texts, vector art, images in brand style, and much more. As of today, it is SOTA in image generation, proven by Hugging Face's industry-leading Text-to-Image Benchmark by Artificial Analysis.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/recraft/v3/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** vector, typography, style

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” A text description of areas to change.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to modify. Must be less than 5 MB in size, have resolution less than 16 MP and max dimension less than 4096 pixels.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Defines the difference with the original image, should lie in [0, 1], where 0 means almost identical, and 1 means miserable similarity
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`style`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"realistic_image"` вҖ” The style of the generated images. Vector images cost 2X as much.
  Possible values: `any`, `realistic_image`, `digital_illustration`, `vector_illustration`, `realistic_image/b_and_w`, `realistic_image/hard_flash`, `realistic_image/hdr`, `realistic_image/natural_light`, `realistic_image/studio_portrait`, `realistic_image/enterprise`, `realistic_image/motion_blur`, `realistic_image/evening_light`, `realistic_image/faded_nostalgia`, `realistic_image/forest_life`, `realistic_image/mystic_naturalism`, `realistic_image/natural_tones`, `realistic_image/organic_calm`, `realistic_image/real_life_glow`, `realistic_image/retro_realism`, `realistic_image/retro_snapshot`, `realistic_image/urban_drama`, `realistic_image/village_realism`, `realistic_image/warm_folk`, `digital_illustration/pixel_art`, `digital_illustration/hand_drawn`, `digital_illustration/grain`, `digital_illustration/infantile_sketch`, `digital_illustration/2d_art_poster`, `digital_illustration/handmade_3d`, `digital_illustration/hand_drawn_outline`, `digital_illustration/engraving_color`, `digital_illustration/2d_art_poster_2`, `digital_illustration/antiquarian`, `digital_illustration/bold_fantasy`, `digital_illustration/child_book`, `digital_illustration/child_books`, `digital_illustration/cover`, `digital_illustration/crosshatch`, `digital_illustration/digital_engraving`, `digital_illustration/expressionism`, `digital_illustration/freehand_details`, `digital_illustration/grain_20`, `digital_illustration/graphic_intensity`, `digital_illustration/hard_comics`, `digital_illustration/long_shadow`, `digital_illustration/modern_folk`, `digital_illustration/multicolor`, `digital_illustration/neon_calm`, `digital_illustration/noir`, `digital_illustration/nostalgic_pastel`, `digital_illustration/outline_details`, `digital_illustration/pastel_gradient`, `digital_illustration/pastel_sketch`, `digital_illustration/pop_art`, `digital_illustration/pop_renaissance`, `digital_illustration/street_art`, `digital_illustration/tablet_sketch`, `digital_illustration/urban_glow`, `digital_illustration/urban_sketching`, `digital_illustration/vanilla_dreams`, `digital_illustration/young_adult_book`, `digital_illustration/young_adult_book_2`, `vector_illustration/bold_stroke`, `vector_illustration/chemistry`, `vector_illustration/colored_stencil`, `vector_illustration/contour_pop_art`, `vector_illustration/cosmics`, `vector_illustration/cutout`, `vector_illustration/depressive`, `vector_illustration/editorial`, `vector_illustration/emotional_flat`, `vector_illustration/infographical`, `vector_illustration/marker_outline`, `vector_illustration/mosaic`, `vector_illustration/naivector`, `vector_illustration/roundish_flat`, `vector_illustration/segmented_colors`, `vector_illustration/sharp_contrast`, `vector_illustration/thin`, `vector_illustration/vector_photo`, `vector_illustration/vivid_shapes`, `vector_illustration/engraving`, `vector_illustration/line_art`, `vector_illustration/line_circuit`, `vector_illustration/linocut`
- **`colors`** *(list<RGBColor>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” An array of preferable colors
- **`style_id`** *(string)* вҖ” The ID of the custom style reference (optional)
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” A text description of undesired elements on an image
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ImageToImageOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<File>)* вҖ” The generated images

---

## fal-ai/recraft/vectorize

**Title:** Recraft
**Description:** Converts a given raster image to SVG format using Recraft model.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/recraft/vectorize`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** stylized, transform

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `VectorizeInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to be vectorized. Must be in PNG, JPG or WEBP format, less than 5 MB in size, have resolution less than 16 MP and max dimension less than 4096 pixels, min dimension more than 256 pixels.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `VectorizeOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The vectorized image.

---

## fal-ai/retoucher

**Title:** Face Retoucher
**Description:** Automatically retouches faces to smooth skin and remove blemishes.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/retoucher`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `RetoucherInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to be retouched.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for reproducibility. Different seeds will make slightly different results.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `RetoucherOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the generation.
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The generated image file info.

---

## fal-ai/rife

**Title:** RIFE
**Description:** Interpolate images with RIFE - Real-Time Intermediate Flow Estimation
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/rife`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** interpolation

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `RIFEImageInput`
**Required fields:** `start_image_url`, `end_image_url`

- **`start_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the first image to use as the starting point for interpolation.
- **`end_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the second image to use as the ending point for interpolation.
- **`output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"images"` вҖ” The type of output to generate; either individual images or a video.
  Possible values: `images`, `video`
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the output images. Only applicable if output_type is 'images'.
  Possible values: `png`, `jpeg`
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of frames to generate between the input images.
  Range: min: 1, max: 64
- **`include_start`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to include the start image in the output.
- **`include_end`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to include the end image in the output.
- **`fps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” Frames per second for the output video. Only applicable if output_type is 'video'.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `RIFEImageOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The generated frames as individual images.
- **`video`** *(?)* вҖ” The generated video file, if output_type is 'video'.

---

## fal-ai/sam-3-1/image

**Title:** Sam 3 1
**Description:** SAM 3.1 builds comes with Object Multiplex, a shared-memory approach for joint multi-object tracking that delivers faster speeds with larger number of objects tracked.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sam-3-1/image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** segmentation, mask, real-time

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SAM31ImageInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to be segmented
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"wheel"` вҖ” Text prompt for segmentation
- **`point_prompts`** *(list<PointPrompt>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of point prompts
- **`box_prompts`** *(list<BoxPrompt>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Box prompt coordinates (x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max). Multiple boxes supported - use object_id to group boxes for the same object or leave empty for separate objects.
- **`apply_mask`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Apply the mask on the image.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If True, the media will be returned as a data URI.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`return_multiple_masks`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If True, upload and return multiple generated masks as defined by `max_masks`.
- **`max_masks`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Maximum number of masks to return when `return_multiple_masks` is enabled.
  Range: min: 1, max: 32
- **`include_scores`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to include mask confidence scores.
- **`include_boxes`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to include bounding boxes for each mask (when available).

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SAM31ImageOutput`
- **`scores`** *(array)* вҖ” Per-mask confidence scores when requested.
- **`metadata`** *(array)* вҖ” Per-mask metadata including scores and boxes.
- **`boxes`** *(array)* вҖ” Per-mask normalized bounding boxes [cx, cy, w, h] when requested.
- **`masks`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Segmented mask images.
- **`image`** *(?)* вҖ” Primary segmented mask preview.

---

## fal-ai/sam-3-1/image-rle

**Title:** Sam 3 1
**Description:** SAM 3.1 builds comes with Object Multiplex, a shared-memory approach for joint multi-object tracking that delivers faster speeds with larger number of objects tracked.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sam-3-1/image-rle`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** segmentation, mask, real-time

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SAM31ImageInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to be segmented
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"wheel"` вҖ” Text prompt for segmentation
- **`point_prompts`** *(list<PointPrompt>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of point prompts
- **`box_prompts`** *(list<BoxPrompt>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Box prompt coordinates (x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max). Multiple boxes supported - use object_id to group boxes for the same object or leave empty for separate objects.
- **`apply_mask`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Apply the mask on the image.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If True, the media will be returned as a data URI.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`return_multiple_masks`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If True, upload and return multiple generated masks as defined by `max_masks`.
- **`max_masks`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Maximum number of masks to return when `return_multiple_masks` is enabled.
  Range: min: 1, max: 32
- **`include_scores`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to include mask confidence scores.
- **`include_boxes`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to include bounding boxes for each mask (when available).

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SAM31RLEOutput`
- **`scores`** *(array)* вҖ” Per-mask confidence scores when requested.
- **`metadata`** *(array)* вҖ” Per-mask metadata when multiple RLEs are returned.
- **`boundingbox_frames_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” Zip file containing per-frame bounding box overlays.
- **`rle`** *(string | array)* вҖ” Run Length Encoding of the mask.
- **`boxes`** *(array)* вҖ” Per-mask normalized bounding boxes [cx, cy, w, h] when requested.

## fal-ai/sam-3/image

**Title:** Segment Anything Model 3
**Description:** SAM 3 is a unified foundation model for promptable segmentation in images and videos. It can detect, segment, and track objects using text or visual prompts such as points, boxes, and masks. 
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sam-3/image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** segmentation, mask, real-time

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SAM3ImageInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to be segmented
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"wheel"` вҖ” Text prompt for segmentation
- **`text_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” [DEPRECATED] Use 'prompt' instead. Kept for backward compatibility.
- **`point_prompts`** *(list<PointPrompt>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of point prompts
- **`box_prompts`** *(list<BoxPrompt>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Box prompt coordinates (x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max). Multiple boxes supported - use object_id to group boxes for the same object or leave empty for separate objects.
- **`apply_mask`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Apply the mask on the image.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If True, the media will be returned as a data URI.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`return_multiple_masks`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If True, upload and return multiple generated masks as defined by `max_masks`.
- **`max_masks`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Maximum number of masks to return when `return_multiple_masks` is enabled.
  Range: min: 1, max: 32
- **`include_scores`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to include mask confidence scores.
- **`include_boxes`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to include bounding boxes for each mask (when available).

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SAM3ImageOutput`
- **`boxes`** *(array)* вҖ” Per-mask normalized bounding boxes [cx, cy, w, h] when requested.
- **`masks`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Segmented mask images.
- **`metadata`** *(array)* вҖ” Per-mask metadata including scores and boxes.
- **`scores`** *(array)* вҖ” Per-mask confidence scores when requested.
- **`image`** *(?)* вҖ” Primary segmented mask preview.

---

- **`point_prompts`** *(list<PointPrompt>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of point prompts
- **`box_prompts`** *(list<BoxPrompt>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Box prompt coordinates (x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max). Multiple boxes supported - use object_id to group boxes for the same object or leave empty for separate objects.
- **`apply_mask`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Apply the mask on the image.

- **`include_boxes`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to include bounding boxes for each mask (when available).

## fal-ai/sam2/auto-segment

**Title:** Segment Anything Model 2
**Description:** SAM 2 is a model for segmenting images automatically. It can return individual masks or a single mask for the entire image.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sam2/auto-segment`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** segmentation, mask

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SAM2AutomaticSegmentationInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to be automatically segmented
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`points_per_side`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `32` вҖ” Number of points to sample along each side of the image.
- **`pred_iou_thresh`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.88` вҖ” Threshold for predicted IOU score.
- **`stability_score_thresh`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.95` вҖ” Threshold for stability score.
- **`min_mask_region_area`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `100` вҖ” Minimum area of a mask region.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SAM2AutomaticSegmentationOutput`
- **`combined_mask`** *()* вҖ” Combined segmentation mask.
- **`individual_masks`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Individual segmentation masks.

---

- **`box_prompts`** *(list<BoxPrompt>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Coordinates for boxes
- **`apply_mask`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Apply the mask on the image.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.

## fal-ai/sd15-depth-controlnet

**Title:** SD 1.5 Depth ControlNet
**Description:** SD 1.5 ControlNet
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sd15-depth-controlnet`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** diffusion, editing, manipulation, controlnet

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToImageControlNetInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `control_image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`control_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`controlnet_conditioning_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The scale of the controlnet conditioning.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image. Leave it none to automatically infer from the control image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `35` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 70
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`enable_deep_cache`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, DeepCache will be enabled. TBD
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the function will wait for the image to be generated and uploaded
            before returning the response. This will increase the latency of the function but
            it allows you to get the image directly in the response without going through the CDN.
        
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The list of LoRA weights to use.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`expand_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the prompt will be expanded with additional prompts.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        

---

## fal-ai/sdxl-controlnet-union/image-to-image

**Title:** SDXL ControlNet Union
**Description:** An efficent SDXL multi-controlnet image-to-image model.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sdxl-controlnet-union/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** diffusion, controlnet, composition

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageToImageControlNetUnionInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`controlnet_conditioning_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The scale of the controlnet conditioning.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as a starting point for the generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use.Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image. Leave it none to automatically infer from the control image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `35` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 70
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.95` вҖ” determines how much the generated image resembles the initial image
  Range: min: 0.05, max: 1
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The list of LoRA weights to use.
- **`embeddings`** *(list<Embedding>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The list of embeddings to use.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`safety_checker_version`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"v1"` вҖ” The version of the safety checker to use. v1 is the default CompVis safety checker. v2 uses a custom ViT model.
  Possible values: `v1`, `v2`
- **`expand_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the prompt will be expanded with additional prompts.
- **`format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`request_id`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            An id bound to a request, can be used with response to identify the request
            itself.
        
- **`preserve_aspect_ratio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
        If set to true, the aspect ratio of the generated image will be preserved even
        if the image size is too large. However, if the image is not a multiple of 32
        in width or height, it will be resized to the nearest multiple of 32. By default,
        this snapping to the nearest multiple of 32 will not preserve the aspect ratio.
        Set crop_output to True, to crop the output to the proper aspect ratio
        after generating.
        
- **`crop_output`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
        If set to true, the output cropped to the proper aspect ratio after generating.
        
- **`openpose_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`openpose_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the openpose image.
- **`depth_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`depth_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the depth image.
- **`teed_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`teed_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the teed image.
- **`canny_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`canny_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the canny image.
- **`normal_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`normal_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the normal image.
- **`segmentation_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`segmentation_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the segmentation image.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        

---

## fal-ai/sdxl-controlnet-union/inpainting

**Title:** SDXL ControlNet Union
**Description:** An efficent SDXL multi-controlnet inpainting model.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sdxl-controlnet-union/inpainting`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** diffusion, controlnet, composition

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `InpaintingControlNetUnionInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `mask_url`, `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`controlnet_conditioning_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The scale of the controlnet conditioning.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as a starting point for the generation.
- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the mask to use for inpainting.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use.Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image. Leave it none to automatically infer from the control image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `35` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 70
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.95` вҖ” determines how much the generated image resembles the initial image
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The list of LoRA weights to use.
- **`embeddings`** *(list<Embedding>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The list of embeddings to use.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`safety_checker_version`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"v1"` вҖ” The version of the safety checker to use. v1 is the default CompVis safety checker. v2 uses a custom ViT model.
  Possible values: `v1`, `v2`
- **`expand_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the prompt will be expanded with additional prompts.
- **`format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`request_id`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            An id bound to a request, can be used with response to identify the request
            itself.
        
- **`openpose_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`openpose_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the openpose image.
- **`depth_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`depth_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the depth image.
- **`teed_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`teed_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the teed image.
- **`canny_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`canny_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the canny image.
- **`normal_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`normal_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the normal image.
- **`segmentation_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`segmentation_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the segmentation image.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.

## fal-ai/seedvr/upscale/image

**Title:** SeedVR2
**Description:** Use SeedVR2 to upscale your images
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/seedvr/upscale/image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** upscale, image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SeedVRImageInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The input image to be processed
- **`upscale_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"factor"` вҖ” The mode to use for the upscale. If 'target', the upscale factor will be calculated based on the target resolution. If 'factor', the upscale factor will be used directly.
  Possible values: `target`, `factor`
- **`upscale_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Upscaling factor to be used. Will multiply the dimensions with this factor when `upscale_mode` is `factor`.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`target_resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1080p"` вҖ” The target resolution to upscale to when `upscale_mode` is `target`.
  Possible values: `720p`, `1080p`, `1440p`, `2160p`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The random seed used for the generation process.
- **`noise_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.1` вҖ” The noise scale to use for the generation process.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpg"` вҖ” The format of the output image.
  Possible values: `png`, `jpg`, `webp`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SeedVRImageOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” Upscaled image file after processing
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The random seed used for the generation process.

---

  Possible values: `target`, `factor`

- **`target_resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1080p"` вҖ” The target resolution to upscale to when `upscale_mode` is `target`.

- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The random seed used for the generation process.

## fal-ai/stable-diffusion-v3-medium/image-to-image

**Title:** Stable Diffusion V3
**Description:** Stable Diffusion 3 Medium (Image to Image) is a Multimodal Diffusion Transformer (MMDiT) model that improves image quality, typography, prompt understanding, and efficiency.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/stable-diffusion-v3-medium/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** diffusion, editing, style

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The image URL to generate an image from.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate an image from.
- **`prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, prompt will be upsampled with more details.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image. Defaults to the conditioning image's size.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.9` вҖ” The strength of the image-to-image transformation.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SD3Output`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” The number of images generated.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

**Description:** Step1X-Edit transforms your photos with simple instructions into stunning, professional-quality editsвҖ”rivaling top proprietary tools.

**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToImageInput`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The image URL to generate an image from. Needs to match the dimensions of the mask.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ImageOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`timings`** *(object)*

## fal-ai/stepx-edit2

**Title:** Stepx Edit2
**Description:** Image-to-image editing with Step1X-Edit v2 from StepFun. Reasoning-enhanced modifications through a thinkingвҖ“editingвҖ“reflection loop with MLLM world knowledge for abstract instruction comprehension.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/stepx-edit2`
**Category:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The image URL to generate an image from. Needs to match the dimensions of the mask.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `6` вҖ” 
            The true CFG scale. Controls how closely the model follows the prompt.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform. Recommended: 50.
  Range: min: 1, max: 100
- **`enable_thinking_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable thinking mode. Uses multimodal language model knowledge to interpret abstract editing instructions.
- **`enable_reflection_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable reflection mode. Reviews outputs, corrects unintended changes, and determines when editing is complete.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ImageOutput`
- **`best_info`** *(array)* вҖ” Reflection analysis (only available when reflection mode is enabled).
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`reformat_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The model's interpretation of your instruction (only available when thinking mode is enabled).
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`think_info`** *(array)* вҖ” Reasoning process details (only available when thinking mode is enabled).
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images

## fal-ai/topaz/upscale/image

**Title:** Topaz
**Description:** Use the powerful and accurate topaz image enhancer to enhance your images.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/topaz/upscale/image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageUpscaleRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"Standard V2"` вҖ” Model to use for image enhancement.
  Possible values: `Low Resolution V2`, `Standard V2`, `CGI`, `High Fidelity V2`, `Text Refine`, `Recovery`, `Redefine`, `Recovery V2`, `Standard MAX`, `Wonder`
- **`upscale_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Factor to upscale the image by (e.g. 2.0 doubles width and height)
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`crop_to_fill`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false`
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Url of the image to be upscaled
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” Output format of the upscaled image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`subject_detection`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"All"` вҖ” Subject detection mode for the image enhancement. Applies to standard enhance and Recovery V2 models.
  Possible values: `All`, `Foreground`, `Background`
- **`face_enhancement`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to apply face enhancement to the image. Applies to standard enhance and Recovery V2 models.
- **`face_enhancement_creativity`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Creativity level for face enhancement. 0.0 means no creativity, 1.0 means maximum creativity. Ignored if face enhancement is disabled.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`face_enhancement_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.8` вҖ” Strength of the face enhancement. 0.0 means no enhancement, 1.0 means maximum enhancement. Ignored if face enhancement is disabled.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`sharpen`** *(number)* вҖ” Sharpening level (0.0-1.0). Applies to Standard V2, Low Resolution V2, CGI, High Fidelity V2, Text Refine, and Redefine models.
- **`denoise`** *(number)* вҖ” Denoising level (0.0-1.0). Applies to Standard V2, Low Resolution V2, CGI, High Fidelity V2, Text Refine, and Redefine models.
- **`fix_compression`** *(number)* вҖ” Compression artifact removal level (0.0-1.0). Applies to Standard V2, Low Resolution V2, High Fidelity V2, and Text Refine models.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Enhancement strength (0.01-1.0). Applies to Text Refine model only.
- **`creativity`** *(integer)* вҖ” Creativity level for generative upscaling (1-6). Higher values produce more creative/hallucinated details. Applies to Redefine model only.
- **`texture`** *(integer)* вҖ” Texture detail level for generative upscaling (1-5). Applies to Redefine model only.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt to guide generative upscaling (max 1024 chars). Applies to Redefine model only.
- **`autoprompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Enable automatic prompt generation for generative upscaling. Applies to Redefine model only.
- **`detail`** *(number)* вҖ” Detail recovery level (0.0-1.0). Applies to Recovery V2 model only.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ImageUpscaleOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The upscaled image.

---

## fal-ai/uno

**Title:** Uno
**Description:** An AI model that transforms input images into new ones based on text prompts, blending reference visuals with your creative directions.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/uno`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `UNOInput`
**Required fields:** `input_image_urls`, `prompt`

- **`input_image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” URL of images to use while generating the image.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” 
            The size of the generated image. You can choose between some presets or custom height and width
            that **must be multiples of 8**.
        
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducible generation. If set none, a random seed will be used.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the function will wait for the image to be generated and uploaded
            before returning the response. This will increase the latency of the function but
            it allows you to get the image directly in the response without going through the CDN.
        

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `UNOOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used to generate the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The URLs of the generated images.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        

---

## fal-ai/uso

**Title:** Uso
**Description:** Use USO to perform subject driven generations using reference image.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/uso`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `USOInputImage`
**Required fields:** `input_image_urls`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Text prompt for generation. Can be empty for pure style transfer.
- **`input_image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” List of image URLs in order: [content_image, style_image, extra_style_image].
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” The size of the generated image. 
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” What you don't want in the image. Use it to exclude unwanted elements, styles, or artifacts.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” Number of denoising steps. More steps can improve quality but increase generation time.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” How closely to follow the prompt. Higher values stick closer to the prompt.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`keep_size`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Preserve the layout and dimensions of the input content image. Useful for style transfer.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate in parallel.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducible generation. Use same seed for consistent results.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, wait for generation and upload before returning. Increases latency but provides immediate access to images.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable NSFW content detection and filtering.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” Output image format. PNG preserves transparency, JPEG is smaller.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `USOOutputImage`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images with applied style and/or subject customization
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” Performance timings for different stages
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” NSFW detection results for each generated image
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed used for generation

---

## fal-ai/vecglypher/image-to-svg

**Title:** Vecglypher
**Description:** Vector font generation with VecGlypher. Create custom glyphs from text descriptions or reference imagesвҖ”outputs clean SVG paths directly without raster-to-vector conversion.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/vecglypher/image-to-svg`
**Category:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageToSVGInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `reference_image_urls`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The target text to generate as vector glyphs, styled to match the reference images.
- **`reference_image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” URLs of reference glyph images whose style should be matched. Provide 1 to 8 images of individual glyph characters.
- **`temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.1` вҖ” Sampling temperature.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable input safety checking.
- **`top_p`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.95` вҖ” Top-p (nucleus) sampling parameter.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`top_k`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Top-k sampling parameter.
  Range: min: -1, max: 100
- **`repetition_penalty`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Repetition penalty.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`max_tokens`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8192` вҖ” Maximum tokens to generate.
  Range: min: 256, max: 16384
- **`output_size`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `512` вҖ” Maximum dimension (width or height) of the output SVG in pixels. The aspect ratio is preserved.
  Range: min: 64, max: 4096
- **`fill_color`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"black"` вҖ” Fill color for the generated glyphs. Accepts any valid SVG/CSS color value.
- **`stroke_color`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional stroke (outline) color for the generated glyphs. When set, adds an outline around each glyph path.
- **`stroke_width`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Stroke width in SVG units. Only applies when stroke_color is set.
  Range: min: 0.1, max: 50

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `VecGlypherOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” Timing breakdown of the generation process.
- **`svg_content`** *(string)* вҖ” Raw SVG content as a string.
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The generated SVG file containing vector glyphs.

---

**Description:** Vidu Reference-to-Image creates images by using a reference images and combining them with a prompt.

**Tags:** images-to-imag, reference-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ReferenceToImageRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `reference_image_urls`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt for video generation, max 1500 characters
- **`reference_image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” URLs of the reference images to use for consistent subject appearance
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"16:9"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the output video
  Possible values: `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for generation

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ReferenceToImageOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The edited image

## fal-ai/vidu/reference-to-image

**Title:** Vidu
**Description:** Vidu Reference-to-Image creates images by using a reference images and combining them with a prompt.

- **`reference_image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” URLs of the reference images to use for consistent subject appearance
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"16:9"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the output video
  Possible values: `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`

## fal-ai/wan-25-preview/image-to-image

**Title:** Wan 2.5 Image to Image
**Description:** Wan 2.5 image-to-image model.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan-25-preview/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_urls`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt describing how to edit the image. Max 2000 characters.
- **`image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” URLs of images to edit. For single-image editing, provide 1 URL. For multi-reference generation, provide up to 2 URLs. If more than 2 URLs are provided, only the first 2 will be used.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Negative prompt to describe content to avoid. Max 500 characters.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square"` вҖ” The size of the generated image. Width and height must be between 384 and 1440 pixels.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate. Values from 1 to 4.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ImageToImageOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The edited images
- **`seeds`** *(list<integer>)* вҖ” The seeds used for each generated image
- **`actual_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The original prompt (prompt expansion is not available for image editing)

---

## fal-ai/wan/v2.2-a14b/image-to-image

**Title:** Wan
**Description:** Wan 2.2's 14B model edit high-resolution, photorealistic images with powerful prompt understanding and fine-grained visual detail
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan/v2.2-a14b/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `WanI2IRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input image.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to guide image generation.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Denoising strength. 1.0 = fully remake; 0.0 = preserve original.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Negative prompt for video generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated image. If 'auto', the aspect ratio will be determined automatically based on the input image.
  Possible values: `auto`, `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `27` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 40
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, input data will be checked for safety before processing.
- **`enable_output_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, output video will be checked for safety after generation.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion. This will use a large language model to expand the prompt with additional details while maintaining the original meaning.
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration level to use. The more acceleration, the faster the generation, but with lower quality. The recommended value is 'regular'.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`guidance_scale_2`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” Guidance scale for the second stage of the model. This is used to control the adherence to the prompt in the second stage of the model.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2`
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)*
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`image_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the output image.
  Possible values: `png`, `jpeg`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanI2IResponse`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The generated image file.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The text prompt used for image generation.

---

## fal-ai/workflow-utilities/extract-nth-frame

**Title:** Workflow Utilities
**Description:** FFMPEG Untility for Extracting nth Frame
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/workflow-utilities/extract-nth-frame`
**Category:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ExtractNthFrameInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video file to extract frames from
- **`frame_interval`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `12` вҖ” Extract every Nth frame (e.g., 3 = every 3rd frame, 12 = every 12th frame)
  Range: min: 1, max: 300
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” Output format for extracted frames
  Possible values: `png`, `jpg`, `jpeg`, `webp`
- **`max_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `100` вҖ” Maximum number of frames to extract
  Range: min: 1, max: 500
- **`quality`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `95` вҖ” Quality for jpg/webp output (1-100)
  Range: min: 1, max: 100

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ExtractNthFrameOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Array of extracted frame images
- **`frame_count`** *(integer)* вҖ” Total number of frames extracted

## fal-ai/z-image/turbo/controlnet

**Title:** Z-Image Turbo
**Description:** Generate images from text and edge, depth or pose images using Z-Image Turbo, Tongyi-MAI's super-fast 6B model.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/z-image/turbo/controlnet`
**Category:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ZImageTurboControlNetInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`, `auto`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion. Note: this will increase the price by 0.0025 credits per request.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of Image for ControlNet generation.
- **`control_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.75` вҖ” The scale of the controlnet conditioning.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`control_start`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The start of the controlnet conditioning.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`control_end`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.8` вҖ” The end of the controlnet conditioning.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`preprocess`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” What kind of preprocessing to apply to the image, if any.
  Possible values: `none`, `canny`, `depth`, `pose`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ZImageTurboControlNetOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” The timings of the generation process.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

---

**Title:** Z-Image Turbo
**Description:** Generate images from text and edge, depth or pose images using custom LoRA and Z-Image Turbo, Tongyi-MAI's super-fast 6B model.

- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.

- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.

  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion. Note: this will increase the price by 0.0025 credits per request.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of Image for ControlNet generation.
- **`control_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.75` вҖ” The scale of the controlnet conditioning.

- **`control_start`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The start of the controlnet conditioning.

  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`preprocess`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” What kind of preprocessing to apply to the image, if any.
  Possible values: `none`, `canny`, `depth`, `pose`
- **`loras`** *(list<LoRAInput>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of LoRA weights to apply (maximum 3).

- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.

- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion. Note: this will increase the price by 0.0025 credits per request.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of Image for Image-to-Image generation.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.6` вҖ” The strength of the image-to-image conditioning.
  Range: max: 1
- **`loras`** *(list<LoRAInput>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of LoRA weights to apply (maximum 3).

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ZImageTurboImageToImageOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” The timings of the generation process.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.

- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion. Note: this will increase the price by 0.0025 credits per request.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of Image for Inpaint generation.
- **`control_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.75` вҖ” The scale of the controlnet conditioning.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`control_start`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The start of the controlnet conditioning.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`control_end`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.8` вҖ” The end of the controlnet conditioning.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`mask_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of Mask for Inpaint generation.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the inpaint conditioning.
  Range: max: 1

- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” The timings of the generation process.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

## rundiffusion-fal/juggernaut-flux-lora/inpainting

**Title:** Juggernaut Flux Lora
**Description:** Juggernaut Base Flux LoRA Inpainting by RunDiffusion is a drop-in replacement for Flux [Dev] inpainting that delivers sharper details, richer colors, and enhanced realism to all your LoRAs and LyCORIS with full compatibility.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/rundiffusion-fal/juggernaut-flux-lora/inpainting`
**Category:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `InpaintInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`, `mask_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 35
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the function will wait for the image to be generated and uploaded
            before returning the response. This will increase the latency of the function but
            it allows you to get the image directly in the response without going through the CDN.
        
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of image to use for inpainting. or img2img
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.85` вҖ” The strength to use for inpainting/image-to-image. Only used if the image_url is provided. 1.0 is completely remakes the image while 0.0 preserves the original.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1
- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” 
            The mask to area to Inpaint in.
        

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        

---

## rundiffusion-fal/juggernaut-flux/base/image-to-image

**Title:** Juggernaut Flux Base
**Description:** Juggernaut Base Flux by RunDiffusion is a drop-in replacement for Flux [Dev] that delivers sharper details, richer colors, and enhanced realism, while instantly boosting LoRAs and LyCORIS with full compatibility.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/rundiffusion-fal/juggernaut-flux/base/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image generation

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `DevImageToImageInput`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to generate an image from.

- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.95` вҖ” The strength of the initial image. Higher strength values are better for this model.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `40` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 10, max: 50

            will output the same image every time.

            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.

## wan/v2.6/image-to-image

**Title:** Wan v2.6 Image to Image
**Description:** Wan 2.6 image-to-image model.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/wan/v2.6/image-to-image`
**Category:** image-to-image
**Tags:** image-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImageEditInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_urls`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt describing the desired image. Supports Chinese and English. Max 2000 characters. Example: 'Generate an image using the style of image 1 and background of image 2'.
- **`image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” Reference images for editing (1-3 images required). Order matters: reference as 'image 1', 'image 2', 'image 3' in prompt. Resolution: 384-5000px each dimension. Max size: 10MB each. Formats: JPEG, JPG, PNG (no alpha), BMP, WEBP.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Content to avoid in the generated image. Max 500 characters.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” Output image size. Use presets like 'square_hd', 'landscape_16_9', 'portrait_9_16', or specify exact dimensions with ImageSize(width=1280, height=720). Total pixels must be between 768*768 and 1280*1280.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate (1-4). Directly affects billing cost.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable LLM prompt optimization. Significantly improves results for simple prompts but adds 3-4 seconds processing time.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility (0-2147483647). Same seed produces more consistent results.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable content moderation for input and output.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ImageEditOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<File>)* вҖ” Generated images in PNG format
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation

---

  Possible values: `2:1`, `20:9`, `19.5:9`, `16:9`, `4:3`, `3:2`, `1:1`, `2:3`, `3:4`, `9:16`, `9:19.5`, `9:20`, `1:2`
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1k"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated image. `1k` for standard resolution, `2k` for high resolution.
  Possible values: `1k`, `2k`

  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
