## fal-ai/meshy/v6-preview/text-to-3d

**Title:** Meshy 6 Preview
**Description:** Meshy-6-Preview is the latest model from Meshy. It generates realistic and production ready 3D models.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/meshy/v6-preview/text-to-3d`
**Category:** text-to-3d
**Tags:** text-to-3d

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextTo3DInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Describe what kind of object the 3D model is. Maximum 600 characters.
- **`mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"full"` вҖ” Generation mode. 'preview' returns untextured geometry only, 'full' returns textured model (preview + refine).
  Possible values: `preview`, `full`
- **`art_style`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"realistic"` вҖ” Desired art style of the object. Note: only 'realistic' is supported for Meshy-6 (latest model); 'sculpture' will be rejected with a 422. For older models, 'sculpture' is supported but enable_pbr should be false when using it.
  Possible values: `realistic`, `sculpture`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for reproducible results. Same prompt and seed usually generate the same result.
- **`topology`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"triangle"` вҖ” Specify the topology of the generated model. Quad for smooth surfaces, Triangle for detailed geometry.
  Possible values: `quad`, `triangle`
- **`target_polycount`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30000` вҖ” Target number of polygons in the generated model
  Range: min: 100, max: 300000
- **`should_remesh`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the remesh phase. When false, returns unprocessed triangular mesh.
- **`symmetry_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Controls symmetry behavior during model generation.
  Possible values: `off`, `auto`, `on`
- **`enable_pbr`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Generate PBR Maps (metallic, roughness, normal) in addition to base color. Should be false for sculpture style.
- **`is_a_t_pose`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Deprecated: use pose_mode instead. When true, generates a T-pose model.
- **`pose_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Pose mode for the generated model. 'a-pose' generates an A-pose, 't-pose' generates a T-pose, empty string for no specific pose.
  Possible values: `a-pose`, `t-pose`, ``
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion. This will use a large language model to expand the prompt with additional details while maintaining the original meaning.
- **`texture_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Additional text prompt to guide the texturing process (only used in 'full' mode)
- **`texture_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” 2D image to guide the texturing process (only used in 'full' mode)
- **`enable_rigging`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Automatically rig the generated model as a humanoid character. Includes basic walking and running animations. Best results with humanoid characters that have clearly defined limbs.
- **`rigging_height_meters`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.7` вҖ” Approximate height of the character in meters. Only used when enable_rigging is true.
- **`enable_animation`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Apply an animation preset to the rigged model. Requires enable_rigging to be true.
- **`animation_action_id`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1001` вҖ” Animation preset ID from Meshy's library (500+ presets). Only used when enable_animation is true. See https://docs.meshy.ai/en/api/animation-library for available action IDs.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, input data will be checked for safety before processing.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `TextTo3DOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation
- **`rigged_character_fbx`** *(?)* вҖ” Rigged character in FBX format. Only present when enable_rigging is true.
- **`animation_fbx`** *(?)* вҖ” Animated 3D model in FBX format. Only present when enable_animation is true.
- **`animation_glb`** *(?)* вҖ” Animated 3D model in GLB format. Only present when enable_animation is true.
- **`rigged_character_glb`** *(?)* вҖ” Rigged character in GLB format. Only present when enable_rigging is true.
- **`model_urls`** *()* вҖ” URLs for different 3D model formats
- **`actual_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The actual prompt used if prompt expansion was enabled
- **`model_glb`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D object in GLB format.
- **`rig_task_id`** *(string)* вҖ” Rigging task ID. Only present when enable_rigging is true.
- **`basic_animations`** *(?)* вҖ” Basic walking and running animations. Only present when enable_rigging is true.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt used for generation
- **`texture_urls`** *(list<TextureFiles>)* вҖ” Array of texture file objects
- **`thumbnail`** *(?)* вҖ” Preview thumbnail of the generated model

---

## fal-ai/meshy/v6/text-to-3d

**Title:** Meshy 6
**Description:** Meshy-6 is the latest model from Meshy. It generates realistic and production ready 3D models.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/meshy/v6/text-to-3d`
**Category:** text-to-3d
**Tags:** text-to-3d

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextTo3DInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Describe what kind of object the 3D model is. Maximum 600 characters.
- **`mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"full"` вҖ” Generation mode. 'preview' returns untextured geometry only, 'full' returns textured model (preview + refine).
  Possible values: `preview`, `full`
- **`art_style`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"realistic"` вҖ” Desired art style of the object. Note: only 'realistic' is supported for Meshy-6 (latest model); 'sculpture' will be rejected with a 422. For older models, 'sculpture' is supported but enable_pbr should be false when using it.
  Possible values: `realistic`, `sculpture`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for reproducible results. Same prompt and seed usually generate the same result.
- **`topology`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"triangle"` вҖ” Specify the topology of the generated model. Quad for smooth surfaces, Triangle for detailed geometry.
  Possible values: `quad`, `triangle`
- **`target_polycount`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30000` вҖ” Target number of polygons in the generated model
  Range: min: 100, max: 300000
- **`should_remesh`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the remesh phase. When false, returns unprocessed triangular mesh.
- **`symmetry_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Controls symmetry behavior during model generation.
  Possible values: `off`, `auto`, `on`
- **`enable_pbr`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Generate PBR Maps (metallic, roughness, normal) in addition to base color. Should be false for sculpture style.
- **`is_a_t_pose`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Deprecated: use pose_mode instead. When true, generates a T-pose model.
- **`pose_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Pose mode for the generated model. 'a-pose' generates an A-pose, 't-pose' generates a T-pose, empty string for no specific pose.
  Possible values: `a-pose`, `t-pose`, ``
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion. This will use a large language model to expand the prompt with additional details while maintaining the original meaning.
- **`texture_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Additional text prompt to guide the texturing process (only used in 'full' mode)
- **`texture_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” 2D image to guide the texturing process (only used in 'full' mode)
- **`enable_rigging`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Automatically rig the generated model as a humanoid character. Includes basic walking and running animations. Best results with humanoid characters that have clearly defined limbs.
- **`rigging_height_meters`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.7` вҖ” Approximate height of the character in meters. Only used when enable_rigging is true.
- **`enable_animation`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Apply an animation preset to the rigged model. Requires enable_rigging to be true.
- **`animation_action_id`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1001` вҖ” Animation preset ID from Meshy's library (500+ presets). Only used when enable_animation is true. See https://docs.meshy.ai/en/api/animation-library for available action IDs.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, input data will be checked for safety before processing.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `TextTo3DOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation
- **`rigged_character_fbx`** *(?)* вҖ” Rigged character in FBX format. Only present when enable_rigging is true.
- **`animation_fbx`** *(?)* вҖ” Animated 3D model in FBX format. Only present when enable_animation is true.
- **`animation_glb`** *(?)* вҖ” Animated 3D model in GLB format. Only present when enable_animation is true.
- **`rigged_character_glb`** *(?)* вҖ” Rigged character in GLB format. Only present when enable_rigging is true.
- **`model_urls`** *()* вҖ” URLs for different 3D model formats
- **`actual_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The actual prompt used if prompt expansion was enabled
- **`model_glb`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D object in GLB format.
- **`rig_task_id`** *(string)* вҖ” Rigging task ID. Only present when enable_rigging is true.
- **`basic_animations`** *(?)* вҖ” Basic walking and running animations. Only present when enable_rigging is true.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt used for generation
- **`texture_urls`** *(list<TextureFiles>)* вҖ” Array of texture file objects
- **`thumbnail`** *(?)* вҖ” Preview thumbnail of the generated model

## tripo3d/h3.1/text-to-3d

**Title:** Tripo H3.1 Text to 3D
**Description:** Generate 3D models from text descriptions using Tripo H3.1.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/tripo3d/h3.1/text-to-3d`
**Category:** text-to-3d
**Tags:** 3d, text-to-3d, 3d-generation, tripo

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FullTextTo3DInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text description of the 3D object to generate. Maximum 1024 characters.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text describing features to avoid in the generated model.
- **`face_limit`** *(integer)* вҖ” Target number of faces for the generated mesh. If not set, the model adaptively determines the count.
- **`texture`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate textures for the model.
- **`pbr`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate PBR (Physically Based Rendering) materials. If true, texture is also enabled.
- **`model_seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for geometry generation reproducibility.
- **`image_seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for the text-to-image step.
- **`texture_seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for texture generation reproducibility.
- **`texture_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"standard"` вҖ” Quality level for textures. 'detailed' produces higher-resolution textures.
  Possible values: `standard`, `detailed`
- **`geometry_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"standard"` вҖ” Quality level for geometry.
  Possible values: `standard`, `detailed`
- **`auto_size`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Auto-scale the model to real-world dimensions (meters).
- **`quad`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Generate quad (4-sided) mesh topology instead of triangles.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Tripo3DOutput`
- **`model_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D model file in GLB format.
- **`model_urls`** *()* вҖ” URLs for different 3D model variants.
- **`rendered_image`** *(?)* вҖ” Preview render of the generated 3D model.

---

## tripo3d/p1/text-to-3d

**Title:** Tripo P1 Text to 3D
**Description:** Generate 3D models from text descriptions using Tripo P1.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/tripo3d/p1/text-to-3d`
**Category:** text-to-3d
**Tags:** 3d, text-to-3d, 3d-generation, tripo

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `P1TextTo3DInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text description of the 3D object to generate. Maximum 1024 characters.
- **`face_limit`** *(integer)* вҖ” Target number of faces for the generated mesh. If not set, the model adaptively determines the count.
- **`texture`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate textures for the model.
- **`model_seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for geometry generation reproducibility.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Tripo3DOutput`
- **`model_mesh`** *()* вҖ” Generated 3D model file in GLB format.
- **`model_urls`** *()* вҖ” URLs for different 3D model variants.
- **`rendered_image`** *(?)* вҖ” Preview render of the generated 3D model.

## CassetteAI/music-generator

**Title:** music generator
**Description:** CassetteAIвҖҷs model generates a 30-second sample in under 2 seconds and a full 3-minute track in under 10 seconds. At 44.1 kHz stereo audio, expect a level of professional consistency with no breaks, no squeaks, and no random interruptions in your creations.


**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/CassetteAI/music-generator`
**Category:** text-to-audio
**Tags:** music, cassetteai

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Input`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `duration`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate music from.
- **`duration`** *(integer)* вҖ” The duration of the generated music in seconds.
  Range: min: 10, max: 180

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `AudioOutput`
- **`audio_file`** *()* вҖ” The generated music

## fal-ai/ace-step

**Title:** ACE-Step
**Description:** Generate music with lyrics from text using ACE-Step
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ace-step`
**Category:** text-to-audio
**Tags:** text-to-audio, text-to-music

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ACEStepTextToAudioRequest`
**Required fields:** `tags`

- **`tags`** *(string)* вҖ” Comma-separated list of genre tags to control the style of the generated audio.
- **`lyrics`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Lyrics to be sung in the audio. If not provided or if [inst] or [instrumental] is the content of this field, no lyrics will be sung. Use control structures like [verse], [chorus] and [bridge] to control the structure of the song.
- **`duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `60` вҖ” The duration of the generated audio in seconds.
  Range: min: 5, max: 240
- **`number_of_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `27` вҖ” Number of steps to generate the audio.
  Range: min: 3, max: 60
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If not provided, a random seed will be used.
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"euler"` вҖ” Scheduler to use for the generation process.
  Possible values: `euler`, `heun`
- **`guidance_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"apg"` вҖ” Type of CFG to use for the generation process.
  Possible values: `cfg`, `apg`, `cfg_star`
- **`granularity_scale`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `10` вҖ” Granularity scale for the generation process. Higher values can reduce artifacts.
  Range: min: -100, max: 100
- **`guidance_interval`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Guidance interval for the generation. 0.5 means only apply guidance in the middle steps (0.25 * infer_steps to 0.75 * infer_steps)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`guidance_interval_decay`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Guidance interval decay for the generation. Guidance scale will decay from guidance_scale to min_guidance_scale in the interval. 0.0 means no decay.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” Guidance scale for the generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 200
- **`minimum_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Minimum guidance scale for the generation after the decay.
  Range: min: 0, max: 200
- **`tag_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Tag guidance scale for the generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`lyric_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.5` вҖ” Lyric guidance scale for the generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ACEStepResponse`
- **`lyrics`** *(string)* вҖ” The lyrics used in the generation process.
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The random seed used for the generation process.
- **`tags`** *(string)* вҖ” The genre tags used in the generation process.

---

## fal-ai/ace-step/prompt-to-audio

**Title:** ACE-Step
**Description:** Generate music from a simple prompt using ACE-Step
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ace-step/prompt-to-audio`
**Category:** text-to-audio
**Tags:** text-to-audio, text-to-music

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ACEStepPromptToAudioRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Prompt to control the style of the generated audio. This will be used to generate tags and lyrics.
- **`instrumental`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to generate an instrumental version of the audio.
- **`duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `60` вҖ” The duration of the generated audio in seconds.
  Range: min: 5, max: 240
- **`number_of_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `27` вҖ” Number of steps to generate the audio.
  Range: min: 3, max: 60
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If not provided, a random seed will be used.
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"euler"` вҖ” Scheduler to use for the generation process.
  Possible values: `euler`, `heun`
- **`guidance_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"apg"` вҖ” Type of CFG to use for the generation process.
  Possible values: `cfg`, `apg`, `cfg_star`
- **`granularity_scale`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `10` вҖ” Granularity scale for the generation process. Higher values can reduce artifacts.
  Range: min: -100, max: 100
- **`guidance_interval`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Guidance interval for the generation. 0.5 means only apply guidance in the middle steps (0.25 * infer_steps to 0.75 * infer_steps)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`guidance_interval_decay`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Guidance interval decay for the generation. Guidance scale will decay from guidance_scale to min_guidance_scale in the interval. 0.0 means no decay.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” Guidance scale for the generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 200
- **`minimum_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Minimum guidance scale for the generation after the decay.
  Range: min: 0, max: 200
- **`tag_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Tag guidance scale for the generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`lyric_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.5` вҖ” Lyric guidance scale for the generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ACEStepResponse`
- **`lyrics`** *(string)* вҖ” The lyrics used in the generation process.
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The random seed used for the generation process.
- **`tags`** *(string)* вҖ” The genre tags used in the generation process.

## fal-ai/csm-1b

- **`context`** *(list<Speaker>)* вҖ” The context to generate an audio from.

## fal-ai/diffrhythm

**Title:** DiffRhythm: Lyrics to Song
**Description:** DiffRhythm is a blazing fast model for transforming lyrics into full songs. It boasts the capability to generate full songs in less than 30 seconds.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/diffrhythm`
**Category:** text-to-audio
**Tags:** music

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToMusicInput`
**Required fields:** `lyrics`

- **`lyrics`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the song from. Must have two sections. Sections start with either [chorus] or a [verse].
- **`reference_audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the reference audio to use for the music generation.
- **`style_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The style prompt to use for the music generation.
- **`music_duration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"95s"` вҖ” The duration of the music to generate.
  Possible values: `95s`, `285s`
- **`cfg_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” The CFG strength to use for the music generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"euler"` вҖ” The scheduler to use for the music generation.
  Possible values: `euler`, `midpoint`, `rk4`, `implicit_adams`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `32` вҖ” The number of inference steps to use for the music generation.
  Range: min: 10, max: 100

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” Generated music file.

---

## fal-ai/elevenlabs/music

**Title:** Elevenlabs Music
**Description:** Generate high quality, realistic music with fine controls using Elevenlabs Music!
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/elevenlabs/music`
**Category:** text-to-audio
**Tags:** music, text-to-music

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MusicRequest`
**Required fields:** _none_

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt describing the music to generate
- **`composition_plan`** *(?)* вҖ” The composition plan for the music
- **`music_length_ms`** *(integer)* вҖ” The length of the song to generate in milliseconds. Used only in conjunction with prompt. Must be between 3000ms and 600000ms. Optional - if not provided, the model will choose a length based on the prompt.
- **`force_instrumental`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, guarantees that the generated song will be instrumental. If false, the song may or may not be instrumental depending on the prompt. Can only be used with prompt.
- **`respect_sections_durations`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Controls how strictly section durations in the composition_plan are enforced. It will only have an effect if it is used with composition_plan. When set to true, the model will precisely respect each section's duration_ms from the plan. When set to false, the model may adjust individual section durations which will generally lead to better generation quality and improved latency, while always preserving the total song duration from the plan.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"mp3_44100_128"` вҖ” Output format of the generated audio. Formatted as codec_sample_rate_bitrate. So an mp3 with 22.05kHz sample rate at 32kbs is represented as mp3_22050_32. MP3 with 192kbps bitrate requires you to be subscribed to Creator tier or above. PCM with 44.1kHz sample rate requires you to be subscribed to Pro tier or above. Note that the Ој-law format (sometimes written mu-law, often approximated as u-law) is commonly used for Twilio audio inputs.
  Possible values: `mp3_22050_32`, `mp3_44100_32`, `mp3_44100_64`, `mp3_44100_96`, `mp3_44100_128`, `mp3_44100_192`, `pcm_8000`, `pcm_16000`, `pcm_22050`, `pcm_24000`, `pcm_44100`, `pcm_48000`, `ulaw_8000`, `alaw_8000`, `opus_48000_32`, `opus_48000_64`, `opus_48000_96`, `opus_48000_128`, `opus_48000_192`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MusicOutput`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated music audio file in MP3 format

---

## fal-ai/elevenlabs/sound-effects/v2

**Title:** Elevenlabs
**Description:** Generate sound effects using ElevenLabs advanced sound effects model.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/elevenlabs/sound-effects/v2`
**Category:** text-to-audio
**Tags:** sound

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SoundEffectRequestV2`
**Required fields:** `text`

- **`text`** *(string)* вҖ” The text describing the sound effect to generate
- **`duration_seconds`** *(number)* вҖ” Duration in seconds (0.5-22). If None, optimal duration will be determined from prompt.
- **`prompt_influence`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.3` вҖ” How closely to follow the prompt (0-1). Higher values mean less variation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"mp3_44100_128"` вҖ” Output format of the generated audio. Formatted as codec_sample_rate_bitrate.
  Possible values: `mp3_22050_32`, `mp3_44100_32`, `mp3_44100_64`, `mp3_44100_96`, `mp3_44100_128`, `mp3_44100_192`, `pcm_8000`, `pcm_16000`, `pcm_22050`, `pcm_24000`, `pcm_44100`, `pcm_48000`, `ulaw_8000`, `alaw_8000`, `opus_48000_32`, `opus_48000_64`, `opus_48000_96`, `opus_48000_128`, `opus_48000_192`
- **`loop`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to create a sound effect that loops smoothly.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SoundEffectOutput`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated sound effect audio file in MP3 format

---

## fal-ai/elevenlabs/text-to-dialogue/eleven-v3

**Title:** Elevenlabs
**Description:** Generate realistic audio dialogues using Eleven-v3 from ElevenLabs.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/elevenlabs/text-to-dialogue/eleven-v3`
**Category:** text-to-audio
**Tags:** audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToDialogueRequest`
**Required fields:** `inputs`

- **`inputs`** *(list<DialogueBlock>)* вҖ” A list of dialogue inputs, each containing text and a voice ID which will be converted into speech.
- **`stability`** *(number)* вҖ” Determines how stable the voice is and the randomness between each generation. Lower values introduce broader emotional range for the voice. Higher values can result in a monotonous voice with limited emotion. Must be one of 0.0, 0.5, 1.0, else it will be rounded to the nearest value.
- **`use_speaker_boost`** *(boolean)* вҖ” This setting boosts the similarity to the original speaker. Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.
- **`pronunciation_dictionary_locators`** *(list<PronunciationDictionaryLocator>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” A list of pronunciation dictionary locators (id, version_id) to be applied to the text. They will be applied in order. You may have up to 3 locators per request
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.
- **`language_code`** *(string)* вҖ” Language code (ISO 639-1) used to enforce a language for the model. An error will be returned if language code is not supported by the model.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `TextToDialogueOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio file

---

## fal-ai/elevenlabs/tts/eleven-v3

**Title:** Elevenlabs
**Description:** Generate text-to-speech audio using Eleven-v3 from ElevenLabs.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/elevenlabs/tts/eleven-v3`
**Category:** text-to-audio
**Tags:** audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToSpeechRequestV3`
**Required fields:** `text`

- **`text`** *(string)* вҖ” The text to convert to speech
- **`voice`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"Rachel"` вҖ” The voice to use for speech generation
- **`stability`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Voice stability (0-1)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`timestamps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to return timestamps for each word in the generated speech
- **`language_code`** *(string)* вҖ” Language code (ISO 639-1) used to enforce a language for the model.
- **`apply_text_normalization`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” This parameter controls text normalization with three modes: 'auto', 'on', and 'off'. When set to 'auto', the system will automatically decide whether to apply text normalization (e.g., spelling out numbers). With 'on', text normalization will always be applied, while with 'off', it will be skipped.
  Possible values: `auto`, `on`, `off`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `TTSOutput`
- **`timestamps`** *(array)* вҖ” Timestamps for each word in the generated speech. Only returned if `timestamps` is set to True in the request.
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio file

---

## fal-ai/elevenlabs/tts/multilingual-v2

**Title:** ElevenLabs TTS Multilingual v2
**Description:** Generate multilingual text-to-speech audio using ElevenLabs TTS Multilingual v2.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/elevenlabs/tts/multilingual-v2`
**Category:** text-to-audio
**Tags:** audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToSpeechRequest`
**Required fields:** `text`

- **`text`** *(string)* вҖ” The text to convert to speech
- **`voice`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"Rachel"` вҖ” The voice to use for speech generation
- **`stability`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Voice stability (0-1)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`similarity_boost`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.75` вҖ” Similarity boost (0-1)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`style`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Style exaggeration (0-1)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`speed`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Speech speed (0.7-1.2). Values below 1.0 slow down the speech, above 1.0 speed it up. Extreme values may affect quality.
  Range: min: 0.7, max: 1.2
- **`timestamps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to return timestamps for each word in the generated speech
- **`previous_text`** *(string)* вҖ” The text that came before the text of the current request. Can be used to improve the speech's continuity when concatenating together multiple generations or to influence the speech's continuity in the current generation.
- **`next_text`** *(string)* вҖ” The text that comes after the text of the current request. Can be used to improve the speech's continuity when concatenating together multiple generations or to influence the speech's continuity in the current generation.
- **`language_code`** *(string)* вҖ” Language code (ISO 639-1) used to enforce a language for the model. An error will be returned if language code is not supported by the model.
- **`apply_text_normalization`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” This parameter controls text normalization with three modes: 'auto', 'on', and 'off'. When set to 'auto', the system will automatically decide whether to apply text normalization (e.g., spelling out numbers). With 'on', text normalization will always be applied, while with 'off', it will be skipped.
  Possible values: `auto`, `on`, `off`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `TTSOutput`
- **`timestamps`** *(array)* вҖ” Timestamps for each word in the generated speech. Only returned if `timestamps` is set to True in the request.
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio file

---

## fal-ai/f5-tts

**Title:** F5 TTS
**Description:** F5 TTS
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/f5-tts`
**Category:** text-to-audio
**Tags:** speech

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TTSInput`
**Required fields:** `gen_text`, `ref_audio_url`, `model_type`

- **`gen_text`** *(string)* вҖ” The text to be converted to speech. Maximum 5000 characters.
- **`ref_audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the reference audio file.
- **`ref_text`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The reference text to be used for TTS. If not provided, an ASR (Automatic Speech Recognition) model will be used to generate the reference text.
- **`model_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” The name of the model to be used for TTS.
  Possible values: `F5-TTS`, `E2-TTS`
- **`remove_silence`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to remove the silence from the audio file.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `TTSOutput`
- **`audio_url`** *()* вҖ” The audio file containing the generated speech.

---

## fal-ai/gemini-tts

**Title:** Gemini TTS
**Description:** Use Gemini TTS Models to convert your prompts to real audio.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/gemini-tts`
**Category:** text-to-audio
**Tags:** text-to-speech, audio, gemini

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `GeminiTTSInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text to convert to speech. Gemini TTS supports natural-language prompting for style, pace, accent, and emotional expression вҖ” include delivery instructions inline with the text (e.g. 'Say cheerfully: Have a wonderful day!'). For multi-speaker synthesis, prefix lines with speaker aliases defined in the speakers field (e.g. 'Alice: Hello!\nBob: Hi!'). Supports inline pace/style markers like [slowly], [whispering], [excited], [extremely fast].
- **`style_instructions`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional style and delivery instructions prepended to the prompt. Controls expressiveness, accent, pace, tone, and emotional expression using natural language. Use this to separate style control from the text content. Examples: 'Speak warmly and slowly', 'Read this as a dramatic newscast', 'Use a British accent with a cheerful tone', 'Whisper mysteriously'.
- **`voice`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"Kore"` вҖ” Voice preset for single-speaker synthesis. 30 distinct voices are available. Ignored when speakers is set. Popular choices: Kore (strong, firm female), Puck (upbeat, lively male), Charon (calm, professional male), Zephyr (bright, clear female), Aoede (warm, melodic female).
  Possible values: `Achernar`, `Achird`, `Algenib`, `Algieba`, `Alnilam`, `Aoede`, `Autonoe`, `Callirrhoe`, `Charon`, `Despina`, `Enceladus`, `Erinome`, `Fenrir`, `Gacrux`, `Iapetus`, `Kore`, `Laomedeia`, `Leda`, `Orus`, `Pulcherrima`, `Puck`, `Rasalgethi`, `Sadachbia`, `Sadaltager`, `Schedar`, `Sulafat`, `Umbriel`, `Vindemiatrix`, `Zephyr`, `Zubenelgenubi`
- **`model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"gemini-2.5-flash-tts"` вҖ” Which Gemini TTS model to use. gemini-2.5-flash-tts: low latency, cost-efficient for everyday applications (recommended). gemini-2.5-pro-tts: highest quality, best for structured workflows like podcasts, audiobooks, and customer support.
  Possible values: `gemini-2.5-flash-tts`, `gemini-2.5-pro-tts`
- **`language_code`** *(string)* вҖ” Language for multilingual synthesis. When set, steers the model to speak in the specified language. Supports 24 GA languages and 60+ Preview languages. If not set, the model auto-detects the language from the text.
  Possible values: `Arabic (Egypt)`, `Bangla (Bangladesh)`, `Dutch (Netherlands)`, `English (India)`, `English (US)`, `French (France)`, `German (Germany)`, `Hindi (India)`, `Indonesian (Indonesia)`, `Italian (Italy)`, `Japanese (Japan)`, `Korean (South Korea)`, `Marathi (India)`, `Polish (Poland)`, `Portuguese (Brazil)`, `Romanian (Romania)`, `Russian (Russia)`, `Spanish (Spain)`, `Tamil (India)`, `Telugu (India)`, `Thai (Thailand)`, `Turkish (Turkey)`, `Ukrainian (Ukraine)`, `Vietnamese (Vietnam)`, `Afrikaans (South Africa)`, `Albanian (Albania)`, `Amharic (Ethiopia)`, `Arabic (World)`, `Armenian (Armenia)`, `Azerbaijani (Azerbaijan)`, `Basque (Spain)`, `Belarusian (Belarus)`, `Bulgarian (Bulgaria)`, `Burmese (Myanmar)`, `Catalan (Spain)`, `Cebuano (Philippines)`, `Chinese Mandarin (China)`, `Chinese Mandarin (Taiwan)`, `Croatian (Croatia)`, `Czech (Czech Republic)`, `Danish (Denmark)`, `English (Australia)`, `English (UK)`, `Estonian (Estonia)`, `Filipino (Philippines)`, `Finnish (Finland)`, `French (Canada)`, `Galician (Spain)`, `Georgian (Georgia)`, `Greek (Greece)`, `Gujarati (India)`, `Haitian Creole (Haiti)`, `Hebrew (Israel)`, `Hungarian (Hungary)`, `Icelandic (Iceland)`, `Javanese (Java)`, `Kannada (India)`, `Konkani (India)`, `Lao (Laos)`, `Latin (Vatican City)`, `Latvian (Latvia)`, `Lithuanian (Lithuania)`, `Luxembourgish (Luxembourg)`, `Macedonian (North Macedonia)`, `Maithili (India)`, `Malagasy (Madagascar)`, `Malay (Malaysia)`, `Malayalam (India)`, `Mongolian (Mongolia)`, `Nepali (Nepal)`, `Norwegian Bokmal (Norway)`, `Norwegian Nynorsk (Norway)`, `Odia (India)`, `Pashto (Afghanistan)`, `Persian (Iran)`, `Portuguese (Portugal)`, `Punjabi (India)`, `Serbian (Serbia)`, `Sindhi (India)`, `Sinhala (Sri Lanka)`, `Slovak (Slovakia)`, `Slovenian (Slovenia)`, `Spanish (Latin America)`, `Spanish (Mexico)`, `Swahili (Kenya)`, `Swedish (Sweden)`, `Urdu (Pakistan)`
- **`speakers`** *(array)* вҖ” Multi-speaker voice configuration. When set, enables multi-speaker synthesis where different parts of the text are spoken by different voices. Each speaker needs a voice and a speaker_id (alias) that matches prefixes in the prompt. Requires gemini-2.5-pro-tts or gemini-2.5-flash-tts model. Not supported with gemini-2.5-flash-lite-preview-tts.
- **`temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Controls the randomness of the speech output. Higher values produce more creative and varied delivery, while lower values make the output more predictable and focused.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"mp3"` вҖ” Audio output format. mp3: compressed, small file size (recommended). wav: uncompressed PCM wrapped in WAV (24 kHz, 16-bit mono). ogg_opus: Ogg container with Opus codec, good quality-to-size ratio.
  Possible values: `wav`, `mp3`, `ogg_opus`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `GeminiTTSOutput`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio file.

---

## fal-ai/minimax-music/v2.5

**Title:** Minimax Music 2.5
**Description:** MiniMax Music 2.5 creates complete tracks with singing, backing music, and detailed arrangements from lyrics and a style description.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/minimax-music/v2.5`
**Category:** text-to-audio
**Tags:** stylized, transform, lipsync

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToMusic25PlusRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” A description of the music style, mood, genre, and scenario. Max 2000 characters.
- **`lyrics`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Lyrics of the song. Use \n to separate lines. Supports structure tags: [Intro], [Verse], [Pre Chorus], [Chorus], [Bridge], [Outro], [Interlude], [Hook], [Break], [Solo], [Inst]. Max 3500 characters. Required when is_instrumental is false and lyrics_optimizer is false.
- **`is_instrumental`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” When true, generates vocal-free instrumental music.
- **`lyrics_optimizer`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” When true and lyrics is empty, auto-generates lyrics from the prompt.
- **`audio_setting`** *()* вҖ” Audio configuration settings

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MusicV25Output`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated music

---

## fal-ai/minimax-music/v2.6

**Title:** Minimax Music 2.6
**Description:** MiniMax Music 2.6 creates complete tracks with singing, backing music, and detailed arrangements from lyrics and a style description.

**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`lyrics`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Lyrics of the song. Use \n to separate lines. Supports structure tags: [Intro], [Verse], [Pre Chorus], [Chorus], [Post Chorus], [Hook], [Bridge], [Interlude], [Transition], [Build Up], [Break], [Inst], [Solo], [Outro]. Max 3500 characters. Required when is_instrumental is false.
- **`lyrics_optimizer`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” When true and lyrics is empty, auto-generates lyrics from the prompt.
- **`is_instrumental`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” When true, generates vocal-free instrumental music.
- **`audio_setting`** *()* вҖ” Audio configuration settings

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MusicV26Output`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated music

## fal-ai/mmaudio-v2/text-to-audio

**Title:** MMAudio V2 Text to Audio
**Description:** MMAudio generates synchronized audio given text inputs. It can generate sounds described by a prompt.

**Category:** text-to-audio
**Tags:** audio, fast

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `AudioInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the audio for.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the audio for.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator
- **`num_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The number of steps to generate the audio for.
  Range: min: 4, max: 50
- **`duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” The duration of the audio to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 30
- **`cfg_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4.5` вҖ” The strength of Classifier Free Guidance.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`mask_away_clip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to mask away the clip.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `AudioOutput`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio.

## fal-ai/stable-audio-25/text-to-audio

## sonauto/v2/inpaint

**Title:** Sonauto V2
**Description:** Replace sections of an existing audio with newly generated content
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/sonauto/v2/inpaint`
**Category:** text-to-audio
**Tags:** music, text-to-music, text-to-audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `InpaintInput`
**Required fields:** `lyrics_prompt`, `audio_url`, `sections`

- **`tags`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” Tags/styles of the music to generate. You can view a list of all available tags at https://sonauto.ai/tag-explorer.
- **`lyrics_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The lyrics sung in the generated song. An empty string will generate an instrumental track.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed to use for generation. Will pick a random seed if not provided. Repeating a request with identical parameters (must use lyrics and tags, not prompt) and the same seed will generate the same song.
- **`prompt_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Controls how strongly your prompt influences the output. Greater values adhere more to the prompt but sound less natural. (This is CFG.)
  Range: min: 1.4, max: 3.1
- **`balance_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” Greater means more natural vocals. Lower means sharper instrumentals. We recommend 0.7.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`num_songs`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Generating 2 songs costs 1.5x the price of generating 1 song. Also, note that using the same seed may not result in identical songs if the number of songs generated is changed.
  Range: min: 1, max: 2
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"wav"`
  Possible values: `flac`, `mp3`, `wav`, `ogg`, `m4a`
- **`output_bit_rate`** *(integer)* вҖ” The bit rate to use for mp3 and m4a formats. Not available for other formats.
  Possible values: `128`, `192`, `256`, `320`
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the audio file to alter. Must be a valid publicly accessible URL.
- **`sections`** *(list<InpaintSection>)* вҖ” List of sections to inpaint. Currently, only one section is supported so the list length must be 1.
- **`selection_crop`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Crop to the selected region

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `InpaintOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation. This can be used to generate an identical song by passing the same parameters with this seed in a future request.
- **`audio`** *(list<File>)* вҖ” The generated audio files.

---

## sonauto/v2/text-to-music

**Title:** Sonauto V2
**Description:** Create full songs in any style
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/sonauto/v2/text-to-music`
**Category:** text-to-audio
**Tags:** music, text-to-music, text-to-audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `GenerateInput`
**Required fields:** _none_

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” A description of the track you want to generate. This prompt will be used to automatically generate the tags and lyrics unless you manually set them. For example, if you set prompt and tags, then the prompt will be used to generate only the lyrics.
- **`tags`** *(array)* вҖ” Tags/styles of the music to generate. You can view a list of all available tags at https://sonauto.ai/tag-explorer.
- **`lyrics_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The lyrics sung in the generated song. An empty string will generate an instrumental track.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed to use for generation. Will pick a random seed if not provided. Repeating a request with identical parameters (must use lyrics and tags, not prompt) and the same seed will generate the same song.
- **`prompt_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Controls how strongly your prompt influences the output. Greater values adhere more to the prompt but sound less natural. (This is CFG.)
  Range: min: 1.4, max: 3.1
- **`balance_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” Greater means more natural vocals. Lower means sharper instrumentals. We recommend 0.7.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`num_songs`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Generating 2 songs costs 1.5x the price of generating 1 song. Also, note that using the same seed may not result in identical songs if the number of songs generated is changed.
  Range: min: 1, max: 2
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"wav"`
  Possible values: `flac`, `mp3`, `wav`, `ogg`, `m4a`
- **`output_bit_rate`** *(integer)* вҖ” The bit rate to use for mp3 and m4a formats. Not available for other formats.
  Possible values: `128`, `192`, `256`, `320`
- **`bpm`** *(integer | string)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The beats per minute of the song. This can be set to an integer or the literal string "auto" to pick a suitable bpm based on the tags. Set bpm to null to not condition the model on bpm information.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `GenerateOutput`
- **`tags`** *(array)* вҖ” The style tags used for generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation. This can be used to generate an identical song by passing the same parameters with this seed in a future request.
- **`lyrics`** *(string)* вҖ” The lyrics used for generation.
- **`audio`** *(list<File>)* вҖ” The generated audio files.

---

## bria/fibo-bbq-preview/generate

**Title:** Fibo Bbq Preview
**Description:** A preview to the next level of control of Text-to-Image models.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/bria/fibo-bbq-preview/generate`
**Category:** text-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `GaiaInputModel`
**Required fields:** _none_

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Prompt for image generation.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1:1"` вҖ” Aspect ratio. Options: 1:1, 2:3, 3:2, 3:4, 4:3, 4:5, 5:4, 9:16, 16:9
  Possible values: `1:1`, `2:3`, `3:2`, `3:4`, `4:3`, `4:5`, `5:4`, `9:16`, `16:9`
- **`structured_prompt`** *(? | string | object)* вҖ” The structured prompt to generate an image from.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Reference image (file or URL).
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `5555` вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.
- **`steps_num`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” Number of inference steps.
  Range: min: 20, max: 50
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Negative prompt for image generation.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Guidance scale for text.
  Range: min: 3, max: 5
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, returns the image directly in the response (increases latency).

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `GaiaOutputModel`
- **`images`** *(list<object>)* вҖ” Generated images.
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” Generated image.
- **`structured_prompt`** *(object)* вҖ” Current prompt.

- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed to use for generating images
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” Classifier free guidance scale
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” The number of inference steps to take
  Range: min: 20, max: 50

## fal-ai/bitdance

**Title:** Bitdance
**Description:** Image generation with BitDance. Fast, high-resolution photorealistic images using an autoregressive LLMвҖ” for efficient, high-quality results.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bitdance`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** text-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Input`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt for image generation.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” The size of the generated image. Will be snapped to the nearest supported resolution.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” Number of diffusion sampling steps per decoding step. Higher values (e.g. 50) improve quality at the cost of speed.
  Range: min: 10, max: 100
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale. Higher values follow the prompt more closely.
  Range: min: 1, max: 15
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. The same seed and prompt will produce the same image.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the media will be returned as a data URI.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed of the generated image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.

---

## fal-ai/bria/text-to-image/base

**Title:** Bria Text-to-Image Base
**Description:** Bria's Text-to-Image model, trained exclusively on licensed data for safe and risk-free commercial use. Available also as source code and weights. For access to weights: https://bria.ai/contact-us
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bria/text-to-image/base`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** image generation

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToImageRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt you would like to use to generate images.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The negative prompt you would like to use to generate images.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” How many images you would like to generate. When using any Guidance Method, Value is set to 1.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1:1"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the image. When a guidance method is being used, the aspect ratio is defined by the guidance image and this parameter is ignored.
  Possible values: `1:1`, `2:3`, `3:2`, `3:4`, `4:3`, `4:5`, `5:4`, `9:16`, `16:9`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” The number of iterations the model goes through to refine the generated image. This parameter is optional.
  Range: min: 20, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`prompt_enhancement`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” When set to true, enhances the provided prompt by generating additional, more descriptive variations, resulting in more diverse and creative output images.
- **`medium`** *(enum)* вҖ” Which medium should be included in your generated images. This parameter is optional.
  Possible values: `photography`, `art`
- **`guidance`** *(list<GuidanceInput>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Guidance images to use for the generation. Up to 4 guidance methods can be combined during a single inference.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed value used for generation.

---

## fal-ai/bria/text-to-image/fast

**Title:** Bria Text-to-Image Fast
**Description:** Bria's Text-to-Image model with perfect harmony of latency and quality. Trained exclusively on licensed data for safe and risk-free commercial use. Available also as source code and weights. For access to weights: https://bria.ai/contact-us
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bria/text-to-image/fast`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** image generation

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FastTextToImageRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt you would like to use to generate images.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The negative prompt you would like to use to generate images.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” How many images you would like to generate. When using any Guidance Method, Value is set to 1.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1:1"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the image. When a guidance method is being used, the aspect ratio is defined by the guidance image and this parameter is ignored.
  Possible values: `1:1`, `2:3`, `3:2`, `3:4`, `4:3`, `4:5`, `5:4`, `9:16`, `16:9`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” The number of iterations the model goes through to refine the generated image. This parameter is optional.
  Range: min: 4, max: 10
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`prompt_enhancement`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” When set to true, enhances the provided prompt by generating additional, more descriptive variations, resulting in more diverse and creative output images.
- **`medium`** *(enum)* вҖ” Which medium should be included in your generated images. This parameter is optional.
  Possible values: `photography`, `art`
- **`guidance`** *(list<GuidanceInput>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Guidance images to use for the generation. Up to 4 guidance methods can be combined during a single inference.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed value used for generation.

---

## fal-ai/bria/text-to-image/hd

**Title:** Bria Text-to-Image HD
**Description:** Bria's Text-to-Image model for HD images. Trained exclusively on licensed data for safe and risk-free commercial use. Available also as source code and weights. For access to weights: https://bria.ai/contact-us
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bria/text-to-image/hd`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** image generation

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToImageRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt you would like to use to generate images.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The negative prompt you would like to use to generate images.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” How many images you would like to generate. When using any Guidance Method, Value is set to 1.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1:1"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the image. When a guidance method is being used, the aspect ratio is defined by the guidance image and this parameter is ignored.
  Possible values: `1:1`, `2:3`, `3:2`, `3:4`, `4:3`, `4:5`, `5:4`, `9:16`, `16:9`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” The number of iterations the model goes through to refine the generated image. This parameter is optional.
  Range: min: 20, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`prompt_enhancement`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” When set to true, enhances the provided prompt by generating additional, more descriptive variations, resulting in more diverse and creative output images.
- **`medium`** *(enum)* вҖ” Which medium should be included in your generated images. This parameter is optional.
  Possible values: `photography`, `art`
- **`guidance`** *(list<GuidanceInput>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Guidance images to use for the generation. Up to 4 guidance methods can be combined during a single inference.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed value used for generation.

---

## fal-ai/bytedance/dreamina/v3.1/text-to-image

**Title:** Bytedance
**Description:** Dreamina showcases superior picture effects, with significant improvements in picture aesthetics, precise and diverse styles, and rich details.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bytedance/dreamina/v3.1/text-to-image`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** text-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `DreaminaInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt used to generate the image
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `{"width":2048,"height":1536}` вҖ” The size of the generated image. Width and height must be between 512 and 2048.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`enhance_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to use an LLM to enhance the prompt
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed to control the stochasticity of image generation.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `DreaminaOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed used for generation
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Generated images

## fal-ai/bytedance/seedream/v3/text-to-image

**Title:** Bytedance
**Description:** Seedream 3.0 is a bilingual (Chinese and English) text-to-image model that excels at text-to-image generation.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bytedance/seedream/v3/text-to-image`
**Category:** text-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SeedDreamInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt used to generate the image
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Use for finer control over the output image size. Will be used over aspect_ratio, if both are provided. Width and height must be between 512 and 2048.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2.5` вҖ” Controls how closely the output image aligns with the input prompt. Higher values mean stronger prompt correlation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed to control the stochasticity of image generation.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SeedDreamOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed used for generation
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Generated images

## fal-ai/bytedance/seedream/v4.5/text-to-image

**Title:** Bytedance
**Description:** A new-generation image creation model ByteDance, Seedream 4.5 integrates image generation and image editing capabilities into a single, unified architecture.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bytedance/seedream/v4.5/text-to-image`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** stylized, transform

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SeedDream45T2IInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt used to generate the image
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `{"width":2048,"height":2048}` вҖ” The size of the generated image. Width and height must be between 1920 and 4096, or total number of pixels must be between 2560*1440 and 4096*4096.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`, `auto_2K`, `auto_4K`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of separate model generations to be run with the prompt.
  Range: min: 1, max: 6
- **`max_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” If set to a number greater than one, enables multi-image generation. The model will potentially return up to `max_images` images every generation, and in total, `num_images` generations will be carried out. In total, the number of images generated will be between `num_images` and `max_images*num_images`.
  Range: min: 1, max: 6
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed to control the stochasticity of image generation.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SeedDream45T2IOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed used for generation
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Generated images

---

## fal-ai/bytedance/seedream/v4/text-to-image

**Title:** Bytedance Seedream v4
**Description:** A new-generation image creation model ByteDance, Seedream 4.0 integrates image generation and image editing capabilities into a single, unified architecture.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bytedance/seedream/v4/text-to-image`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** stylized, transform

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SeedDream4T2IInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt used to generate the image
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `{"width":2048,"height":2048}` вҖ” The size of the generated image. Total pixels must be between 960x960 and 4096x4096.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`, `auto`, `auto_2K`, `auto_4K`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of separate model generations to be run with the prompt.
  Range: min: 1, max: 6
- **`max_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” If set to a number greater than one, enables multi-image generation. The model will potentially return up to `max_images` images every generation, and in total, `num_images` generations will be carried out. In total, the number of images generated will be between `num_images` and `max_images*num_images`.
  Range: min: 1, max: 6
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed to control the stochasticity of image generation.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`enhance_prompt_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"standard"` вҖ” The mode to use for enhancing prompt enhancement. Standard mode provides higher quality results but takes longer to generate. Fast mode provides average quality results but takes less time to generate.
  Possible values: `standard`, `fast`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SeedDream4T2IOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed used for generation
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Generated images

**Description:** Text to Image endpoint for the fast Lite version of Seedream 5.0, supporting high quality intelligent text-to-image generation.

  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`, `auto_2K`, `auto_3K`
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of separate model generations to be run with the prompt.

The model will potentially return up to `max_images` images every generation, and in total, `num_images` generations will be carried out. In total, the number of images generated will be between `num_images` and `max_images*num_images`.
  Range: min: 1, max: 6
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.

- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Generated images

## fal-ai/cogview4

**Title:** CogView
**Description:** Generate high quality images from text prompts using CogView4. Longer text prompts will result in better quality images.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/cogview4`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** stylized

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"landscape_4_3"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ImageOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

---

## fal-ai/dreamo

**Title:** DreamO
**Description:** DreamO is an image customization framework designed to support a wide range of tasks while facilitating seamless integration of multiple conditions.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/dreamo`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** stylized, realism

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `DreamOInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`first_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of first reference image to use for generation.
- **`second_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of second reference image to use for generation.
- **`first_reference_task`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"ip"` вҖ” Task for first reference image (ip/id/style).
  Possible values: `ip`, `id`, `style`
- **`second_reference_task`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"ip"` вҖ” Task for second reference image (ip/id/style).
  Possible values: `ip`, `id`, `style`
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `12` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the function will wait for the image to be generated and uploaded
            before returning the response. This will increase the latency of the function but
            it allows you to get the image directly in the response without going through the CDN.
        
- **`ref_resolution`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `512` вҖ” Resolution for reference images.
  Range: min: 512, max: 1024
- **`true_cfg`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The weight of the CFG loss.
  Range: min: 1, max: 5
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `DreamOOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used to generate the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The URLs of the generated images.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        

---

## fal-ai/dreamshaper

**Title:** Dreamshaper
**Description:** Dreamshaper model.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/dreamshaper`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** stylized, diffusion

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `DreamshaperTextToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`model_name`** *(enum)* вҖ” The Dreamshaper model to use.
  Possible values: `Lykon/dreamshaper-xl-1-0`, `Lykon/dreamshaper-xl-v2-turbo`, `Lykon/dreamshaper-8`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"(worst quality, low quality, normal quality, lowres, low details, oversaturated, undersaturated, overexposed, underexposed, grayscale, bw, bad photo, bad photography, bad art:1.4), (watermark, signature, text font, username, error, logo, words, letters, digits, autograph, trademark, name:1.2), (blur, blurry, grainy), morbid, ugly, asymmetrical, mutated malformed, mutilated, poorly lit, bad shadow, draft, cropped, out of frame, cut off, censored, jpeg artifacts, out of focus, glitch, duplicate, (airbrushed, cartoon, anime, semi-realistic, cgi, render, blender, digital art, manga, amateur:1.3), (3D ,3D Game, 3D Game Scene, 3D Character:1.1), (bad hands, bad anatomy, bad body, bad face, bad teeth, bad arms, bad legs, deformities:1.3)"` вҖ” The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want in the image.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `{"height":1024,"width":1024}`
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `35` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 70
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The list of LoRA weights to use.
- **`embeddings`** *(list<Embedding>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The list of embeddings to use.
- **`expand_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the prompt will be expanded with additional prompts.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the function will wait for the image to be generated and uploaded
            before returning the response. This will increase the latency of the function but
            it allows you to get the image directly in the response without going through the CDN.
        
- **`format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`safety_checker_version`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"v1"` вҖ” The version of the safety checker to use. v1 is the default CompVis safety checker. v2 uses a custom ViT model.
  Possible values: `v1`, `v2`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.

## fal-ai/emu-3.5-image/text-to-image

**Title:** Emu 3.5 Image
**Description:** Generate images from text using Emu 3.5 Image
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/emu-3.5-image/text-to-image`
**Category:** text-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Emu35ImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to create the image.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” The resolution of the output image.
  Possible values: `480p`, `720p`
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1:1"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the output image.
  Possible values: `21:9`, `16:9`, `4:3`, `3:2`, `1:1`, `2:3`, `3:4`, `9:16`, `9:21`
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the inference.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the output image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to return the image in sync mode.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Emu35Output`
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The edited image.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the inference.

## fal-ai/ernie-image

**Title:** Ernie Image
**Description:** High-quality text-to-image model by Baidu. Supports English, Chinese, and Japanese prompts with built-in prompt expansion.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ernie-image`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** realism, chinese, multilingual, portrait, photorealistic

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ErnieImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt for image generation. Supports English, Chinese, and Japanese.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Negative prompt to guide what should not be in the image.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`, `square_uhd`, `portrait_3_2`, `landscape_3_2`, `portrait_hd`, `landscape_hd`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” Number of denoising steps.
  Range: min: 1, max: 100
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If True, the prompt will be enhanced using an LLM for more detailed results.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable NSFW safety checking on the generated images.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” Output image format.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If True, the image will be returned as a data URI instead of a URL.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ErnieImageOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed used for generation.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” List of generated images.
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” Timing information.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

**Title:** Ernie Image
**Description:** High-quality text-to-image model by Baidu. Supports English, Chinese, and Japanese prompts with built-in prompt expansion.

**Category:** text-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ErnieImageLoRAInput`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt for image generation. Supports English, Chinese, and Japanese.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Negative prompt to guide what should not be in the image.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`, `square_uhd`, `portrait_3_2`, `landscape_3_2`, `portrait_hd`, `landscape_hd`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” Number of denoising steps.
  Range: min: 1, max: 100
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If True, the prompt will be enhanced using an LLM for more detailed results.

  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`

- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The LoRAs to use for image generation. You can use up to 3 LoRAs and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
