**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** qwen, 2512, lora

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LoraInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate an image from.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"landscape_4_3"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” The guidance scale to use for the image generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. You can use up to 3 LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `QwenImage2512Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.

---

## fal-ai/qwen-image-max/text-to-image

**Title:** Qwen Image Max
**Description:** Text-to-Image endpoint for Qwen-Image-Max. Qwen Image Max improves upon the Qwen Image Plus series by enhancing the realism and naturalness of images.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/qwen-image-max/text-to-image`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** qwen-image, max

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `QwenImageMaxTextToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt describing the desired image. Supports Chinese and English. Max 800 characters.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Content to avoid in the generated image. Max 500 characters.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable LLM prompt optimization for better results.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility (0-2147483647).
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable content moderation for input and output.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `QwenImageMaxTextToImageOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<File>)* вҖ” Generated images.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation

---

## fal-ai/realistic-vision

**Title:** Realistic Vision
**Description:** Generate realistic images.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/realistic-vision`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** realism, diffusion

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `RealisticVisionTextToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`model_name`** *(string)* вҖ” The Realistic Vision model to use.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"(worst quality, low quality, normal quality, lowres, low details, oversaturated, undersaturated, overexposed, underexposed, grayscale, bw, bad photo, bad photography, bad art:1.4), (watermark, signature, text font, username, error, logo, words, letters, digits, autograph, trademark, name:1.2), (blur, blurry, grainy), morbid, ugly, asymmetrical, mutated malformed, mutilated, poorly lit, bad shadow, draft, cropped, out of frame, cut off, censored, jpeg artifacts, out of focus, glitch, duplicate, (airbrushed, cartoon, anime, semi-realistic, cgi, render, blender, digital art, manga, amateur:1.3), (3D ,3D Game, 3D Game Scene, 3D Character:1.1), (bad hands, bad anatomy, bad body, bad face, bad teeth, bad arms, bad legs, deformities:1.3)"` вҖ” The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want in the image.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `{"height":1024,"width":1024}`
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `35` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 70
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The list of LoRA weights to use.
- **`embeddings`** *(list<Embedding>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The list of embeddings to use.
- **`expand_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the prompt will be expanded with additional prompts.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`safety_checker_version`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"v1"` вҖ” The version of the safety checker to use. v1 is the default CompVis safety checker. v2 uses a custom ViT model.
  Possible values: `v1`, `v2`
- **`guidance_rescale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The rescale factor for the CFG.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`request_id`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            An id bound to a request, can be used with response to identify the request
            itself.
        

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.

- **`style`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"realistic_image"` вҖ” The style of the generated images. Vector images cost 2X as much.

## fal-ai/recraft/v4/pro/text-to-image

**Title:** Recraft V4 Pro
**Description:** Recraft V4 was developed with designers to bring true visual taste to AI image generation. Built for brand systems and production-ready workflows, it goes beyond prompt accuracy вҖ” delivering stronger composition, refined lighting, realistic materials, and a cohesive aesthetic. The result is imagery shaped by professional design judgment, ready for immediate real-world use without additional post-processing.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/recraft/v4/pro/text-to-image`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** text-to-image

  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`colors`** *(list<RGBColor>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” An array of preferable colors
- **`background_color`** *(?)* вҖ” The preferable background color of the generated images.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `RecraftV4ProTextToImageOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<File>)*

## fal-ai/sana

**Title:** Sana
**Description:** Sana can synthesize high-resolution, high-quality images with strong text-image alignment at a remarkably fast speed, with the ability to generate 4K images in less than a second.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sana`
**Category:** text-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `{"height":2160,"width":3840}` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `18` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`style_name`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"(No style)"` вҖ” The style to generate the image in.
  Possible values: `(No style)`, `Cinematic`, `Photographic`, `Anime`, `Manga`, `Digital Art`, `Pixel art`, `Fantasy art`, `Neonpunk`, `3D Model`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

---

**Description:** Sana v1.5 1.6B is a lightweight text-to-image model that delivers 4K image generation with impressive efficiency.

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `{"height":2160,"width":3840}` вҖ” The size of the generated image.

  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.

            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want

- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`style_name`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"(No style)"` вҖ” The style to generate the image in.
  Possible values: `(No style)`, `Cinematic`, `Photographic`, `Anime`, `Manga`, `Digital Art`, `Pixel art`, `Fantasy art`, `Neonpunk`, `3D Model`

## fal-ai/sdxl-controlnet-union

**Title:** SDXL ControlNet Union
**Description:** An efficent SDXL multi-controlnet text-to-image model.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sdxl-controlnet-union`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** diffusion, controlnet, composition

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToImageControlNetUnionInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`controlnet_conditioning_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The scale of the controlnet conditioning.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image. Leave it none to automatically infer from the control image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `35` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 70
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The list of LoRA weights to use.
- **`embeddings`** *(list<Embedding>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The list of embeddings to use.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`safety_checker_version`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"v1"` вҖ” The version of the safety checker to use. v1 is the default CompVis safety checker. v2 uses a custom ViT model.
  Possible values: `v1`, `v2`
- **`expand_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the prompt will be expanded with additional prompts.
- **`format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`request_id`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            An id bound to a request, can be used with response to identify the request
            itself.
        
- **`openpose_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`openpose_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the openpose image.
- **`depth_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`depth_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the depth image.
- **`teed_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`teed_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the teed image.
- **`canny_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`canny_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the canny image.
- **`normal_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`normal_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the normal image.
- **`segmentation_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control image.
- **`segmentation_preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the segmentation image.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*

- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.

## fal-ai/stable-cascade

**Title:** Stable Cascade
**Description:** Stable Cascade: Image generation on a smaller & cheaper latent space.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/stable-cascade`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** diffusion, lcm

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `StableCascadeInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`first_stage_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `20` вҖ” Number of steps to run the first stage for.
  Range: min: 4, max: 40
- **`second_stage_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `10` вҖ” Number of steps to run the second stage for.
  Range: min: 4, max: 24
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`second_stage_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Cascade
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to false, the safety checker will be disabled.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the image will be returned as base64 encoded string.
        

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.

- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`
- **`controlnet`** *()* вҖ” 
            ControlNet for inference.
        
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” The size of the generated image. Defaults to landscape_4_3 if no controlnet has been passed, otherwise defaults to the size of the controlnet conditioning image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`ip_adapter`** *(?)* вҖ” 
            IP-Adapter to use during inference.
        

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

---

## fal-ai/stable-diffusion-v35-medium

**Title:** Stable Diffusion 3.5 Medium
**Description:** Stable Diffusion 3.5 Medium is a Multimodal Diffusion Transformer (MMDiT) text-to-image model that features improved performance in image quality, typography, complex prompt understanding, and resource-efficiency.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/stable-diffusion-v35-medium`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** diffusion, typography, style

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"landscape_4_3"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `40` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the function will wait for the image to be generated and uploaded
            before returning the response. This will increase the latency of the function but
            it allows you to get the image directly in the response without going through the CDN.
        
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.

## fal-ai/switti

**Title:** Switti 1024
**Description:** Switti is a scale-wise transformer for fast text-to-image generation that outperforms existing T2I AR models and competes with state-of-the-art T2I diffusion models while being faster than distilled diffusion models.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/switti`
**Category:** text-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`sampling_top_k`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `400` вҖ” The number of top-k tokens to sample from.
  Range: min: 10, max: 1000
- **`sampling_top_p`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.95` вҖ” The top-p probability to sample from.
  Range: min: 0.1, max: 1
- **`more_smooth`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Smoothing with Gumbel softmax sampling
- **`more_diverse`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” More diverse sampling
- **`smooth_start_si`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Smoothing starting scale
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`turn_off_cfg_start_si`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” Disable CFG starting scale
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`last_scale_temp`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.1` вҖ” Temperature after disabling CFG
  Range: min: 0.1, max: 10
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `6` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the function will wait for the image to be generated and uploaded
            before returning the response. This will increase the latency of the function but
            it allows you to get the image directly in the response without going through the CDN.
        
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SwittiOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated images
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        

---

## fal-ai/vecglypher

**Title:** Vecglypher
**Description:** Vector font generation with VecGlypher. Create custom glyphs from text descriptions or reference imagesвҖ”outputs clean SVG paths directly without raster-to-vector conversion.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/vecglypher`
**Category:** text-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToSVGInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The target text to generate as vector glyphs. Each character is rendered as a separate SVG path element.
- **`style_description`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"italic style, 400 weight, serif, text, elegant"` вҖ” Font style description using typography terms such as weight (100-900), style (italic, oblique), category (serif, sans-serif, display, handwriting, monospace), and characteristics (geometric, humanist, condensed, rounded).
- **`temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.1` вҖ” Sampling temperature. Lower values produce more deterministic output.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable input safety checking.
- **`top_p`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.95` вҖ” Top-p (nucleus) sampling parameter.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`top_k`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Top-k sampling parameter.
  Range: min: -1, max: 100
- **`repetition_penalty`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Repetition penalty to reduce repeated SVG path segments.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`max_tokens`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8192` вҖ” Maximum tokens to generate. Increase for longer text.
  Range: min: 256, max: 16384
- **`output_size`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `512` вҖ” Maximum dimension (width or height) of the output SVG in pixels. The aspect ratio is preserved.
  Range: min: 64, max: 4096
- **`fill_color`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"black"` вҖ” Fill color for the generated glyphs. Accepts any valid SVG/CSS color value.
- **`stroke_color`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional stroke (outline) color for the generated glyphs. When set, adds an outline around each glyph path.
- **`stroke_width`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Stroke width in SVG units. Only applies when stroke_color is set.
  Range: min: 0.1, max: 50

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `VecGlypherOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” Timing breakdown of the generation process.
- **`svg_content`** *(string)* вҖ” Raw SVG content as a string.
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The generated SVG file containing vector glyphs.

---

## fal-ai/vidu/q2/text-to-image

**Title:** Vidu
**Description:** Use vidu Text-to-Image to turn your prompts into reality.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/vidu/q2/text-to-image`
**Category:** text-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToImageRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt for video generation, max 1500 characters
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"16:9"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the output video
  Possible values: `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for generation

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `TextToImageOutput`
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The edited image

## fal-ai/wan-25-preview/text-to-image

**Title:** Wan 2.5 Text to Image
**Description:** Wan 2.5 text-to-image model.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan-25-preview/text-to-image`
**Category:** text-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt for image generation. Supports Chinese and English, max 2000 characters.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Negative prompt to describe content to avoid. Max 500 characters.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of images to generate. Values from 1 to 4.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square"` вҖ” The size of the generated image. Can use preset names like 'square', 'landscape_16_9', etc., or specific dimensions. Total pixels must be between 768Г—768 and 1440Г—1440, with aspect ratio between [1:4, 4:1].
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt rewriting using LLM. Improves results for short prompts but increases processing time.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `TextToImageOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The generated images
- **`seeds`** *(list<integer>)* вҖ” The seeds used for each generated image
- **`actual_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The actual prompt used if prompt rewriting was enabled

---

## fal-ai/wan/v2.2-5b/text-to-image

**Title:** Wan
**Description:** Wan 2.2's 5B model generates high-resolution, photorealistic images with powerful prompt understanding and fine-grained visual detail
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan/v2.2-5b/text-to-image`
**Category:** text-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `WanSmallT2IRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to guide image generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Negative prompt for video generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `40` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, input data will be checked for safety before processing.
- **`enable_output_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, output video will be checked for safety after generation.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion. This will use a large language model to expand the prompt with additional details while maintaining the original meaning.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale. Higher values give better adherence to the prompt but may decrease quality.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Shift value for the image. Must be between 1.0 and 10.0.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`image_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the output image.
  Possible values: `png`, `jpeg`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanSmallT2IResponse`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The generated image file.

---

## fal-ai/wan/v2.2-a14b/text-to-image

**Title:** Wan
**Description:** Wan 2.2's 14B model generates high-resolution, photorealistic images with powerful prompt understanding and fine-grained visual detail
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan/v2.2-a14b/text-to-image`
**Category:** text-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `WanT2IRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to guide image generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Negative prompt for video generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `27` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 40
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, input data will be checked for safety before processing.
- **`enable_output_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, output video will be checked for safety after generation.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion. This will use a large language model to expand the prompt with additional details while maintaining the original meaning.
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration level to use. The more acceleration, the faster the generation, but with lower quality. The recommended value is 'regular'.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale. Higher values give better adherence to the prompt but may decrease quality.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`guidance_scale_2`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” Guidance scale for the second stage of the model. This is used to control the adherence to the prompt in the second stage of the model.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Shift value for the image. Must be between 1.0 and 10.0.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanT2IResponse`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`image`** *()* вҖ” The generated image file.

---

- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `27` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 40

- **`enable_output_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, output video will be checked for safety after generation.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion. This will use a large language model to expand the prompt with additional details while maintaining the original meaning.
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration level to use. The more acceleration, the faster the generation, but with lower quality. The recommended value is 'regular'.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale. Higher values give better adherence to the prompt but may decrease quality.

- **`guidance_scale_2`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” Guidance scale for the second stage of the model. This is used to control the adherence to the prompt in the second stage of the model.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Shift value for the image. Must be between 1.0 and 10.0.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`loras`** *(list<LoRAWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” LoRA weights to be used in the inference.
- **`reverse_video`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the video will be reversed.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`image_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the output image.
  Possible values: `png`, `jpeg`

## fal-ai/wan/v2.7/pro/text-to-image

**Title:** Wan
**Description:** Generate premium-quality images from text prompts using the enhanced WAN 2.7 Pro model with superior detail and composition.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan/v2.7/pro/text-to-image`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** wan, text-to-image, pro

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Wan27TextToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt describing the desired output. Supports Chinese and English.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Content to avoid in the generated image. Max 500 characters.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” Output image size.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`max_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Maximum number of images to generate (1-5).
  Range: min: 1, max: 5
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility (0-2147483647).
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable content moderation for input and output.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Wan27TextToImageOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`generated_text`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional generated text returned by the model.
- **`images`** *(list<File>)* вҖ” Generated images.

## fal-ai/wan/v2.7/text-to-image

**Title:** Wan
**Description:** Generate high-quality images from text prompts using the WAN 2.7 model with advanced prompt understanding and detailed output.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan/v2.7/text-to-image`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** wan, text-to-image, image-generation

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Wan27TextToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt describing the desired output. Supports Chinese and English.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Content to avoid in the generated image. Max 500 characters.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” Output image size.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`max_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Maximum number of images to generate (1-5).
  Range: min: 1, max: 5
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility (0-2147483647).
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable content moderation for input and output.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Wan27TextToImageOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`generated_text`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional generated text returned by the model.
- **`images`** *(list<File>)* вҖ” Generated images.

---

## fal-ai/z-image/base

**Title:** Z Image Base
**Description:** Z-Image is the foundation model of the Z- Image family, engineered for good quality, robust generative diversity, broad stylistic coverage, and precise prompt adherence.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/z-image/base`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** z-image, base

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ZImageBaseTextToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"landscape_4_3"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” The guidance scale to use for the image generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The negative prompt to use for the image generation.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ZImageBaseOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” The timings of the generation process.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

---

## fal-ai/z-image/turbo/tiling

**Title:** Z-Image Turbo Seamless Tiling
**Description:** Generate seamlessly tiling photorealistic images from text using Z-Image Turbo
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/z-image/turbo/tiling`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** z-image, turbo, seamless, tiling

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ZImageTurboTilingInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"square_hd"` вҖ” The size of the generated image. Use 'auto' to match the input image size (or 1024x1024 if no image).
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`, `auto`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 8
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"png"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`, `webp`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion. Note: this will increase the price by 0.0025 credits per request.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of an image for image-to-image or inpainting. When provided without mask_image_url, performs image-to-image; with mask_image_url, performs inpainting.
- **`mask_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of a mask image for inpainting. White regions are regenerated, black regions are preserved. Requires image_url.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.6` вҖ” How much to transform the input image. Only used when image_url is provided.
  Range: max: 1
- **`tile_size`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `128` вҖ” Tile size in latent space. Must be even (divisible by 2) for patchification. (64 = 512px, 128 = 1024px, 256 = 2048px).
  Range: min: 32, max: 256
- **`tile_stride`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `64` вҖ” Tile stride in latent space. Must be even (divisible by 2). (32 = 256px, 64 = 512px, 128 = 1024px).
  Range: min: 16, max: 128
- **`tiling_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"both"` вҖ” Tiling direction: 'both' (omnidirectional), 'horizontal' (left-right only), 'vertical' (top-bottom only).
  Possible values: `both`, `horizontal`, `vertical`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ZImageTurboTilingOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” The timings of the generation process.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.

---

- **`tile_stride`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `64` вҖ” Tile stride in latent space. Must be even (divisible by 2). (32 = 256px, 64 = 512px, 128 = 1024px).
  Range: min: 16, max: 128
- **`tiling_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"both"` вҖ” Tiling direction: 'both' (omnidirectional), 'horizontal' (left-right only), 'vertical' (top-bottom only).
  Possible values: `both`, `horizontal`, `vertical`
- **`loras`** *(list<LoRAInput>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of LoRA weights to apply (maximum 3).

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ZImageTurboTilingOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<ImageFile>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” The timings of the generation process.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.

- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1:1"` вҖ” Image aspect ratio: 1:1, 3:1, 1:3, 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, 3:2, 2:3
  Possible values: `1:1`, `3:1`, `1:3`, `16:9`, `9:16`, `4:3`, `3:4`, `3:2`, `2:3`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for generation. 0 and -1 are treated as random.

  Possible values: `1:1`, `16:9`, `9:16`, `4:3`, `3:4`, `3:1`, `1:3`, `3:2`, `2:3`

## imagineart/imagineart-2.0-preview/text-to-image

**Title:** Imagineart 2.0 Preview
**Description:** ImagineArt 2.0 is ImagineArt's latest state-of-the-art visual reasoning text-to-image model, generating high-fidelity, professional-grade visuals with lifelike realism, cinematic effects, and strong aesthetic quality.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/imagineart/imagineart-2.0-preview/text-to-image`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** stylized, transform, typography

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ImagineArt_2_0_Input`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt describing the desired image
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1:1"` вҖ” Image aspect ratio: 1:1, 3:1, 1:3, 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, 3:2, 2:3, 21:9, 4:5, 5:4, 4:1, 1:4
  Possible values: `1:1`, `16:9`, `9:16`, `4:3`, `3:4`, `3:1`, `1:3`, `3:2`, `2:3`, `21:9`, `4:5`, `5:4`, `4:1`, `1:4`
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"2K"` вҖ” Output resolution: '1K' targets ~1024px and will be faster. '2K' targets ~2048px image size and will be slower but with higher quality.
  Possible values: `1K`, `2K`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default is random (0 and -1 are treated as random)
- **`reasoning`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"low"` вҖ” Visual Understanding Level: low (default) or high. High reasoning takes more time but generates better images.
  Possible values: `high`, `low`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ImagineArt_2_0_Output`
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” Generated image

## rundiffusion-fal/juggernaut-flux-lora

**Title:** Juggernaut Flux Base LoRA
**Description:** Juggernaut Base Flux LoRA by RunDiffusion is a drop-in replacement for Flux [Dev] that delivers sharper details, richer colors, and enhanced realism to all your LoRAs and LyCORIS with full compatibility.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/rundiffusion-fal/juggernaut-flux-lora`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** image generation

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToImageInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"landscape_4_3"` вҖ” The size of the generated image.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 35
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the function will wait for the image to be generated and uploaded
            before returning the response. This will increase the latency of the function but
            it allows you to get the image directly in the response without going through the CDN.
        
- **`num_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of images to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"jpeg"` вҖ” The format of the generated image.
  Possible values: `jpeg`, `png`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the image.
- **`images`** *(list<Image>)* вҖ” The generated image files info.
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`has_nsfw_concepts`** *(list<boolean>)* вҖ” Whether the generated images contain NSFW concepts.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.

## wan/v2.6/text-to-image

**Title:** Wan v2.6 Text to Image
**Description:** Wan 2.6 text-to-image model.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/wan/v2.6/text-to-image`
**Category:** text-to-image
**Tags:** text-to-image

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToImageWanInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt describing the desired image. Supports Chinese and English. Max 2000 characters.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional reference image (0 or 1). When provided, can be used for style guidance. Resolution: 384-5000px each dimension. Max size: 10MB. Formats: JPEG, JPG, PNG (no alpha), BMP, WEBP.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Content to avoid in the generated image. Max 500 characters.
- **`image_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Output image size. If not set: matches input image size (up to 1280*1280). Use presets like 'square_hd', 'landscape_16_9', or specify exact dimensions.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`max_images`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Maximum number of images to generate (1-5). Actual count may be less depending on model inference.
  Range: min: 1, max: 5
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility (0-2147483647).
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable content moderation for input and output.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `TextToImageWanOutput`
- **`images`** *(list<File>)* вҖ” Generated images in PNG format
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation
- **`generated_text`** *(string)* вҖ” Generated text content (in mixed text-and-image mode). May be None if only images were generated.

## fal-ai/chatterbox/text-to-speech

**Title:** Chatterbox
**Description:** Whether you're working on memes, videos, games, or AI agents, Chatterbox brings your content to life. Use the first tts from resemble ai.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/chatterbox/text-to-speech`
**Category:** text-to-speech
**Tags:** text-to-speech

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ChatterboxRequest`
**Required fields:** `text`

- **`text`** *(string)* вҖ” The text to be converted to speech (maximum 5000 characters). You can additionally add the following emotive tags: <laugh>, <chuckle>, <sigh>, <cough>, <sniffle>, <groan>, <yawn>, <gasp>
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"https://storage.googleapis.com/chatterbox-demo-samples/prompts/male_rickmorty.mp3"` вҖ” Optional URL to an audio file to use as a reference for the generated speech. If provided, the model will try to match the style and tone of the reference audio.
- **`exaggeration`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.25` вҖ” Exaggeration factor for the generated speech (0.0 = no exaggeration, 1.0 = maximum exaggeration).
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” Temperature for generation (higher = more creative).
  Range: min: 0.05, max: 2
- **`cfg`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5`
  Range: min: 0.1, max: 1
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Useful to control the reproducibility of the generated audio. Assuming all other properties didn't change, a fixed seed should always generate the exact same audio file. Set to 0 for random seed..

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ChatterboxOutput`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated speech audio

---

## fal-ai/chatterbox/text-to-speech/multilingual

**Title:** Chatterbox
**Description:** Whether you're working on memes, videos, games, or AI agents, Chatterbox brings your content to life. Use the first tts from resemble ai.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/chatterbox/text-to-speech/multilingual`
**Category:** text-to-speech
**Tags:** text-to-speech, multilingual

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ChatterboxMultilingualRequest`
**Required fields:** `text`

- **`text`** *(string)* вҖ” The text to be converted to speech (maximum 300 characters). Supports 23 languages including English, French, German, Spanish, Italian, Portuguese, Hindi, Arabic, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, and more.
- **`voice`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"english"` вҖ” Language code for synthesis. In case using custom please provide audio url and select custom_audio_language. 
- **`custom_audio_language`** *(string)* вҖ” If using a custom audio URL, specify the language of the audio here. Ignored if voice is not a custom url.
  Possible values: `english`, `arabic`, `danish`, `german`, `greek`, `spanish`, `finnish`, `french`, `hebrew`, `hindi`, `italian`, `japanese`, `korean`, `malay`, `dutch`, `norwegian`, `polish`, `portuguese`, `russian`, `swedish`, `swahili`, `turkish`, `chinese`
- **`exaggeration`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Controls speech expressiveness and emotional intensity (0.25-2.0). 0.5 is neutral, higher values increase expressiveness. Extreme values may be unstable.
  Range: min: 0.25, max: 2
- **`temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.8` вҖ” Controls randomness and variation in generation (0.05-5.0). Higher values create more varied speech patterns.
  Range: min: 0.05, max: 5
- **`cfg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Configuration/pace weight controlling generation guidance (0.0-1.0). Use 0.0 for language transfer to mitigate accent inheritance.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducible results. Set to 0 for random generation, or provide a specific number for consistent outputs.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ChatterboxMultilingualOutput`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated multilingual speech audio file

## fal-ai/elevenlabs/tts/turbo-v2.5

**Title:** ElevenLabs TTS Turbo v2.5
**Description:** Generate high-speed text-to-speech audio using ElevenLabs TTS Turbo v2.5.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/elevenlabs/tts/turbo-v2.5`
**Category:** text-to-speech
**Tags:** audio

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToSpeechRequest`
**Required fields:** `text`

- **`text`** *(string)* вҖ” The text to convert to speech
- **`voice`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"Rachel"` вҖ” The voice to use for speech generation
- **`stability`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Voice stability (0-1)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`similarity_boost`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.75` вҖ” Similarity boost (0-1)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`style`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Style exaggeration (0-1)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`speed`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Speech speed (0.7-1.2). Values below 1.0 slow down the speech, above 1.0 speed it up. Extreme values may affect quality.
  Range: min: 0.7, max: 1.2
- **`timestamps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to return timestamps for each word in the generated speech
- **`previous_text`** *(string)* вҖ” The text that came before the text of the current request. Can be used to improve the speech's continuity when concatenating together multiple generations or to influence the speech's continuity in the current generation.
- **`next_text`** *(string)* вҖ” The text that comes after the text of the current request. Can be used to improve the speech's continuity when concatenating together multiple generations or to influence the speech's continuity in the current generation.
- **`language_code`** *(string)* вҖ” Language code (ISO 639-1) used to enforce a language for the model. An error will be returned if language code is not supported by the model.
- **`apply_text_normalization`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” This parameter controls text normalization with three modes: 'auto', 'on', and 'off'. When set to 'auto', the system will automatically decide whether to apply text normalization (e.g., spelling out numbers). With 'on', text normalization will always be applied, while with 'off', it will be skipped.
  Possible values: `auto`, `on`, `off`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `TTSOutput`
- **`timestamps`** *(array)* вҖ” Timestamps for each word in the generated speech. Only returned if `timestamps` is set to True in the request.
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio file

---

## fal-ai/gemini-3.1-flash-tts

**Title:** Gemini 3.1 Flash Tts
**Description:** Newest audio model from Google introduces granular audio tags that give you precise control to direct AI speech for expressive audio generation.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/gemini-3.1-flash-tts`
**Category:** text-to-speech
**Tags:** lipsync, avatar, 

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Gemini31FlashTTSInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text to convert to speech. Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS supports natural-language prompting for style, pace, accent, and emotional expression вҖ” include delivery instructions inline with the text (e.g. 'Say cheerfully: Have a wonderful day!'). Supports expressive audio tags like [sigh], [laughing], [whispering], [short pause] for fine-grained control over delivery. For multi-speaker synthesis, prefix lines with speaker aliases defined in the speakers field (e.g. 'Alice: Hello!\nBob: Hi!').
- **`style_instructions`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional style and delivery instructions prepended to the prompt. Controls expressiveness, accent, pace, tone, and emotional expression using natural language. Use this to separate style control from the text content. Examples: 'Speak warmly and slowly', 'Read this as a dramatic newscast', 'Use a British accent with a cheerful tone', 'Whisper mysteriously'.
- **`voice`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"Kore"` вҖ” Voice preset for single-speaker synthesis. 30 distinct voices are available. Ignored when speakers is set. Popular choices: Kore (strong, firm female), Puck (upbeat, lively male), Charon (calm, professional male), Zephyr (bright, clear female), Aoede (warm, melodic female).
  Possible values: `Achernar`, `Achird`, `Algenib`, `Algieba`, `Alnilam`, `Aoede`, `Autonoe`, `Callirrhoe`, `Charon`, `Despina`, `Enceladus`, `Erinome`, `Fenrir`, `Gacrux`, `Iapetus`, `Kore`, `Laomedeia`, `Leda`, `Orus`, `Pulcherrima`, `Puck`, `Rasalgethi`, `Sadachbia`, `Sadaltager`, `Schedar`, `Sulafat`, `Umbriel`, `Vindemiatrix`, `Zephyr`, `Zubenelgenubi`
- **`language_code`** *(string)* вҖ” Language for multilingual synthesis. When set, steers the model to speak in the specified language. If not set, the model auto-detects the language from the text.
  Possible values: `Arabic (Egypt)`, `Bangla (Bangladesh)`, `Dutch (Netherlands)`, `English (India)`, `English (US)`, `French (France)`, `German (Germany)`, `Hindi (India)`, `Indonesian (Indonesia)`, `Italian (Italy)`, `Japanese (Japan)`, `Korean (South Korea)`, `Marathi (India)`, `Polish (Poland)`, `Portuguese (Brazil)`, `Romanian (Romania)`, `Russian (Russia)`, `Spanish (Spain)`, `Tamil (India)`, `Telugu (India)`, `Thai (Thailand)`, `Turkish (Turkey)`, `Ukrainian (Ukraine)`, `Vietnamese (Vietnam)`, `Afrikaans (South Africa)`, `Albanian (Albania)`, `Amharic (Ethiopia)`, `Arabic (World)`, `Armenian (Armenia)`, `Azerbaijani (Azerbaijan)`, `Basque (Spain)`, `Belarusian (Belarus)`, `Bulgarian (Bulgaria)`, `Burmese (Myanmar)`, `Catalan (Spain)`, `Cebuano (Philippines)`, `Chinese Mandarin (China)`, `Chinese Mandarin (Taiwan)`, `Croatian (Croatia)`, `Czech (Czech Republic)`, `Danish (Denmark)`, `English (Australia)`, `English (UK)`, `Estonian (Estonia)`, `Filipino (Philippines)`, `Finnish (Finland)`, `French (Canada)`, `Galician (Spain)`, `Georgian (Georgia)`, `Greek (Greece)`, `Gujarati (India)`, `Haitian Creole (Haiti)`, `Hebrew (Israel)`, `Hungarian (Hungary)`, `Icelandic (Iceland)`, `Javanese (Java)`, `Kannada (India)`, `Konkani (India)`, `Lao (Laos)`, `Latin (Vatican City)`, `Latvian (Latvia)`, `Lithuanian (Lithuania)`, `Luxembourgish (Luxembourg)`, `Macedonian (North Macedonia)`, `Maithili (India)`, `Malagasy (Madagascar)`, `Malay (Malaysia)`, `Malayalam (India)`, `Mongolian (Mongolia)`, `Nepali (Nepal)`, `Norwegian Bokmal (Norway)`, `Norwegian Nynorsk (Norway)`, `Odia (India)`, `Pashto (Afghanistan)`, `Persian (Iran)`, `Portuguese (Portugal)`, `Punjabi (India)`, `Serbian (Serbia)`, `Sindhi (India)`, `Sinhala (Sri Lanka)`, `Slovak (Slovakia)`, `Slovenian (Slovenia)`, `Spanish (Latin America)`, `Spanish (Mexico)`, `Swahili (Kenya)`, `Swedish (Sweden)`, `Urdu (Pakistan)`
- **`speakers`** *(array)* вҖ” Multi-speaker voice configuration. When set, enables multi-speaker synthesis where different parts of the text are spoken by different voices. Each speaker needs a voice and a speaker_id (alias) that matches prefixes in the prompt.
- **`temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Controls the randomness of the speech output. Higher values produce more creative and varied delivery, while lower values make the output more predictable and focused.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"mp3"` вҖ” Audio output format. mp3: compressed, small file size (recommended). wav: uncompressed PCM wrapped in WAV (24 kHz, 16-bit mono). ogg_opus: Ogg container with Opus codec, good quality-to-size ratio.
  Possible values: `wav`, `mp3`, `ogg_opus`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `GeminiTTSOutput`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio file.

---

## fal-ai/index-tts-2/text-to-speech

**Title:** Index TTS 2.0
**Description:** Generate natural, clear speeches using Index TTS 2.0 from IndexTeam
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/index-tts-2/text-to-speech`
**Category:** text-to-speech
**Tags:** text-to-speech

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `IndexTTS2Input`
**Required fields:** `audio_url`, `prompt`

- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The audio file to generate the speech from.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The speech prompt to generate
- **`emotional_audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The emotional reference audio file to extract the style from.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the emotional style transfer. Higher values result in stronger emotional influence.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`emotional_strengths`** *(?)* вҖ” The strengths of individual emotions for fine-grained control. 
- **`should_use_prompt_for_emotion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to use the `prompt` to calculate emotional strengths, if enabled it will overwrite the `emotional_strengths` values. If `emotion_prompt` is provided, it will be used to instead of `prompt` to extract the emotional style.
- **`emotion_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The emotional prompt to influence the emotional style. Must be used together with should_use_prompt_for_emotion.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `IndexTTS2Output`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio file in base64 format.

---

## fal-ai/lux-tts

**Title:** Lux TTS
**Description:** High-quality voice cloning TTS model that generates 48kHz speech from text and a reference audio. Distilled to 4 steps for fast inference.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/lux-tts`
**Category:** text-to-speech
**Tags:** tts, voice-cloning, speech-synthesis

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Input`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `audio_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text to be converted to speech.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the reference audio file for voice cloning. The model will mimic the voice characteristics from this audio.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” Number of flow-matching inference steps. 4 is recommended for best efficiency.
  Range: min: 1, max: 16
- **`max_ref_length`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Maximum length of the reference audio to use for voice encoding, in seconds. Longer durations capture more voice characteristics but increase processing time.
  Range: min: 1, max: 15
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale. Higher values increase adherence to the reference voice at the cost of diversity.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated speech audio file at 48kHz.
- **`seed`** *(integer)*

---

## fal-ai/maya

**Title:** Maya1
**Description:** Maya1 is a state-of-the-art speech model by Maya Research for expressive voice generation, built to capture real human emotion and precise voice design.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/maya`
**Category:** text-to-speech
**Tags:** text-to-speech, tts

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MayaVoiceInput`
**Required fields:** `text`, `prompt`

- **`text`** *(string)* вҖ” The text to synthesize into speech. You can embed emotion tags anywhere in the text using the format <emotion_name>. Available emotions: laugh, laugh_harder, sigh, chuckle, gasp, angry, excited, whisper, cry, scream, sing, snort, exhale, gulp, giggle, sarcastic, curious. Example: 'Hello world! <excited> This is amazing!' or 'I can't believe this <sigh> happened again.'
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Description of the voice/character. Includes attributes like age, accent, pitch, timbre, pacing, tone, and intensity. See examples for format.
- **`temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.4` вҖ” Sampling temperature. Lower values (0.2-0.5) produce more stable/consistent audio. Higher values add variation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`top_p`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.9` вҖ” Nucleus sampling parameter. Controls diversity of token selection.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`max_tokens`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `2000` вҖ” Maximum number of SNAC tokens to generate (7 tokens per frame). Controls maximum audio length.
  Range: min: 28, max: 4000
- **`repetition_penalty`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.1` вҖ” Penalty for repeating tokens. Higher values reduce repetition artifacts.
  Range: min: 1, max: 2
- **`sample_rate`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"48 kHz"` вҖ” Output audio sample rate. 48 kHz provides higher quality audio, 24 kHz is faster.
  Possible values: `48 kHz`, `24 kHz`
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"wav"` вҖ” Output audio format for the generated speech
  Possible values: `wav`, `mp3`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MayaVoiceOutput`
- **`rtf`** *(number)* вҖ” Real-time factor (generation_time / audio_duration). Lower is better.
- **`duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Duration of the generated audio in seconds
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio file containing the speech (WAV or MP3 format, 24kHz or 48kHz mono depending on upsampler)
- **`sample_rate`** *(string)* вҖ” Sample rate of the generated audio
- **`generation_time`** *(number)* вҖ” Time taken to generate the audio in seconds

---

## fal-ai/maya/batch

**Title:** Maya
**Description:** Maya1 is a state-of-the-art speech model by Maya Research for expressive voice generation, built to capture real human emotion and precise voice design.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/maya/batch`
**Category:** text-to-speech
**Tags:** text-to-speech, tts

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MayaVoiceBatchInput`
**Required fields:** `texts`, `prompts`

- **`texts`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” List of texts to synthesize into speech. You can embed emotion tags in each text using the format <emotion_name>.
- **`prompts`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” List of voice descriptions for each text. Must match the length of texts list. Each describes the voice/character attributes.
- **`temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.4` вҖ” Sampling temperature for all generations.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`top_p`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.9` вҖ” Nucleus sampling parameter for all generations.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`max_tokens`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `2000` вҖ” Maximum SNAC tokens per generation.
  Range: min: 28, max: 4000
- **`repetition_penalty`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.1` вҖ” Repetition penalty for all generations.
  Range: min: 1, max: 2
- **`sample_rate`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"48 kHz"` вҖ” Output audio sample rate for all generations. 48 kHz provides higher quality, 24 kHz is faster.
  Possible values: `48 kHz`, `24 kHz`
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"wav"` вҖ” Output audio format for all generated speech files
  Possible values: `wav`, `mp3`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MayaVoiceBatchOutput`
- **`average_rtf`** *(number)* вҖ” Average real-time factor across all generations
- **`total_generation_time`** *(number)* вҖ” Total time taken to generate all audio files in seconds
- **`sample_rate`** *(string)* вҖ” Sample rate of all generated audio files
- **`audios`** *(list<File>)* вҖ” List of generated audio files
- **`durations`** *(list<number>)* вҖ” Duration of each generated audio in seconds

---

## fal-ai/maya/stream

**Title:** Maya
**Description:** Maya1 is a state-of-the-art speech model by Maya Research for expressive voice generation, built to capture real human emotion and precise voice design.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/maya/stream`
**Category:** text-to-speech
**Tags:** text-to-speech, tts

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MayaVoiceStreamingInput`
**Required fields:** `text`, `prompt`

- **`text`** *(string)* вҖ” The text to synthesize into speech. You can embed emotion tags anywhere in the text using the format <emotion_name>. Available emotions: laugh, laugh_harder, sigh, chuckle, gasp, angry, excited, whisper, cry, scream, sing, snort, exhale, gulp, giggle, sarcastic, curious. Example: 'Hello world! <excited> This is amazing!' or 'I can't believe this <sigh> happened again.'
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Description of the voice/character. Includes attributes like age, accent, pitch, timbre, pacing, tone, and intensity. See examples for format.
- **`temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.4` вҖ” Sampling temperature. Lower values (0.2-0.5) produce more stable/consistent audio. Higher values add variation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`top_p`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.9` вҖ” Nucleus sampling parameter. Controls diversity of token selection.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`max_tokens`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `2000` вҖ” Maximum number of SNAC tokens to generate (7 tokens per frame). Controls maximum audio length.
  Range: min: 28, max: 4000
- **`repetition_penalty`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.1` вҖ” Penalty for repeating tokens. Higher values reduce repetition artifacts.
  Range: min: 1, max: 2
- **`sample_rate`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"24 kHz"` вҖ” Output audio sample rate. 48 kHz uses upsampling for higher quality audio, 24 kHz is native SNAC output (faster, lower latency).
  Possible values: `48 kHz`, `24 kHz`
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"mp3"` вҖ” Output audio format. 'mp3' for browser-playable audio, 'wav' for uncompressed audio, 'pcm' for raw PCM (lowest latency, requires client-side decoding).
  Possible values: `mp3`, `wav`, `pcm`

---

## fal-ai/minimax/preview/speech-2.5-hd

**Title:** Minimax
**Description:** Generate speech from text prompts and different voices using the MiniMax Speech-02 HD model, which leverages advanced AI techniques to create high-quality text-to-speech.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/minimax/preview/speech-2.5-hd`
**Category:** text-to-speech
**Tags:** speech

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TextToSpeechHDv25Request`
**Required fields:** `text`

- **`text`** *(string)* вҖ” Text to convert to speech (max 5000 characters, minimum 1 non-whitespace character)
- **`voice_setting`** *()* вҖ” Default: `{"pitch":0,"voice_id":"Wise_Woman","english_normalization":false,"speed":1,"vol":1}` вҖ” Voice configuration settings
- **`audio_setting`** *()* вҖ” Audio configuration settings
- **`language_boost`** *(string)* вҖ” Enhance recognition of specified languages and dialects
  Possible values: `Persian`, `Filipino`, `Tamil`, `Chinese`, `Chinese,Yue`, `English`, `Arabic`, `Russian`, `Spanish`, `French`, `Portuguese`, `German`, `Turkish`, `Dutch`, `Ukrainian`, `Vietnamese`, `Indonesian`, `Japanese`, `Italian`, `Korean`, `Thai`, `Polish`, `Romanian`, `Greek`, `Czech`, `Finnish`, `Hindi`, `Bulgarian`, `Danish`, `Hebrew`, `Malay`, `Slovak`, `Swedish`, `Croatian`, `Hungarian`, `Norwegian`, `Slovenian`, `Catalan`, `Nynorsk`, `Afrikaans`, `auto`
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"hex"` вҖ” Format of the output content (non-streaming only)
  Possible values: `url`, `hex`
- **`pronunciation_dict`** *(?)* вҖ” Custom pronunciation dictionary for text replacement

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `TextToSpeechOutput`
- **`audio`** *()* вҖ” The generated audio file
- **`duration_ms`** *(integer)* вҖ” Duration of the audio in milliseconds

---
