## clarityai/crystal-video-upscaler

**Title:** Crystal Upscaler [Video]
**Description:** Do high precision video upscaling that respects the original video perfectly using Crystal Upscaler's new video upscaling method!
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/clarityai/crystal-video-upscaler`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** upscale, video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `CrystalVideoUpscaleInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the input video.
- **`scale_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Scale factor. The scale factor must be chosen such that the upscaled video does not exceed 5K resolution.
  Range: min: 1, max: 200

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `CrystalVideoUpscaleOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” URL to the upscaled video

---

## decart/lucy-edit/pro

**Title:** Lucy Edit [Pro]
**Description:** Edit outfits, objects, faces, or restyle your video - all with maximum detail retention.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/decart/lucy-edit/pro`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-edit

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LucyEditProInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text description of the desired video content
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video to edit
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video
  Possible values: `720p`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the function will wait for the video to be generated
            and uploaded before returning the response. This will increase the
            latency of the function but it allows you to get the video directly
            in the response without going through the CDN.
        
- **`enhance_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enhance the prompt for better results.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LucyEditProOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video

---

## decart/lucy-restyle

**Title:** Lucy Restyle
**Description:** Restyle videos up to 30 min long - maintaining maximum detail quality.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/decart/lucy-restyle`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-edit

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LucyRestyleInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text description of the desired video content
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video to edit
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for video generation
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video
  Possible values: `720p`
- **`enhance_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enhance the prompt for better results.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” 
            If set to true, the function will wait for the video to be generated
            and uploaded before returning the response. This will increase the
            latency of the function but it allows you to get the video directly
            in the response without going through the CDN.
        

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LucyRestyleOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video

---

## fal-ai/amt-interpolation

**Title:** AMT Interpolation
**Description:** Interpolate between video frames
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/amt-interpolation`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** interpolation, editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `AMTInterpolationInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video to be processed
- **`output_fps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `24` вҖ” Output frames per second
- **`recursive_interpolation_passes`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Number of recursive interpolation passes

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `AMTInterpolationOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” Generated video

---

## fal-ai/auto-caption

**Title:** Auto-Captioner
**Description:** Automatically generates text captions for your videos from the audio as per text colour/font specifications
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/auto-caption`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** captioning, video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `CaptionInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the .mp4 video with audio. Only videos of size <400MB are allowed.
- **`txt_color`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"white"` вҖ” Colour of the text. Can be a RGB tuple, a color name, or an hexadecimal notation.
- **`txt_font`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"Standard"` вҖ” Font for generated captions. Choose one in 'Arial','Standard','Garamond', 'Times New Roman','Georgia', or pass a url to a .ttf file
- **`font_size`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `24` вҖ” Size of text in generated captions.
- **`stroke_width`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Width of the text strokes in pixels
- **`left_align`** *(string | number)* вҖ” Default: `"center"` вҖ” Left-to-right alignment of the text. Can be a string ('left', 'center', 'right') or a float (0.0-1.0)
- **`top_align`** *(string | number)* вҖ” Default: `"center"` вҖ” Top-to-bottom alignment of the text. Can be a string ('top', 'center', 'bottom') or a float (0.0-1.0)
- **`refresh_interval`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.5` вҖ” Number of seconds the captions should stay on screen. A higher number will also result in more text being displayed at once.
  Range: min: 0.5, max: 3

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the caption .mp4 video.

---

## fal-ai/ben/v2/video

**Title:** Ben-Video-Bg-Rm
**Description:** A model for high quality and smooth background removal for videos.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ben/v2/video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** segmentation, background removal

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Ben2InputVideo`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of video to be used for background removal.
- **`background_color`** *(array)* вҖ” Optional RGB values (0-255) for the background color. If not provided, the background will be transparent. For ex: [0, 0, 0]
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"mp4"` вҖ” Output video format. Use "webm" for true transparency support (VP9 codec with alpha channel). MP4 format does not support transparency and will render transparent areas as black.
  Possible values: `mp4`, `webm`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducible generation.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Ben2OutputVideo`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated Image. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        

---

## fal-ai/birefnet/v2/video

**Title:** Birefnet
**Description:** Video background removal version of bilateral reference framework (BiRefNet) for high-resolution dichotomous image segmentation (DIS)

**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/birefnet/v2/video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** utility, editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `VideoInputV2`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"General Use (Light)"` вҖ” 
            Model to use for background removal.
            The 'General Use (Light)' model is the original model used in the BiRefNet repository.
            The 'General Use (Light 2K)' model is the original model used in the BiRefNet repository but trained with 2K images.
            The 'General Use (Heavy)' model is a slower but more accurate model.
            The 'Matting' model is a model trained specifically for matting images.
            The 'Portrait' model is a model trained specifically for portrait images.
            The 'General Use (Dynamic)' model supports dynamic resolutions from 256x256 to 2304x2304.
            The 'General Use (Light)' model is recommended for most use cases.

            The corresponding models are as follows:
            - 'General Use (Light)': BiRefNet
            - 'General Use (Light 2K)': BiRefNet_lite-2K
            - 'General Use (Heavy)': BiRefNet_lite
            - 'Matting': BiRefNet-matting
            - 'Portrait': BiRefNet-portrait
            - 'General Use (Dynamic)': BiRefNet_dynamic
        
  Possible values: `General Use (Light)`, `General Use (Light 2K)`, `General Use (Heavy)`, `Matting`, `Portrait`, `General Use (Dynamic)`
- **`operating_resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1024x1024"` вҖ” The resolution to operate on. The higher the resolution, the more accurate the output will be for high res input images. The '2304x2304' option is only available for the 'General Use (Dynamic)' model.
  Possible values: `1024x1024`, `2048x2048`, `2304x2304`
- **`output_mask`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to output the mask used to remove the background
- **`refine_foreground`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to refine the foreground using the estimated mask
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video to remove background from
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `VideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” Video with background removed
- **`mask_video`** *(?)* вҖ” Mask used to remove the background

---

## fal-ai/bytedance-upscaler/upscale/video

**Title:** Bytedance Upscaler
**Description:** Upscale videos with Bytedance's video upscaler.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bytedance-upscaler/upscale/video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** upscaler, video, bytedance

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `UpscaleInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to upscale.
- **`target_resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1080p"` вҖ” The target resolution of the video to upscale.
  Possible values: `1080p`, `2k`, `4k`
- **`target_fps`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"30fps"` вҖ” The target FPS of the video to upscale.
  Possible values: `30fps`, `60fps`
- **`enhancement_preset`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"general"` вҖ” The enhancement preset optimized for specific video scenarios. 'general' is a general-purpose template, 'ugc' targets user-generated short videos, 'short_series' is for short dramas, 'aigc' is for AI-generated content, and 'old_film' is for classic film restoration.
  Possible values: `general`, `ugc`, `short_series`, `aigc`, `old_film`
- **`enhancement_tier`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"standard"` вҖ” The enhancement quality tier. 'fast' provides essential upscaling with good speed, 'standard' uses adaptive algorithms for better visual texture, 'pro' uses large-model restoration for cinematic quality (longer processing time), and 10 times the cost of `standard` and `fast`.
  Possible values: `fast`, `standard`, `pro`
- **`scale_ratio`** *(number)* вҖ” The scaling ratio for the output video resolution. When set, overrides target_resolution and scales the input resolution by this factor (e.g., 2.0 doubles the resolution). Range: 1.1 to 10.0. Please note that this is valid only up to 4k resolution, and trying to scale beyond 4k will result in an error. (4k is defined as having atotal pixel count of 3840x2160).
- **`fidelity`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The enhancement intensity. 'high' applies mild enhancement while keeping visual texture close to the source video. 'medium' provides a balanced image quality enhancement.
  Possible values: `high`, `medium`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `UpscaleOutput`
- **`duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Duration of audio input/video output as used for billing.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” Generated video file

---

## fal-ai/bytedance/dreamactor/v2

**Title:** Bytedance
**Description:** Transfer motion from a video to characters in an image using Dreamactor v2. Great performance for non-human and multiple characters
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/bytedance/dreamactor/v2`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** motion-control, dreamactor

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `DreamActor2Input`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `video_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the reference image to animate. Supports real people, animation, pets, etc. Format: jpeg, jpg or png. Max size: 4.7 MB. Resolution: between 480x480 and 1920x1080 (larger images will be proportionally reduced).
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the driving template video providing motion, facial expressions, and lip movement reference. Max duration: 30 seconds. Format: mp4, mov or webm. Resolution: between 200x200 and 2048x1440. Supports full face and body driving.
- **`trim_first_second`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to crop the first second of the output video. The output has a 1-second transition at the beginning; enable this to remove it.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `DreamActor2Output`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” Generated video file.

---

## fal-ai/cogvideox-5b/video-to-video

**Title:** CogVideoX-5B
**Description:** Generate videos from videos and prompts using CogVideoX-5B
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/cogvideox-5b/video-to-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `VideoToVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video from.
- **`video_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `{"width":720,"height":480}` вҖ” The size of the generated video.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate video from
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. We currently support one lora.
        
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of the model
            will output the same video every time.
        
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related video to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`use_rife`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Use RIFE for video interpolation
- **`export_fps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `16` вҖ” The target FPS of the video
  Range: min: 4, max: 32
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The video to generate the video from.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.8` вҖ” The strength to use for Video to Video.  1.0 completely remakes the video while 0.0 preserves the original.
  Range: min: 0.05, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`timings`** *(object)*
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            Seed of the generated video. It will be the same value of the one passed in the
            input or the randomly generated that was used in case none was passed.
        
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generating the video.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The URL to the generated video

---

## fal-ai/controlnext

**Title:** ControlNeXt SVD
**Description:** Animate a reference image with a driving video using ControlNeXt.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/controlnext`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** animation, stylized

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ControlNeXtInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `video_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the reference image.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video.
- **`height`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1024` вҖ” Height of the output video.
  Range: min: 64, max: 1024
- **`width`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `576` вҖ” Width of the output video.
  Range: min: 64, max: 1024
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Guidance scale for the diffusion process.
  Range: min: 0.1, max: 10
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” Number of inference steps.
  Range: min: 1, max: 100
- **`max_frame_num`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `240` вҖ” Maximum number of frames to process.
  Range: min: 1, max: 1000
- **`batch_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `24` вҖ” Number of frames to process in each batch.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`overlap`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `6` вҖ” Number of overlapping frames between batches.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`sample_stride`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Stride for sampling frames from the input video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`decode_chunk_size`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Chunk size for decoding frames.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`motion_bucket_id`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `127` вҖ” Motion bucket ID for the pipeline.
  Range: min: 0, max: 255
- **`fps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `7` вҖ” Frames per second for the output video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`controlnext_cond_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Condition scale for ControlNeXt.
  Range: min: 0.1, max: 10

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ControlNeXtOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.

---

## fal-ai/cosmos-predict-2.5/video-to-video

**Title:** Cosmos Predict 2.5 2B
**Description:** Generate video from text and videos using NVIDIA's 2B Cosmos Post-Trained Model
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/cosmos-predict-2.5/video-to-video`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `VideoToVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt describing the video to generate.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"The video captures a series of frames showing ugly scenes, static with no motion, motion blur, over-saturation, shaky footage, low resolution, grainy texture, pixelated images, poorly lit areas, underexposed and overexposed scenes, poor color balance, washed out colors, choppy sequences, jerky movements, low frame rate, artifacting, color banding, unnatural transitions, outdated special effects, fake elements, unconvincing visuals, poorly edited content, jump cuts, visual noise, and flickering. Overall, the video is of poor quality."` вҖ” A negative prompt to guide generation away from undesired content.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `93` вҖ” Number of frames to generate. Must be between 9 and 93.
  Range: min: 9, max: 93
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `35` вҖ” Number of denoising steps. More steps yield higher quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale. Higher values increase prompt adherence.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducible generation.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The format of the output video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the output video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video to use as conditioning.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `VideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The random seed used for generation.

---

## fal-ai/depth-anything-video

**Title:** Depth Anything Video
**Description:** Generates depth maps from video using Video Depth Anything (CVPR 2025). Produces per-frame depth estimation with temporal consistency across frames. Supports 3 model sizes (Small, Base, Large), 5 colormaps including grayscale, side-by-side comparison with the original video, and raw depth export as .npz. Useful for 3D reconstruction, video effects, compositing, and scene understanding.    
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/depth-anything-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video to video, motion, edit

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `DepthAnythingVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video to estimate depth for.
- **`model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"VDA-Large"` вҖ” Depth estimation model size. VDA-Large = best quality, VDA-Small = fastest.
  Possible values: `VDA-Small`, `VDA-Base`, `VDA-Large`
- **`colormap`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"grayscale"` вҖ” Colormap for depth visualization. 'turbo' (recommended) shows near=warm, far=cool. 'grayscale' for raw normalized depth. 'inferno'/'magma' for perceptually uniform. 'viridis' for colorblind-friendly.
  Possible values: `grayscale`, `turbo`, `inferno`, `magma`, `viridis`
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Output resolution. 'auto' preserves input (max 1080p). Options: 'auto', '360p', '480p', '720p', '1080p'.
  Possible values: `auto`, `360p`, `480p`, `720p`, `1080p`
- **`max_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Max frames to process (max 1800). None = up to 1800.
- **`output_fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Output video FPS. None = same as input.
- **`side_by_side`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Output original | depth comparison video.
- **`include_raw_depths`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Export raw float32 depths as .npz file with: 'depths' [N,H,W], 'min_depth', 'max_depth', 'fps', 'model', 'shape'.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `DepthAnythingVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” Depth visualization video (MP4, H.264).
- **`raw_depths`** *(?)* вҖ” Raw depth values as .npz (if include_raw_depths=True).

---

- **`do_lipsync`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to lip sync the audio to the video

  Possible values: `full-pose`, `body-pose`, `face-pose`, `hand-pose`, `face-hand-mask`, `face-mask`, `hand-mask`

## fal-ai/editto

**Title:** Editto
**Description:** Edit videos using instruction-based prompting using Editto model!
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/editto`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-edit, wan-vace

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `EdittoInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to guide video generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"letterboxing, borders, black bars, bright colors, overexposed, static, blurred details, subtitles, style, artwork, painting, picture, still, overall gray, worst quality, low quality, JPEG compression residue, ugly, incomplete, extra fingers, poorly drawn hands, poorly drawn faces, deformed, disfigured, malformed limbs, fused fingers, still picture, cluttered background, three legs, many people in the background, walking backwards"` вҖ” Negative prompt for video generation.
- **`match_input_num_frames`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the number of frames in the generated video will match the number of frames in the input video. If false, the number of frames will be determined by the num_frames parameter.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `81` вҖ” Number of frames to generate. Must be between 81 to 241 (inclusive).
  Range: min: 17, max: 241
- **`match_input_frames_per_second`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the frames per second of the generated video will match the input video. If false, the frames per second will be determined by the frames_per_second parameter.
- **`frames_per_second`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `16` вҖ” Frames per second of the generated video. Must be between 5 to 30. Ignored if match_input_frames_per_second is true.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `240p`, `360p`, `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `16:9`, `1:1`, `9:16`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Guidance scale for classifier-free guidance. Higher values encourage the model to generate images closely related to the text prompt.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`sampler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"unipc"` вҖ” Sampler to use for video generation.
  Possible values: `unipc`, `dpm++`, `euler`
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Shift parameter for video generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 15
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the source video file. Required for inpainting.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`acceleration`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration to use for inference. Options are 'none' or 'regular'. Accelerated inference will very slightly affect output, but will be significantly faster.
  Possible values: `none`, `low`, `regular`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`num_interpolated_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Number of frames to interpolate between the original frames. A value of 0 means no interpolation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`temporal_downsample_factor`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Temporal downsample factor for the video. This is an integer value that determines how many frames to skip in the video. A value of 0 means no downsampling. For each downsample factor, one upsample factor will automatically be applied.
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`enable_auto_downsample`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the model will automatically temporally downsample the video to an appropriate frame length for the model, then will interpolate it back to the original frame length.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`return_frames_zip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, also return a ZIP file containing all generated frames.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `EdittoOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation.
- **`frames_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” ZIP archive of all video frames if requested.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated image to video file.

---

## fal-ai/fast-animatediff/turbo/video-to-video

**Title:** AnimateDiff Turbo
**Description:** Re-animate your videos in lightning speed!
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/fast-animatediff/turbo/video-to-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** animation, stylized, turbo

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `AnimateDiffV2VTurboInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video.
- **`first_n_seconds`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The first N number of seconds of video to animate.
  Range: min: 2, max: 4
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"(bad quality, worst quality:1.2), ugly faces, bad anime"` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `12` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform. 4-12 is recommended for turbo mode.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” The strength of the input video in the final output.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`fps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” Number of frames per second to extract from the video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 16

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `AnimateDiffV2VOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” Generated video file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed used for generating the video.

---

## fal-ai/fast-animatediff/video-to-video

**Title:** AnimateDiff
**Description:** Re-animate your videos!
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/fast-animatediff/video-to-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** animation, stylized

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `AnimateDiffV2VInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video.
- **`first_n_seconds`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The first N number of seconds of video to animate.
  Range: min: 2, max: 4
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to use for generating the image. Be as descriptive as possible for best results.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"(bad quality, worst quality:1.2), ugly faces, bad anime"` вҖ” 
            The negative prompt to use. Use it to address details that you don't want
            in the image. This could be colors, objects, scenery and even the small details
            (e.g. moustache, blurry, low resolution).
        
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The number of inference steps to perform.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” The strength of the input video in the final output.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7.5` вҖ” 
            The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt when looking for a related image to show you.
        
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” 
            The same seed and the same prompt given to the same version of Stable Diffusion
            will output the same image every time.
        
- **`fps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” Number of frames per second to extract from the video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 16
- **`motions`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” The motions to apply to the video.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `AnimateDiffV2VOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” Generated video file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed used for generating the video.

---

## fal-ai/ffmpeg-api/compose

**Title:** FFmpeg API Compose
**Description:** Compose videos from multiple media sources using FFmpeg API.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ffmpeg-api/compose`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** ffmpeg

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Input`
**Required fields:** `tracks`

- **`tracks`** *(list<Track>)* вҖ” List of tracks to be combined into the final media

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ComposeOutput`
- **`thumbnail_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video's thumbnail image
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the processed video file

---

## fal-ai/ffmpeg-api/merge-audio-video

**Title:** Ffmpeg Api Merge Audio-Video
**Description:** Merge videos with standalone audio files or audio from video files.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ffmpeg-api/merge-audio-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** ffmpeg

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `CombineInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`, `audio_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video file to use as the video track
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the audio file to use as the audio track
- **`start_offset`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Offset in seconds for when the audio should start relative to the video
  Range: min: 0

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `CombineOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” Output video with merged audio.

---

## fal-ai/ffmpeg-api/merge-videos

**Title:** Ffmpeg Api
**Description:** Use ffmpeg capabilities to merge 2 or more videos.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ffmpeg-api/merge-videos`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MergeVideosInput`
**Required fields:** `video_urls`

- **`video_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” List of video URLs to merge in order
- **`target_fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Target FPS for the output video. If not provided, uses the lowest FPS from input videos.
- **`resolution`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Resolution of the final video. Width and height must be between 512 and 2048.
  Possible values: `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MergeVideosOutput`
- **`metadata`** *(object)* вҖ” Metadata about the merged video including original video info
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” Merged video file

---

## fal-ai/film/video

**Title:** FILM
**Description:** Interpolate videos with FILM - Frame Interpolation for Large Motion
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/film/video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** interpolation

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FILMVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to use for interpolation.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of frames to generate between the input video frames.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`use_scene_detection`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If True, the input video will be split into scenes before interpolation. This removes smear frames between scenes, but can result in false positives if the scene detection is not accurate. If False, the entire video will be treated as a single scene.
- **`use_calculated_fps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If True, the function will use the calculated FPS of the input video multiplied by the number of frames to determine the output FPS. If False, the passed FPS will be used.
- **`fps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” Frames per second for the output video. Only applicable if use_calculated_fps is False.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`loop`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If True, the final frame will be looped back to the first frame to create a seamless loop. If False, the final frame will not loop back.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the output video. Only applicable if output_type is 'video'.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the output video. Only applicable if output_type is 'video'.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `FILMVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file with interpolated frames.

---

## fal-ai/flashvsr/upscale/video

**Title:** Flashvsr
**Description:** Upscale your videos using FlashVSR with the fastest speeds!
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/flashvsr/upscale/video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** upscale, video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FlashVSRPlusVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`upscale_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Upscaling factor to be used.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The random seed used for the generation process.
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned inline and not stored in history.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The input video to be upscaled
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration mode for VAE decoding. Options: regular (best quality), high (balanced), full (fastest). More accerleation means longer duration videos can be processed too.
  Possible values: `regular`, `high`, `full`
- **`color_fix`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Color correction enabled.
- **`quality`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `70` вҖ” Quality level for tile blending (0-100). Controls overlap between tiles to prevent grid artifacts. Higher values provide better quality with more overlap. Recommended: 70-85 for high-res videos, 50-70 for faster processing.
  Range: min: 0, max: 100
- **`preserve_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Copy the original audio tracks into the upscaled video using FFmpeg when possible.
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The format of the output video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`output_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the output video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`output_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the output video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `FlashVSRPlusVideoOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The random seed used for the generation process.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” Upscaled video file after processing

---

`Spanish (Nicaragua)`, `Spanish (Panama)`, `Spanish (Paraguay)`, `Spanish (Peru)`, `Spanish (Puerto Rico)`, `Spanish (Spain)`, `Spanish (United States)`, `Spanish (Uruguay)`, `Spanish (Venezuela)`, `Sundanese (Indonesia)`, `Swahili (Kenya)`, `Swahili (Tanzania)`, `Swedish (Sweden)`, `Tamil (India)`, `Tamil (Malaysia)`, `Tamil (Singapore)`, `Tamil (Sri Lanka)`, `Telugu (India)`, `Thai (Thailand)`, `Turkish (TГјrkiye)`, `Ukrainian (Ukraine)`, `Urdu (India)`, `Urdu (Pakistan)`, `Uzbek (Latin, Uzbekistan)`, `Vietnamese (Vietnam)`, `Welsh (United Kingdom)`, `Zulu (South Africa)`, `English - Your Accent`, `English - American Accent`
- **`translate_audio_only`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Translate only the audio, ignore the faces and only translate the voice track
- **`speaker_num`** *(integer)* вҖ” Number of speakers in the video
- **`enable_dynamic_duration`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable dynamic duration to enhance conversational fluidity between languages with different speaking rates

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `VideoTranslateOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The translated video file

## fal-ai/heygen/v3/lipsync/precision

**Title:** Heygen Lipsync - Precision
**Description:** Replace or dub audio on an existing video with high-accuracy avatar-inference lip-sync.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/heygen/v3/lipsync/precision`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** lipsync, stylized, transform, 

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `V3LipsyncBaseRequest`
**Required fields:** `video_url`, `audio_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the source video to lip-sync.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the replacement audio file.
- **`title`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional title for the lipsync job.
- **`enable_caption`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Generate captions in the output video.
- **`enable_dynamic_duration`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Allow duration adjustment to match the new audio.
- **`disable_music_track`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Remove background music from the source video.
- **`enable_speech_enhancement`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Enhance the replacement audio quality.
- **`start_time`** *(number)* вҖ” Start time in seconds for partial lipsync.
- **`end_time`** *(number)* вҖ” End time in seconds for partial lipsync.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `V3LipsyncOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The lip-synced video file with new audio synced.
- **`caption_file`** *(?)* вҖ” Generated caption file when enable_caption is enabled and HeyGen returns one.

---

## fal-ai/heygen/v3/lipsync/speed

**Title:** Heygen Lipsync - Speed
**Description:** Replace or dub audio on an existing video with fast audio-only lip-sync.

**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/heygen/v3/lipsync/speed`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** stylized, transform, lipsync

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `V3LipsyncBaseRequest`
**Required fields:** `video_url`, `audio_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the source video to lip-sync.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the replacement audio file.
- **`title`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional title for the lipsync job.
- **`enable_caption`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Generate captions in the output video.
- **`enable_dynamic_duration`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Allow duration adjustment to match the new audio.
- **`disable_music_track`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Remove background music from the source video.
- **`enable_speech_enhancement`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Enhance the replacement audio quality.
- **`start_time`** *(number)* вҖ” Start time in seconds for partial lipsync.
- **`end_time`** *(number)* вҖ” End time in seconds for partial lipsync.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `V3LipsyncOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The lip-synced video file with new audio synced.
- **`caption_file`** *(?)* вҖ” Generated caption file when enable_caption is enabled and HeyGen returns one.

## fal-ai/hunyuan-video-foley

**Title:** Hunyuan Video Foley
**Description:** Use the capabilities of the hunyuan foley model to bring life to your videos by adding sound effect to them.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/hunyuan-video-foley`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-to-video, add-sound

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `HunyuanFoleyRequest`
**Required fields:** `video_url`, `text_prompt`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to generate audio for.
- **`text_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text description of the desired audio (optional).
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"noisy, harsh"` вҖ” Negative prompt to avoid certain audio characteristics.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4.5` вҖ” Guidance scale for audio generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `50` вҖ” Number of inference steps for generation.
  Range: min: 10, max: 100
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducible generation.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `HunyuanFoleyResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” List of generated video files with audio.

---

## fal-ai/hunyuan-video-lora/video-to-video

**Title:** Hunyuan Video LoRA Inference (Video-to-Video)
**Description:** Hunyuan Video is an Open video generation model with high visual quality, motion diversity, text-video alignment, and generation stability. Use this endpoint to generate videos from videos.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/hunyuan-video-lora/video-to-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video to video, motion, lora

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `HunyuanV2VRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed to use for generating the video.
- **`pro_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” By default, generations are done with 35 steps. Pro mode does 55 steps which results in higher quality videos but will take more time and cost 2x more billing units.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"16:9"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the video to generate.
  Possible values: `16:9`, `9:16`
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” The resolution of the video to generate.
  Possible values: `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`num_frames`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `129` вҖ” The number of frames to generate.
  Possible values: `129`, `85`
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`loras`** *(list<LoraWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” 
            The LoRAs to use for the image generation. You can use any number of LoRAs
            and they will be merged together to generate the final image.
        
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.75` вҖ” Strength of video-to-video
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `HunyuanV2VResponse`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generating the video.
- **`video`** *()*

---

## fal-ai/hunyuan-video/video-to-video

**Title:** Hunyuan Video (Video-to-Video)
**Description:** Hunyuan Video is an Open video generation model with high visual quality, motion diversity, text-video alignment, and generation stability. Use this endpoint to generate videos from videos.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/hunyuan-video/video-to-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video to video, motion

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `HunyuanV2VRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed to use for generating the video.
- **`pro_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” By default, generations are done with 35 steps. Pro mode does 55 steps which results in higher quality videos but will take more time and cost 2x more billing units.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"16:9"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the video to generate.
  Possible values: `16:9`, `9:16`
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” The resolution of the video to generate.
  Possible values: `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`num_frames`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `129` вҖ” The number of frames to generate.
  Possible values: `129`, `85`
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video input.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.85` вҖ” Strength for Video-to-Video
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `HunyuanT2VResponse`
- **`video`** *()*
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generating the video.

---

## fal-ai/infinitalk

**Title:** Infinitalk
**Description:** Infinitalk model generates a talking avatar video from an image and audio file. The avatar lip-syncs to the provided audio with natural facial expressions.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/infinitalk`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** stylized, transform

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `InfiniTalkSingleAudioRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `audio_url`, `prompt`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input image. If the input image does not match the chosen aspect ratio, it is resized and center cropped.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the audio file.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to guide video generation.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `145` вҖ” Number of frames to generate. Must be between 41 to 721.
  Range: min: 41, max: 721
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"480p"` вҖ” Resolution of the video to generate. Must be either 480p or 720p.
  Possible values: `480p`, `720p`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `42` вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use for generation.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `AvatarSingleAudioResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.

---

## fal-ai/infinitalk/video-to-video

**Title:** Infinitalk
**Description:** Infinitalk model generates a talking avatar video from an image and audio file. The avatar lip-syncs to the provided audio with natural facial expressions.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/infinitalk/video-to-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `InfiniTalkVid2VidAudioRequest`
**Required fields:** `video_url`, `audio_url`, `prompt`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the audio file.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to guide video generation.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `145` вҖ” Number of frames to generate. Must be between 81 to 129 (inclusive). If the number of frames is greater than 81, the video will be generated with 1.25x more billing units.
  Range: min: 41, max: 241
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"480p"` вҖ” Resolution of the video to generate. Must be either 480p or 720p.
  Possible values: `480p`, `720p`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `42` вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use for generation.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `InfinitalkVid2VidResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.

---

**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema
_No schema available_

## fal-ai/kling-video/o1/standard/video-to-video/reference

**Title:** Kling O1 Reference Video to Video [Standard]
**Description:** Kling O1 Omni generates new shots guided by an input reference video, preserving cinematic language such as motion, and camera style to produce seamless scene continuity.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/kling-video/o1/standard/video-to-video/reference`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `OmniV2VReferenceInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Use @Element1, @Element2 to reference elements and @Image1, @Image2 to reference images in order.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Reference video URL. Only .mp4/.mov formats supported, 3-10 seconds duration, 720-2160px resolution, max 200MB.

Max file size: 200.0MB, Min width: 720px, Min height: 720px, Max width: 2160px, Max height: 2160px, Min duration: 3.0s, Max duration: 10.05s, Min FPS: 24.0, Max FPS: 60.0, Timeout: 30.0s
- **`keep_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to keep the original audio from the video.
- **`image_urls`** *(array)* вҖ” Reference images for style/appearance. Reference in prompt as @Image1, @Image2, etc. Maximum 4 total (elements + reference images) when using video.
- **`elements`** *(array)* вҖ” Elements (characters/objects) to include. Reference in prompt as @Element1, @Element2, etc. Maximum 4 total (elements + reference images) when using video.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the generated video frame. If 'auto', the aspect ratio will be determined automatically based on the input video, and the closest aspect ratio to the input video will be used.
  Possible values: `auto`, `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`
- **`duration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"5"` вҖ” Video duration in seconds.
  Possible values: `3`, `4`, `5`, `6`, `7`, `8`, `9`, `10`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `OmniV2VReferenceOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.

---

**Description:** Kling O3 Omni generates new shots guided by an input reference video, preserving cinematic language such as motion, and camera style to produce seamless scene continuity.

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Reference video URL. Only .mp4/.mov formats, 3-10s duration, 720-2160px resolution, max 200MB.
- **`image_urls`** *(array)* вҖ” Reference images for style/appearance. Reference in prompt as @Image1, @Image2, etc. Maximum 4 total (elements + reference images) when using video.

- **`shot_type`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"customize"` вҖ” The type of multi-shot video generation.

## fal-ai/kling-video/v2.6/pro/motion-control

**Title:** Kling Video v2.6 Motion Control [Pro]
**Description:** Transfer movements from a reference video to any character image. Pro mode delivers higher quality output, ideal for complex dance moves and gestures.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/kling-video/v2.6/pro/motion-control`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MotionControlRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `video_url`, `character_orientation`

- **`prompt`** *(string)*
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Reference image URL. The characters, backgrounds, and other elements in the generated video are based on this reference image. Characters should have clear body proportions, avoid occlusion, and occupy more than 5% of the image area.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Reference video URL. The character actions in the generated video will be consistent with this reference video. Should contain a realistic style character with entire body or upper body visible, including head, without obstruction. Duration limit depends on character_orientation: 10s max for 'image', 30s max for 'video'.
- **`keep_original_sound`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to keep the original sound from the reference video.
- **`character_orientation`** *(enum)* вҖ” Controls whether the output character's orientation matches the reference image or video. 'video': orientation matches reference video - better for complex motions (max 30s). 'image': orientation matches reference image - better for following camera movements (max 10s).
  Possible values: `image`, `video`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MotionControlOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video

---

## fal-ai/kling-video/v2.6/standard/motion-control

**Title:** Kling Video v2.6 Motion Control [Standard]
**Description:** Transfer movements from a reference video to any character image. Cost-effective mode for motion transfer, perfect for portraits and simple animations.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/kling-video/v2.6/standard/motion-control`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MotionControlRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `video_url`, `character_orientation`

- **`prompt`** *(string)*
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Reference image URL. The characters, backgrounds, and other elements in the generated video are based on this reference image. Characters should have clear body proportions, avoid occlusion, and occupy more than 5% of the image area.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Reference video URL. The character actions in the generated video will be consistent with this reference video. Should contain a realistic style character with entire body or upper body visible, including head, without obstruction. Duration limit depends on character_orientation: 10s max for 'image', 30s max for 'video'.
- **`keep_original_sound`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to keep the original sound from the reference video.
- **`character_orientation`** *(enum)* вҖ” Controls whether the output character's orientation matches the reference image or video. 'video': orientation matches reference video - better for complex motions (max 30s). 'image': orientation matches reference image - better for following camera movements (max 10s).
  Possible values: `image`, `video`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MotionControlOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video

---

## fal-ai/kling-video/v3/pro/motion-control

**Title:** Kling Video
**Description:** Transfer movements from a reference video to any character image. Cost-effective mode for motion transfer, perfect for portraits and simple animations.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/kling-video/v3/pro/motion-control`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** stylized, transform, editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MotionControlV3ProRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `video_url`, `character_orientation`

- **`prompt`** *(string)*
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Reference image URL. The characters, backgrounds, and other elements in the generated video are based on this reference image. Characters should have clear body proportions, avoid occlusion, and occupy more than 5% of the image area.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Reference video URL. The character actions in the generated video will be consistent with this reference video. Should contain a realistic style character with entire body or upper body visible, including head, without obstruction. Duration limit depends on character_orientation: 10s max for 'image', 30s max for 'video'.
- **`keep_original_sound`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to keep the original sound from the reference video.
- **`character_orientation`** *(enum)* вҖ” Controls whether the output character's orientation matches the reference image or video. 'video': orientation matches reference video - better for complex motions (max 30s). 'image': orientation matches reference image - better for following camera movements (max 10s).
  Possible values: `image`, `video`
- **`elements`** *(array)* вҖ” Optional element for facial consistency binding. Upload a facial element to enhance identity preservation in the generated video. Only 1 element is supported. Reference in prompt as @Element1. Element binding is only supported when character_orientation is 'video'.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MotionControlV3ProOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video

---

## fal-ai/kling-video/v3/standard/motion-control

**Title:** Kling Video
**Description:** Transfer movements from a reference video to any character image. Cost-effective mode for motion transfer, perfect for portraits and simple animations.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/kling-video/v3/standard/motion-control`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** stylized, transform, editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MotionControlV3StandardRequest`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `video_url`, `character_orientation`

- **`prompt`** *(string)*
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Reference image URL. The characters, backgrounds, and other elements in the generated video are based on this reference image. Characters should have clear body proportions, avoid occlusion, and occupy more than 5% of the image area.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Reference video URL. The character actions in the generated video will be consistent with this reference video. Should contain a realistic style character with entire body or upper body visible, including head, without obstruction. Duration limit depends on character_orientation: 10s max for 'image', 30s max for 'video'.
- **`keep_original_sound`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to keep the original sound from the reference video.
- **`character_orientation`** *(enum)* вҖ” Controls whether the output character's orientation matches the reference image or video. 'video': orientation matches reference video - better for complex motions (max 30s). 'image': orientation matches reference image - better for following camera movements (max 10s).
  Possible values: `image`, `video`
- **`elements`** *(array)* вҖ” Optional element for facial consistency binding. Upload a facial element to enhance identity preservation in the generated video. Only 1 element is supported. Reference in prompt as @Element1. Element binding is only supported when character_orientation is 'video'.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MotionControlV3StandardOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video

---

## fal-ai/krea-wan-14b/video-to-video

**Title:** Krea Wan 14B
**Description:** Superfast video model based on Wan 2.1 14b by Krea, excelling at real-time video-editing.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/krea-wan-14b/video-to-video`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `VideoToVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Prompt for the video-to-video generation.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.85` вҖ” Denoising strength for the video-to-video generation. 0.0 preserves the original, 1.0 completely remakes the video.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion. This will use a large language model to expand the prompt with additional details while maintaining the original meaning.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video. Currently, only outputs of 16:9 aspect ratio and 480p resolution are supported. Video duration should be less than 1000 frames at 16fps, and output frames will be 6 plus a multiple of 12, for example 18, 30, 42, etc.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Seed for the video-to-video generation.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `VideoToVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.

---

## fal-ai/latentsync

**Title:** LatentSync
**Description:** LatentSync is a video-to-video model that generates lip sync animations from audio using advanced algorithms for high-quality synchronization.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/latentsync`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** animation, lip sync

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Input`
**Required fields:** `video_url`, `audio_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to generate the lip sync for.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the audio to generate the lip sync for.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Guidance scale for the model inference
  Range: min: 1, max: 2
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for generation. If None, a random seed will be used.
- **`loop_mode`** *(string)* вҖ” Video loop mode when audio is longer than video. Options: pingpong, loop
  Possible values: `pingpong`, `loop`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video with the lip sync.

---

## fal-ai/lightx/recamera

**Title:** Lightx
**Description:** Use the capabilities of lightx to relight and recamera your videos.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/lightx/recamera`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-to-video, recamera, relight

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LightXRecameraRequest`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional text prompt. If omitted, Light-X will auto-caption the video.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`camera`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"traj"` вҖ” Camera control mode.
  Possible values: `traj`, `target`
- **`mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"gradual"` вҖ” Camera motion mode.
  Possible values: `gradual`, `bullet`, `direct`, `dolly-zoom`
- **`trajectory`** *(?)* вҖ” Camera trajectory parameters (required for recamera mode).
- **`target_pose`** *(array)* вҖ” Target camera pose [theta, phi, radius, x, y] (required when camera='target').

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LightXOutput`
- **`input_video`** *(?)* вҖ” Optional: normalized/processed input video (if produced by the pipeline).
- **`viz_video`** *(?)* вҖ” Optional: visualization/debug video (if produced by the pipeline).
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.

---

## fal-ai/lightx/relight

**Title:** Lightx
**Description:** Use tlightx capabilities to relight and recamera your videos.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/lightx/relight`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LightXRelightRequest`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional text prompt. If omitted, Light-X will auto-caption the video.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`relit_cond_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"ic"` вҖ” Relight condition type.
  Possible values: `ic`, `ref`, `hdr`, `bg`
- **`relight_parameters`** *(?)* вҖ” Relighting parameters (required for relight_condition_type='ic'). Not used for 'bg' (which expects a background image URL instead).
- **`relit_cond_img_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of conditioning image. Required for relight_condition_type='ref'/'hdr'. Also required for relight_condition_type='bg' (background image).
- **`ref_id`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Frame index to use as referencen to relight the video with reference.
  Range: min: 0

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LightXOutput`
- **`input_video`** *(?)* вҖ” Optional: normalized/processed input video (if produced by the pipeline).
- **`viz_video`** *(?)* вҖ” Optional: visualization/debug video (if produced by the pipeline).
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.

---

## fal-ai/ltx-2-19b/distilled/extend-video

**Title:** LTX-2 19B Distilled
**Description:** Extend videos with audio using LTX-2 Distilled
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltx-2-19b/distilled/extend-video`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LTX2DistilledExtendVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video from.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to extend.
- **`end_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as the end of the extended video.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `121` вҖ” The number of frames to generate.
  Range: min: 9, max: 481
- **`video_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The size of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`generate_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate audio for the video.
- **`use_multiscale`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use multi-scale generation. If True, the model will generate the video at a smaller scale first, then use the smaller video to guide the generation of a video at or above your requested size. This results in better coherence and details.
- **`match_input_fps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When true, match the output FPS to the input video's FPS instead of using the default target FPS.
- **`fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The frames per second of the generated video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`, `full`
- **`camera_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Possible values: `dolly_in`, `dolly_out`, `dolly_left`, `dolly_right`, `jib_up`, `jib_down`, `static`, `none`
- **`camera_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"blurry, out of focus, overexposed, underexposed, low contrast, washed out colors, excessive noise, grainy texture, poor lighting, flickering, motion blur, distorted proportions, unnatural skin tones, deformed facial features, asymmetrical face, missing facial features, extra limbs, disfigured hands, wrong hand count, artifacts around text, inconsistent perspective, camera shake, incorrect depth of field, background too sharp, background clutter, distracting reflections, harsh shadows, inconsistent lighting direction, color banding, cartoonish rendering, 3D CGI look, unrealistic materials, uncanny valley effect, incorrect ethnicity, wrong gender, exaggerated expressions, wrong gaze direction, mismatched lip sync, silent or muted audio, distorted voice, robotic voice, echo, background noise, off-sync audio,incorrect dialogue, added dialogue, repetitive speech, jittery movement, awkward pauses, incorrect timing, unnatural transitions, inconsistent framing, tilted camera, flat lighting, inconsistent tone, cinematic oversaturation, stylized filters, or AI artifacts."` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the video from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`extend_direction`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"forward"` вҖ” Direction to extend the video. 'forward' extends from the end of the video, 'backward' extends from the beginning.
  Possible values: `forward`, `backward`
- **`num_context_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The number of frames to use as context for the extension.
  Range: min: 0, max: 121
- **`video_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Video conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Audio conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`end_image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the end image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTX2ExtendVideoOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the random number generator.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.

---

**Title:** LTX-2 19B Distilled
**Description:** Extend videos with audio using LTX-2 Distilled and custom LoRA

**Category:** video-to-video

**Required fields:** `loras`, `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to extend.
- **`end_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as the end of the extended video.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `121` вҖ” The number of frames to generate.
  Range: min: 9, max: 481

- **`generate_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate audio for the video.
- **`use_multiscale`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use multi-scale generation. If True, the model will generate the video at a smaller scale first, then use the smaller video to guide the generation of a video at or above your requested size. This results in better coherence and details.
- **`match_input_fps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When true, match the output FPS to the input video's FPS instead of using the default target FPS.
- **`fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The frames per second of the generated video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`, `full`
- **`camera_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Possible values: `dolly_in`, `dolly_out`, `dolly_left`, `dolly_right`, `jib_up`, `jib_down`, `static`, `none`
- **`camera_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"blurry, out of focus, overexposed, underexposed, low contrast, washed out colors, excessive noise, grainy texture, poor lighting, flickering, motion blur, distorted proportions, unnatural skin tones, deformed facial features, asymmetrical face, missing facial features, extra limbs, disfigured hands, wrong hand count, artifacts around text, inconsistent perspective, camera shake, incorrect depth of field, background too sharp, background clutter, distracting reflections, harsh shadows, inconsistent lighting direction, color banding, cartoonish rendering, 3D CGI look, unrealistic materials, uncanny valley effect, incorrect ethnicity, wrong gender, exaggerated expressions, wrong gaze direction, mismatched lip sync, silent or muted audio, distorted voice, robotic voice, echo, background noise, off-sync audio,incorrect dialogue, added dialogue, repetitive speech, jittery movement, awkward pauses, incorrect timing, unnatural transitions, inconsistent framing, tilted camera, flat lighting, inconsistent tone, cinematic oversaturation, stylized filters, or AI artifacts."` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the video from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`loras`** *(list<LoRAInput>)* вҖ” The LoRAs to use for the generation.
- **`extend_direction`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"forward"` вҖ” Direction to extend the video. 'forward' extends from the end of the video, 'backward' extends from the beginning.
  Possible values: `forward`, `backward`
- **`num_context_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The number of frames to use as context for the extension.
  Range: min: 0, max: 121
- **`video_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Video conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Audio conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`end_image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the end image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTX2ExtendVideoOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the random number generator.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.
