## fal-ai/ltx-2-19b/distilled/video-to-video

**Title:** LTX-2 19B Distilled
**Description:** Generate video with audio from videos using LTX-2 Distilled
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltx-2-19b/distilled/video-to-video`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LTX2DistilledVideoToVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video from.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to generate the video from.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” An optional URL of an audio to use as the audio for the video. If not provided, any audio present in the input video will be used.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” An optional URL of an image to use as the first frame of the video.
- **`end_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as the end of the video.
- **`match_video_length`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When enabled, the number of frames will be calculated based on the video duration and FPS. When disabled, use the specified num_frames.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `121` вҖ” The number of frames to generate.
  Range: min: 9, max: 481
- **`video_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The size of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`generate_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate audio for the video.
- **`use_multiscale`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use multi-scale generation. If True, the model will generate the video at a smaller scale first, then use the smaller video to guide the generation of a video at or above your requested size. This results in better coherence and details.
- **`match_input_fps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When true, match the output FPS to the input video's FPS instead of using the default target FPS.
- **`fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The frames per second of the generated video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`, `full`
- **`camera_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Possible values: `dolly_in`, `dolly_out`, `dolly_left`, `dolly_right`, `jib_up`, `jib_down`, `static`, `none`
- **`camera_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"blurry, out of focus, overexposed, underexposed, low contrast, washed out colors, excessive noise, grainy texture, poor lighting, flickering, motion blur, distorted proportions, unnatural skin tones, deformed facial features, asymmetrical face, missing facial features, extra limbs, disfigured hands, wrong hand count, artifacts around text, inconsistent perspective, camera shake, incorrect depth of field, background too sharp, background clutter, distracting reflections, harsh shadows, inconsistent lighting direction, color banding, cartoonish rendering, 3D CGI look, unrealistic materials, uncanny valley effect, incorrect ethnicity, wrong gender, exaggerated expressions, wrong gaze direction, mismatched lip sync, silent or muted audio, distorted voice, robotic voice, echo, background noise, off-sync audio,incorrect dialogue, added dialogue, repetitive speech, jittery movement, awkward pauses, incorrect timing, unnatural transitions, inconsistent framing, tilted camera, flat lighting, inconsistent tone, cinematic oversaturation, stylized filters, or AI artifacts."` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the video from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`preprocessor`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The preprocessor to use for the video. When a preprocessor is used and `ic_lora_type` is set to `match_preprocessor`, the IC-LoRA will be loaded based on the preprocessor type.
  Possible values: `depth`, `canny`, `pose`, `none`
- **`ic_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"match_preprocessor"` вҖ” The type of IC-LoRA to load. In-Context LoRA weights are used to condition the video based on edge, depth, or pose videos. Only change this from `match_preprocessor` if your videos are already preprocessed (or you are using the detailer.)
  Possible values: `match_preprocessor`, `canny`, `depth`, `pose`, `detailer`, `none`
- **`ic_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the IC-LoRA to use. This allows you to control the strength of the IC-LoRA.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`end_image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the end image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`video_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Video conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Audio conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTX2VideoToVideoOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the random number generator.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.

## fal-ai/ltx-2-19b/distilled/video-to-video/lora

**Title:** LTX-2 19B Distilled
**Description:** Generate video with audio from videos using LTX-2 Distilled and custom LoRA
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltx-2-19b/distilled/video-to-video/lora`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LTX2LoRADistilledVideoToVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `loras`, `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video from.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to generate the video from.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” An optional URL of an audio to use as the audio for the video. If not provided, any audio present in the input video will be used.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” An optional URL of an image to use as the first frame of the video.
- **`end_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as the end of the video.
- **`match_video_length`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When enabled, the number of frames will be calculated based on the video duration and FPS. When disabled, use the specified num_frames.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `121` вҖ” The number of frames to generate.
  Range: min: 9, max: 481
- **`video_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The size of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`generate_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate audio for the video.
- **`use_multiscale`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use multi-scale generation. If True, the model will generate the video at a smaller scale first, then use the smaller video to guide the generation of a video at or above your requested size. This results in better coherence and details.
- **`match_input_fps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When true, match the output FPS to the input video's FPS instead of using the default target FPS.
- **`fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The frames per second of the generated video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`, `full`
- **`camera_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Possible values: `dolly_in`, `dolly_out`, `dolly_left`, `dolly_right`, `jib_up`, `jib_down`, `static`, `none`
- **`camera_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"blurry, out of focus, overexposed, underexposed, low contrast, washed out colors, excessive noise, grainy texture, poor lighting, flickering, motion blur, distorted proportions, unnatural skin tones, deformed facial features, asymmetrical face, missing facial features, extra limbs, disfigured hands, wrong hand count, artifacts around text, inconsistent perspective, camera shake, incorrect depth of field, background too sharp, background clutter, distracting reflections, harsh shadows, inconsistent lighting direction, color banding, cartoonish rendering, 3D CGI look, unrealistic materials, uncanny valley effect, incorrect ethnicity, wrong gender, exaggerated expressions, wrong gaze direction, mismatched lip sync, silent or muted audio, distorted voice, robotic voice, echo, background noise, off-sync audio,incorrect dialogue, added dialogue, repetitive speech, jittery movement, awkward pauses, incorrect timing, unnatural transitions, inconsistent framing, tilted camera, flat lighting, inconsistent tone, cinematic oversaturation, stylized filters, or AI artifacts."` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the video from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`loras`** *(list<LoRAInput>)* вҖ” The LoRAs to use for the generation.
- **`preprocessor`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The preprocessor to use for the video. When a preprocessor is used and `ic_lora_type` is set to `match_preprocessor`, the IC-LoRA will be loaded based on the preprocessor type.
  Possible values: `depth`, `canny`, `pose`, `none`
- **`ic_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"match_preprocessor"` вҖ” The type of IC-LoRA to load. In-Context LoRA weights are used to condition the video based on edge, depth, or pose videos. Only change this from `match_preprocessor` if your videos are already preprocessed (or you are using the detailer.)
  Possible values: `match_preprocessor`, `canny`, `depth`, `pose`, `detailer`, `none`
- **`ic_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the IC-LoRA to use. This allows you to control the strength of the IC-LoRA.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`end_image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the end image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`video_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Video conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Audio conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTX2VideoToVideoOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the random number generator.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.

---

## fal-ai/ltx-2-19b/extend-video

**Title:** LTX-2 19B
**Description:** Extend video with audio using LTX-2
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltx-2-19b/extend-video`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LTX2ExtendVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video from.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to extend.
- **`end_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as the end of the extended video.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `121` вҖ” The number of frames to generate.
  Range: min: 9, max: 481
- **`video_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The size of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`generate_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate audio for the video.
- **`use_multiscale`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use multi-scale generation. If True, the model will generate the video at a smaller scale first, then use the smaller video to guide the generation of a video at or above your requested size. This results in better coherence and details.
- **`match_input_fps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When true, match the output FPS to the input video's FPS instead of using the default target FPS.
- **`fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The frames per second of the generated video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The guidance scale to use.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `40` вҖ” The number of inference steps to use.
  Range: min: 8, max: 50
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`, `full`
- **`camera_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Possible values: `dolly_in`, `dolly_out`, `dolly_left`, `dolly_right`, `jib_up`, `jib_down`, `static`, `none`
- **`camera_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"blurry, out of focus, overexposed, underexposed, low contrast, washed out colors, excessive noise, grainy texture, poor lighting, flickering, motion blur, distorted proportions, unnatural skin tones, deformed facial features, asymmetrical face, missing facial features, extra limbs, disfigured hands, wrong hand count, artifacts around text, inconsistent perspective, camera shake, incorrect depth of field, background too sharp, background clutter, distracting reflections, harsh shadows, inconsistent lighting direction, color banding, cartoonish rendering, 3D CGI look, unrealistic materials, uncanny valley effect, incorrect ethnicity, wrong gender, exaggerated expressions, wrong gaze direction, mismatched lip sync, silent or muted audio, distorted voice, robotic voice, echo, background noise, off-sync audio,incorrect dialogue, added dialogue, repetitive speech, jittery movement, awkward pauses, incorrect timing, unnatural transitions, inconsistent framing, tilted camera, flat lighting, inconsistent tone, cinematic oversaturation, stylized filters, or AI artifacts."` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the video from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`extend_direction`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"forward"` вҖ” Direction to extend the video. 'forward' extends from the end of the video, 'backward' extends from the beginning.
  Possible values: `forward`, `backward`
- **`num_context_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The number of frames to use as context for the extension.
  Range: min: 0, max: 121
- **`video_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Video conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Audio conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`end_image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the end image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTX2ExtendVideoOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the random number generator.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.

- **`end_image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the end image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`video_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Video conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Audio conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTX2VideoToVideoOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the random number generator.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.

## fal-ai/ltx-2-19b/video-to-video/lora

**Title:** LTX-2 19B
**Description:** Generate video with audio from videos using LTX-2 and custom LoRA
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltx-2-19b/video-to-video/lora`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LTX2LoRAVideoToVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `loras`, `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video from.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to generate the video from.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” An optional URL of an audio to use as the audio for the video. If not provided, any audio present in the input video will be used.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” An optional URL of an image to use as the first frame of the video.
- **`end_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as the end of the video.
- **`match_video_length`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When enabled, the number of frames will be calculated based on the video duration and FPS. When disabled, use the specified num_frames.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `121` вҖ” The number of frames to generate.
  Range: min: 9, max: 481
- **`video_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The size of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`generate_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate audio for the video.
- **`use_multiscale`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use multi-scale generation. If True, the model will generate the video at a smaller scale first, then use the smaller video to guide the generation of a video at or above your requested size. This results in better coherence and details.
- **`match_input_fps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When true, match the output FPS to the input video's FPS instead of using the default target FPS.
- **`fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The frames per second of the generated video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The guidance scale to use.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `40` вҖ” The number of inference steps to use.
  Range: min: 8, max: 50
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`, `full`
- **`camera_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Possible values: `dolly_in`, `dolly_out`, `dolly_left`, `dolly_right`, `jib_up`, `jib_down`, `static`, `none`
- **`camera_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"blurry, out of focus, overexposed, underexposed, low contrast, washed out colors, excessive noise, grainy texture, poor lighting, flickering, motion blur, distorted proportions, unnatural skin tones, deformed facial features, asymmetrical face, missing facial features, extra limbs, disfigured hands, wrong hand count, artifacts around text, inconsistent perspective, camera shake, incorrect depth of field, background too sharp, background clutter, distracting reflections, harsh shadows, inconsistent lighting direction, color banding, cartoonish rendering, 3D CGI look, unrealistic materials, uncanny valley effect, incorrect ethnicity, wrong gender, exaggerated expressions, wrong gaze direction, mismatched lip sync, silent or muted audio, distorted voice, robotic voice, echo, background noise, off-sync audio,incorrect dialogue, added dialogue, repetitive speech, jittery movement, awkward pauses, incorrect timing, unnatural transitions, inconsistent framing, tilted camera, flat lighting, inconsistent tone, cinematic oversaturation, stylized filters, or AI artifacts."` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the video from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`loras`** *(list<LoRAInput>)* вҖ” The LoRAs to use for the generation.
- **`preprocessor`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The preprocessor to use for the video. When a preprocessor is used and `ic_lora_type` is set to `match_preprocessor`, the IC-LoRA will be loaded based on the preprocessor type.
  Possible values: `depth`, `canny`, `pose`, `none`
- **`ic_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"match_preprocessor"` вҖ” The type of IC-LoRA to load. In-Context LoRA weights are used to condition the video based on edge, depth, or pose videos. Only change this from `match_preprocessor` if your videos are already preprocessed (or you are using the detailer.)
  Possible values: `match_preprocessor`, `canny`, `depth`, `pose`, `detailer`, `none`
- **`ic_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the IC-LoRA to use. This allows you to control the strength of the IC-LoRA.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`end_image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the end image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`video_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Video conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Audio conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTX2VideoToVideoOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the random number generator.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.

---

## fal-ai/ltx-2.3-22b/distilled/reference-video-to-video

**Title:** LTX-2.3 22B Distilled

**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video from.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to reference.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” An optional URL of an audio to use as the audio for the video. If not provided, any audio present in the input video will be used.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” An optional URL of an image to use as the first frame of the video.
- **`end_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as the end of the video.
- **`match_video_length`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When enabled, the number of frames will be calculated based on the video duration and FPS. When disabled, use the specified num_frames.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `121` вҖ” The number of frames to generate.
  Range: min: 9, max: 481
- **`video_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The size of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`generate_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate audio for the video.
- **`use_multiscale`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use multi-scale generation. If True, the model will generate the video at a smaller scale first, then use the smaller video to guide the generation of a video at or above your requested size. This results in better coherence and details.
- **`match_input_fps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When true, match the output FPS to the input video's FPS instead of using the default target FPS.
- **`fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `24` вҖ” The frames per second of the generated video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"ltx2"` вҖ” The scheduler to use.
  Possible values: `ltx2`, `linear_quadratic`, `beta`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`, `full`
- **`camera_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Possible values: `dolly_in`, `dolly_out`, `dolly_left`, `dolly_right`, `jib_up`, `jib_down`, `static`, `none`
- **`camera_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"news broadcast, 3d animation, computer graphics, pc game, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, watermark, logo, text, on screen text, subtitles, titles, signature, slowmo, static"` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the video from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`distill_lora_second_pass_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The scale of the distill LoRA to use for the second and subsequent passes.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`video_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Video conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Audio conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`preprocessor`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The preprocessor to use for the generation.
  Possible values: `depth`, `canny`, `pose`, `none`
- **`ic_lora_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"union"` вҖ” The IC LoRA type to use for the generation.
  Possible values: `match_preprocessor`, `union`, `detailer`, `none`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTX23ReferenceVideoToVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the random number generator.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the generation.

- **`video_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The size of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`generate_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate audio for the video.
- **`use_multiscale`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use multi-scale generation. If True, the model will generate the video at a smaller scale first, then use the smaller video to guide the generation of a video at or above your requested size. This results in better coherence and details.
- **`match_input_fps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When true, match the output FPS to the input video's FPS instead of using the default target FPS.
- **`fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `24` вҖ” The frames per second of the generated video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"ltx2"` вҖ” The scheduler to use.
  Possible values: `ltx2`, `linear_quadratic`, `beta`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`, `full`
- **`camera_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Possible values: `dolly_in`, `dolly_out`, `dolly_left`, `dolly_right`, `jib_up`, `jib_down`, `static`, `none`
- **`camera_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"news broadcast, 3d animation, computer graphics, pc game, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, watermark, logo, text, on screen text, subtitles, titles, signature, slowmo, static"` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the video from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`loras`** *(list<LoRAInput>)* вҖ” The LoRAs to use for the generation.
- **`distill_lora_second_pass_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The scale of the distill LoRA to use for the second and subsequent passes.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`video_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.01` вҖ” Video conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.01` вҖ” Audio conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTX23VideoToVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the random number generator.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the generation.

## fal-ai/ltx-2.3-22b/extend-video

**Title:** LTX-2.3 22B
**Description:** Extend video with audio using LTX-2.3
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltx-2.3-22b/extend-video`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LTX23ExtendVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video from.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to extend.
- **`end_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as the end of the extended video.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `121` вҖ” The number of frames to generate.
  Range: min: 9, max: 481
- **`video_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The size of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`generate_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate audio for the video.
- **`use_multiscale`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use multi-scale generation. If True, the model will generate the video at a smaller scale first, then use the smaller video to guide the generation of a video at or above your requested size. This results in better coherence and details.
- **`match_input_fps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When true, match the output FPS to the input video's FPS instead of using the default target FPS.
- **`fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `24` вҖ” The frames per second of the generated video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `40` вҖ” The number of inference steps to use.
  Range: min: 8, max: 50
- **`video_cfg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) scale for the video. Higher values result in more consistent and focused video content.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`video_stg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The Spatiotemporal Guidance (STG) scale for the video. Higher values result in more consistent and focused video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`video_rescaling_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” The rescaling scale for the video. Controls the ratio between classifier-free guidance and spatiotemporal guidance.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`video_modality_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The modality scale for the video. Controls the ratio between video and audio modalities.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`audio_cfg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7` вҖ” The Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) scale for the audio. Higher values result in more consistent and focused audio content.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`audio_stg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The Spatiotemporal Guidance (STG) scale for the audio. Higher values result in more consistent and focused audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`audio_rescaling_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” The rescaling scale for the audio. Controls the ratio between classifier-free guidance and spatiotemporal guidance.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_modality_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The modality scale for the audio. Controls the ratio between video and audio modalities.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`gradient_estimation_gamma`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” The gamma of gradient estimation during denoising. Set to 0 to disable.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`use_restart_sampling`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to use restart sampling. This will inject a small amount of noise during each denoising step, which can help improve the quality of the generated video.
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"ltx2"` вҖ” The scheduler to use.
  Possible values: `ltx2`, `linear_quadratic`, `beta`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`, `full`
- **`camera_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Possible values: `dolly_in`, `dolly_out`, `dolly_left`, `dolly_right`, `jib_up`, `jib_down`, `static`, `none`
- **`camera_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"news broadcast, 3d animation, computer graphics, pc game, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, watermark, logo, text, on screen text, subtitles, titles, signature, slowmo, static"` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the video from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`distill_lora_first_pass_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.2` вҖ” The scale of the distill LoRA to use for the first pass. Set to 0 to disable.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`distill_lora_second_pass_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The scale of the distill LoRA to use for the second and subsequent passes.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`extend_direction`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"forward"` вҖ” Direction to extend the video. 'forward' extends from the end of the video, 'backward' extends from the beginning.
  Possible values: `forward`, `backward`
- **`num_context_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The number of frames to use as context for the extension.
  Range: min: 0, max: 121
- **`video_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Video conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Audio conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`end_image_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The strength of the end image to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTX23ExtendVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the random number generator.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the generation.

## fal-ai/ltx-2.3-22b/reference-video-to-video

**Title:** LTX-2.3 22B
**Description:** Generate video with audio from reference video, text and images using LTX-2.3
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltx-2.3-22b/reference-video-to-video`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LTX23ReferenceVideoToVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video from.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to reference.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” An optional URL of an audio to use as the audio for the video. If not provided, any audio present in the input video will be used.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” An optional URL of an image to use as the first frame of the video.
- **`end_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as the end of the video.
- **`match_video_length`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When enabled, the number of frames will be calculated based on the video duration and FPS. When disabled, use the specified num_frames.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `121` вҖ” The number of frames to generate.
  Range: min: 9, max: 481
- **`video_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The size of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`generate_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate audio for the video.
- **`use_multiscale`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use multi-scale generation. If True, the model will generate the video at a smaller scale first, then use the smaller video to guide the generation of a video at or above your requested size. This results in better coherence and details.
- **`match_input_fps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When true, match the output FPS to the input video's FPS instead of using the default target FPS.
- **`fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `24` вҖ” The frames per second of the generated video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `40` вҖ” The number of inference steps to use.
  Range: min: 8, max: 50
- **`video_cfg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) scale for the video. Higher values result in more consistent and focused video content.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`video_stg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The Spatiotemporal Guidance (STG) scale for the video. Higher values result in more consistent and focused video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`video_rescaling_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” The rescaling scale for the video. Controls the ratio between classifier-free guidance and spatiotemporal guidance.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`video_modality_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The modality scale for the video. Controls the ratio between video and audio modalities.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`audio_cfg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7` вҖ” The Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) scale for the audio. Higher values result in more consistent and focused audio content.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`audio_stg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The Spatiotemporal Guidance (STG) scale for the audio. Higher values result in more consistent and focused audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`audio_rescaling_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” The rescaling scale for the audio. Controls the ratio between classifier-free guidance and spatiotemporal guidance.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_modality_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The modality scale for the audio. Controls the ratio between video and audio modalities.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`gradient_estimation_gamma`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” The gamma of gradient estimation during denoising. Set to 0 to disable.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`use_restart_sampling`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to use restart sampling. This will inject a small amount of noise during each denoising step, which can help improve the quality of the generated video.
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"ltx2"` вҖ” The scheduler to use.
  Possible values: `ltx2`, `linear_quadratic`, `beta`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`, `full`
- **`camera_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Possible values: `dolly_in`, `dolly_out`, `dolly_left`, `dolly_right`, `jib_up`, `jib_down`, `static`, `none`
- **`camera_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"news broadcast, 3d animation, computer graphics, pc game, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, watermark, logo, text, on screen text, subtitles, titles, signature, slowmo, static"` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the video from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`distill_lora_first_pass_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.2` вҖ” The scale of the distill LoRA to use for the first pass. Set to 0 to disable.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`distill_lora_second_pass_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The scale of the distill LoRA to use for the second and subsequent passes.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`video_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Video conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Audio conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`preprocessor`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The preprocessor to use for the generation.
  Possible values: `depth`, `canny`, `pose`, `none`
- **`ic_lora_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"union"` вҖ” The IC LoRA type to use for the generation.
  Possible values: `match_preprocessor`, `union`, `detailer`, `none`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTX23ReferenceVideoToVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the random number generator.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the generation.

## fal-ai/ltx-2.3-22b/reference-video-to-video/lora

**Title:** LTX 2.3 22B
**Description:** Generate video with audio from reference video, text and images using LTX-2.3 and custom LoRA
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltx-2.3-22b/reference-video-to-video/lora`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LTX23LoRAReferenceVideoToVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `loras`, `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video from.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to reference.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” An optional URL of an audio to use as the audio for the video. If not provided, any audio present in the input video will be used.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” An optional URL of an image to use as the first frame of the video.
- **`end_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as the end of the video.
- **`match_video_length`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When enabled, the number of frames will be calculated based on the video duration and FPS. When disabled, use the specified num_frames.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `121` вҖ” The number of frames to generate.
  Range: min: 9, max: 481
- **`video_size`** *(? | string)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The size of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `square_hd`, `square`, `portrait_4_3`, `portrait_16_9`, `landscape_4_3`, `landscape_16_9`
- **`generate_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate audio for the video.
- **`use_multiscale`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to use multi-scale generation. If True, the model will generate the video at a smaller scale first, then use the smaller video to guide the generation of a video at or above your requested size. This results in better coherence and details.
- **`match_input_fps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When true, match the output FPS to the input video's FPS instead of using the default target FPS.
- **`fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `24` вҖ” The frames per second of the generated video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `40` вҖ” The number of inference steps to use.
  Range: min: 8, max: 50
- **`video_cfg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) scale for the video. Higher values result in more consistent and focused video content.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`video_stg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The Spatiotemporal Guidance (STG) scale for the video. Higher values result in more consistent and focused video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`video_rescaling_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” The rescaling scale for the video. Controls the ratio between classifier-free guidance and spatiotemporal guidance.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`video_modality_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The modality scale for the video. Controls the ratio between video and audio modalities.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`audio_cfg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `7` вҖ” The Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) scale for the audio. Higher values result in more consistent and focused audio content.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`audio_stg_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The Spatiotemporal Guidance (STG) scale for the audio. Higher values result in more consistent and focused audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`audio_rescaling_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.7` вҖ” The rescaling scale for the audio. Controls the ratio between classifier-free guidance and spatiotemporal guidance.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_modality_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” The modality scale for the audio. Controls the ratio between video and audio modalities.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`gradient_estimation_gamma`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” The gamma of gradient estimation during denoising. Set to 0 to disable.
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`use_restart_sampling`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to use restart sampling. This will inject a small amount of noise during each denoising step, which can help improve the quality of the generated video.
- **`scheduler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"ltx2"` вҖ” The scheduler to use.
  Possible values: `ltx2`, `linear_quadratic`, `beta`
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” The acceleration level to use.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`, `high`, `full`
- **`camera_lora`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Possible values: `dolly_in`, `dolly_out`, `dolly_left`, `dolly_right`, `jib_up`, `jib_down`, `static`, `none`
- **`camera_lora_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The scale of the camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"news broadcast, 3d animation, computer graphics, pc game, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, watermark, logo, text, on screen text, subtitles, titles, signature, slowmo, static"` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the video from.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`loras`** *(list<LoRAInput>)* вҖ” The LoRAs to use for the generation.
- **`distill_lora_first_pass_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.2` вҖ” The scale of the distill LoRA to use for the first pass. Set to 0 to disable.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`distill_lora_second_pass_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The scale of the distill LoRA to use for the second and subsequent passes.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`video_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Video conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the video content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`audio_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Audio conditioning strength. Lower values represent more freedom given to the model to change the audio content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`preprocessor`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” The preprocessor to use for the generation.
  Possible values: `depth`, `canny`, `pose`, `none`
- **`ic_lora_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"union"` вҖ” The IC LoRA type to use for the generation.
  Possible values: `match_preprocessor`, `union`, `detailer`, `none`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTX23ReferenceVideoToVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for the random number generator.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for the generation.

## fal-ai/ltx-2.3/extend-video

**Title:** LTX Video 2.3 Pro
**Description:** LTX-2.3 is a high-quality, fast AI video model available in Pro and Fast variants for text-to-video, image-to-video, and audio-to-video.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltx-2.3/extend-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** stylized, transform, lipsync

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LTXV23ExtendVideoRequest`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to extend
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Description of what should happen in the extended portion of the video.
- **`duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Duration in seconds to extend the video. Maximum 20 seconds.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20
- **`mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"end"` вҖ” Where to extend the video: 'end' extends at the end, 'start' extends at the beginning.
  Possible values: `start`, `end`
- **`context`** *(number)* вҖ” Number of seconds from the input video to use as context for the extension (minimum 1 second, maximum 20 seconds). If not provided, defaults to maximize available context within the 505 frame limit.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTXV23ExtendVideoResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The extended video file

---

## fal-ai/ltx-2.3/retake-video

**Title:** LTX Video 2.3 Pro
**Description:** LTX-2.3 is a high-quality, fast AI video model available in Pro and Fast variants for text-to-video, image-to-video, and audio-to-video.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltx-2.3/retake-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** stylized, transform, lipsync

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LTXV23RetakeVideoRequest`
**Required fields:** `video_url`, `prompt`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to retake
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to retake the video with
- **`start_time`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The start time of the video to retake in seconds
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” The duration of the video to retake in seconds
  Range: min: 2, max: 20
- **`retake_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"replace_audio_and_video"` вҖ” The retake mode to use for the retake
  Possible values: `replace_audio`, `replace_video`, `replace_audio_and_video`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LTXV23RetakeVideoResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file

## fal-ai/ltx-video-13b-dev/extend

**Title:** LTX Video-0.9.7 13B
**Description:** Extend videos using LTX Video-0.9.7 13B and custom LoRA
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltx-video-13b-dev/extend`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video, ltx-video, video-to-video, extend-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ExtendVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt to guide generation
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"worst quality, inconsistent motion, blurry, jittery, distorted"` вҖ” Negative prompt for generation
- **`loras`** *(list<LoRAWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” LoRA weights to use for generation
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video.
  Possible values: `480p`, `720p`
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the video.
  Possible values: `9:16`, `1:1`, `16:9`, `auto`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for generation
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `121` вҖ” The number of frames in the video.
  Range: min: 9, max: 1441
- **`first_pass_num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” Number of inference steps during the first pass.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`second_pass_num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” Number of inference steps during the second pass.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`second_pass_skip_initial_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `17` вҖ” The number of inference steps to skip in the initial steps of the second pass. By skipping some steps at the beginning, the second pass can focus on smaller details instead of larger changes.
  Range: min: 0, max: 49
- **`frame_rate`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `24` вҖ” The frame rate of the video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`expand_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to expand the prompt using a language model.
- **`reverse_video`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to reverse the video.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`enable_detail_pass`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to use a detail pass. If True, the model will perform a second pass to refine the video and enhance details. This incurs a 2.0x cost multiplier on the base price.
- **`temporal_adain_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The factor for adaptive instance normalization (AdaIN) applied to generated video chunks after the first. This can help deal with a gradual increase in saturation/contrast in the generated video by normalizing the color distribution across the video. A high value will ensure the color distribution is more consistent across the video, while a low value will allow for more variation in color distribution.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`tone_map_compression_ratio`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The compression ratio for tone mapping. This is used to compress the dynamic range of the video to improve visual quality. A value of 0.0 means no compression, while a value of 1.0 means maximum compression.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`constant_rate_factor`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `29` вҖ” The constant rate factor (CRF) to compress input media with. Compressed input media more closely matches the model's training data, which can improve motion quality.
  Range: min: 0, max: 51
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” Video to be extended.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ExtendVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation.

---

- **`loras`** *(list<LoRAWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” LoRA weights to use for generation
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video.
  Possible values: `480p`, `720p`
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the video.
  Possible values: `9:16`, `1:1`, `16:9`, `auto`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for generation
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `121` вҖ” The number of frames in the video.
  Range: min: 9, max: 1441
- **`first_pass_num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” Number of inference steps during the first pass.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`second_pass_num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” Number of inference steps during the second pass.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`second_pass_skip_initial_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `17` вҖ” The number of inference steps to skip in the initial steps of the second pass. By skipping some steps at the beginning, the second pass can focus on smaller details instead of larger changes.
  Range: min: 0, max: 49
- **`frame_rate`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `24` вҖ” The frame rate of the video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`expand_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to expand the prompt using a language model.
- **`reverse_video`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to reverse the video.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`enable_detail_pass`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to use a detail pass. If True, the model will perform a second pass to refine the video and enhance details. This incurs a 2.0x cost multiplier on the base price.
- **`temporal_adain_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The factor for adaptive instance normalization (AdaIN) applied to generated video chunks after the first. This can help deal with a gradual increase in saturation/contrast in the generated video by normalizing the color distribution across the video. A high value will ensure the color distribution is more consistent across the video, while a low value will allow for more variation in color distribution.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`tone_map_compression_ratio`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The compression ratio for tone mapping. This is used to compress the dynamic range of the video to improve visual quality. A value of 0.0 means no compression, while a value of 1.0 means maximum compression.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`constant_rate_factor`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `29` вҖ” The constant rate factor (CRF) to compress input media with. Compressed input media more closely matches the model's training data, which can improve motion quality.
  Range: min: 0, max: 51
- **`images`** *(list<ImageConditioningInput>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” URL of images to use as conditioning
- **`videos`** *(list<VideoConditioningInput>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Videos to use as conditioning

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MultiConditioningVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation.

## fal-ai/ltx-video-lora/multiconditioning

**Title:** LTX Video-0.9.7 LoRA
**Description:** Generate videos from prompts, images, and videos using LTX Video-0.9.7 and custom LoRA
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltx-video-lora/multiconditioning`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video, ltx-video, video-to-video, multicondition-to-video, image-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MulticonditioningVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video from.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"blurry, low quality, low resolution, inconsistent motion, jittery, distorted"` вҖ” The negative prompt to use.
- **`loras`** *(list<LoRAWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The LoRA weights to use for generation.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” The resolution of the video.
  Possible values: `480p`, `720p`
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the video.
  Possible values: `16:9`, `1:1`, `9:16`, `auto`
- **`number_of_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `89` вҖ” The number of frames in the video.
  Range: min: 9, max: 161
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” The number of inference steps to use.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`frames_per_second`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The frame rate of the video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed to use for generation.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to expand the prompt using the LLM.
- **`reverse_video`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to reverse the video.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`images`** *(list<ImageCondition>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The image conditions to use for generation.
- **`videos`** *(list<VideoCondition>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” The video conditions to use for generation.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MulticonditioningVideoOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.

- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video (480p or 720p).

- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `40` вҖ” Number of inference steps
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`expand_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to expand the prompt using the model's own capabilities.
- **`images`** *(list<ImageConditioningInput>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” URL of images to use as conditioning
- **`videos`** *(list<VideoConditioningInput>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Videos to use as conditioning

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MulticonditioningVideoOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.

---

## fal-ai/ltxv-13b-098-distilled/extend

**Title:** LTX-Video 13B 0.9.8 Distilled
**Description:** Extend videos using LTX Video-0.9.8 13B Distilled and custom LoRA
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/ltxv-13b-098-distilled/extend`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** ltx-video, extend

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `DistilledExtendVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text prompt to guide generation
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"worst quality, inconsistent motion, blurry, jittery, distorted"` вҖ” Negative prompt for generation
- **`loras`** *(list<LoRAWeight>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” LoRA weights to use for generation
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video.
  Possible values: `480p`, `720p`
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the video.
  Possible values: `9:16`, `1:1`, `16:9`, `auto`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for generation
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `121` вҖ” The number of frames in the video.
  Range: min: 9, max: 1441
- **`first_pass_num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” Number of inference steps during the first pass.
  Range: min: 2, max: 12
- **`second_pass_num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” Number of inference steps during the second pass.
  Range: min: 2, max: 12
- **`second_pass_skip_initial_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” The number of inference steps to skip in the initial steps of the second pass. By skipping some steps at the beginning, the second pass can focus on smaller details instead of larger changes.
  Range: min: 1, max: 11
- **`frame_rate`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `24` вҖ” The frame rate of the video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`expand_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to expand the prompt using a language model.
- **`reverse_video`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to reverse the video.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`enable_detail_pass`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to use a detail pass. If True, the model will perform a second pass to refine the video and enhance details. This incurs a 2.0x cost multiplier on the base price.
- **`temporal_adain_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” The factor for adaptive instance normalization (AdaIN) applied to generated video chunks after the first. This can help deal with a gradual increase in saturation/contrast in the generated video by normalizing the color distribution across the video. A high value will ensure the color distribution is more consistent across the video, while a low value will allow for more variation in color distribution.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`tone_map_compression_ratio`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The compression ratio for tone mapping. This is used to compress the dynamic range of the video to improve visual quality. A value of 0.0 means no compression, while a value of 1.0 means maximum compression.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`constant_rate_factor`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `29` вҖ” The constant rate factor (CRF) to compress input media with. Compressed input media more closely matches the model's training data, which can improve motion quality.
  Range: min: 0, max: 51
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” Video to be extended.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ExtendVideoOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.

---

  Range: min: 1, max: 11
- **`frame_rate`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `24` вҖ” The frame rate of the video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`expand_prompt`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to expand the prompt using a language model.
- **`reverse_video`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to reverse the video.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`enable_detail_pass`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to use a detail pass. If True, the model will perform a second pass to refine the video and enhance details. This incurs a 2.0x cost multiplier on the base price.

This can help deal with a gradual increase in saturation/contrast in the generated video by normalizing the color distribution across the video. A high value will ensure the color distribution is more consistent across the video, while a low value will allow for more variation in color distribution.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`tone_map_compression_ratio`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The compression ratio for tone mapping. This is used to compress the dynamic range of the video to improve visual quality. A value of 0.0 means no compression, while a value of 1.0 means maximum compression.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`constant_rate_factor`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `29` вҖ” The constant rate factor (CRF) to compress input media with. Compressed input media more closely matches the model's training data, which can improve motion quality.
  Range: min: 0, max: 51
- **`images`** *(list<ImageConditioningInput>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” URL of images to use as conditioning
- **`videos`** *(list<VideoConditioningInput>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Videos to use as conditioning

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MultiConditioningVideoOutput`
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.

## fal-ai/luma-dream-machine/ray-2-flash/modify

**Title:** Luma Ray 2 Flash Modify
**Description:** Ray2 Flash Modify is a video generative model capable of restyling or retexturing the entire shot, from turning live-action into CG or stylized animation, to changing wardrobe, props, or the overall aesthetic and swap environments or time periods, giving you control over background, location, or even weather.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/luma-dream-machine/ray-2-flash/modify`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** modify, restyle

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ModifyVideoRequest`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video to modify
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional URL of the first frame image for modification
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Instruction for modifying the video
- **`mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"flex_1"` вҖ” Amount of modification to apply to the video, adhere_1 is the least amount of modification, reimagine_3 is the most
  Possible values: `adhere_1`, `adhere_2`, `adhere_3`, `flex_1`, `flex_2`, `flex_3`, `reimagine_1`, `reimagine_2`, `reimagine_3`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ModifyOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” URL of the modified video

---

## fal-ai/luma-dream-machine/ray-2-flash/reframe

**Title:** Luma Ray 2 Flash Reframe
**Description:** Adjust and enhance videos with Ray-2 Reframe. This advanced tool seamlessly reframes videos to your desired aspect ratio, intelligently inpainting missing regions to ensure realistic visuals and coherent motion, delivering exceptional quality and creative flexibility.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/luma-dream-machine/ray-2-flash/reframe`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** reframe, outpaint, flash

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ReframeVideoRequest`
**Required fields:** `video_url`, `aspect_ratio`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video to reframe
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” The aspect ratio of the reframed video
  Possible values: `1:1`, `16:9`, `9:16`, `4:3`, `3:4`, `21:9`, `9:21`
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional URL of the first frame image for reframing
- **`grid_position_x`** *(integer)* вҖ” X position of the grid for reframing
- **`grid_position_y`** *(integer)* вҖ” Y position of the grid for reframing
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional prompt for reframing
- **`x_end`** *(integer)* вҖ” End X coordinate for reframing
- **`x_start`** *(integer)* вҖ” Start X coordinate for reframing
- **`y_end`** *(integer)* вҖ” End Y coordinate for reframing
- **`y_start`** *(integer)* вҖ” Start Y coordinate for reframing

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ReframeOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” URL of the reframed video

---

- **`grid_position_x`** *(integer)* вҖ” X position of the grid for reframing
