## fal-ai/magi-distilled/extend-video

**Title:** MAGI-1 (Distilled)
**Description:** MAGI-1 distilled extends videos faster with an exceptional understanding of physical interactions and prompts
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/magi-distilled/extend-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-to-video, video-extend

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MagiVideoExtensionRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to guide video generation.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video to represent the beginning of the video. If the input video does not match the chosen aspect ratio, it is resized and center cropped.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `96` вҖ” Number of frames to generate. Must be between 96 and 192 (inclusive). Each additional 24 frames beyond 96 incurs an additional billing unit.
- **`start_frame`** *(integer)* вҖ” The frame to begin the generation from, with the remaining frames will be treated as the prefix video. The final video will contain the frames up until this number unchanged, followed by the generated frames. The default start frame is 32 frames before the end of the video, which gives optimal results.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video (480p or 720p). 480p is 0.5 billing units, and 720p is 1 billing unit.
  Possible values: `480p`, `720p`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `16` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Possible values: `4`, `8`, `16`, `32`
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated video. If 'auto', the aspect ratio will be determined automatically based on the input image.
  Possible values: `auto`, `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MagiVideoExtensionResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.

---

## fal-ai/magi/extend-video

**Title:** MAGI-1
**Description:** MAGI-1 extends videos with an exceptional understanding of physical interactions and prompts
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/magi/extend-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MagiVideoExtensionRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to guide video generation.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video to represent the beginning of the video. If the input video does not match the chosen aspect ratio, it is resized and center cropped.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `96` вҖ” Number of frames to generate. Must be between 96 and 192 (inclusive). Each additional 24 frames beyond 96 incurs an additional billing unit.
- **`start_frame`** *(integer)* вҖ” The frame to begin the generation from, with the remaining frames will be treated as the prefix video. The final video will contain the frames up until this number unchanged, followed by the generated frames. The default start frame is 32 frames before the end of the video, which gives optimal results.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video (480p or 720p). 480p is 0.5 billing units, and 720p is 1 billing unit.
  Possible values: `480p`, `720p`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `16` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Possible values: `4`, `8`, `16`, `32`, `64`
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated video. If 'auto', the aspect ratio will be determined automatically based on the input image.
  Possible values: `auto`, `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MagiVideoExtensionResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.

---

## fal-ai/mmaudio-v2

**Title:** MMAudio V2
**Description:** MMAudio generates synchronized audio given video and/or text inputs. It can be combined with video models to get videos with audio.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/mmaudio-v2`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** ai video, fast

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `BaseInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`, `prompt`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to generate the audio for.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the audio for.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the audio for.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator
- **`num_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `25` вҖ” The number of steps to generate the audio for.
  Range: min: 4, max: 50
- **`duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” The duration of the audio to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 30
- **`cfg_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4.5` вҖ” The strength of Classifier Free Guidance.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`mask_away_clip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to mask away the clip.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video with the lip sync.

---

## fal-ai/one-to-all-animation/1.3b

**Title:** One To All Animation
**Description:** One-to-All Animation is a pose driven video model that animates characters from a single reference image, enabling flexible, alignment-free motion transfer across diverse styles and scenes
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/one-to-all-animation/1.3b`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video to video, motion

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `OneToALLAnimationRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `negative_prompt`, `image_url`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video from.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the video from.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as a reference for the video generation.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to use as a reference for the video generation.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"480p"` вҖ” The resolution of the video to generate.
  Possible values: `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” The number of inference steps to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 2, max: 30
- **`image_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” The image guidance scale to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`pose_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1.5` вҖ” The pose guidance scale to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `OneToALLAnimationResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.

---

## fal-ai/one-to-all-animation/14b

**Title:** One To All Animation
**Description:** One-to-All Animation is a pose driven video model that animates characters from a single reference image, enabling flexible, alignment-free motion transfer across diverse styles and scenes

  Possible values: `480p`, `580p`, `720p`

- **`image_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” The image guidance scale to use for the video generation.

## fal-ai/pixverse/extend

**Title:** Pixverse
**Description:** PixVerse Extend model is a video extending tool for your videos using with high-quality video extending techniques 
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/pixverse/extend`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** utility, editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ExtendRequest`
**Required fields:** `video_url`, `prompt`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video to extend
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Prompt describing how to extend the video
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Negative prompt to be used for the generation
- **`style`** *(string)* вҖ” The style of the extended video
  Possible values: `anime`, `3d_animation`, `clay`, `comic`, `cyberpunk`
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” The resolution of the generated video
  Possible values: `360p`, `540p`, `720p`, `1080p`
- **`duration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"5"` вҖ” The duration of the generated video in seconds. 1080p videos are limited to 5 seconds
  Possible values: `5`, `8`
- **`model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"v4.5"` вҖ” The model version to use for generation
  Possible values: `v3.5`, `v4`, `v4.5`, `v5`, `v5.5`, `v5.6`, `v6`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for generation
- **`generate_audio_switch`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Enable audio generation (BGM, SFX, dialogue). Supported in v5.6+ models.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ExtendOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The extended video

---

## fal-ai/pixverse/extend/fast

**Title:** Pixverse
**Description:** PixVerse Extend model is a video extending tool for your videos using with high-quality video extending techniques 
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/pixverse/extend/fast`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** utility, editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `FastExtendRequest`
**Required fields:** `video_url`, `prompt`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video to extend
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Prompt describing how to extend the video
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Negative prompt to be used for the generation
- **`style`** *(string)* вҖ” The style of the extended video
  Possible values: `anime`, `3d_animation`, `clay`, `comic`, `cyberpunk`
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” The resolution of the generated video. Fast mode doesn't support 1080p
  Possible values: `360p`, `540p`, `720p`
- **`model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"v4.5"` вҖ” The model version to use for generation
  Possible values: `v3.5`, `v4`, `v4.5`, `v5`, `v5.5`, `v5.6`, `v6`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for generation
- **`generate_audio_switch`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Enable audio generation (BGM, SFX, dialogue). Supported in v5.6+ models.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ExtendOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The extended video

---

## fal-ai/pixverse/lipsync

**Title:** Pixverse
**Description:** Generate realistic lipsync animations from audio using advanced algorithms for high-quality synchronization with PixVerse Lipsync model
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/pixverse/lipsync`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** animation, lip sync

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LipsyncRequest`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input audio. If not provided, TTS will be used.
- **`voice_id`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"Auto"` вҖ” Voice to use for TTS when audio_url is not provided
  Possible values: `Emily`, `James`, `Isabella`, `Liam`, `Chloe`, `Adrian`, `Harper`, `Ava`, `Sophia`, `Julia`, `Mason`, `Jack`, `Oliver`, `Ethan`, `Auto`
- **`text`** *(string)* вҖ” Text content for TTS when audio_url is not provided

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LipsyncOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Description of the sound effect to generate. If empty, a random sound effect will be generated

**Title:** Pixverse

**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-to-video, extend

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Prompt describing how to extend the video
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Negative prompt to be used for the generation
- **`style`** *(string)* вҖ” The style of the extended video
  Possible values: `anime`, `3d_animation`, `clay`, `comic`, `cyberpunk`
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” The resolution of the generated video
  Possible values: `360p`, `540p`, `720p`, `1080p`
- **`duration`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” The duration of the generated video in seconds. v6 supports values from 1 to 15 seconds
  Range: min: 1, max: 15
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for generation
- **`generate_audio_switch`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Enable audio generation (BGM, SFX, dialogue).

## fal-ai/rife/video

**Title:** RIFE
**Description:** Interpolate videos with RIFE - Real-Time Intermediate Flow Estimation
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/rife/video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** interpolation

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `RIFEVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to use for interpolation.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” The number of frames to generate between the input video frames.
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`use_scene_detection`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If True, the input video will be split into scenes before interpolation. This removes smear frames between scenes, but can result in false positives if the scene detection is not accurate. If False, the entire video will be treated as a single scene.
- **`use_calculated_fps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If True, the function will use the calculated FPS of the input video multiplied by the number of frames to determine the output FPS. If False, the passed FPS will be used.
- **`fps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8` вҖ” Frames per second for the output video. Only applicable if use_calculated_fps is False.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`loop`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If True, the final frame will be looped back to the first frame to create a seamless loop. If False, the final frame will not loop back.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `RIFEVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file with interpolated frames.

---

## fal-ai/sam-3-1/video

**Title:** Sam 3 1
**Description:** SAM 3.1 builds comes with Object Multiplex, a shared-memory approach for joint multi-object tracking that delivers faster speeds with larger number of objects tracked.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sam-3-1/video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** segmentation, mask, real-time

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SAM31VideoInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to be segmented.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Text prompt for segmentation. Use commas to track multiple objects (e.g., 'person, cloth').
- **`point_prompts`** *(list<PointPromptBase>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of point prompts
- **`box_prompts`** *(list<BoxPromptBase>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of box prompt coordinates (x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max).
- **`apply_mask`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Apply the mask on the video.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`
- **`detection_threshold`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Detection confidence threshold (0.0-1.0). Lower = more detections but less precise.
  Range: min: 0.1, max: 1
- **`max_num_objects`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `16` вҖ” Maximum number of objects to track in the video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 128

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SAM31VideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The segmented video.
- **`boundingbox_frames_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” Zip file containing per-frame bounding box overlays.

---

## fal-ai/sam-3-1/video-rle

**Title:** Sam 3 1
**Description:** SAM 3.1 builds comes with Object Multiplex, a shared-memory approach for joint multi-object tracking that delivers faster speeds with larger number of objects tracked.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sam-3-1/video-rle`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** segmentation, mask, real-time

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SAM31VideoRLEInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to be segmented.
- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the mask to be applied initially.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Text prompt for segmentation. Use commas to track multiple objects (e.g., 'person, cloth').
- **`point_prompts`** *(list<PointPrompt>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of point prompts with frame indices.
- **`box_prompts`** *(list<BoxPrompt>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of box prompts with optional frame_index.
- **`apply_mask`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Apply the mask on the video.
- **`boundingbox_zip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Return per-frame bounding box overlays as a zip archive.
- **`detection_threshold`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Detection confidence threshold (0.0-1.0). Lower = more detections but less precise. Defaults: 0.5 for existing, 0.7 for new objects. Try 0.2-0.3 if text prompts fail.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1
- **`frame_index`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Frame index used for initial interaction when mask_url is provided.
- **`max_num_objects`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `16` вҖ” Maximum number of objects to track in the video.
  Range: min: 1, max: 128

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SAM31VideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The segmented video.
- **`boundingbox_frames_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” Zip file containing per-frame bounding box overlays.

---

## fal-ai/sam-3/video

**Title:** Sam 3
**Description:** SAM 3 is a unified foundation model for promptable segmentation in images and videos. It can detect, segment, and track objects using text or visual prompts such as points, boxes, and masks. 
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sam-3/video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** segmentation, mask, real-time

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SAM3VideoInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to be segmented.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Text prompt for segmentation. Use commas to track multiple objects (e.g., 'person, cloth').
- **`text_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” [DEPRECATED] Use 'prompt' instead. Kept for backward compatibility.
- **`point_prompts`** *(list<PointPromptBase>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of point prompts
- **`box_prompts`** *(list<BoxPromptBase>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of box prompt coordinates (x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max).
- **`apply_mask`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Apply the mask on the video.
- **`video_output_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The output type of the generated video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`
- **`detection_threshold`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Detection confidence threshold (0.0-1.0). Lower = more detections but less precise. 
  Range: min: 0.1, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SAM3VideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The segmented video.
- **`boundingbox_frames_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” Zip file containing per-frame bounding box overlays.

---

**Title:** Sam 3
**Description:** SAM 3 is a unified foundation model for promptable segmentation in images and videos. It can detect, segment, and track objects using text or visual prompts such as points, boxes, and masks.

- **`mask_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the mask to be applied initially.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Text prompt for segmentation. Use commas to track multiple objects (e.g., 'person, cloth').
- **`point_prompts`** *(list<PointPrompt>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of point prompts with frame indices.
- **`box_prompts`** *(list<BoxPrompt>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” List of box prompts with optional frame_index.
- **`apply_mask`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Apply the mask on the video.
- **`boundingbox_zip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Return per-frame bounding box overlays as a zip archive.
- **`detection_threshold`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Detection confidence threshold (0.0-1.0). Lower = more detections but less precise. Defaults: 0.5 for existing, 0.7 for new objects. Try 0.2-0.3 if text prompts fail.
  Range: min: 0.01, max: 1

- **`box_prompts`** *(list<BoxPrompt>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” Coordinates for boxes
- **`apply_mask`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Apply the mask on the video.

## fal-ai/scail

**Title:** Scail
**Description:** SCAIL is a character animation model that uses 3D consistent pose representations to animate reference images with coherent motion, supporting complex movements.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/scail`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ScailRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_url`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to guide video generation.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the image to use as a reference for the video generation.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to use as a reference for the video generation.
- **`multi_character`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Enable multi-character mode. Use when driving video has multiple people.
- **`resolution`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"512p"` вҖ” Output resolution. Outputs 896x512 (landscape) or 512x896 (portrait) based on the input image aspect ratio.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `28` вҖ” The number of inference steps to use for the video generation.
  Range: min: 2, max: 30

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ScailResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.

---

## fal-ai/seedvr/upscale/video

**Title:** SeedVR2
**Description:** Upscale your videos using SeedVR2 with temporal consistency!
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/seedvr/upscale/video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** upscale, video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SeedVRVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The input video to be processed
- **`upscale_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"factor"` вҖ” The mode to use for the upscale. If 'target', the upscale factor will be calculated based on the target resolution. If 'factor', the upscale factor will be used directly.
  Possible values: `target`, `factor`
- **`upscale_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Upscaling factor to be used. Will multiply the dimensions with this factor when `upscale_mode` is `factor`.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`target_resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1080p"` вҖ” The target resolution to upscale to when `upscale_mode` is `target`.
  Possible values: `720p`, `1080p`, `1440p`, `2160p`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The random seed used for the generation process.
- **`noise_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.1` вҖ” The noise scale to use for the generation process.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`output_format`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"X264 (.mp4)"` вҖ” The format of the output video.
  Possible values: `X264 (.mp4)`, `VP9 (.webm)`, `PRORES4444 (.mov)`, `GIF (.gif)`
- **`output_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the output video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`output_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the output video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SeedVRVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” Upscaled video file after processing
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The random seed used for the generation process.

---

## fal-ai/sora-2/video-to-video/remix

**Title:** Sora 2
**Description:** Video-to-video remix endpoint for Sora 2, OpenAIвҖҷs advanced model that transforms existing videos based on new text or image prompts allowing rich edits, style changes, and creative reinterpretations while preserving motion and structure
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sora-2/video-to-video/remix`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video to video, audio, sora, 

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `RemixInput`
**Required fields:** `video_id`, `prompt`

- **`video_id`** *(string)* вҖ” The video_id from a previous Sora 2 generation. Note: You can only remix videos that were generated by Sora (via text-to-video or image-to-video endpoints), not arbitrary uploaded videos.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Updated text prompt that directs the remix generation
- **`delete_video`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to delete the video after generation for privacy reasons. If True, the video cannot be used for remixing and will be permanently deleted.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `RemixOutput`
- **`thumbnail`** *(?)* вҖ” Thumbnail image for the video
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video
- **`spritesheet`** *(?)* вҖ” Spritesheet image for the video
- **`video_id`** *(string)* вҖ” The ID of the generated video

---

## fal-ai/steady-dancer

**Title:** Steady Dancer
**Description:** Create smooth, realistic videos from a single photo while keeping the original appearance intactвҖ”precise motion control without losing identity or visual quality.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/steady-dancer`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SteadyDancerRequest`
**Required fields:** _none_

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"https://v3b.fal.media/files/b/0a85edaa/GDUCMPrdvOMcI5JpEcU7f.png"` вҖ” URL of the reference image to animate. This is the person/character whose appearance will be preserved.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"https://v3b.fal.media/files/b/0a84de68/jXDWywjhagRfR-GuZjoRs_video.mp4"` вҖ” URL of the driving pose video. The motion from this video will be transferred to the reference image.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"A person dancing with smooth and natural movements."` вҖ” Text prompt describing the desired animation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"blurred, distorted face, bad anatomy, extra limbs, poorly drawn hands, poorly drawn feet, disfigured, out of frame, duplicate, watermark, signature, text"` вҖ” Negative prompt for video generation.
- **`frames_per_second`** *(integer)* вҖ” Frames per second of the generated video. Must be between 5 to 24. If not specified, uses the FPS from the input video.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"576p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video. 576p is default, 720p for higher quality. 480p is lower quality.
  Possible values: `480p`, `576p`, `720p`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `6` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 4, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale for prompt adherence.
  Range: min: 1, max: 6
- **`pose_guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Pose guidance scale for pose control strength.
  Range: min: 0.5, max: 3
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Shift parameter for video generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`pose_guidance_start`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.1` вҖ” Start ratio for pose guidance. Controls when pose guidance begins.
  Range: min: 0, max: 0.5
- **`pose_guidance_end`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.4` вҖ” End ratio for pose guidance. Controls when pose guidance ends.
  Range: min: 0.2, max: 1
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"aggressive"` вҖ” Acceleration levels.
  Possible values: `light`, `moderate`, `aggressive`
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated video. If 'auto', will be determined from the reference image.
  Possible values: `auto`, `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Number of frames to generate. If not specified, uses the frame count from the input video (capped at 241). Will be adjusted to nearest valid value (must satisfy 4k+1 pattern).
- **`preserve_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If enabled, copies audio from the input driving video to the output video.
- **`use_turbo`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, applies quality enhancement for faster generation with improved quality. When enabled, parameters are automatically optimized (num_inference_steps=6, guidance_scale=1.0) and uses the LightX2V distillation LoRA.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `SteadyDancerResponse`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” The actual number of frames generated (aligned to 4k+1 pattern).
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated dance animation video.

---

## fal-ai/sync-lipsync

**Title:** sync.so -- lipsync 1.9.0-beta
**Description:** Generate realistic lipsync animations from audio using advanced algorithms for high-quality synchronization.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sync-lipsync`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** animation, lip sync

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LipSyncInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`, `audio_url`

- **`model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"lipsync-1.9.0-beta"` вҖ” The model to use for lipsyncing
  Possible values: `lipsync-1.8.0`, `lipsync-1.7.1`, `lipsync-1.9.0-beta`
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input audio
- **`sync_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"cut_off"` вҖ” Lipsync mode when audio and video durations are out of sync.
  Possible values: `cut_off`, `loop`, `bounce`, `silence`, `remap`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LipSyncOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video

---

## fal-ai/sync-lipsync/react-1

**Title:** Sync React-1
**Description:** Use React-1 from SyncLabs to refine human emotions and do realistic lip-sync without losing details!
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/sync-lipsync/react-1`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** lipsync, video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `React1Input`
**Required fields:** `video_url`, `audio_url`, `emotion`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the input video. Must be **15 seconds or shorter**.
- **`audio_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the input audio. Must be **15 seconds or shorter**.
- **`emotion`** *(enum)* вҖ” Emotion prompt for the generation. Currently supports single-word emotions only.
  Possible values: `happy`, `angry`, `sad`, `neutral`, `disgusted`, `surprised`
- **`model_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"face"` вҖ” Controls the edit region and movement scope for the model. Available options:
- `lips`: Only lipsync using react-1 (minimal facial changes).
- `face`: Lipsync + facial expressions without head movements.
- `head`: Lipsync + facial expressions + natural talking head movements.
  Possible values: `lips`, `face`, `head`
- **`lipsync_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"bounce"` вҖ” Lipsync mode when audio and video durations are out of sync.
  Possible values: `cut_off`, `loop`, `bounce`, `silence`, `remap`
- **`temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.5` вҖ” Controls the expresiveness of the lipsync.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `React1Output`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video with synchronized lip and facial movements.

---

- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `24` вҖ” The number of inference steps for audio generation.

## fal-ai/topaz/upscale/video

**Title:** Topaz Video Upscale
**Description:** Professional-grade video upscaling using Topaz technology. Enhance your videos with high-quality upscaling.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/topaz/upscale/video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** upscaling, high-res

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `VideoUpscaleRequest`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video to upscale
- **`model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"Proteus"` вҖ” Video enhancement model. Proteus is best for most videos, Artemis for denoise+sharpen, Nyx for dedicated denoising, Gaia HQ/CG for rendered content, Gaia 2 for animation and motion graphics at 2x, and Starlight for generative diffusion-based upscaling and enhancement.
  Possible values: `Proteus`, `Artemis HQ`, `Artemis MQ`, `Artemis LQ`, `Nyx`, `Nyx Fast`, `Nyx XL`, `Nyx HF`, `Gaia HQ`, `Gaia CG`, `Gaia 2`, `Starlight Precise 1`, `Starlight Precise 2`, `Starlight Precise 2.5`, `Starlight HQ`, `Starlight Mini`, `Starlight Sharp`, `Starlight Fast 1`, `Starlight Fast 2`
- **`upscale_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Factor to upscale the video by (e.g. 2.0 doubles width and height)
  Range: min: 1, max: 4
- **`target_fps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Target FPS for frame interpolation. If set, frame interpolation will be enabled.
- **`compression`** *(number)* вҖ” Compression artifact removal level (0.0-1.0). Default varies by model.
- **`noise`** *(number)* вҖ” Noise reduction level (0.0-1.0). Default varies by model.
- **`halo`** *(number)* вҖ” Halo reduction level (0.0-1.0). Default varies by model.
- **`grain`** *(number)* вҖ” Film grain amount (0.0-1.0). Default varies by model.
- **`recover_detail`** *(number)* вҖ” Recover original detail level (0.0-1.0). Higher values preserve more original detail.
- **`H264_output`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to use H264 codec for output video. Default is H265.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `VideoUpscaleOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The upscaled video file

---

## fal-ai/veo3.1/extend-video

**Title:** Veo 3.1
**Description:** Extend Veo-Created Videos up to 30 seconds
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/veo3.1/extend-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** extend-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Veo31VideoToVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt describing how the video should be extended
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `16:9`, `9:16`
- **`duration`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"7s"` вҖ” The duration of the generated video.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” A negative prompt to guide the video generation.
- **`resolution`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” The resolution of the generated video.
- **`generate_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate audio for the video.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`auto_fix`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to automatically attempt to fix prompts that fail content policy or other validation checks by rewriting them.
- **`safety_tolerance`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"4"` вҖ” The safety tolerance level for content moderation. 1 is the most strict (blocks most content), 6 is the least strict.
  Possible values: `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `5`, `6`
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video to extend. The video should be 720p or 1080p resolution in 16:9 or 9:16 aspect ratio.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Veo31VideoToVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The extended video.

---

## fal-ai/video-as-prompt

**Title:** Video As Prompt
**Description:** A model for unified semantic control in video generation. It animates a static reference image using the motion and semantics from a reference video as a prompt.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/video-as-prompt`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-as-prompt, semantic control, 

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `VideoEffectInputWan`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`, `image_url`, `video_description`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate an image from.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” reference video to generate effect video from.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Input image to generate the effect video for.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducible generation. If set none, a random seed will be used.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `49` вҖ” The number of frames to generate.
  Range: min: 1, max: 100
- **`fps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `16` вҖ” Frames per second for the output video. Only applicable if output_type is 'video'.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`video_description`** *(string)* вҖ” A brief description of the input video content.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"9:16"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated video.
  Possible values: `16:9`, `9:16`
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"480p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video.
  Possible values: `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Guidance scale for generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 20

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `VideoEffectOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The URLs of the generated video.

  Possible values: `2`, `3`, `4`, `5`, `6`, `7`

## fal-ai/void-video-inpainting

**Title:** Void Video Inpainting
**Description:** VOID removes objects from videos along with all interactions they induce on the scene
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/void-video-inpainting`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** utility, editing

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Input`
**Required fields:** `video_url`, `prompt`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video containing the object to remove.
- **`quad_mask_video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” URL of a mask video for the removal target. For best results this should be a VOID-style quadmask video with 4 grayscale values: 0=object to remove, 63=overlap, 127=affected region, 255=background to keep. A simple binary mask (0=remove, 255=keep) also works. If omitted, the app will generate a temporary mask video from `mask_prompt` using the SAM-3 video endpoint and convert it to a quadmask automatically.
- **`mask_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text description of what should be masked in the input video, such as the object or person to remove. Used to generate a temporary mask video with SAM-3 when `quad_mask_video_url` is not provided.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text description of the desired background after object removal.
- **`enable_pass2_refinement`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Run VOID Pass 2 warped-noise refinement after Pass 1. This is slower but can improve temporal consistency on longer clips.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"The video is not of a high quality, it has a low resolution. Watermark present in each frame. The background is solid. Strange body and strange trajectory. Distortion."` вҖ” Negative prompt to guide generation away from undesired outputs.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” Number of denoising steps. Higher values improve quality but increase latency.
  Range: min: 1, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale.
  Range: min: 0, max: 20
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Denoising strength. 1.0 means full denoising.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `85` вҖ” Temporal window size for inference. The backend snaps this to the nearest CogVideoX-safe value that works with temporal compression and patching. Valid outputs are 69, 77, 85, ..., 197.
  Range: min: 1, max: 197
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable content safety checking on the output.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The output video with the object removed.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” Timing breakdown for pipeline stages.

---

## fal-ai/wan-22-vace-fun-a14b/depth

**Title:** Wan 2.2 VACE Fun A14B
**Description:** VACE Fun for Wan 2.2 A14B from Alibaba-PAI
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan-22-vace-fun-a14b/depth`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `WanVACEDepthRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to guide video generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"letterboxing, borders, black bars, bright colors, overexposed, static, blurred details, subtitles, style, artwork, painting, picture, still, overall gray, worst quality, low quality, JPEG compression residue, ugly, incomplete, extra fingers, poorly drawn hands, poorly drawn faces, deformed, disfigured, malformed limbs, fused fingers, still picture, cluttered background, three legs, many people in the background, walking backwards"` вҖ” Negative prompt for video generation.
- **`match_input_num_frames`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the number of frames in the generated video will match the number of frames in the input video. If false, the number of frames will be determined by the num_frames parameter.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `81` вҖ” Number of frames to generate. Must be between 81 to 241 (inclusive).
  Range: min: 17, max: 241
- **`match_input_frames_per_second`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the frames per second of the generated video will match the input video. If false, the frames per second will be determined by the frames_per_second parameter.
- **`frames_per_second`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `16` вҖ” Frames per second of the generated video. Must be between 5 to 30. Ignored if match_input_frames_per_second is true.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `240p`, `360p`, `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `16:9`, `1:1`, `9:16`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Guidance scale for classifier-free guidance. Higher values encourage the model to generate images closely related to the text prompt.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`sampler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"unipc"` вҖ” Sampler to use for video generation.
  Possible values: `unipc`, `dpm++`, `euler`
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Shift parameter for video generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 15
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the source video file. Required for depth task.
- **`ref_image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” URLs to source reference image. If provided, the model will use this image as reference.
- **`first_frame_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the first frame of the video. If provided, the model will use this frame as a reference.
- **`last_frame_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the last frame of the video. If provided, the model will use this frame as a reference.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the input video.
- **`acceleration`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration to use for inference. Options are 'none' or 'regular'. Accelerated inference will very slightly affect output, but will be significantly faster.
  Possible values: `none`, `low`, `regular`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`num_interpolated_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Number of frames to interpolate between the original frames. A value of 0 means no interpolation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`temporal_downsample_factor`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Temporal downsample factor for the video. This is an integer value that determines how many frames to skip in the video. A value of 0 means no downsampling. For each downsample factor, one upsample factor will automatically be applied.
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`enable_auto_downsample`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the model will automatically temporally downsample the video to an appropriate frame length for the model, then will interpolate it back to the original frame length.
- **`auto_downsample_min_fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” The minimum frames per second to downsample the video to. This is used to help determine the auto downsample factor to try and find the lowest detail-preserving downsample factor. The default value is appropriate for most videos, if you are using a video with very fast motion, you may need to increase this value. If your video has a very low amount of motion, you could decrease this value to allow for higher downsampling and thus longer sequences.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`interpolator_model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"film"` вҖ” The model to use for frame interpolation. Options are 'rife' or 'film'.
  Possible values: `rife`, `film`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`transparency_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"content_aware"` вҖ” The transparency mode to apply to the first and last frames. This controls how the transparent areas of the first and last frames are filled.
  Possible values: `content_aware`, `white`, `black`
- **`return_frames_zip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, also return a ZIP file containing all generated frames.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanVACEDepthResponse`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`frames_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” ZIP archive of all video frames if requested.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated depth video file.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation.

## fal-ai/wan-22-vace-fun-a14b/inpainting

**Title:** Wan 2.2 VACE Fun A14B
**Description:** VACE Fun for Wan 2.2 A14B from Alibaba-PAI
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan-22-vace-fun-a14b/inpainting`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `WanVACEInpaintingRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to guide video generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"letterboxing, borders, black bars, bright colors, overexposed, static, blurred details, subtitles, style, artwork, painting, picture, still, overall gray, worst quality, low quality, JPEG compression residue, ugly, incomplete, extra fingers, poorly drawn hands, poorly drawn faces, deformed, disfigured, malformed limbs, fused fingers, still picture, cluttered background, three legs, many people in the background, walking backwards"` вҖ” Negative prompt for video generation.
- **`match_input_num_frames`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the number of frames in the generated video will match the number of frames in the input video. If false, the number of frames will be determined by the num_frames parameter.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `81` вҖ” Number of frames to generate. Must be between 81 to 241 (inclusive).
  Range: min: 17, max: 241
- **`match_input_frames_per_second`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the frames per second of the generated video will match the input video. If false, the frames per second will be determined by the frames_per_second parameter.
- **`frames_per_second`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `16` вҖ” Frames per second of the generated video. Must be between 5 to 30. Ignored if match_input_frames_per_second is true.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `240p`, `360p`, `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `16:9`, `1:1`, `9:16`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Guidance scale for classifier-free guidance. Higher values encourage the model to generate images closely related to the text prompt.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`sampler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"unipc"` вҖ” Sampler to use for video generation.
  Possible values: `unipc`, `dpm++`, `euler`
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Shift parameter for video generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 15
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the source video file. Required for inpainting.
- **`mask_video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the source mask file. Required for inpainting.
- **`mask_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the guiding mask file. If provided, the model will use this mask as a reference to create masked video using salient mask tracking. Will be ignored if mask_video_url is provided.
- **`ref_image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” Urls to source reference image. If provided, the model will use this image as reference.
- **`first_frame_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the first frame of the video. If provided, the model will use this frame as a reference.
- **`last_frame_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the last frame of the video. If provided, the model will use this frame as a reference.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the input video.
- **`acceleration`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration to use for inference. Options are 'none' or 'regular'. Accelerated inference will very slightly affect output, but will be significantly faster.
  Possible values: `none`, `low`, `regular`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`num_interpolated_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Number of frames to interpolate between the original frames. A value of 0 means no interpolation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`temporal_downsample_factor`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Temporal downsample factor for the video. This is an integer value that determines how many frames to skip in the video. A value of 0 means no downsampling. For each downsample factor, one upsample factor will automatically be applied.
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`enable_auto_downsample`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the model will automatically temporally downsample the video to an appropriate frame length for the model, then will interpolate it back to the original frame length.
- **`auto_downsample_min_fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” The minimum frames per second to downsample the video to. This is used to help determine the auto downsample factor to try and find the lowest detail-preserving downsample factor. The default value is appropriate for most videos, if you are using a video with very fast motion, you may need to increase this value. If your video has a very low amount of motion, you could decrease this value to allow for higher downsampling and thus longer sequences.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`interpolator_model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"film"` вҖ” The model to use for frame interpolation. Options are 'rife' or 'film'.
  Possible values: `rife`, `film`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`transparency_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"content_aware"` вҖ” The transparency mode to apply to the first and last frames. This controls how the transparent areas of the first and last frames are filled.
  Possible values: `content_aware`, `white`, `black`
- **`return_frames_zip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, also return a ZIP file containing all generated frames.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanVACEInpaintingResponse`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`frames_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” ZIP archive of all video frames if requested.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated inpainting video file.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation.

---

- **`expand_bottom`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to expand the video to the bottom.

If your video has a very low amount of motion, you could decrease this value to allow for higher downsampling and thus longer sequences.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`interpolator_model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"film"` вҖ” The model to use for frame interpolation. Options are 'rife' or 'film'.
  Possible values: `rife`, `film`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`transparency_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"content_aware"` вҖ” The transparency mode to apply to the first and last frames. This controls how the transparent areas of the first and last frames are filled.
  Possible values: `content_aware`, `white`, `black`
- **`return_frames_zip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, also return a ZIP file containing all generated frames.
- **`zoom_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Zoom factor for the video. When this value is greater than 0, the video will be zoomed in by this factor (in relation to the canvas size,) cutting off the edges of the video. A value of 0 means no zoom.
  Range: min: 0, max: 0.9
- **`trim_borders`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to trim borders from the video.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanVACEReframeResponse`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`frames_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” ZIP archive of all video frames if requested.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated reframe video file.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation.

---

## fal-ai/wan-fun-control

**Title:** Wan 2.2 Fun Control
**Description:** Generate pose or depth controlled video using Alibaba-PAI's Wan 2.2 Fun
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan-fun-control`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** wan, pose, depth

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `WanFunControlRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `control_video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate the video.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The negative prompt to generate the video.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `27` вҖ” The number of inference steps.
  Range: min: 4, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `6` вҖ” The guidance scale.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” The shift for the scheduler.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed for the random number generator.
- **`match_input_num_frames`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to match the number of frames in the input video.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `81` вҖ” The number of frames to generate. Only used when match_input_num_frames is False.
  Range: min: 49, max: 121
- **`match_input_fps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to match the fps in the input video.
- **`fps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `16` вҖ” The fps to generate. Only used when match_input_fps is False.
  Range: min: 4, max: 60
- **`control_video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the control video to use as a reference for the video generation.
- **`preprocess_video`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the video. If True, the video will be preprocessed to depth or pose.
- **`preprocess_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"depth"` вҖ” The type of preprocess to apply to the video. Only used when preprocess_video is True.
  Possible values: `depth`, `pose`
- **`reference_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the reference image to use as a reference for the video generation.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanFunControlResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The video generated by the model.

---

## fal-ai/wan-motion

**Title:** Wan Motion
**Description:** Wan Motion is a streamlined character animation model that transfers motion from a driving video onto a reference character image. Based on Wan-Animate which preserves the original character's proportions, Simple uses pose retargeting to adapt the driving video's skeleton to match the reference character's body shape, producing more natural results when the two have different builds. It outputs at 720p with optimized defaults for fast, high-quality generation вҖ” just provide a video, an image, and an optional prompt.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan-motion`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `WanAnimateSimpleInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`, `image_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the driving video (provides the motion).
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the reference image (provides the character appearance).
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Optional text prompt to guide the generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility.
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration level to use. 'regular' enables caching for faster generation, 'none' disables it.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`
- **`adapt_motion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Adapts the driving video's motion to match the reference image's body proportions. Recommended when the driving video subject and reference image have different body shapes or sizes.
- **`enhance_identity`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Enhances identity preservation by preprocessing the reference image with Flux Kontext Edit before animation. Produces more faithful face and appearance transfer at the cost of slightly longer processing time.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, input and output will be checked for safety.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanAnimateSimpleOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The prompt used for generation.

---

## fal-ai/wan-vace

**Title:** Vace
**Description:** Vace a video generation model that uses a source image, mask, and video to create prompted videos with controllable sources.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan-vace`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-to-video, image-to-video, text-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `WanT2VRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to guide video generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"bright colors, overexposed, static, blurred details, subtitles, style, artwork, painting, picture, still, overall gray, worst quality, low quality, JPEG compression residue, ugly, incomplete, extra fingers, poorly drawn hands, poorly drawn faces, deformed, disfigured, malformed limbs, fused fingers, still picture, cluttered background, three legs, many people in the background, walking backwards"` вҖ” Negative prompt for video generation.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `81` вҖ” Number of frames to generate. Must be between 81 to 100 (inclusive). Works only with only reference images as input if source video or mask video is provided output len would be same as source up to 241 frames
- **`frames_per_second`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `16` вҖ” Frames per second of the generated video. Must be between 5 to 24.
- **`task`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"depth"` вҖ” Task type for the model.
  Possible values: `depth`, `inpainting`
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Shift parameter for video generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video (480p,580p, or 720p).
  Possible values: `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"16:9"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated video (16:9 or 9:16).
  Possible values: `auto`, `9:16`, `16:9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 40
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the source video file. If provided, the model will use this video as a reference.
- **`mask_video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the source mask file. If provided, the model will use this mask as a reference.
- **`mask_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the guiding mask file. If provided, the model will use this mask as a reference to create masked video. If provided mask video url will be ignored.
- **`ref_image_urls`** *(array)* вҖ” Urls to source reference image. If provided, the model will use this image as reference.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the input video.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanT2VResponse`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.

---

## fal-ai/wan-vace-1-3b

**Title:** Wan Vace 1 3b
**Description:** Vace a video generation model that uses a source image, mask, and video to create prompted videos with controllable sources.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan-vace-1-3b`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `WanT2VRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to guide video generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"bright colors, overexposed, static, blurred details, subtitles, style, artwork, painting, picture, still, overall gray, worst quality, low quality, JPEG compression residue, ugly, incomplete, extra fingers, poorly drawn hands, poorly drawn faces, deformed, disfigured, malformed limbs, fused fingers, still picture, cluttered background, three legs, many people in the background, walking backwards"` вҖ” Negative prompt for video generation.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `81` вҖ” Number of frames to generate. Must be between 81 to 100 (inclusive). Works only with only reference images as input if source video or mask video is provided output len would be same as source up to 241 frames
  Range: min: 81, max: 240
- **`frames_per_second`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `16` вҖ” Frames per second of the generated video. Must be between 5 to 24.
  Range: min: 5, max: 24
- **`task`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"depth"` вҖ” Task type for the model.
  Possible values: `depth`, `inpainting`, `pose`
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Shift parameter for video generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video (480p,580p, or 720p).
  Possible values: `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"16:9"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated video (16:9 or 9:16).
  Possible values: `auto`, `9:16`, `16:9`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 40
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the source video file. If provided, the model will use this video as a reference.
- **`mask_video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the source mask file. If provided, the model will use this mask as a reference.
- **`mask_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the guiding mask file. If provided, the model will use this mask as a reference to create masked video. If provided mask video url will be ignored.
- **`ref_image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” Urls to source reference image. If provided, the model will use this image as reference.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the input video.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanT2VResponse`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.

## fal-ai/wan-vace-14b

**Title:** Wan VACE 14B
**Description:** VACE is a video generation model that uses a source image, mask, and video to create prompted videos with controllable sources.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan-vace-14b`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** image-to-video, video-to-video, text-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `WanVACERequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to guide video generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"letterboxing, borders, black bars, bright colors, overexposed, static, blurred details, subtitles, style, artwork, painting, picture, still, overall gray, worst quality, low quality, JPEG compression residue, ugly, incomplete, extra fingers, poorly drawn hands, poorly drawn faces, deformed, disfigured, malformed limbs, fused fingers, still picture, cluttered background, three legs, many people in the background, walking backwards"` вҖ” Negative prompt for video generation.
- **`match_input_num_frames`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the number of frames in the generated video will match the number of frames in the input video. If false, the number of frames will be determined by the num_frames parameter.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `81` вҖ” Number of frames to generate. Must be between 81 to 241 (inclusive).
  Range: min: 17, max: 241
- **`match_input_frames_per_second`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the frames per second of the generated video will match the input video. If false, the frames per second will be determined by the frames_per_second parameter.
- **`frames_per_second`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `16` вҖ” Frames per second of the generated video. Must be between 5 to 30. Ignored if match_input_frames_per_second is true.
- **`task`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"depth"` вҖ” Task type for the model.
  Possible values: `depth`, `pose`, `inpainting`, `outpainting`, `reframe`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `240p`, `360p`, `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `16:9`, `1:1`, `9:16`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Guidance scale for classifier-free guidance. Higher values encourage the model to generate images closely related to the text prompt.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`sampler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"unipc"` вҖ” Sampler to use for video generation.
  Possible values: `unipc`, `dpm++`, `euler`
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Shift parameter for video generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 15
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the source video file. If provided, the model will use this video as a reference.
- **`mask_video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the source mask file. If provided, the model will use this mask as a reference.
- **`mask_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the guiding mask file. If provided, the model will use this mask as a reference to create masked video. If provided mask video url will be ignored.
- **`ref_image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” URLs to source reference image. If provided, the model will use this image as reference.
- **`first_frame_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the first frame of the video. If provided, the model will use this frame as a reference.
- **`last_frame_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the last frame of the video. If provided, the model will use this frame as a reference.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the input video.
- **`acceleration`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration to use for inference. Options are 'none' or 'regular'. Accelerated inference will very slightly affect output, but will be significantly faster.
  Possible values: `none`, `low`, `regular`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`num_interpolated_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Number of frames to interpolate between the original frames. A value of 0 means no interpolation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`temporal_downsample_factor`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Temporal downsample factor for the video. This is an integer value that determines how many frames to skip in the video. A value of 0 means no downsampling. For each downsample factor, one upsample factor will automatically be applied.
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`enable_auto_downsample`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the model will automatically temporally downsample the video to an appropriate frame length for the model, then will interpolate it back to the original frame length.
- **`auto_downsample_min_fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” The minimum frames per second to downsample the video to. This is used to help determine the auto downsample factor to try and find the lowest detail-preserving downsample factor. The default value is appropriate for most videos, if you are using a video with very fast motion, you may need to increase this value. If your video has a very low amount of motion, you could decrease this value to allow for higher downsampling and thus longer sequences.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`interpolator_model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"film"` вҖ” The model to use for frame interpolation. Options are 'rife' or 'film'.
  Possible values: `rife`, `film`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`transparency_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"content_aware"` вҖ” The transparency mode to apply to the first and last frames. This controls how the transparent areas of the first and last frames are filled.
  Possible values: `content_aware`, `white`, `black`
- **`return_frames_zip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, also return a ZIP file containing all generated frames.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanVACEResponse`
- **`frames_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” ZIP archive of all video frames if requested.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.

---

- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Shift parameter for video generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 15
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the source video file. Required for depth task.
- **`ref_image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” URLs to source reference image. If provided, the model will use this image as reference.
- **`first_frame_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the first frame of the video. If provided, the model will use this frame as a reference.
- **`last_frame_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the last frame of the video. If provided, the model will use this frame as a reference.

- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the input video.
- **`acceleration`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration to use for inference. Options are 'none' or 'regular'. Accelerated inference will very slightly affect output, but will be significantly faster.
  Possible values: `none`, `low`, `regular`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`num_interpolated_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Number of frames to interpolate between the original frames. A value of 0 means no interpolation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`temporal_downsample_factor`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Temporal downsample factor for the video. This is an integer value that determines how many frames to skip in the video. A value of 0 means no downsampling. For each downsample factor, one upsample factor will automatically be applied.
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`enable_auto_downsample`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the model will automatically temporally downsample the video to an appropriate frame length for the model, then will interpolate it back to the original frame length.
- **`auto_downsample_min_fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” The minimum frames per second to downsample the video to. This is used to help determine the auto downsample factor to try and find the lowest detail-preserving downsample factor. The default value is appropriate for most videos, if you are using a video with very fast motion, you may need to increase this value. If your video has a very low amount of motion, you could decrease this value to allow for higher downsampling and thus longer sequences.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`interpolator_model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"film"` вҖ” The model to use for frame interpolation. Options are 'rife' or 'film'.
  Possible values: `rife`, `film`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`transparency_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"content_aware"` вҖ” The transparency mode to apply to the first and last frames. This controls how the transparent areas of the first and last frames are filled.
  Possible values: `content_aware`, `white`, `black`
- **`return_frames_zip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, also return a ZIP file containing all generated frames.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanVACEDepthResponse`
- **`frames_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” ZIP archive of all video frames if requested.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated depth video file.
