## fal-ai/wan-vace-14b/inpainting

**Title:** Wan VACE 14B
**Description:** VACE is a video generation model that uses a source image, mask, and video to create prompted videos with controllable sources.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan-vace-14b/inpainting`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** image-to-video, video-to-video, text-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `WanVACEInpaintingRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to guide video generation.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"letterboxing, borders, black bars, bright colors, overexposed, static, blurred details, subtitles, style, artwork, painting, picture, still, overall gray, worst quality, low quality, JPEG compression residue, ugly, incomplete, extra fingers, poorly drawn hands, poorly drawn faces, deformed, disfigured, malformed limbs, fused fingers, still picture, cluttered background, three legs, many people in the background, walking backwards"` вҖ” Negative prompt for video generation.
- **`match_input_num_frames`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the number of frames in the generated video will match the number of frames in the input video. If false, the number of frames will be determined by the num_frames parameter.
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `81` вҖ” Number of frames to generate. Must be between 81 to 241 (inclusive).
  Range: min: 17, max: 241
- **`match_input_frames_per_second`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the frames per second of the generated video will match the input video. If false, the frames per second will be determined by the frames_per_second parameter.
- **`frames_per_second`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `16` вҖ” Frames per second of the generated video. Must be between 5 to 30. Ignored if match_input_frames_per_second is true.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `240p`, `360p`, `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `16:9`, `1:1`, `9:16`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Guidance scale for classifier-free guidance. Higher values encourage the model to generate images closely related to the text prompt.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`sampler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"unipc"` вҖ” Sampler to use for video generation.
  Possible values: `unipc`, `dpm++`, `euler`
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Shift parameter for video generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 15
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the source video file. Required for inpainting.
- **`mask_video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the source mask file. Required for inpainting.
- **`mask_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the guiding mask file. If provided, the model will use this mask as a reference to create masked video using salient mask tracking. Will be ignored if mask_video_url is provided.
- **`ref_image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” Urls to source reference image. If provided, the model will use this image as reference.
- **`first_frame_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the first frame of the video. If provided, the model will use this frame as a reference.
- **`last_frame_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the last frame of the video. If provided, the model will use this frame as a reference.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`preprocess`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to preprocess the input video.
- **`acceleration`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration to use for inference. Options are 'none' or 'regular'. Accelerated inference will very slightly affect output, but will be significantly faster.
  Possible values: `none`, `low`, `regular`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`num_interpolated_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Number of frames to interpolate between the original frames. A value of 0 means no interpolation.
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`temporal_downsample_factor`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Temporal downsample factor for the video. This is an integer value that determines how many frames to skip in the video. A value of 0 means no downsampling. For each downsample factor, one upsample factor will automatically be applied.
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`enable_auto_downsample`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the model will automatically temporally downsample the video to an appropriate frame length for the model, then will interpolate it back to the original frame length.
- **`auto_downsample_min_fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” The minimum frames per second to downsample the video to. This is used to help determine the auto downsample factor to try and find the lowest detail-preserving downsample factor. The default value is appropriate for most videos, if you are using a video with very fast motion, you may need to increase this value. If your video has a very low amount of motion, you could decrease this value to allow for higher downsampling and thus longer sequences.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`interpolator_model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"film"` вҖ” The model to use for frame interpolation. Options are 'rife' or 'film'.
  Possible values: `rife`, `film`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`transparency_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"content_aware"` вҖ” The transparency mode to apply to the first and last frames. This controls how the transparent areas of the first and last frames are filled.
  Possible values: `content_aware`, `white`, `black`
- **`return_frames_zip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, also return a ZIP file containing all generated frames.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanVACEInpaintingResponse`
- **`frames_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” ZIP archive of all video frames if requested.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated inpainting video file.

---

**Title:** Wan VACE 14B

- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.

- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated video.

- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Guidance scale for classifier-free guidance. Higher values encourage the model to generate images closely related to the text prompt.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`sampler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"unipc"` вҖ” Sampler to use for video generation.
  Possible values: `unipc`, `dpm++`, `euler`
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Shift parameter for video generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 15
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the source video file. Required for outpainting.
- **`ref_image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” URLs to source reference image. If provided, the model will use this image as reference.
- **`first_frame_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the first frame of the video. If provided, the model will use this frame as a reference.

- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion.
- **`acceleration`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration to use for inference. Options are 'none' or 'regular'. Accelerated inference will very slightly affect output, but will be significantly faster.
  Possible values: `none`, `low`, `regular`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.

- **`temporal_downsample_factor`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Temporal downsample factor for the video. This is an integer value that determines how many frames to skip in the video. A value of 0 means no downsampling. For each downsample factor, one upsample factor will automatically be applied.
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`enable_auto_downsample`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the model will automatically temporally downsample the video to an appropriate frame length for the model, then will interpolate it back to the original frame length.
- **`auto_downsample_min_fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” The minimum frames per second to downsample the video to. This is used to help determine the auto downsample factor to try and find the lowest detail-preserving downsample factor. The default value is appropriate for most videos, if you are using a video with very fast motion, you may need to increase this value. If your video has a very low amount of motion, you could decrease this value to allow for higher downsampling and thus longer sequences.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`interpolator_model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"film"` вҖ” The model to use for frame interpolation. Options are 'rife' or 'film'.

  Possible values: `content_aware`, `white`, `black`
- **`return_frames_zip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, also return a ZIP file containing all generated frames.
- **`expand_left`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to expand the video to the left.
- **`expand_right`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to expand the video to the right.
- **`expand_top`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to expand the video to the top.
- **`expand_bottom`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to expand the video to the bottom.
- **`expand_ratio`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.25` вҖ” Amount of expansion. This is a float value between 0 and 1, where 0.25 adds 25% to the original video size on the specified sides.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanVACEOutpaintingResponse`
- **`frames_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” ZIP archive of all video frames if requested.

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation.

  Possible values: `unipc`, `dpm++`, `euler`

  Range: min: 1, max: 15
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the source video file. Required for pose task.
- **`ref_image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” URLs to source reference image. If provided, the model will use this image as reference.

  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.

- **`temporal_downsample_factor`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Temporal downsample factor for the video. This is an integer value that determines how many frames to skip in the video. A value of 0 means no downsampling. For each downsample factor, one upsample factor will automatically be applied.
  Range: min: 0, max: 5
- **`enable_auto_downsample`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the model will automatically temporally downsample the video to an appropriate frame length for the model, then will interpolate it back to the original frame length.
- **`auto_downsample_min_fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” The minimum frames per second to downsample the video to. This is used to help determine the auto downsample factor to try and find the lowest detail-preserving downsample factor. The default value is appropriate for most videos, if you are using a video with very fast motion, you may need to increase this value. If your video has a very low amount of motion, you could decrease this value to allow for higher downsampling and thus longer sequences.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60

- **`enable_auto_downsample`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the model will automatically temporally downsample the video to an appropriate frame length for the model, then will interpolate it back to the original frame length.
- **`auto_downsample_min_fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” The minimum frames per second to downsample the video to. This is used to help determine the auto downsample factor to try and find the lowest detail-preserving downsample factor. The default value is appropriate for most videos, if you are using a video with very fast motion, you may need to increase this value. If your video has a very low amount of motion, you could decrease this value to allow for higher downsampling and thus longer sequences.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`interpolator_model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"film"` вҖ” The model to use for frame interpolation. Options are 'rife' or 'film'.
  Possible values: `rife`, `film`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`transparency_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"content_aware"` вҖ” The transparency mode to apply to the first and last frames. This controls how the transparent areas of the first and last frames are filled.
  Possible values: `content_aware`, `white`, `black`
- **`return_frames_zip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, also return a ZIP file containing all generated frames.
- **`zoom_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Zoom factor for the video. When this value is greater than 0, the video will be zoomed in by this factor (in relation to the canvas size,) cutting off the edges of the video. A value of 0 means no zoom.
  Range: min: 0, max: 0.9
- **`trim_borders`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to trim borders from the video.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanVACEReframeResponse`
- **`frames_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” ZIP archive of all video frames if requested.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated reframe video file.

---

## fal-ai/wan-vace-apps/long-reframe

**Title:** Wan 2.1 VACE Long Reframe
**Description:** Reframe entire videos scene-by-scene using Wan VACE 2.1
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan-vace-apps/long-reframe`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `LongWanVACEReframeRequest`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The text prompt to guide video generation. Optional for reframing.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"letterboxing, borders, black bars, bright colors, overexposed, static, blurred details, subtitles, style, artwork, painting, picture, still, overall gray, worst quality, low quality, JPEG compression residue, ugly, incomplete, extra fingers, poorly drawn hands, poorly drawn faces, deformed, disfigured, malformed limbs, fused fingers, still picture, cluttered background, three legs, many people in the background, walking backwards"` вҖ” Negative prompt for video generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `240p`, `360p`, `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `16:9`, `1:1`, `9:16`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 50
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Guidance scale for classifier-free guidance. Higher values encourage the model to generate images closely related to the text prompt.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`sampler`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"unipc"` вҖ” Sampler to use for video generation.
  Possible values: `unipc`, `dpm++`, `euler`
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Shift parameter for video generation.
  Range: min: 1, max: 15
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL to the source video file. This video will be used as a reference for the reframe task.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
- **`acceleration`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration to use for inference. Options are 'none' or 'regular'. Accelerated inference will very slightly affect output, but will be significantly faster.
  Possible values: `none`, `low`, `regular`
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the generated video.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the generated video.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`enable_auto_downsample`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable auto downsample.
- **`auto_downsample_min_fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `6` вҖ” Minimum FPS for auto downsample.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`interpolator_model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"film"` вҖ” The model to use for frame interpolation. Options are 'rife' or 'film'.
  Possible values: `rife`, `film`
- **`sync_mode`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.
- **`transparency_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"content_aware"` вҖ” The transparency mode to apply to the first and last frames. This controls how the transparent areas of the first and last frames are filled.
  Possible values: `content_aware`, `white`, `black`
- **`return_frames_zip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, also return a ZIP file containing all generated frames.
- **`zoom_factor`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Zoom factor for the video. When this value is greater than 0, the video will be zoomed in by this factor (in relation to the canvas size,) cutting off the edges of the video. A value of 0 means no zoom.
  Range: min: 0, max: 0.9
- **`trim_borders`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to trim borders from the video.
- **`scene_threshold`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `30` вҖ” Threshold for scene detection sensitivity (0-100). Lower values detect more scenes.
  Range: min: 0, max: 100
- **`paste_back`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to paste back the reframed scene to the original video.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `LongWanVACEReframeResponse`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The output video file.

---

## fal-ai/wan-vace-apps/video-edit

**Title:** Wan VACE Video Edit
**Description:** Edit videos using plain language and Wan VACE
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan-vace-apps/video-edit`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-edit, wan-vace

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `WanVACEVideoEditRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Prompt to edit the video.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video.
- **`video_type`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” The type of video you're editing. Use 'general' for most videos, and 'human' for videos emphasizing human subjects and motions. The default value 'auto' means the model will guess based on the first frame of the video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `general`, `human`
- **`image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” Default: `[]` вҖ” URLs of the input images to use as a reference for the generation.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Resolution of the edited video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `240p`, `360p`, `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`acceleration`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration to use for inference. Options are 'none' or 'regular'. Accelerated inference will very slightly affect output, but will be significantly faster.
  Possible values: `none`, `low`, `regular`
- **`enable_auto_downsample`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable automatic downsampling. If your video has a high frame rate or is long, enabling longer sequences to be generated. The video will be interpolated back to the original frame rate after generation.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the edited video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`
- **`auto_downsample_min_fps`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `15` вҖ” The minimum frames per second to downsample the video to.
  Range: min: 1, max: 60
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable the safety checker.
- **`return_frames_zip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to include a ZIP archive containing all generated frames.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanVACEVideoEditResponse`
- **`frames_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” ZIP archive of generated frames if requested.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The edited video.

---

## fal-ai/wan-vision-enhancer

**Title:** Wan Vision Enhancer
**Description:** Wan Vision Enhancer for magnify/enhance video with high fidelity and creativity.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan-vision-enhancer`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** stylized, transform

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Input`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to enhance with Wan Video. Maximum 200MB file size. Videos longer than 500 frames will have only the first 500 frames processed (~8-21 seconds depending on fps).
- **`target_resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” Target output resolution for the enhanced video. 720p (native, fast) or 1080p (upscaled, slower). Processing is always done at 720p, then upscaled if 1080p selected.
  Possible values: `720p`, `1080p`
- **`creativity`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Controls how much the model enhances/changes the video. 0 = Minimal change (preserves original), 1 = Subtle enhancement (default), 2 = Medium enhancement, 3 = Strong enhancement, 4 = Maximum enhancement.
  Range: min: 0, max: 4
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional prompt to prepend to the VLM-generated description. Leave empty to use only the auto-generated description from the video.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"oversaturated, overexposed, static, blurry details, subtitles, stylized, artwork, painting, still frame, overall gray, worst quality, low quality, JPEG artifacts, ugly, mutated, extra fingers, poorly drawn hands, poorly drawn face, deformed, disfigured, malformed limbs, fused fingers, static motion, cluttered background, three legs, crowded background, walking backwards"` вҖ” Negative prompt to avoid unwanted features.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If not provided, a random seed will be used.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Output`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The enhanced video file.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” The timings of the different steps in the workflow.

---

## fal-ai/wan/v2.2-14b/animate/move

**Title:** Wan-2.2 Animate Move
**Description:** Wan-Animate is a video model that generates high-fidelity character videos by replicating the expressions and movements of characters from reference videos.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan/v2.2-14b/animate/move`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video to video, motion

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `WanAnimateMoveRequest`
**Required fields:** `video_url`, `image_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video.
- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input image. If the input image does not match the chosen aspect ratio, it is resized and center cropped.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale. Higher values give better adherence to the prompt but may decrease quality.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"480p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video (480p, 580p, or 720p).
  Possible values: `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `20` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 40
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, input data will be checked for safety before processing.
- **`enable_output_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, output video will be checked for safety after generation.
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Shift value for the video. Must be between 1.0 and 10.0.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the output video. Higher quality means better visual quality but larger file size.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the output video. Faster write mode means faster results but larger file size, balanced write mode is a good compromise between speed and quality, and small write mode is the slowest but produces the smallest file size.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`return_frames_zip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, also return a ZIP archive containing per-frame images generated on GPU (lossless).
- **`use_turbo`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, applies quality enhancement for faster generation with improved quality. When enabled, parameters are automatically optimized for best results.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanAnimateMoveResponse`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation
- **`frames_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” ZIP archive of generated frames (if requested).
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation (auto-generated by the model)

---

## fal-ai/wan/v2.2-14b/animate/replace

**Title:** Wan-2.2 Animate Replace
**Description:** Wan-Animate Replace is a model that can integrate animated characters into reference videos, replacing the original character while preserving the sceneвҖҷs lighting and color tone for seamless environmental integration.

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input image. If the input image does not match the chosen aspect ratio, it is resized and center cropped.
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale. Higher values give better adherence to the prompt but may decrease quality.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"480p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video (480p, 580p, or 720p).
  Possible values: `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `20` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 40
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, input data will be checked for safety before processing.
- **`enable_output_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, output video will be checked for safety after generation.
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Shift value for the video. Must be between 1.0 and 10.0.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the output video. Higher quality means better visual quality but larger file size.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the output video. Faster write mode means faster results but larger file size, balanced write mode is a good compromise between speed and quality, and small write mode is the slowest but produces the smallest file size.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`return_frames_zip`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, also return a ZIP archive containing per-frame images generated on GPU (lossless).
- **`use_turbo`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, applies quality enhancement for faster generation with improved quality. When enabled, parameters are automatically optimized for best results.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanAnimateReplaceResponse`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation
- **`frames_zip`** *(?)* вҖ” ZIP archive of generated frames (if requested).
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt used for generation (auto-generated by the model)

## fal-ai/wan/v2.2-a14b/video-to-video

**Title:** Wan
**Description:** Wan-2.2 video-to-video is a video model that generates high-quality videos with high visual quality and motion diversity from text prompts and source videos.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan/v2.2-a14b/video-to-video`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `WanV2VRequest`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The text prompt to guide video generation.
- **`strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.9` вҖ” Strength of the video transformation. A value of 1.0 means the output will be completely based on the prompt, while a value of 0.0 means the output will be identical to the input video.
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`num_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `81` вҖ” Number of frames to generate. Must be between 17 to 161 (inclusive).
  Range: min: 17, max: 161
- **`frames_per_second`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `16` вҖ” Frames per second of the generated video. Must be between 4 to 60. When using interpolation and `adjust_fps_for_interpolation` is set to true (default true,) the final FPS will be multiplied by the number of interpolated frames plus one. For example, if the generated frames per second is 16 and the number of interpolated frames is 1, the final frames per second will be 32. If `adjust_fps_for_interpolation` is set to false, this value will be used as-is.
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Negative prompt for video generation.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"720p"` вҖ” Resolution of the generated video (480p, 580p, or 720p).
  Possible values: `480p`, `580p`, `720p`
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated video. If 'auto', the aspect ratio will be determined automatically based on the input video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`
- **`num_inference_steps`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `27` вҖ” Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
  Range: min: 2, max: 40
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, input data will be checked for safety before processing.
- **`enable_output_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If set to true, output video will be checked for safety after generation.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt expansion. This will use a large language model to expand the prompt with additional details while maintaining the original meaning.
- **`acceleration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"regular"` вҖ” Acceleration level to use. The more acceleration, the faster the generation, but with lower quality. The recommended value is 'regular'.
  Possible values: `none`, `regular`
- **`guidance_scale`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `3.5` вҖ” Classifier-free guidance scale. Higher values give better adherence to the prompt but may decrease quality.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`guidance_scale_2`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `4` вҖ” Guidance scale for the second stage of the model. This is used to control the adherence to the prompt in the second stage of the model.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`shift`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `5` вҖ” Shift value for the video. Must be between 1.0 and 10.0.
  Range: min: 1, max: 10
- **`interpolator_model`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"film"` вҖ” The model to use for frame interpolation. If None, no interpolation is applied.
  Possible values: `none`, `film`, `rife`
- **`num_interpolated_frames`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Number of frames to interpolate between each pair of generated frames. Must be between 0 and 4.
  Range: min: 0, max: 4
- **`adjust_fps_for_interpolation`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If true, the number of frames per second will be multiplied by the number of interpolated frames plus one. For example, if the generated frames per second is 16 and the number of interpolated frames is 1, the final frames per second will be 32. If false, the passed frames per second will be used as-is.
- **`video_quality`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"high"` вҖ” The quality of the output video. Higher quality means better visual quality but larger file size.
  Possible values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `maximum`
- **`video_write_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"balanced"` вҖ” The write mode of the output video. Faster write mode means faster results but larger file size, balanced write mode is a good compromise between speed and quality, and small write mode is the slowest but produces the smallest file size.
  Possible values: `fast`, `balanced`, `small`
- **`resample_fps`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” If true, the video will be resampled to the passed frames per second. If false, the video will not be resampled.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `WanV2VResponse`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file.
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” The text prompt used for video generation.

---

## fal-ai/wan/v2.7/edit-video

**Title:** Wan
**Description:** Wan 2.7 is the latest generation AI video model, delivering enhanced motion smoothness, superior scene fidelity, and greater visual coherence.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/wan/v2.7/edit-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** stylized, transform, lipsync

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Wan27VideoEditInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Editing instruction or style transfer description.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the input video to edit. Format: MP4, MOV. Duration: 2-10s. Max 100 MB.

Max file size: 100.0MB, Min duration: 2s, Max duration: 10s, Timeout: 30.0s
- **`reference_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Reference image URL for reference-based editing.
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1080p"` вҖ” Output video resolution tier.
  Possible values: `720p`, `1080p`
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(string)* вҖ” Aspect ratio of the generated video. If not provided, uses the aspect ratio of the input video.
  Possible values: `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`, `4:3`, `3:4`
- **`duration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Output video duration in seconds. Default 0 means match input video duration. When set (2-10), truncates from the start.
  Possible values: `0`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `5`, `6`, `7`, `8`, `9`, `10`
- **`audio_setting`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"auto"` вҖ” Audio handling. 'auto': model decides whether to regenerate audio. 'origin': preserve original audio from input video.
  Possible values: `auto`, `origin`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility (0-2147483647).
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable content moderation for input and output.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `Wan27VideoEditOutput`
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation.
- **`actual_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The actual prompt used if prompt rewriting was enabled.
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The edited video file.

---

## fal-ai/workflow-utilities/auto-subtitle

**Title:** Workflow Utilities
**Description:** Add automatic subtitles to videos
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/workflow-utilities/auto-subtitle`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** auto-subtitle, captioning

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `AutoSubtitleInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video file to add automatic subtitles to
- **`language`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"en"` вҖ” Language code for transcription (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'fr', 'de', 'it', 'pt', 'nl', 'ja', 'zh', 'ko') or 3-letter ISO code (e.g., 'eng', 'spa', 'fra')
- **`font_name`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"Montserrat"` вҖ” Any Google Font name from fonts.google.com (e.g., 'Montserrat', 'Poppins', 'BBH Sans Hegarty')
- **`font_size`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `100` вҖ” Font size for subtitles (TikTok style uses larger text)
  Range: min: 20, max: 150
- **`font_weight`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"bold"` вҖ” Font weight (TikTok style typically uses bold or black)
  Possible values: `normal`, `bold`, `black`
- **`font_color`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"white"` вҖ” Subtitle text color for non-active words
  Possible values: `white`, `black`, `red`, `green`, `blue`, `yellow`, `orange`, `purple`, `pink`, `brown`, `gray`, `cyan`, `magenta`
- **`highlight_color`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"purple"` вҖ” Color for the currently speaking word (karaoke-style highlight)
  Possible values: `white`, `black`, `red`, `green`, `blue`, `yellow`, `orange`, `purple`, `pink`, `brown`, `gray`, `cyan`, `magenta`
- **`stroke_width`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Text stroke/outline width in pixels (0 for no stroke)
  Range: min: 0, max: 10
- **`stroke_color`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"black"` вҖ” Text stroke/outline color
  Possible values: `black`, `white`, `red`, `green`, `blue`, `yellow`, `orange`, `purple`, `pink`, `brown`, `gray`, `cyan`, `magenta`
- **`background_color`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"none"` вҖ” Background color behind text ('none' or 'transparent' for no background)
  Possible values: `black`, `white`, `red`, `green`, `blue`, `yellow`, `orange`, `purple`, `pink`, `brown`, `gray`, `cyan`, `magenta`, `none`, `transparent`
- **`background_opacity`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” Background opacity (0.0 = fully transparent, 1.0 = fully opaque)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`position`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"bottom"` вҖ” Vertical position of subtitles
  Possible values: `top`, `center`, `bottom`
- **`y_offset`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `75` вҖ” Vertical offset in pixels (positive = move down, negative = move up)
  Range: min: -200, max: 200
- **`words_per_subtitle`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `3` вҖ” Maximum number of words per subtitle segment. Use 1 for single-word display, 2-3 for short phrases, or 8-12 for full sentences.
  Range: min: 1, max: 12
- **`enable_animation`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Enable animation effects for subtitles (bounce style entrance)

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `AutoSubtitleOutput`
- **`transcription_metadata`** *(object)* вҖ” Additional transcription metadata from ElevenLabs (language, segments, etc.)
- **`words`** *(array)* вҖ” Word-level timing information from transcription service
- **`transcription`** *(string)* вҖ” Full transcription text
- **`subtitle_count`** *(integer)* вҖ” Number of subtitle segments generated
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The video with automatic subtitles

---

## fal-ai/workflow-utilities/blend-video

**Title:** Workflow Utilities
**Description:** FFMPEG Utility for Blending Videos
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/workflow-utilities/blend-video`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `BlendVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `top_video_url`, `bottom_video_url`

- **`top_video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the top layer video
- **`bottom_video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the bottom layer video
- **`blend_mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"overlay"` вҖ” Blend mode to use for combining the videos
  Possible values: `addition`, `average`, `burn`, `darken`, `difference`, `divide`, `dodge`, `exclusion`, `grainextract`, `grainmerge`, `hardlight`, `lighten`, `multiply`, `negation`, `normal`, `overlay`, `phoenix`, `pinlight`, `reflect`, `screen`, `softlight`, `subtract`, `vividlight`
- **`opacity`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Opacity of the top layer (0.0-1.0)
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`shortest`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” End output when the shortest input ends

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `BlendVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The blended video output

---

## fal-ai/workflow-utilities/scale-video

**Title:** Workflow Utilities
**Description:** FFMPEG Utilities to Scale Videos
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/workflow-utilities/scale-video`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ScaleVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video file to scale/resize. Height and Width of the video must be even numbers for compatibility with video codecs.
- **`width`** *(integer)* вҖ” Target width in pixels. If only width is provided, height is auto-calculated to preserve aspect ratio. At least one of width or height must be provided.
- **`height`** *(integer)* вҖ” Target height in pixels. If only height is provided, width is auto-calculated to preserve aspect ratio. At least one of width or height must be provided.
- **`mode`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"stretch"` вҖ” Scaling mode. 'stretch' scales the video to the exact target dimensions (may distort aspect ratio). 'pad' scales to fit within the target dimensions while preserving aspect ratio, then pads with the chosen color to fill the remaining space (letterbox/pillarbox). 'crop' scales to cover the target dimensions while preserving aspect ratio, then center-crops to the exact target size.
  Possible values: `stretch`, `pad`, `crop`
- **`pad_color`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"black"` вҖ” Padding color when mode is 'pad'. Ignored for other modes.
  Possible values: `black`, `white`, `red`, `green`, `blue`, `gray`
- **`codec`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"libx264"` вҖ” Video codec to use for encoding. libx264 (H.264) is widely compatible, libx265 (H.265/HEVC) offers better compression.
  Possible values: `libx264`, `libx265`
- **`preset`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"fast"` вҖ” Encoding speed preset. Slower presets give better compression but take longer.
  Possible values: `ultrafast`, `fast`, `medium`, `slow`
- **`crf`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `18` вҖ” Constant Rate Factor for quality (0-51). Lower values mean better quality and larger files. 18 is visually lossless for most content.
  Range: min: 0, max: 51

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ScaleVideoOutput`
- **`scaled_width`** *(integer)* вҖ” Width of the output video in pixels
- **`original_height`** *(integer)* вҖ” Height of the original video in pixels
- **`scaled_height`** *(integer)* вҖ” Height of the output video in pixels
- **`original_width`** *(integer)* вҖ” Width of the original video in pixels
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The scaled/resized video

---

## fal-ai/workflow-utilities/trim-video

**Title:** Workflow Utilities
**Description:** FFMPEG Utility for Trim Video
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/workflow-utilities/trim-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `TrimVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the video file to trim
- **`start_time`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Start time in seconds
  Range: min: 0
- **`end_time`** *(number)* вҖ” End time in seconds. If not provided, uses duration instead.
- **`duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” Duration in seconds from start_time. Ignored if end_time is provided.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `TrimVideoOutput`
- **`original_duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Duration of the original video in seconds
- **`trimmed_duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Duration of the trimmed video in seconds
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The trimmed video

---

## mirelo-ai/sfx-v1.5/video-to-video

**Title:** Mirelo SFX V1.5
**Description:** Generate synced sounds for any video, and return it with its new sound track (like MMAudio)

**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-to-video, sfx

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `Input`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” A video url that can accessed from the API to process and add sound effects
- **`text_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Additional description to guide the model
- **`num_samples`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `2` вҖ” The number of samples to generate from the model
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `8069` вҖ” The seed to use for the generation. If not provided, a random seed will be used
- **`duration`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `10` вҖ” The duration of the generated audio in seconds
- **`start_offset`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0` вҖ” The start offset in seconds to start the audio generation from

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `VideoOutput`
- **`video`** *(list<Video-Output>)* вҖ” The processed video with sound effects

## moonvalley/marey/motion-transfer

**Title:** Marey Realism V1.5
**Description:** Pull motion from a reference video and apply it to new subjects or scenes.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/moonvalley/marey/motion-transfer`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MareyInputMotionTransfer`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The prompt to generate a video from
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” The URL of the video to use as the control video.
- **`first_frame_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"https://video-editor-files-prod.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/users/1e4d46df-0702-4491-95ce-763592f33f34/uploaded-images/9b9dce1c-abd0-46c0-bac9-9454f8893b06/original"` вҖ” Optional first frame image URL to use as the first frame of the generated video
- **`reference_image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Optional reference image URL to use for pose control or as a starting frame
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"<synthetic> <scene cut> low-poly, flat shader, bad rigging, stiff animation, uncanny eyes, low-quality textures, looping glitch, cheap effect, overbloom, bloom spam, default lighting, game asset, stiff face, ugly specular, AI artifacts"` вҖ” Negative prompt used to guide the model away from undesirable features.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `-1` вҖ” Seed for random number generation. Use -1 for random seed each run.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MareyOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video.

---

- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `"<synthetic> <scene cut> low-poly, flat shader, bad rigging, stiff animation, uncanny eyes, low-quality textures, looping glitch, cheap effect, overbloom, bloom spam, default lighting, game asset, stiff face, ugly specular, AI artifacts"` вҖ” Negative prompt used to guide the model away from undesirable features.

## veed/video-background-removal

**Title:** Video Background Removal
**Description:** Remove background from any video with people and objects. No green screen needed.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/veed/video-background-removal`
**Category:** video-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `GeneralRembgInput`
**Required fields:** `video_url`

- **`video_url`** *(string)*
- **`output_codec`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"vp9"` вҖ” Single VP9 video with alpha channel or two videos (rgb and alpha) in H264 format. H264 is recommended for better RGB quality.
  Possible values: `vp9`, `h264`
- **`refine_foreground_edges`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Improves the quality of the extracted object's edges.
- **`subject_is_person`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Set to False if the subject is not a person.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `GeneralRembgOutput`
- **`video`** *(list<File>)*

---

  Possible values: `vp9`, `h264`
- **`refine_foreground_edges`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Improves the quality of the extracted object's edges.

  Possible values: `vp9`, `h264`
- **`spill_suppression_strength`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.8` вҖ” Increase the value if green spots remain in the video, decrease if color changes are noticed on the extracted subject.

## wan/v2.6/reference-to-video

**Title:** Wan v2.6 Reference to Video
**Description:** Wan 2.6 reference-to-video model.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/wan/v2.6/reference-to-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** reference-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ReferenceToVideoInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_urls`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Use @Video1, @Video2, @Video3 to reference subjects from your videos. Works for people, animals, or objects. For multi-shot prompts: '[0-3s] Shot 1. [3-6s] Shot 2.' Max 800 characters.
- **`video_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” Reference videos for subject consistency (1-3 videos). Videos' FPS must be at least 16 FPS.Reference in prompt as @Video1, @Video2, @Video3. Works for people, animals, or objects.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"16:9"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the generated video.
  Possible values: `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`, `4:3`, `3:4`
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1080p"` вҖ” Video resolution tier. R2V only supports 720p and 1080p (no 480p).
  Possible values: `720p`, `1080p`
- **`duration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"5"` вҖ” Duration of the generated video in seconds. R2V supports only 5 or 10 seconds (no 15s).
  Possible values: `5`, `10`
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Negative prompt to describe content to avoid. Max 500 characters.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt rewriting using LLM.
- **`multi_shots`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When true (default), enables intelligent multi-shot segmentation for coherent narrative videos with multiple shots. When false, generates single continuous shot. Only active when enable_prompt_expansion is True.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ReferenceToVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation
- **`actual_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The actual prompt used if prompt rewriting was enabled

---

## wan/v2.6/reference-to-video/flash

**Title:** V2.6
**Description:** Wan 2.6 reference-to-video flash model.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/wan/v2.6/reference-to-video/flash`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** reference-to-video

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `ReferenceToVideoFlashInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Use Character1, Character2, etc. to reference subjects from your reference files. Works for people, animals, or objects. For multi-shot prompts: '[0-3s] Shot 1. [3-6s] Shot 2.' Max 1500 characters. Reference order: video_urls first, then image_urls.
- **`video_urls`** *(array)* вҖ” Reference videos for subject consistency (0-3 videos). Videos' FPS must be at least 16 FPS. Combined with image_urls, total references cannot exceed 5. Reference order: video_urls are numbered first (Character1, Character2...), then image_urls continue the sequence.
- **`image_urls`** *(array)* вҖ” Reference images for subject consistency (0-5 images). Combined with video_urls, total references cannot exceed 5. Formats: JPEG, JPG, PNG (no alpha), BMP, WEBP. Resolution: 240-5000px. Max 10MB each. Reference order: image_urls continue numbering after video_urls.
- **`aspect_ratio`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"16:9"` вҖ” The aspect ratio of the generated video.
  Possible values: `16:9`, `9:16`, `1:1`, `4:3`, `3:4`
- **`resolution`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"1080p"` вҖ” Video resolution tier. R2V Flash only supports 720p and 1080p.
  Possible values: `720p`, `1080p`
- **`duration`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"5"` вҖ” Duration of the generated video in seconds. R2V Flash supports only 5 or 10 seconds.
  Possible values: `5`, `10`
- **`negative_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Default: `""` вҖ” Negative prompt to describe content to avoid. Max 500 characters.
- **`enable_prompt_expansion`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to enable prompt rewriting using LLM.
- **`multi_shots`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” When true (default), enables intelligent multi-shot segmentation for coherent narrative videos with multiple shots. When false, generates single continuous shot. Only active when enable_prompt_expansion is True.
- **`enable_audio`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to generate a video with audio. Set to false for silent video generation. Silent videos are faster and cost 25% of the audio version price.
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.
- **`enable_safety_checker`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ReferenceToVideoOutput`
- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The generated video file
- **`seed`** *(integer)* вҖ” The seed used for generation
- **`actual_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” The actual prompt used if prompt rewriting was enabled

---

## xai/grok-imagine-video/extend-video

**Title:** Grok Imagine Extend Video
**Description:** Extend videos with xAI's Grok Imagine video model
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/xai/grok-imagine-video/extend-video`
**Category:** video-to-video
**Tags:** video-edit, v2v, grok, xai

**Required fields:** `prompt`, `video_url`

- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Text description of what should happen next in the video.
- **`video_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the source video to extend. Must be MP4 format (H.264, H.265, or AV1 codec), 2-15 seconds long.
- **`duration`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `6` вҖ” Length of the extension in seconds.
  Range: min: 2, max: 10

- **`video`** *()* вҖ” The extended video (original + extension stitched together).

**Title:** Arbiter
**Description:** Semantic image alignment measurements

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `SemanticImageMeasurementInput`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MultiMeasurementOutput`
- **`values`** *(list<object>)* вҖ” The values of the measurements.

## fal-ai/moondream/batched

**Title:** Moondream
**Description:** Answer questions from the images.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/moondream/batched`
**Category:** vision
**Tags:** multimodal, vision

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `BatchedMoondreamInput`
**Required fields:** `inputs`

- **`model_id`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"vikhyatk/moondream2"` вҖ” Model ID to use for inference
  Possible values: `vikhyatk/moondream2`, `fal-ai/moondream2-docci`
- **`inputs`** *(list<MoondreamInputParam>)* вҖ” List of input prompts and image URLs
- **`max_tokens`** *(integer)* вҖ” Default: `64` вҖ” Maximum number of new tokens to generate
  Range: min: 32, max: 1024
- **`temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `0.2` вҖ” Temperature for sampling
  Range: max: 1
- **`top_p`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Top P for sampling
  Range: min: 0, max: 1
- **`repetition_penalty`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” Repetition penalty for sampling
  Range: min: 1, max: 2

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `BatchedMoondreamOutput`
- **`filenames`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” Filenames of the images processed
- **`outputs`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” List of generated outputs
- **`partial`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether the output is partial
- **`timings`** *(object)* вҖ” Timings for different parts of the process

---

## fal-ai/moondream3-preview/caption

**Title:** Moondream3 Preview [Caption]
**Description:** Moondream 3 is a vision language model that brings frontier-level visual reasoning with native object detection, pointing, and OCR capabilities to real-world applications requiring fast, inexpensive inference at scale.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/moondream3-preview/caption`
**Category:** vision
**Tags:** Vision

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MoondreamCaptionInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to be processed
- **`length`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"normal"` вҖ” Length of the caption to generate
  Possible values: `short`, `normal`, `long`
- **`temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 1. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic. If not set, defaults to 0.
- **`top_p`** *(number)* вҖ” Nucleus sampling probability mass to use, between 0 and 1.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MoondreamCaptionOutput`
- **`finish_reason`** *(string)* вҖ” Reason for finishing the output generation
- **`output`** *(string)* вҖ” Generated caption for the image
- **`usage_info`** *()* вҖ” Usage information for the request

---

- **`finish_reason`** *(string)* вҖ” Reason for finishing the output generation
- **`objects`** *(list<Object>)* вҖ” List of detected objects with their bounding boxes
- **`usage_info`** *()* вҖ” Usage information for the request

## fal-ai/moondream3-preview/point

**Title:** Moondream3 Preview [Point]
**Description:** Moondream 3 is a vision language model that brings frontier-level visual reasoning with native object detection, pointing, and OCR capabilities to real-world applications requiring fast, inexpensive inference at scale.

- **`points`** *(list<Point>)* вҖ” List of points marking the detected objects

## fal-ai/moondream3-preview/query

**Title:** Moondream 3 Preview [Query]
**Description:** Moondream 3 is a vision language model that brings frontier-level visual reasoning with native object detection, pointing, and OCR capabilities to real-world applications requiring fast, inexpensive inference at scale.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/fal-ai/moondream3-preview/query`
**Category:** vision
**Tags:** Vision

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `MoondreamQueryInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `prompt`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” URL of the image to be processed
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Query to be asked in the image
- **`reasoning`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `true` вҖ” Whether to include detailed reasoning behind the answer
- **`temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 1. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic. If not set, defaults to 0.
- **`top_p`** *(number)* вҖ” Nucleus sampling probability mass to use, between 0 and 1.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `MoondreamQueryOutput`
- **`finish_reason`** *(string)* вҖ” Reason for finishing the output generation
- **`output`** *(string)* вҖ” Answer to the query about the image
- **`reasoning`** *(string)* вҖ” Detailed reasoning behind the answer, if enabled
- **`usage_info`** *()* вҖ” Usage information for the request

---

## openrouter/router/vision

**Title:** OpenRouter [Vision]
**Description:** Run any Vision Language Model with fal. Analyze and understand images using Claude (Anthropic), GPT-5 / GPT-4o (OpenAI), Gemini (Google), Grok (xAI), Llama (Meta), Qwen, Pixtral (Mistral), and more. Send one or multiple images for captioning, analysis, OCR, or visual Q&A. Powered by OpenRouter. 
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/openrouter/router/vision`
**Category:** vision

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `VisionInput`
**Required fields:** `prompt`, `image_urls`, `model`

- **`image_urls`** *(list<string>)* вҖ” List of image URLs to be processed
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Prompt to be used for the image
- **`system_prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” System prompt to provide context or instructions to the model
- **`model`** *(string)* вҖ” Name of the model to use. Charged based on actual token usage.
- **`reasoning`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Should reasoning be the part of the final answer.
- **`temperature`** *(number)* вҖ” Default: `1` вҖ” This setting influences the variety in the model's responses. Lower values lead to more predictable and typical responses, while higher values encourage more diverse and less common responses. At 0, the model always gives the same response for a given input.
  Range: min: 0, max: 2
- **`max_tokens`** *(integer)* вҖ” This sets the upper limit for the number of tokens the model can generate in response. It won't produce more than this limit. The maximum value is the context length minus the prompt length.

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `VisionOutput`
- **`output`** *(string)* вҖ” Generated output
- **`usage`** *(?)* вҖ” Token usage information

---

## perceptron/isaac-01

**Title:** Isaac 0.1
**Description:** Isaac-01 is a multimodal vision-language model from Perceptron for various vision language tasks.
**Endpoint:** `https://fal.run/perceptron/isaac-01`
**Category:** vision
**Tags:** multimodal, vision

### рҹ“Ҙ Input Schema: `VisionInput`
**Required fields:** `image_url`, `prompt`

- **`image_url`** *(string)* вҖ” Image URL to be processed
- **`prompt`** *(string)* вҖ” Prompt to be used for the image
- **`response_style`** *(enum)* вҖ” Default: `"text"` вҖ” 
Response style to be used for the image.

- text: Model will output text. Good for descriptions and captioning.
- box: Model will output a combination of text and bounding boxes. Good for
localization.
- point: Model will output a combination of text and points. Good for counting many
objects.
- polygon: Model will output a combination of text and polygons. Good for granular
segmentation.

  Possible values: `text`, `box`, `point`, `polygon`

### рҹ“Ө Output Schema: `ChatOutput`
- **`usage`** *(?)* вҖ” Usage information
- **`error`** *(string)* вҖ” Error message if an error occurred
- **`partial`** *(boolean)* вҖ” Default: `false` вҖ” Whether the output is partial
- **`output`** *(string)* вҖ” Generated output
